The North-West plume of NGC 2936 and the North faint tidal bridge between galaxy would be the result of that initial configuration of the system: the perpendicular orientation of the spiral disk with respect to the orbital plane.

sources.summary: NOEMA CO maps show Arp 142 has molecular gas spread through a distorted disk, an off-center ring-like structure, strong internal SFE variation, and evidence for a recent high-speed head-on collision.

quotes.quote: The CO in Arp 142 is widely distributed over a highly distorted disk without any nuclear concentration, and an off-centric ring-like structure is discovered in channel maps. The SFE varies significantly within Arp 142, with a starburst region (Region 1) near the eastern tip of the distorted disk showing an SFE 0.3 dex above the mean of the control sample of isolated galaxies, and the SFE of the main disk (Region 4) 0.43 dex lower than the mean of the control sample. In contrast, the CO emission in Arp 238 is detected only in two compact sources at the galactic centers. Compared to the control sample, Arp 238-E shows an SFE enhancement of more than 1 dex whereas Arp 238-W has an enhancement of 0.7 dex. We suggest that the extended CO distribution and the large SFE variation in Arp 142 are due to an expanding large-scale ring triggered by a recent high-speed head-on collision between the spiral galaxy and the elliptical galaxy, and the compact CO sources with high SFEs in Arp 238 are associated with nuclear starbursts induced by gravitational tidal torques in a low-speed coplanar interaction.

Interestingly, there appears to be an off-centric ring-like structure in the channel maps of v < 7000 km s^ 1 , most clearly visible in the channel of v = 6893 km s^ 1 . It seems to grow in size from v =6765 km s^ 1 to v =6957 km s^ 1 . This could be an expanding cone-like structure in 3-D with the top of the cone moving toward the observer (i.e. blue-shifted), which is also the direction of the motion of the elliptical companion ( z=0.02265 , corresponding to v=6795 km s^ 1 ). Notably, most CO of v > 7000 km s^ 1 is in the north, and the channel maps are consistent with a highly distorted rotating disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp142 as an arc-like system without a definable ring, with emission concentrated near the northern nucleus and spiral structure visible at longer wavelengths.

quotes.quote: Arp142: No ring structure can be defined in this arc-like galaxy. Hence, the plotted ellipse is not a match to the ring, and is used only to obtain azimuthally averaged profiles. Note that the nucleus is located at the northern part of the arc, from where most of the \ emission originates. The position of the 20 cm radio continuum emission mapped by mol92 coincides with the bright \ emitting zone. At longer wavelengths, a two-armed spiral structure is clearly seen emanating from the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper cites Arp 142 as a close S+E major-merger pair with resolved CO emission, depressed disk SFE in its star-forming galaxy, and a high-speed head-on collision that suppresses star formation.

quotes.quote: resolved the CO (1-0) emission in two close major-merger pairs, Arp 238 (S+S) and Arp 142 (S+E). They found significant differences in local SFE among the two systems.
On the other hand, the disk of the star-forming galaxy of Arp 142 has an SFE 0.4 dex below the mean SFE of control galaxies.

These authors attribute this difference to the very different interaction orbits: while the low-speed coplanar orbit of Arp 238 results in strong tidal torque which triggers nuclear starbursts, the high-speed head-on collision in Arp 142 produces a density-wave shock that suppresses the SFR in the system.

gmw_00009850.name: Arp291
gmw_00009850.overview: Arp291 is a galaxy whose continuum structure resembles a tightly wound, one-armed spiral rather than a classical ring galaxy [arXiv:0807.1477]. Its ionized emission is concentrated in regions surrounding the nucleus and along a minor-axis filament, with no spectroscopically confirmed companion detected [arXiv:0807.1477].

sources.summary: The paper argues Arp291's ionized emission is nuclear and filamentary rather than ring-associated, and that its continuum structure may be a tightly wound one-armed spiral.

quotes.quote: Arp291: \ emission in this galaxy is not associated with the ring that is seen in the continuum bands. Most of the \ emission comes from regions surrounding the nucleus, and extends along a filament along the minor axis of the ring. The \ emission at the nucleus itself is weak. A faint spur seen on the northeastern side of the nucleus on the continuum images gives this galaxy an appearance of a tightly-wound one-armed spiral, and hence this galaxy may not be a classical ring galaxy. There is no spectroscopically confirmed companion for this galaxy.

gmw_00009851.name: IZw45
gmw_00009851.overview: IZw45 is a ring galaxy that features a continuum bridge connecting its nucleus to a companion galaxy [arXiv:0807.1477]. Its emission traces most of the ring structure, with the brightest emitting region coinciding with the nucleus [arXiv:0807.1477].

sources.summary: The paper finds IZw45's emission traces most of its ring, with the brightest emission at the nucleus and a continuum bridge connecting toward the companion.

quotes.quote: IZw45: \ emission traces most of the ring, with the brightest \ emitting region coinciding with the nucleus. The continuum ring is slightly flatter and bigger than the \ ring. The continuum images show a bridge that connects the nucleus of the ring galaxy to the companion. This is the only galaxy where the colors at the center of the ring are bluer than at the ring itself. This is most likely due to the contamination of the radial profiles by the presence of the nucleus that extends all the way to the center of the ring..

gmw_00009852.name: VIIZw466
gmw_00009852.overview: VIIZw466 is a ring galaxy whose emission is concentrated primarily in the western half of its ring, with almost no emission detected from its red nucleus [arXiv:0807.1477]. Star formation activity is also detected in its edge-on companion galaxy, which has been identified as the intruder responsible for the ring structure [arXiv:0807.1477].

sources.summary: The paper reports that VIIZw466's emission is concentrated in the western ring, weak at the red nucleus, and also detected in its edge-on intruder companion.

quotes.quote: VIIZw466: Most of the \ emission originates from the western half of the ring, with hardly any emission detected from the red nucleus. Comparable \ emission is also detected from the edge-on companion G2 . This detection confirms the earlier suggestion of star formation activity in this galaxy by app99 based on mid infrared emission. acs96 had confirmed G2 as the intruder galaxy responsible for the ring in VIIZw466. The object to the northwest of G1 is a background galaxy.

gmw_00009853.name: NGC5410
gmw_00009853.overview: NGC5410 is a disturbed barred spiral galaxy rather than a ring galaxy [arXiv:0807.1477]. It exhibits emission throughout its structure and shows clear signs of interaction with its companion galaxy [arXiv:0807.1477].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC5410 as resembling a disturbed barred spiral rather than a ring galaxy, with emission throughout the galaxy and companion interaction signatures.

quotes.quote: NGC5410: In morphology, this galaxy resembles a disturbed barred spiral galaxy, rather than a ring galaxy. \ emission is detected from throughout the galaxy and its companion. The companion shows clear signs of interaction.

gmw_00009854.name: IRAS 03152--4427
gmw_00009854.overview: IRAS 03152--4427 is a luminous infrared galaxy located in the Abell 3112 cluster [arXiv:1003.2629]. It contains a substantial mass of cold dust and exhibits a moderate rate of star formation [arXiv:1003.2629].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 03152--4427 as a luminous infrared galaxy in Abell 3112, with far-infrared data indicating moderate star formation and substantial cold dust.

quotes.quote: Of the five selected galaxies, BLAST J031700--441605 is also the IRAS source IRAS 03152--4427.

For IRAS 03152--4427, data at 60 and 100 are also available from the IRAS Faint Source catalogue ( Moshir90 ). In particular, 100 data allow for better constraints on the shape of the far-IR SED (the 60 point already samples the warm dust emission and is not suitable to fit a single-component SED). We find for IRAS 03152--4427 a best-fit temperature T=(23.4 0.8) K, a total far-infrared (FIR) luminosity of (1.2 0.1) 10^ 11 L _ , a cold dust mass of (1.7 0.1) 10^8 M _ and an SFR of (20.2 1.9) . This source is thus identified as a luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) with moderate star-formation.

gmw_00009855.name: The brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in A3112
aliases_local: The brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in A3112
gmw_00009855.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy in A3112 is an optical-line and radio source [arXiv:1003.2629]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus, which is supported by the presence of a coincident X-ray point source and radio lobes [arXiv:1003.2629].

sources.original_name: The brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in A3112
sources.summary: The paper describes A3112's BCG as an optical-line and radio source whose weak far-infrared limits contrast with Spitzer MIR emission likely affected by AGN activity.

quotes.quote: The brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in A3112 exhibits strong optical line emission and a powerful radio source. It was targeted with Spitzer IRAC and MIPS observations by Quillen08 , who find a weak MIR excess at 24 . They derive an infrared luminosity of 2.2 10^ 10 L _ which equates to an SFR of 4 ( O'Dea08 ). The BLAST results are at face value inconsistent with this SFR, with a 2 detection at 250 of 57 28 mJy and upper limits of 64 and 46 mJy at 350 and 500 (assuming three times the map .m.s.). This translates to an upper limit of 3.1 10^ 9 L _ for the FIR luminosity and 0.6 for the SFR.

However, the Spitzer and BLAST luminosities are derived using different methods and the Spitzer derived value is potentially over-estimated due to the MIR contribution of an AGN. The presence of an AGN is consistent with the results of Takizawa03 , who find an X-ray point source coincident with the core of the BCG and an X-ray excess associated with radio lobes.

gmw_00009856.name: SDSS J0019+00
gmw_00009856.overview: SDSS J0019+00 is an S0 galaxy with a barred spiral structure and a bright Seyfert 2 nucleus [arXiv:1910.11912]. It consists primarily of an old stellar population with little ongoing star formation, and it exhibits low-level optical variability [arXiv:1910.11912].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J0019+00 as a nearby S0 Seyfert 2 TDE host with little ongoing star formation, an old stellar population, and high central concentration.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: From Gaia , the nucleus of the host galaxy is (R.A., decl.) = ( 00^ h 19^ m 47^ s .33493, +00^ 35'26''.8126 )

quotes.quote: SDSS J0019+00 is a S0 galaxy huertas-company2011 , lacking any evidence of large-scale spiral arms, having a bright nucleus reminiscent of Seyfert 2 galaxies. The yellow/red appearance of the galaxy in the three-color image indicates that there is no substantial ongoing star formation. A barred spiral structure, having a angular scale of 5 -10 (diameter), is seen in the three-color image. There may be a fainter disk/shell-like feature extending beyond this spiral structure, possibly indicative of a past merger, although this will have to be verified through a more substantive analysis. The SDSS light curve between 2002 and 2008 shows small amplitude optical variability (about 0.5 mag in u and bands), indicating low-level (optical) AGN activity.

Based on the 4000 break strength and the Balmer absorption-line index H _A , estimate the mean stellar age of SDSS J0019+00 to be about 1.5 Gyr. The specific SFR is about 5 10^ 11 per year e.g. chang2015,vanDriel2016 . These findings confirm that SDSS J0019+00 consists primarily of old stars, and lacks significant ongoing star formation.

gmw_00009857.name: NGC 4469
gmw_00009857.overview: NGC 4469 is an edge-on SB0 lenticular galaxy that exhibits a boxy outer structure tilted with respect to its inner disk [arXiv:2006.14896]. The major axis of the galaxy is heavily obscured by dust, yet it still displays a robust peanut-shaped signature [arXiv:1603.00019].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4469 as an SB0 edge-on galaxy with a boxy tilted outer structure, likely produced by galaxy harassment or satellite accretion.

quotes.quote: We consider two SB0 edge-on galaxies, NGC 4469 and NGC 4452, which exhibit apparent tilted outer discs with respect to the inner structure. In NGC 4469, this structure has a boxy shape, inclined by PA 3 with respect to the inner disc, whereas NGC 4452 harbours a discy outer structure with PA 6 . In spite of the different shapes, both structures have surface brightness profiles close to exponential and make a large contribution ( 30 ) to the total galaxy luminosity.

For NGC 4469, a plausible explanation may also be galaxy harassment, which resulted in a tilted or even a tumbling dark matter halo. A less likely possibility is accretion of gas-rich satellites several Gyr ago.

sources.summary: The paper notes heavy dust obscuration in NGC 4469, uses the reddest SDSS image, and still recovers a robust noisy B6 peanut signature.

quotes.quote: NGC 4469 -- The major axis of this galaxy is heavily obscured by dust. As there is no NIR image available, we used the reddest SDSS passband ( z ) image, which has relatively poor S/N, and still shows the dust slightly, in the inner regions (see residual map). Nevertheless, the B_6 profile, though noisy, is robust and very nicely shows the peanut signature.

gmw_00009858.name: Object 54061
gmw_00009858.overview: Object 54061 is a nearby, low-mass Lyman-break galaxy analogue with a redshift of 0.074 [arXiv:1705.07655]. It is a young, metal-poor system with a stellar mass of approximately 10 to the power of 8 solar masses and a detected radio flux [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 54061 is a nearby, low-mass, young, metal-poor LBA with FIRST radio detection, elongated optical structure, measured size, and likely galaxy-wide superwinds.

quotes.quote: Object 54061 is the most nearby object with z=0.074 and the least massive of our radio sample with log(mass) 8.0 from SED fitting. Additionally, it is among the youngest objects in our sample with a best-fitting age of 16 Myr and has a metallicity of 0.23 Z _ . These physical properties make it a good local analogue to high-redshift LBGs. It is the only source in our large LBA sample which is identified in FIRST radio source catalogue with an integrated flux of 570 Jy (compared to our measurements of 470 70 Jy).

gmw_00009859.name: Object 27473
gmw_00009859.overview: Object 27473 is a metal-poor and dust-free galaxy with an estimated age of 500 million years [arXiv:1705.07655]. It serves as a local analogue for Lyman-break galaxies, exhibiting a low radio star formation rate surface density [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 27473 is metal- and dust-poor, an acceptable local analogue, with VLA/ATCA observations, low radio SFR surface density, and upper-limit size caveats.

quotes.quote: Object 27473 is both one of our most metal-poor sources with Z=0.18 Z _ , as well as one of the most dust-free objects in our radio sample with E(B-V)=0.05 from Balmer decrement measurements using SDSS spectroscopy. Together with its SED-derived mass of log(mass/M _ ) 9.2 and age of 500 Myr this makes this object an acceptable local analogue. It was observed by both the VLA and ATCA, with a measured radio slope of = -0.16 0.57 . Its large measured projected size of 3.97 0.97 arcsec and low radio SFR of 0.8 0.2 result in the lowest _ SFR of the radio sample of 0.1 M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 .

gmw_00009860.name: Object 16911
gmw_00009860.overview: Object 16911 is a massive, high-metallicity galaxy with an estimated age of 400 million years [arXiv:1705.07655]. It exhibits a steep radio slope and is considered an unlikely analogue to z ~ 5 Lyman-break galaxies [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 16911 is a high-metallicity, massive source unlikely to mimic z 5 LBGs; its radio slope and BPT position suggest possible obscured AGN activity.

quotes.quote: Object 16911 is an intriguing source and can be seen in figure tab:Contours1 : despite fulfilling the optical and ultraviolet selection criteria, this source has by far the highest metallicity in our radio sample at 1.62 Z _ (though it should be noted that the metallicity conversion used is unlikely to produce reliable results in such an extreme object). SED-fitting indicates a mass of log(mass/M _ ) 9.8 and age of 400 Myr. These physical properties make Object 16911 an unlikely analogue to z 5 LBGs. It was observed by both the VLA and ATCA, producing the steepest of the observed radio slopes with = -0.49 0.29 .

gmw_00009861.name: Object 23734
gmw_00009861.overview: Object 23734 is a compact star-forming galaxy with a young dominant stellar population aged 16 million years [arXiv:1705.07655]. Although previously classified as a potential Seyfert 1 galaxy, its radio spectral slope and high star formation rate density indicate its emission is star-formation driven [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 23734 is a compact, high-SFR-density source whose radio slope and strong star formation favor a star-forming galaxy despite prior Seyfert classification.

quotes.quote: Object 23734 was observed by both the VLA and ATCA with >6 detections in both, giving rise to a radio spectral slope of = -0.19 0.21. It had previously been classified as a potential Seyfert 1 galaxies due to its strong emission lines; however, both its spectral slope and high radio star formation rate (SFR=6.0M _ yr ^ 1 ), indicate a star-formation driven galaxy. Since it has one of the smallest projected area of the sources in this sample (see figure tab:extendedContours for its contour plot), the _ SFR determined for this source is among the highest in our sample at 3.5 M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 . Its SED-derived mass of log(mass/M _ ) 8.3 and young dominant stellar population (with an age of 16 Myr), as well as its low dust attenuation ( E(B-V) 0.26 ) and metallicity (0.34 Z _ ) make Object 23734 a good local analogue to high-redshift LBGs.

gmw_00009862.name: Object 83073
gmw_00009862.overview: Object 83073 is a moderately massive galaxy with an estimated stellar population age of 600 Myr [arXiv:1705.07655]. It is characterized as a very dust- and metal-poor system that has been detected in the radio spectrum by the VLA [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 83073 is a VLA-detected, dust- and metal-poor galaxy with moderate mass and older stellar population, making it acceptable but not ideal as an analogue.

quotes.quote: Object 83073 was detected at 3.2 in our VLA observations. It is a very dust- and metal-poor galaxy with a Balmer-derived E(B-V) value of 0.09, and 0.14 Z _ . With an SED-derived mass of log(mass) 9.6, and a stellar population age of 600 Myr, it is an acceptable, though not ideal, local analogue. Its SDSS detection and radio contours are shown in figure tab:Contours1 .

gmw_00009863.name: Object 76428
gmw_00009863.overview: Object 76428 is a galaxy with a modest radio-derived star formation rate of 2 solar masses per year [arXiv:1705.07655]. It has a low star formation rate surface density and likely experiences strong superwinds [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 76428 has modest radio SFR, likely strong superwinds, intermediate mass and age, and a plausible recent starburst behind its radio deficit.

quotes.quote: Object 76428 was observed with the VLA, with an observed SNR of 4.5. It has a radio-derived SFR of 2 M _ yr ^ 1 , and one of the lowest _ SFR at 0.2 M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 . However, as the imfit-derived projected areas are upper limits, this _ SFR is a lower limit (its radio contours are shown in figure tab:Contours1 ). As it lies above the critical value found by Heckman2002 , it is likely that the galaxy experiences strong superwinds.

gmw_00009864.name: Object 24784
gmw_00009864.overview: Object 24784 is a Lyman-break galaxy analogue with a very young stellar population and low dust content [arXiv:1705.07655]. Its flat radio spectral slope is consistent with zero, indicating star-formation driven radio emission that has not yet produced sufficient supernovae to establish strong synchrotron emission [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 24784 was observed by VLA, ATCA, and APEX; its flat radio slope, very young population, low dust, and low mass make it a strong LBG analogue.

quotes.quote: Object 24784 is one of two sources in this sample which were observed with VLA, ATCA, and APEX. It was detected at 4.3 and 5.6 in the VLA and ATCA data respectively, but undetected in APEX. Radio contours and its SDSS detection are shown in figure tab:Contours1 . The derived radio spectral slope is consistent with zero (see Table table:Results_withSpecSlope ), and indicative of star-formation driven radio emission, with a stellar population that has not yet produced sufficient supernovae to establish a strong synchrotron emission spectrum.

gmw_00009865.name: Object 80573
gmw_00009865.overview: Object 80573 is a massive galaxy with an estimated stellar population age of 1 Gyr [arXiv:1705.07655]. Its radio star formation rate exceeds its H-alpha star formation rate, which is consistent with an established supernova-driven radio continuum [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 80573 is massive and old, with radio SFR above H-alpha SFR, consistent with an established supernova-driven continuum and poor z 5 analogue status.

quotes.quote: Object 80573 is both one of the most massive of our radio sources with log(mass) 10 from SED-fitting and one of the oldest objects with an SED-derived age of 1 Gyr. It was detected in our VLA observations with a SNR of 5.6 (see figure tab:Contours1 for its contour plot), and is one of four objects in our sample whose radio SFR exceeds the H SFR at 2.7 0.5 M _ yr ^ 1 and 1.8 M _ yr ^ 1 respectively. This appears consistent with a massive galaxy whose dominant stellar populating is reaching an age at which a supernova-driven radio continuum has been established. It is hence unlikely that this object would make a good local analogue to z 5 LBGs.

gmw_00009866.name: Object 08959
gmw_00009866.overview: Object 08959 is a compact galaxy detected by the Very Large Array [arXiv:1705.07655]. It exhibits a high star formation rate density and shows signs of a recent starburst [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 08959 is a compact VLA detection with high SFR density, recent starburst signs, low dust and metallicity, and good high-redshift LBG analogue properties.

quotes.quote: Object 08959 produced a 7.3 detection in our VLA observations. It has one of the smallest projected areas derived from the radio observations, resulting in one of the highest _ SFR of our radio sample at 1.5 M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 . The radio detection is clearly coincident with the SDSS detection, as shown in figure tab:Contours1 . At an SED-derived age of 100 Myr and an H SFR of 7.6 M _ yr ^ 1 (a factor of 2 higher than its radio star formation rate), this source appears to show a recent starburst which has not yet established a strong radio continuum.

gmw_00009867.name: Object 53150
gmw_00009867.overview: Object 53150 is a dust-poor local analogue to distant galaxies with a stellar mass of log(mass) 9.3 [arXiv:1705.07655]. It has a best-fitting age of 250 Myr, a subsolar gas-phase metallicity, and a moderate star formation rate [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 53150 has VLA radio detection, a moderate SFR, 250 Myr age, low dust, and subsolar metallicity, making it a good distant-galaxy analogue.

quotes.quote: Object 53150 was observed by the VLA, producing an 8.8 detection with an observered radio flux indicating a SFR of 3.3 M _ yr ^ 1 . With a best-fitting age of 250 Myr and a stellar mass of log(mass) 9.3 , Object 53150 is a good local analogue to distant galaxies. It is very dust-poor with an E(B-V) value of 0.11 derived from Balmer-decrement measurements, and has a gas-phase metallicity of 0.35 Z _ .

gmw_00009868.name: Object 60392
gmw_00009868.overview: Object 60392 is a massive and old radio source with a stellar mass of approximately 10^10.3 solar masses [arXiv:1705.07655]. It has an estimated age of 10^9.8 years, making it an unlikely local analogue galaxy [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 60392 is one of the most massive and oldest radio sources, unlikely as a local analogue, with a strong but scan-sensitive radio SFR measurement.

quotes.quote: Object 60392 was observed by both the VLA and ATCA. SED-fitting indicates that this is both one of the most massive of our radio sources with log(mass) 10.3, and the oldest one at log(age)=9.8. This suggests that it is unlikely to be a good local analogue galaxy. Similarly to Object 16911, this source was well-detected with a SNR of 6.8 when combining all scans, but produced non-detections in two out of three scans when split into its three constituent scans.

gmw_00009869.name: Object 77821
gmw_00009869.overview: Object 77821 is a massive galaxy with a stellar mass of approximately 10 to the power of 9.9 solar masses [arXiv:1705.07655]. It has an estimated age of 250 million years and exhibits a radio star formation rate that exceeds its H-alpha star formation rate [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 77821 is massive, VLA-detected, and has radio SFR exceeding H-alpha SFR, suggesting an established supernova-driven continuum and questionable LBA status.

quotes.quote: Object 77821 was observed with the VLA, producing a 6 detection. Interestingly, it is, similarly to Object 80573, one of the most massive of our radio sources with log(mass) 9.9 (from SED-fitting) while also having a higher radio SFR than that calculated from H , with 6.7 1.1 M _ yr ^ 1 and 3.9 M _ yr ^ 1 respectively. Given its SED-derived age of 250 Myr, this suggests that Object 77821 did not recently undergo a starburst, and might in fact have an established supernova-driven continuum. In addition, its high mass makes it a questionable LBA candidate.

gmw_00009870.name: Object 76079
gmw_00009870.overview: Object 76079 is a local analogue galaxy with a dominant stellar population aged approximately 400 million years [arXiv:1705.07655]. It is characterized as both dust- and metal-poor, with a stellar mass of log(mass) ~ 9.7 [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 76079 is a high-SNR VLA detection with deconvolved size, intermediate-age stellar population, low dust and metallicity, and acceptable analogue properties.

quotes.quote: Object 76079 produced one of the highest signal-to-noise ratio of our VLA observations, with an 11.3 detection (see figure tab:Contours1 for its contour plot). Casa's `imfit' procedure was able to deconvolve the source from the beam, measuring an angular size of (2.11 0.45) (0.48 0.45) arcsec ^2 . SED-fitting indicates that the source has a dominant stellar population aged 400 Myr and a stellar mass of log(mass) 9.7 , making Object 76079 an acceptable local analogue galaxy. It is both dust- and metal-poor with an E(B-V) value of 0.15 derived from Balmer-decrement measures, and a metallicity 0.22 Z _ .

gmw_00009871.name: Object 37518
gmw_00009871.overview: Object 37518 is a galaxy detected in VLA radio observations with an angular size of approximately 3.41 by 0.99 arcseconds [arXiv:1705.07655]. At an age of 630 million years, it exhibits close agreement between its radio and H-alpha star formation rates [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 37518 is an older VLA-detected galaxy with deconvolved size and agreement between radio and H-alpha SFRs, making it an unlikely local analogue.

quotes.quote: Object 37518 was detected at 5.8 in our VLA observations. The `imfit' procedure was able to deconvolve the source from the beam, indicating an angular size of (3.41 1.6) (0.99 0.87) (its radio contours and SDSS detection are shown in figure tab:Contours1 ). At an age of 630 Myr, this object is one of the oldest of our sample. This might explain why we find good agreement between the derived radio SFR of 2.40 0.41 M _ yr ^ 1 and its H derived SFR of 2.7 M _ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00009872.name: Object 62100
gmw_00009872.overview: Object 62100 is a young, low-mass galaxy with a stellar mass of approximately 10 to the power of 8.7 solar masses [arXiv:1705.07655]. Having a low gas-phase metallicity and an estimated age of 20 million years, it serves as a local analogue to high-redshift Lyman-break galaxies [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 62100 is a young, low-mass, very metal-poor analogue with tentative radio slope and low calibrated radio SFR likely affected by its young age.

quotes.quote: Object 62100 was observed with the VLA, producing a 4 detection, and with ATCA, where it was marginally detected at 2.4 . The resulting radio spectral slope of 0.90 0.53 is an outlier in our sample, and should be considered tentative given the low SNR in the ATCA observations. With an SED-derived age of 20 Myr, a stellar mass of log(mass) 8.7 , and a gas-phase metallicity of 0.09 Z _ , this is a good analogue to z>5 LBGs. We find that it has a relatively low 1.5 GHz SFR of 0.7 M _ yr ^ 1 ; however, this does not take into account that the standard SFR calibrators assume a stable stellar population of >100 Myr, making it likely that the true SFR within Object 62100 is substantially higher.

gmw_00009873.name: Object 57993
gmw_00009873.overview: Object 57993 is a local analogue galaxy with a stellar mass of log(mass) ~ 9.7 and a stellar population age of 300 Myr [arXiv:1705.07655]. It has a metallicity of 0.40 Z_sun, little dust extinction, and a large deconvolved angular size [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 57993 is a VLA-detected source with large deconvolved size, moderate mass and age, little dust, subsolar metallicity, and good analogue status.

quotes.quote: Object 57993 was detected at 4.7 in our VLA observations. Casa's `imfit' procedure was able to deconvolve the source from the beam and derived one of the largest angular sizes in our sample, (4.4 1.3) (3.3 1.2) arcsec ^2 , for it (see figure tab:Contours2 ). With an SED-derived stellar mass of log(mass) 9.7 and stellar population age of 300 Myr, Object 57993 is a good local analogue galaxy. It has little dust extinction ( E(B-V) 0.13 ), and a metallicity of 0.40 Z _ .

gmw_00009874.name: Object 91412
gmw_00009874.overview: Object 91412 is a metal- and dust-poor galaxy with a stellar mass of approximately 10 to the power of 9.6 solar masses [arXiv:1705.07655]. It has a young stellar population age of 200 million years, making it an acceptable local analogue to the earliest galaxies [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 91412 is a VLA-detected, metal- and dust-poor acceptable analogue whose radio deficit relative to H-alpha is plausibly caused by young stellar age.

quotes.quote: Object 91412 was observed by the VLA and detected at 6.0 . It is clearly coincident with its SDSS detection, as shown in figure tab:Contours2 . With a best-fitting stellar population age of 200 Myr, and a stellar mass of log(mass) 9.6, this source is an acceptable local analogue to the earliest galaxies. It is a very metal- and dust-poor galaxy with a gas-phase metallicity of 0.24 Z _ and a Balmer-decrememnt derived dust extinction of E(B-V) 0.05 .

gmw_00009875.name: Object 34524
gmw_00009875.overview: Object 34524 is an old, massive galaxy with an estimated stellar population age of 800 million years [arXiv:1705.07655]. It is undetected in 1.5 GHz radio observations, indicating a low upper limit for its star formation rate [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 34524 is old and massive, undetected in the VLA above the limit, and probably an outlier for z 5 but possible for z 2-3 analogues.

quotes.quote: Object 34524 is one of the oldest and most massive sources in our sample with an SED-derived stellar population age of 800 Myr and a mass of log(mass) 9.9 . This object would hence be an extreme outlier of the z 5 LBG population, but might make a good analogue to the more nearby z 2-3 galaxy population. The source is not detected above a 3 limit in our VLA observations, indicating an upper 1.5 GHz radio SFR of <2.5 M _ yr ^ 1 . Its SDSS detection is shown in figure tab:Contours2 .

gmw_00009876.name: Object 23218
gmw_00009876.overview: Object 23218 is a Lyman-break galaxy at z ~ 5 with a high stellar mass of log(M) ~ 9.9 [arXiv:1705.07655]. It features an old stellar population age of over 600 Myr and a low star formation rate density of 0.06 solar masses per year per square kiloparsec [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 23218 is VLA-detected with one of the largest deconvolved sizes, low SFR density, high mass, and old population compared with z 5 LBGs.

quotes.quote: Object 23218 was detected at 4.9 in VLA observations. At (3.6 1.1) (3.2 1.3) arcsec ^2 , its deconvolved angular size (according to casa's `imfit' procedure; see figure tab:Contours2 for a contour plot) is one of the largest in the sample. Together with a radio-derived SFR of 3.2 M _ yr ^ 1 , this indicates a low _ SFR of 0.06 M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 for this source. Its SED-derived mass of log(mass) 9.9 , and stellar population age of >600 Myr make Object 23218 an extreme object compared to the typical z 5 LBG population.

gmw_00009877.name: Object 05083
gmw_00009877.overview: Object 05083 is a massive and old radio source with an estimated age of 500 million years [arXiv:1705.07655]. It is characterized by low dust reddening and low metallicity, and it was not detected during APEX observations [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 05083 is massive and old but low-dust and metal-poor, APEX-undetected and VLA/ATCA-detected, with radio properties supporting recent starburst interpretation.

quotes.quote: Object 05083 is the second of our sources which was observed by VLA, ATCA, and APEX. It is one of the most massive and oldest of our radio sources with log(mass) 9.9 and an age of 500 Myr. It has low dust reddening with an E(B-V) value derived from the Balmer decrement of 0.12, and a low metallicity of 0.34 Z _ , making it a potentially acceptable local analogue. It was observed, but not detected, using APEX, with a total observing time of 1.2 hours and an rms of 3.9 Jy.

gmw_00009878.name: Object 71294
gmw_00009878.overview: Object 71294 is a young starburst galaxy with an estimated age of 50 million years [arXiv:1705.07655]. It has a low metallicity and a radio spectral slope consistent with thermal emission and synchrotron radiation [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 71294 has VLA and ATCA detections, a radio slope consistent with thermal plus synchrotron emission, young age, low metallicity, and good LBG-analogue status.

quotes.quote: Object 71294 was observed by both the VLA and ATCA, with detections in both bands, allowing us to constrain its radio spectral slope to 0.6 0.42 . This is consistent with a radio spectrum arising from thermal emission, as well as sychrotron radiation due to a recent starburst. This interpretation is given further support by the object's SED-derived age of 50 Myr. Being one of our most metal-poor objects in our radio sample with Z = 0.16 Z _ makes this a good analogue galaxy to the distant LBG populaiton.

gmw_00009879.name: Object 08586
gmw_00009879.overview: Object 08586 is a Lyman-break galaxy with a high stellar mass and a stellar population age of approximately 500 million years [arXiv:1705.07655]. It has been detected with the Very Large Array, indicating a radio star formation rate of 2.7 solar masses per year [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 08586 is VLA-detected and deconvolved, but its high stellar mass and 500 Myr age make it likely an outlier for typical z 5 LBGs.

quotes.quote: Object 08586 was observed with the VLA, producing a 5.6 detection and indicating a radio SFR of 2.7 M _ yr ^ 1 . Casa's `imfit' procedure was able to deconvolve the object from the beam (see figure tab:Contours2 ), determining an angular size of (2.17 0.72) (1.74 0.9) arcsec ^2 . However, with an SED-derived stellar mass of log(mass) 9.9 , and a best-fitting stellar population age of 500 Myr, this source is an likely to constitute an outlier of the typical z 5 LBG sample.

gmw_00009880.name: Object 72856
gmw_00009880.overview: Object 72856 is a dust-poor galaxy with a stellar mass of log(mass) 9.4 and an estimated age of 100 million years [arXiv:1705.07655]. Non-detections in both APEX and VLA observations place an upper limit on its radio star formation rate [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 72856 is dust-poor with acceptable LBA physical properties, but both APEX and VLA non-detections provide only an upper limit on radio SFR.

quotes.quote: Object 72856 is one of the most dust-poor objects in our radio sample with E(B-V)= 0.09 from Balmer decrement measurements. Its SED-derived age of 100 Myr, stellar mass of log(mass) 9.4, and metallicity of 0.24 Z _ make it an acceptable LBA candidate. Both APEX and VLA observations of this source resulted in non-detections, producing an upper 1.5 GHz SFR limit of <2.4 M _ yr ^ 1 . Figure tab:Contours2 shows its radio contours overplotted on its SDSS detection.

gmw_00009881.name: Object 29942
gmw_00009881.overview: Object 29942 is a relatively massive galaxy with a stellar mass of approximately 10 to the power of 9.7 solar masses [arXiv:1705.07655]. It is an older source with an estimated age of 500 million years, and radio observations provide a star formation rate upper limit of less than 2 solar masses per year [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 29942 is relatively massive and old, making it an unlikely local analogue, and the VLA observations give only an upper limit on 1.5 GHz SFR.

quotes.quote: Object 29942 is a relatively massive source in our sample with an SED-derived stellar mass of log(mass) 9.7. It is also among the older sources in this sample (with an SED-derived age of 500 Myr), making it an unlikely local analogue. It was undetected in VLA observations, producing an upper 1.5 GHz SFR limit of <2 M _ yr ^ 1 (see figure tab:Contours2 for a contour plot of this source).

gmw_00009882.name: Object 32420
gmw_00009882.overview: Object 32420 is a massive galaxy with an estimated stellar population age of 500 million years [arXiv:1705.07655]. It is highly star-forming, with a radio-inferred star formation rate of 11.0 solar masses per year [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 32420 is very massive and highly star-forming, unlikely as an LBA candidate, APEX-undetected but VLA-detected with an inferred 1.5 GHz SFR.

quotes.quote: Object 32420 is both one of the most massive of our radio sources with log(mass) 10.2 found using SED-fitting, and the most highly star forming galaxy in our radio sample with H SFR = 41.1M _ yr ^ 1 . Its stellar population age of 500 Myr, together with its other physical properties, makes it an unlikely LBA candidate. The galaxy was observed with both APEX and the VLA, resulting in a non-detection at 345 GHz, but a 5 detection at 1.5GHz (see figure tab:Contours3 for its radio contours and SDSS detection). Its 1.5 GHz flux indicates a SFR of 11.0 2.2 M _ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00009883.name: Object 08755
gmw_00009883.overview: Object 08755 is a distant galaxy with a stellar mass of log(mass) 9.7 and a dominant stellar population age of 125 Myr [arXiv:1705.07655]. It is an acceptable local analogue to the distant galaxy population and has the highest star formation rate in its radio sample [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 08755 is a distant VLA/ATCA-detected source with measured radio slope, acceptable analogue properties, and the highest radio-sample SFR.

quotes.quote: Object 08755 is our most distant source observed with both the VLA and ATCA, producing strong ( >7 ) detections in both (see figure tab:Contours3 for its radio contours). From this, a radio spectral slope of 0.36 0.20 could be determined. With a stellar mass of log(mass) 9.7 and a dominant stellar population age of 125 Myr, this source is an acceptable local analogue to the distant galaxy population. Its strong VLA-detected flux resulted in Object 08755 having the highest SFR of the sources in our radio sample.

gmw_00009884.name: Object 38857
gmw_00009884.overview: Object 38857 is a low-metallicity galaxy with low dust obscuration and a stellar mass of approximately 10^9.7 solar masses [arXiv:1705.07655]. It has an estimated stellar age of 100 million years and exhibits a high radio star formation rate [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 38857 is VLA-detected with high radio SFR, low dust, low metallicity, and age and mass consistent with an acceptable distant-galaxy analogue.

quotes.quote: Object 38857 was detected in our VLA observations, with one of the highest inferred radio SFRs (of 12.0 1.8 M _ yr ^ 1 ). Its stellar mass of log(mass) 9.7, SED-derived age of 100 Myr, low dust obscuration and 0.28 Z _ make this source an acceptable analogue to the distant galaxy population. It is clearly coincident with its SDSS detection, as shown in figure tab:Contours3 .

gmw_00009885.name: Object 55849
gmw_00009885.overview: Object 55849 is a galaxy with a stellar mass of approximately 10^9.7 solar masses and a stellar population age of 250 million years [arXiv:1705.07655]. It was undetected in VLA observations, placing an upper limit on its star formation rate at less than 5.8 solar masses per year [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 55849 is VLA-undetected with an SFR upper limit; its mass and 250 Myr stellar age make it unlikely as a z 5 LBG analogue.

quotes.quote: Object 55849 was not detected above the noise level in our VLA observations, producing an upper limit on the SFR within it of <5.8 M _ yr ^ 1 . With a stellar mass of log(mass) 9.7 and a stellar population age of 250 Myr, this is an unlikely analogue to z 5 LBGs. See figure tab:Contours3 for its SDSS detection.

gmw_00009886.name: Object 15004
gmw_00009886.overview: Object 15004 is a compact local analogue galaxy with a low dust content and low metallicity [arXiv:1705.07655]. It is highly star-forming, showing a strong radio detection and a stellar population age of 200 million years [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 15004 is strongly VLA-detected, highly star-forming, plausibly a low-dust, low-metallicity analogue, and compact in both radio and SDSS imaging.

quotes.quote: Object 15004 produced a very strong detection of 11 in our VLA observations. In both H and radio, it is among the most star forming of our detected radio sources. We note that this source had been suggested to be a Seyfert 1 on SIMBAD due to its strong emission lines. Its stellar mass of log(mass) 9.6 and population age of 200 Myr make this a plausible local analogue galaxy with low dust ( E(B-V)=0.15 ) and metallicity (0.26 Z _ ).

gmw_00009887.name: Object 95952
gmw_00009887.overview: Object 95952 is a distant radio-detected galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.198 [arXiv:1705.07655]. It has a stellar mass of log(mass) 9.6, a low extinction, and an above-average H-alpha star formation rate of 10.4 solar masses per year [arXiv:1705.07655].

sources.summary: Object 95952 is the most distant radio object, VLA-detected but unresolved, with acceptable analogue age, mass, metallicity, low extinction, and above-average H-alpha SFR.

quotes.quote: Object 95952 is the most distant of our radio objects at z=0.198. It was detected, though not resolved, at 3 in our VLA observations (see figure tab:Contours3 for its SDSS detection and radio contours). Its SED-derived age of 150 Myr, stellar mass of log(mass) 9.6, and 0.4 Z _ make it an acceptable local analogue. It has an above-average H SFR of 10.4 M _ yr ^ 1 , and a radio SFR of 3.3 M _ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00009888.name: FSC 1630+15
gmw_00009888.overview: FSC 1630+15 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1007.2367] classified near the LINER and composite starburst-AGN boundary [arXiv:0908.4271, arXiv:1007.2367]. The galaxy hosts an OH megamaser [arXiv:1007.2367] and exhibits blueshifted low-ionization absorption indicative of galactic winds [arXiv:0908.4271].

sources.summary: The paper classifies FSC 1630+15 near the LINER/composite boundary and reports deep, blueshifted low-ionization absorption with similar Mg and Na trough kinematics.

quotes.quote: The more recent Kewley (2006) division of these optical-emission-line ratios into excitation types classifies FSC 0039-13 as a composite starburst-AGN, FSC 1009+47 as a Seyfert 2, FSC 1407+05 at the Seyfert/LINER boundary, FSC 1630+15 at the LINER/Composite boundary, and FSC 2349+24 as a Seyfert 2.
In FSC1630+15 and FSC2349+24, the minima in the absorption troughs lie at v 400 .

FSC1630+15: In FSC1630+15, the shape of the \ and \ profiles are the same from 0 to -620 after scaling by a factor of 2.0. Maximum absorption is offset to -400 . The 2796 absorption trough is detected to -825 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies J163221+155147 as the sample's lone emission-line LINER, classed as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy and OH megamaser, uniquely IRAS-detected among the sources, and unresolved in FIRST.

quotes.quote: The one exception, J163221+155147, showed emission lines of [ O II ] , H , [ O III ] , [ O I ] , H , [ N II ] and [ S II ] , typical of a LINER that has undergone recent star formation; it is classified as an ultraluminous infrared galaxy and an OH megamaser (Darling Giovanelli 2000).
Only J163221+155147 in the sample of 129 sources given in Table tab:129nvss was detected in the IRAS catalogue, indicating that few of these sources are luminous star-forming galaxies.
The LINER/OH megamaser, J163221+155147, is also found to be unresolved in the FIRST survey.

gmw_00009889.name: FSC 2349+24
gmw_00009889.overview: FSC 2349+24 is a ultraluminous infrared galaxy classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:0908.4271]. It exhibits the strongest active galactic nucleus signatures among its sample, featuring a galactic outflow with a trough reaching speeds of -650 km/s [arXiv:0908.4271].

sources.summary: The paper describes FSC 2349+24 as the strongest-AGN-signature Seyfert 2 ULIRG in the sample, with Mg emission filling absorption and an outflow trough reaching about -650.

quotes.quote: The more recent Kewley (2006) division of these optical-emission-line ratios into excitation types classifies FSC 0039-13 as a composite starburst-AGN, FSC 1009+47 as a Seyfert 2, FSC 1407+05 at the Seyfert/LINER boundary, FSC 1630+15 at the LINER/Composite boundary, and FSC 2349+24 as a Seyfert 2. Among our five targets, FSC 2349+24 clearly presents the strongest AGN signatures, but AGN contribute less than half of the bolometric luminosity in the other four ULIRGs (Veilleux 2009a).

FSC2349+24: The FSC2349+24 spectrum shows strong \ emission. The 2796 emission fills in the bluest portion of the 2803 line profile. The 2796 trough reaches -650 . We compare the trough to without any scaling. The SNR is not adequte to detect the trough at outflow speeds greater than -500 .

gmw_00009890.name: GAMA ID: 209807
gmw_00009890.overview: GAMA ID: 209807 is an isolated disk galaxy with a stellar mass of 10.8 solar masses and an inclination angle of approximately 43 degrees [arXiv:1407.2411]. Its emission lines reveal overlapping kinematic components, including a narrow component from HII regions and broader components consistent with shock excitation [arXiv:1407.2411].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes isolated disk galaxy GAMA ID 209807, decomposing its emission lines into multiple components and attributing broad shocked gas to starburst-driven bipolar outflows.

quotes.quote: \ (GAMA ID: 209807; hereafter, ; Fig. pointings ) is an isolated disk galaxy located in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey field SDSS; York:2000qe , with a SDSS band magnitude of 15.1, and a logarithmic stellar mass of 10.8 M_ Taylor:2011kx . The SDSS imaging data do not show any potential companion galaxies within a 1 radius of \ (63 kpc at the galaxy redshift); this is consistent with the disk-like morphology and the lack of tidal features of . The inclination angle of , as estimated from the axis ratio of 0.75 on the SDSS band image, is approximately 43^ Padilla:2008fk .

In the integral field datasets, the galaxy presents skewed line profiles changing with position in the galaxy. The skewed line profiles are caused by different kinematic components overlapping in the line-of-sight direction. We perform spectral decomposition to separate the line profiles in each spatial pixel as combinations of (1) a narrow kinematic component consistent with HII regions, (2) a broad kinematic component consistent with shock excitation, and (3) an intermediate component consistent with shock excitation and photoionisation mixing. The three kinematic components have distinctly different velocity fields, velocity dispersions, line ratios, and electron densities.

gmw_00009891.name: TN J1338--1942
gmw_00009891.overview: TN J1338--1942 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=4.1 within a massive protocluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0601223]. The galaxy is highly luminous in the rest-frame ultraviolet, optical, and submillimeter bands, indicating a high star formation rate [arXiv:astro-ph/0601223].

sources.summary: The paper studies TN J1338--1942 as a z=4.1 radio galaxy embedded in a massive protocluster, noting its luminous UV/optical/submillimeter emission, high star formation rate, and complex feedback-driven morphology.

quotes.quote: Here, we will present a detailed analysis of the ACS observations of protocluster TN J1338--1942 at z=4.1 , augmented by ground-based observations with the VLT. This structure is amongst the handful of overdense regions so far discovered at z>4 , as evidenced by 37 LAEs that represent a surface overdensity of 5 compared to other fields venemans02,venemans07 . The FWHM of the velocity distribution of the LAEs is 625 km s ^ 1 . The mass overdensity as well as the velocity structure is consistent with the global properties of z 4 protoclusters derived from simulations e.g. delucia04,suwa06 .

The radio galaxy itself is highly luminous in the rest-frame UV, optical and the sub-mm, indicating a high star formation rate. It has a complex morphology which we have interpreted as arising from AGN feedback on the forming ISM and a massive starburst-driven wind zirm05 .

gmw_00009892.name: Kaz 102
gmw_00009892.overview: Kaz 102 is an active galactic nucleus that lacks signs of heavy absorption or an iron K spectral line [arXiv:astro-ph/0603699]. Its spectrum is well described by a single power-law, which is corrected using a warm-absorber model [arXiv:astro-ph/0603699].

sources.summary: The paper says Kaz 102 lacks heavy absorption and an Fe K line, has a hard ASCA power law, and is corrected with a warm-absorber model.

quotes.quote: Kaz 102 The results of detailed analysis of our own ASCA data of this object have been reported by Miyaji2003 (M03). Kaz 102 has no sign of heavy absorption or a Fe K line. A single power-law with 1 described the ASCA spectrum well, while it had softer spectra when it was observed by ROSAT .

This hard spectrum is probably due to extreme warm absorber component and/or reflection as discussed in M03. In this study, we employ the warm absorber model with intrinsic 1.9 in M03 for absorption correction. No neutral absorber is detected in the analysis and thus we assumed a zero N_ H for the neutral gas.

gmw_00009893.name: SIT 30 G1
gmw_00009893.overview: SIT 30 G1 is an Sb spiral galaxy characterized by a luminous bulge and a huge surrounding halo [arXiv:2103.14266]. Its structure features symmetric inner arms, multiple surface brightness profile breaks at large radii, and residual spiral arms after a two-component decomposition [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 30 G1 is described as an Sb spiral with symmetric inner arms, multiple large-radius breaks, and residual spiral arms after two-component decomposition.

quotes.quote: G1 and G2 are two spiral galaxies (Sb) with a prominent luminous bulge and a huge halo surrounding each of them Oh2013 .
The surface brightness profile of G1 (Figure 30_SB , left panel) shows multiple breaks at large radii.
A two-dimensional decomposition of G1 with de Vaucouleur ( Dev ) plus Exp leaves residuals of spiral arms with ^2_ = 1.70 (Figure 30G1resid2 ).

gmw_00009894.name: SIT 30 G2
gmw_00009894.overview: SIT 30 G2 is classified as an Sb spiral galaxy characterized by a luminous bulge and a surrounding halo [arXiv:2103.14266]. Although its spiral arms are not clearly observed, the galaxy exhibits a bar-like intensity profile and a peanut-shaped halo in its residuals [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 30 G2 is described as an Sb spiral whose arms are unclear, with a bar-like intensity feature and a peanut-shaped halo in residuals.

quotes.quote: G1 and G2 are two spiral galaxies (Sb) with a prominent luminous bulge and a huge halo surrounding each of them Oh2013 . The image of G1 shows two symmetric spiral arms closed to its center, while the spiral arms of G2 are not clearly observed.
Although G2 is classified as Sb, an evidence of a bar is remarked in its intensity profile between 2 ''< SMA < 3.5 '' .
Decomposition of G2 using Ser and Exp profiles produce a residual image with ``peanut-shaped'' halo extending out to 7.9 '' from the center of the galaxy and a ^2_ = 1.15 (Figure 30G2resid2 ).

gmw_00009895.name: SIT 30 G3
gmw_00009895.overview: SIT 30 G3 is a lenticular galaxy that contains a disk and a weakly elongated component [arXiv:2103.14266]. Its intensity profile is a simple exponential type I profile without breaks, and its fit residuals show only faint structures [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 30 G3 is described as a lenticular galaxy with a disk, weak elongation, a simple type I profile, and faint residual structures.

quotes.quote: G3 is a lenticular galaxy with evidence of a disk and a weak elongated component Oh2013 .
The intensity profile of G3 (Figure 30_SB , right panel) shows a simple exponential type I profile without breaks.
Figure 30G3resid2 shows the residuals of the fit for G3 using a Ser + Exp profile with only faint structures visible and a ^2_ of 1.05.

gmw_00009896.name: SIT 101 G1
gmw_00009896.overview: SIT 101 G1 is a lenticular galaxy featuring a slightly elongated central disk without a definite substructure [arXiv:2103.14266]. Its intensity profile reveals a type III-d antitruncation, and two-component decomposition leaves two small positive nodes on both sides of the galaxy as residuals [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 101 G1 is described as a lenticular disk galaxy with type III-d antitruncation and residual positive nodes after two-component fitting.

quotes.quote: G1 is a lenticular galaxy with a slightly elongated central disk without definite substructure along it Khim2015 .

The intensity profiles of G1 and G2 (Figures 101_SB , left and middel panels) reveal anti-truncations of type III-d with a break at SMA = 11 '' and 10.5 '' , respectively, while G3 represents a type II.i profile with truncation at 8.3 '' (Figure 101_SB , right panel).
The decomposition of G1 using Ser plus Exp profiles leave two small positive nodes in both sides of the galaxy as residuals with a ^2_ of 0.97, as shown in Figure 101G1resid2 .

gmw_00009897.name: SIT 101 G2
gmw_00009897.overview: SIT 101 G2 is a barred spiral galaxy characterized by two faint spiral arms that emerge from the ends of its bar [arXiv:2103.14266]. Its structure features a type III-d intensity profile antitruncation and an S-shaped nuclear residual, which likely indicates a circumnuclear dust ring [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 101 G2 is described as a barred spiral with faint arms, type III-d antitruncation, an S-shaped nuclear residual, and faint spiral-arm traces.

quotes.quote: G2 and G3 are barred spiral galaxies with a set of two faint arms, emerging from the end of their bars Willett2013 . The intensity profiles of G1 and G2 (Figures 101_SB , left and middel panels) reveal anti-truncations of type III-d with a break at SMA = 11 '' and 10.5 '' , respectively, while G3 represents a type II.i profile with truncation at 8.3 '' (Figure 101_SB , right panel).

Using dev + Exp profiles upon decomposing G2 reveals a residual image (Figure 101G2resid2 ) with an S-Shape structure around the nucleus that is presumably caused by a circum-nuclear dust ring with ^2_ = 1.15. Furthermore, the residuals shows the trace of two faint spiral arms extended in the East and West directions.

gmw_00009898.name: SIT 101 G3
gmw_00009898.overview: SIT 101 G3 is a barred spiral galaxy characterized by a set of two faint arms that emerge from the ends of its bar [arXiv:2103.14266]. Structural decomposition reveals a bar associated with two symmetric spiral arms and a type II.i intensity profile [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 101 G3 is described as a barred spiral with faint arms, a type II.i profile, and residual bar plus symmetric spiral arms.

quotes.quote: G2 and G3 are barred spiral galaxies with a set of two faint arms, emerging from the end of their bars Willett2013 . The intensity profiles of G1 and G2 (Figures 101_SB , left and middel panels) reveal anti-truncations of type III-d with a break at SMA = 11 '' and 10.5 '' , respectively, while G3 represents a type II.i profile with truncation at 8.3 '' (Figure 101_SB , right panel).
Decomposition of G3 using Ser + Exp profiles show, in Figure 101G3resid2 ), a prominent bar associated with two symmetric spiral arms in the East and the West directions and a ^2_ of 1.05.

gmw_00009899.name: SIT 104 G1
gmw_00009899.overview: SIT 104 G1 is an elliptical or E/S0 galaxy that features a cuspy core [arXiv:2103.14266]. The galaxy exhibits a down-bending type II profile with an outer ring, as well as a nuclear stellar disk visible in residual imaging [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 104 G1 is described as an elliptical/E-S0 galaxy with cuspy core, down-bending profile, outer ring, and nuclear stellar disk residual.

quotes.quote: G1 is an elliptical galaxy with cuspy core without evidence of a disk component, while the elliptical galaxy G2 shows a central elongated bulge structure with an evidence of disk component.

The 1D fitting of G1 (Figure 104_SB , left panel) shows a down-bending truncation at SMA = 9.5 '' of type II profile with an outer ring. An E/S0 classification might be more appropriate for this galaxy.

Decomposition of G1 using Ser + Exp confirms the presence of a nuclear stellar disk in G1 with a semi-major axis of SMA = 2.76 '' in the residual image with a ^2_ of 1.06 (Figure 104G1resid2 ). An E/S0 classification of this galaxy is more reasonable.

gmw_00009900.name: SIT 104 G2
gmw_00009900.overview: SIT 104 G2 is classified as an S0 galaxy [arXiv:2103.14266]. It features an elongated central bulge structure and exhibits a type I intensity profile [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 104 G2 is described as an S0-like galaxy with an elongated central bulge, type I profile, clean residuals, and confirmed S0 classification.

quotes.quote: G1 is an elliptical galaxy with cuspy core without evidence of a disk component, while the elliptical galaxy G2 shows a central elongated bulge structure with an evidence of disk component. This galaxy is classified as S0 by .

The intensity profile of G2 is clearly distinguished as type I profile (Figure 104_SB , middel panel), while the intensity profile of G3 resembles a type III-s profile with a break at SMA = 6.5 '' (Figure 104_SB , right panel).
Decomposition of G2 with Ser + Exp profile admits a clear fit without residuals (see Figure 104G2resid2 ), and the ^2_ of 1.12. This result confirm the classification of G2 as an S0 galaxy Nair2010 .

gmw_00009901.name: SIT 104 G3
gmw_00009901.overview: SIT 104 G3 is an elongated galaxy whose optical image does not reveal spiral arms or a bar component [arXiv:2103.14266]. Although initially considered a spiral-barred candidate, structural decomposition favors classifying it as an unbarred spiral with a type III-s intensity profile [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 104 G3 is described as an elongated spiral-barred candidate whose profile is type III-s and whose decomposition favors an unbarred spiral classification.

quotes.quote: The optical image of the spiral barred galaxy G3 does not reveal the existence of neither spiral arms nor bar component on its elongated disk component.

The intensity profile of G2 is clearly distinguished as type I profile (Figure 104_SB , middel panel), while the intensity profile of G3 resembles a type III-s profile with a break at SMA = 6.5 '' (Figure 104_SB , right panel).

Figure 104G3resid2 illustrate the decomposition of G3 using Ser + Exp profiles, which covers the whole components of the galaxy and a ^2_ of 1.06 were obtained. This galaxy could be classified as an unbarred spiral rather than SB galaxy, where the bar structure could not be identified in neither the 1D and 2D decomposition, nor the position angle profile of this galaxy.

gmw_00009902.name: SIT 125 G1
gmw_00009902.overview: SIT 125 G1 is an S0 galaxy characterized by an extremely bright nucleus and a circular disk [arXiv:2103.14266]. The galaxy features an outer ring, a type III-d profile, and residual evidence of an ongoing interaction with its neighbor, G2 [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 125 G1 is described as an S0 galaxy with bright nucleus, type III-d break, an outer ring, and residual evidence of interaction with G2.

quotes.quote: G1 is an S0 galaxy with an extremely bright nucleus and a clear circular disk around it Nair2010 .
G1 represents a type III-d profile with an up-bending break at SMA = 22 '' (Figure 125_SB ).

Decomposition of G1 using a single Ser and Exp profile reveals a significant bulge with an outer ring at 12.6 '' and a ^2_ of 1.08 (Figure 125G1resid2 . Decomposition of G2 , using Ser + Exp , reveals a bi-nuclei in the residual image with a strong tidal tail at the Northern side of the galaxy and a ^2_ of 1.43 (Figure 125G2resid2 ). These residuals affirm the state of interaction between G2 and G1 .

gmw_00009903.name: SIT 125 G2
gmw_00009903.overview: SIT 125 G2 is classified as a SABa galaxy with two asymmetric arms [arXiv:2103.14266]. The galaxy is interacting with G1, displaying a northern arm that forms a bridge toward its neighbor, a strong northern tidal tail, and a bi-nuclei structure in its residuals [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 125 G2 is described as a SABa galaxy with asymmetric arms, a bridge toward G1, two profile breaks, and a strong northern tidal tail.

quotes.quote: G2 is classified as SABa galaxy Nair2010 with two asymmetric arms at the N and the S directions. The northern arm forms a bridge extending toward G1 while the southern arm is faint and short.
The intensity profile of G2 (Figure 125_SB , middle panel) shows two breaks at SMA 7 '' and SMA = 24.5 '' .

Decomposition of G2 , using Ser + Exp , reveals a bi-nuclei in the residual image with a strong tidal tail at the Northern side of the galaxy and a ^2_ of 1.43 (Figure 125G2resid2 ). These residuals affirm the state of interaction between G2 and G1 .

gmw_00009904.name: SIT 125 G3
gmw_00009904.overview: SIT 125 G3 is classified as an Sc spiral galaxy featuring fuzzy, bluish arms that bifurcate outwards [arXiv:2103.14266]. Its outermost regions form an asymmetric structure of possible tidal origin, though profile fitting reveals an unperturbed, pure exponential disk [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 125 G3 is described as an Sc galaxy with fuzzy bifurcating arms, asymmetric outer structure of possible tidal origin, and unperturbed exponential disk residuals.

quotes.quote: G3 is an Sc galaxy with a set of fuzzy bluish arms that bifurcate outwards Nair2010 . At the outer most regions, the arms winded clockwise enclosing the galaxy and forming an asymmetric structure, which might be of tidal origin.
The intensity profile of G3 matches well a type II-o with a down-bending truncation at SMA 16.5x '' (Figure 125_SB ).
Fitting G3 with Ser and Exp profile leaves the spiral arms as residuals revealing a pure exponential unperturbed d isk galaxy and a ^2_ of 1.06 has been achieved (Figure 125G3resid2 ).

gmw_00009905.name: SIT 197 G1
gmw_00009905.overview: SIT 197 G1 is an elliptical galaxy characterized by nearly circular isophotes and a bright, cuspy core at its center [arXiv:2103.14266]. Although classified as an elliptical galaxy, its structure is best fit by a two-component model combining a Sersic and an exponential profile [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 197 G1 is described as an elliptical member with nearly circular isophotes, a fitted single-Sersic intensity profile, and two-component decomposition residuals.

quotes.quote: G1 and G2 have nearly circular isophotes with a bright cuspy core at the center while, G3 shows elongated isophotes, with higher ellipticity. The intensity profiles of the three elliptical galaxies were fitted by using single Ser component with Ser index ( n )of 1.5, 1.5 and 1, respectively (Figures 197_SB ).
Despite G1 being an elliptical galaxy, two profiles ( Ser + Exp ) were required to fit the galaxy components with a ^2_ of 0.80 (Figure 197G1resid2 ).

gmw_00009906.name: SIT 197 G2
gmw_00009906.overview: SIT 197 G2 is an elliptical galaxy characterized by nearly circular isophotes and a bright, cuspy core at its center [arXiv:2103.14266]. Its intensity profile can be fitted with a single Sersic index of 1.5, while a Sersic plus exponential decomposition successfully covers its fine structures [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 197 G2 is described as an elliptical member with nearly circular isophotes, a single-Sersic fitted profile, and two-component decomposition covering its structures.

quotes.quote: G1 and G2 have nearly circular isophotes with a bright cuspy core at the center while, G3 shows elongated isophotes, with higher ellipticity. The intensity profiles of the three elliptical galaxies were fitted by using single Ser component with Ser index ( n )of 1.5, 1.5 and 1, respectively (Figures 197_SB ).
Similarly, the decomposition of G2 using Ser + Exp covers nearly all the fine structures of the galaxy and a ^2_ of 0.91 was achieved (Figure 197G2resid2 ).

gmw_00009907.name: SIT 197 G3
gmw_00009907.overview: SIT 197 G3 is an early-type galaxy classified as a possible elliptical or lenticular galaxy [arXiv:2103.14266]. It exhibits elongated isophotes with high ellipticity, a single-Sérsic intensity profile, and central dark bipolar residual patches [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 197 G3 is described as an elongated early-type member, possibly E/S0, with single-Sersic profile and central dark bipolar residual patches.

quotes.quote: G1 and G2 have nearly circular isophotes with a bright cuspy core at the center while, G3 shows elongated isophotes, with higher ellipticity. The intensity profiles of the three elliptical galaxies were fitted by using single Ser component with Ser index ( n )of 1.5, 1.5 and 1, respectively (Figures 197_SB ). An E/S0 classification might be more suitable for G3 .
Decomposition of G3 using two models ( Ser + Exp ) reveals a central dark bipolar patches as a residuals with ^2_ of 1.12 (Figure 197G3resid2 ).

gmw_00009908.name: SIT 217 G1
gmw_00009908.overview: SIT 217 G1 is an elliptical galaxy characterized by a bulge [arXiv:2103.14266]. Its light distribution is fitted by a single-Seersic intensity profile with no breaks, and structural modeling reveals a low, diffuse positive halo in its central region [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 217 G1 is described as a simple elliptical galaxy with prominent bulge, single-Sersic intensity profile, and diffuse central halo after decomposition.

quotes.quote: The optical image of G1 presents a simple elliptical galaxy with a prominent bulge.

Figure 217_SB clarifies the intensity profile of G1 fitted by a single Ser component with no breaks, while the middle panel of Figure 217_SB presents the complicated profile of G2 with multiple breaks.

Using Ser plus Exp model for G1 and G2 reveals a low diffuse positive halo at the central region of both galaxies and an asymmetric arm in G2 with ^2_ of 0.9 and 1.17, respectively, (Figures 217G1resid2 and 217G2resid2 ).

gmw_00009909.name: SIT 217 G2
gmw_00009909.overview: SIT 217 G2 is classified as an Sc or SBc galaxy featuring a bar-like hump and a complicated intensity profile with multiple breaks [arXiv:2103.14266]. It possesses a set of fuzzy, anti-clockwise spiral arms and exhibits a type II.o truncation in its exponential profile [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 217 G2 is described as an Sc galaxy with fuzzy fragmented arms, a complicated multi-break profile, a bar-like hump, and type II.o truncation.

quotes.quote: A set of two anti-clockwise fuzzy spiral arms enclosing the core of G2 are clearly observed. The northern arm appears somewhat fragmented and extended apart from the entire galaxy, while the southern arm seems fainter and bifurcated.

Figure 217_SB clarifies the intensity profile of G1 fitted by a single Ser component with no breaks, while the middle panel of Figure 217_SB presents the complicated profile of G2 with multiple breaks. An extended excess hump, representing a bar, between 2 '' < SMA < 3.6 '' is well-defined.At SMA < 9.5 '' , the intensity profile starts to bend downward causing a truncation in the exponential profile representing a type II.o. An SBc classification might be more appropriate for this galaxy.

gmw_00009910.name: SIT 217 G3
gmw_00009910.overview: SIT 217 G3 is an early-type candidate galaxy with an apparent eye-shape that encloses a bulge [arXiv:2103.14266]. The galaxy's intensity profile exhibits a type III-d antitruncation, and its structure is well-fitted by a two-component decomposition [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 217 G3 is described as an eye-shaped early-type candidate with prominent bulge, type III-d antitruncation, and well-fitted two-component decomposition.

quotes.quote: G3 has an apparent eye-shape enclosing a prominent bulge. This raises the possibility to categorize G3 as a S0 or as an early type spiral (Figure fig:SIT217 ).
The intensity profile of G3 (Figure 217_SB , right panel) presents type III-d profile with an anti-truncation at SMA = 4.3 '' .
The decomposition of G3 using Ser plus Exp model fits nearly all its components and a ^2_ of 1.08 is obtained (Figure 217G3resid2 ).

gmw_00009911.name: SIT 263 G1
gmw_00009911.overview: SIT 263 G1 is an interacting spiral galaxy connected to its neighbor, G3, by a bridge [arXiv:2103.14266]. Its structure features a type III-d intensity profile, an offset bar, and two distorted spiral arms [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 263 G1 is described as an interacting spiral connected by a bridge to G3, with type III-d profile and distorted residual arms.

quotes.quote: It comprises three luminous spiral galaxies, in which G1 and G3 show a strong evidence of interaction or a merger state evinced by the bridge between them with high bifurcated asymmetric spiral arms emerging from the center of both galaxies.
The intensity profile of G1 shows an anti-truncation of type III-d at SMA 9.5 '' (Figure 263_SB , left panel).

Decomposition of G1 using Ser + Exp reveals many residuals including an off-set bar, two distorted spiral arms, and a negative diffused portion that cause the arise of a ``Butterfly-Shape'' (Figure 263G1resid2 ).

gmw_00009912.name: SIT 263 G2
gmw_00009912.overview: SIT 263 G2 is a spiral galaxy featuring a pair of tightly closed spiral arms and a cuspy core [arXiv:2103.14266]. Its intensity profile exhibits a type III inner break, and its structure displays complex residual arm and bulge discontinuities [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 263 G2 is described as a spiral with tightly closed arms, type III inner break, and complex residual arm and bulge discontinuities.

quotes.quote: G2 is a spiral galaxy with a set of two tightly closed spiral arms and a cuspy core in the center. It is located at a distance of 1.61 arcmin from G1 .

The intensity profile of G2 shows an inner break at SMA 6.5 '' of type III profile (Figure 263_SB , middle panel), similarly, the profile of G3 is no doubt, considered as type III-d profile with a break at SMA 5.8 '' (Figure 263_SB , right panel).

Similarly, the decomposition of G2 by Ser + Exp reveals many residuals with two over-subtracted arms and two other non-subtracted arms, negative and positive discontinuity portions are also remarked in the bulge region with a ^2_ of 1.23 (Figure 263G2resid2 ).

gmw_00009913.name: SIT 263 G3
gmw_00009913.overview: SIT 263 G3 is an interacting spiral galaxy that is connected to the neighboring galaxy G1 by a bridge [arXiv:2103.14266]. Structural analysis reveals that the galaxy exhibits a type III-d intensity profile, with residuals showing a ring and two asymmetric spiral arms [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 263 G3 is described as an interacting spiral bridged to G1, with type III-d profile and ring plus asymmetric-arm residuals.

quotes.quote: It comprises three luminous spiral galaxies, in which G1 and G3 show a strong evidence of interaction or a merger state evinced by the bridge between them with high bifurcated asymmetric spiral arms emerging from the center of both galaxies.

The intensity profile of G2 shows an inner break at SMA 6.5 '' of type III profile (Figure 263_SB , middle panel), similarly, the profile of G3 is no doubt, considered as type III-d profile with a break at SMA 5.8 '' (Figure 263_SB , right panel).
Decomposition of G3 using Ser + Exp shows a ring and two asymmetric spiral arms as residuals with ^2_ of 0.85 (Figure 263G3resid2 ).

gmw_00009914.name: SIT 264 G1
gmw_00009914.overview: SIT 264 G1 is an Sc-type galaxy characterized by an offset center and bifurcated inner spiral arms [arXiv:2103.14266]. Its outer region features two asymmetric, fuzzy arms that may have a tidal origin, alongside heavy dust-lane-like residuals [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 264 G1 is described as an Sc galaxy with bifurcated inner arms, asymmetric tidal-looking outer arms, failed two-component fit, dust-lane-like residuals, and offset center.

quotes.quote: The optical image of G1 shows an elongated central component with a set of inner spiral arms that bifurcated as they wind out. In the outer region, another two asymmetric and fuzzy arms appear enclosing the galaxy in an anti-clockwise direction. These arms could be of a tidal origin.

The decomposition of G1 was not successful when combining a Ser with an Exp model ( ^2_ = 1.63). The residual image showed an inverse ``S-shape'' structure around the bulge of the galaxy that might be explained by a heavy dust lanes. In addition, an off-set in the center of the galaxy and two asymmetric spiral arms are observed (Figure 264G1resid2 ).

gmw_00009915.name: SIT 264 G3
gmw_00009915.overview: SIT 264 G3 is a faint galaxy with the visual appearance of an early-type galaxy [arXiv:2103.14266]. Although previously classified as an S0 galaxy, its structure is better described as a spiral galaxy with a down-bending intensity profile [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 264 G3 is described as a faint early-type-looking member with type II.o down-bending profile, simple Sersic fit, and a revised spiral classification.

quotes.quote: The visual appearance of the elliptical galaxy G3 resembles a faint early-type galaxy without additional components.
The intensity profile of G3 (Figure 264_SB , right panel) shows a clear down-bending profile of type II.o at SMA 7.8 '' .

A single Ser model with n =0.85 , was successfully capable to fit the simple exponential component of G3 with a ^2_ of 1.06 (Figure 264G3resid1 ). Although this galaxy has been classified as S0 by , our classification agrees better with the one proposed by who classify it as a spiral galaxy.

gmw_00009916.name: SIT 280 G1
gmw_00009916.overview: SIT 280 G1 is classified as an elliptical galaxy featuring a definite bulge surrounded by a faint halo [arXiv:2103.14266]. Its intensity profile is characterized by a pure-bulge Sersic profile, and structural decomposition reveals a well-defined central ring extending up to 4.44 arcseconds [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 280 G1 is described as an elliptical galaxy with definite bulge and halo, pure-bulge Sersic profile, and a well-defined central ring residual.

quotes.quote: G1 and G2 are elliptical galaxies with a definite bulge surrounded by a faint halo.
The intensity profile of G1 (Figure 280_SB , left panel) was fitted by a single Ser profile that defines the pure bulge component of this elliptical galaxy.

Decomposition of G1 by Dev and Ser model lefts a well defined ring in the central region of the galaxy that extends up to 4.44 '' and a negative over-subtracted halo in the outer region of the galaxy (Figure 280G1resid2 ).

gmw_00009917.name: SIT 280 G2
gmw_00009917.overview: SIT 280 G2 is an early-type galaxy classified as either an S0 or Sa galaxy [arXiv:2103.14266]. It features a definite bulge surrounded by a faint halo, a type III-d disk break, and residual traces of spiral arms [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 280 G2 is described as an early-type member with halo, type III-d break, S0/Sa classification, and residual traces of spiral arms.

quotes.quote: G1 and G2 are elliptical galaxies with a definite bulge surrounded by a faint halo.

While, G2 shows a break at SMA = 10.8 '' of type III-d, where most of the light dominated from the main disk of the galaxy (Figure 280_SB , middle panel). This galaxy match well the classification of who classified it as S0 or Sa galaxy.

Performing a decomposition of G2 using Ser and Exp model reveals a residual image with a traces of spiral arms and a ^2_ of 1.03 (Figure 280G2resid2 ). This confirm the classification of this galaxy as S0 or Sa.

gmw_00009918.name: SIT 280 G3
gmw_00009918.overview: SIT 280 G3 is classified as an SBc galaxy that features an elongated bar structure and two symmetric spiral arms [arXiv:2103.14266]. Its intensity profile is truncated, and photometric decomposition of the galaxy is complicated by a very bright projected star near its core [arXiv:2103.14266].

sources.summary: SIT 280 G3 is described as an SBc galaxy with bar and arms, projected-star contamination, truncated profile, and complicated residual decomposition.

quotes.quote: G3 is an SBc galaxy with an elongated bar structure and two symmetric arms.

The SDSS image of G3 is strongly affected by the extremely bright star projected at the south just beyond the galaxy's core, which could not be masked before applying our analysis. The projected output light of this star covers most of the central region of G3 . Consequently, the intensity profile should be considered somewhat uncertain with a truncated profile at SMA = 6.8 '' representing type II.o (Figure 280_SB , right panel).

Decomposition of G3 represents one of the most complicated cases in our sample. The projected star is clearly recognized in the residual image of this galaxy as shown in Figures 280G3resid1 and 280G3resid2 . A clear mis-modeling of the core, as well as over-subtracted regions in the East and the West directions were observed while using Ser and Exp model.
