The new stars are also and are located in the outskirts of Hyd I, at 16 and 21 from the center, which means that the RRLs extend up to 3.2 r_h . We renamed here the two stars reported by koposov18 as V1 and V2, and assigned V3 and V4 to the new RRLs.

sources.summary: The paper includes Hydrus I in its velocity-dependent annihilation J-factor analysis, models membership with Gaia proper motions, and finds J factors comparable to earlier work.

quotes.quote: We also apply the same analysis to three additional dSphs: Crater II , Hydrus I , and Sagittarius II
The literature properties we use for our modeling are as follows: D=117.5 1.1 kpc, r_p=31.1 2.5 arcmin ; D=27.6 0.5 kpc, r_p=7.42 0.58 arcmin, =0.21 0.11 ; and D=73.1 0.9 kpc, r_p=1.7 0.05 arcmin .

For Crater II and Hydrus I, we determine membership with a mixture model including a dSph and MW foreground component (see Ref. for details on the mixture model). We include Gaia DR2 proper motions, which helps identify dSph stars . The J factors of Hydrus I and Crater II have been presented before , and our results are comparable to previous measurements.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Hyi1 as one of four ultra-faint dwarf galaxies whose angular momentum, positions, and velocities are consistent with infall as part of the LMC system.

quotes.quote: We conclude that 4 of these galaxies (Hor1, Car2, Car3 and Hyi1) have come in with the Magellanic Clouds, constituting the first confirmation of the type of satellite infall predicted by LCDM.

Hor1, Hyi1, Car3, and Ret2 are clearly consistent with the angular momentum direction of the LMC system, listed in descending order of significance as represented by the density of LMCa debris in the same region.

At least 4 dwarfs show clearly consistent positions and velocity measurements compared to our predictions: Hyi1, Car3, Car2 and Hor1 (red labels), presenting a compelling case of probable membership to the LMC group.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Hyd1 as an ultrafaint LMC-system member; its orbital consistency and low density strengthen its Magellanic association.

quotes.quote: Recent Gaia proper motions confirm new members of the LMC-system in addition to the previously known SMC, including two classical dwarf galaxies ( > 10^5 ; Carina and Fornax) as well as several ultra-faint dwarfs (Car2, Car3, Hor1, and Hyd1).
With the new 6D information, kallivayalil2018MCsats confirmed a likely association for 4 ultrafaints: Car2, Car3, Hor1 and Hyd1.

From that perspective, the likely associations for Hyd1 with = 4.7 km/s and Car2 with = 5.9 km/s, both within 100 pc, or the possibly associated Hyd2 with = 6.2 km/s, seem favored compared to the more dense Car3, Hor1, or Dra2 with 8 km/s at 50 pc data from simon2019UFDs .

sources.summary: The paper identifies Hyi 1 among six dwarfs likely accreted with the LMC and notes that an independent simulation-based technique also selected it as an LMC satellite.

quotes.quote: We study the orbits of ultra-faint dwarf galaxies in the combined presence of the Milky Way and LMC and we find 6 dwarfs which were likely accreted with the LMC (Car 2, Car 3, Hor 1, Hyi 1, Phe 2, Ret 2), in addition to the SMC, representing strong evidence of dwarf galaxy group infall. Using a different technique based on a single cosmological simulation, they identified Car 2, Car 3, Hor 1, and Hyi 1 as likely LMC satellites, in agreement with this work. In addition to the SMC, we identify six dwarf galaxies as being highly likely bound to the Cloud previously: Car 2, Car 3, Hor 1, Hyi 1, Phe 2, and Ret 2.

gmw_00009960.name: CGCG 436-020
gmw_00009960.overview: CGCG 436-020 is a galaxy that exhibits a flat radio spectral index in its center which steepens rapidly toward the outer regions [arXiv:1408.4177]. Due to these radio properties, it is classified as a transition object between an active galactic nucleus and a starburst galaxy [arXiv:1408.4177].

sources.summary: The paper finds CGCG 436-020 has a flat central radio spectral-index range that steepens outward, yielding an ambiguous AGN/SB radio classification with possible HST-bulge evidence for an AGN.

quotes.quote: CGCG 436-020 The map and the histogram show a flat range of values ( 0.3) in the centre, rapidly steepening outwards. The values of barely exceed our theoretical cut value to separate AGN from SB of = 1.1 if we take into account the uncertainties. We thus classify it as AGN/SB in the radio. We note, there is a HST bulge associated with the centre of radio emission haan11 , as additional evidence an AGN could precent.

gmw_00009961.name: ESO 550-IG025 (N)
gmw_00009961.overview: ESO 550-IG025 (N) is the northern component of a galaxy pair [arXiv:1408.4177]. It is classified as an active galactic nucleus/starburst hybrid [arXiv:1408.4177] and is brighter in the radio than its southern companion [arXiv:1408.4177].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the northern component of ESO 550-IG025 as radio-brighter than its southern companion and classifies it as AGN/SB because of outward radio-index steepening despite low brightness temperature.

quotes.quote: ESO 550-IG025 (N) This is the north radio component of the galaxy pair. This object is brighter in the radio than the IR, unlike the south companion where we observe the opposite. This gives a low value of q_ IR , but not lower than the 1 uncertainty (see Fig. qir ).

The north component presents a flat radio-spectral-index distribution steepening outwards. We have to note though that the brightness temperature is low for this object to have a powerful AGN ( 10^ 3 K; Table tab:Tb ). Due to the uncertainties in the steep values of we classify it as AGN/SB.

gmw_00009962.name: SDSS J131339.98 + 515128.3
gmw_00009962.overview: SDSS J131339.98 + 515128.3 is a gravitationally lensed, radio-quiet quasar located at a redshift of 1.875 [arXiv:0709.0525, arXiv:1811.07932]. It is lensed into two images by a foreground galaxy at a redshift of 0.194, which also causes reddening and microlensing [arXiv:0709.0525, arXiv:1811.07932].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J131339.98 + 515128.3 as a newly discovered gravitationally lensed quasar at z=1.875, with a detected lensing galaxy at z=0.194 causing reddening/microlensing and near-IR excess.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a new gravitationally lensed quasar, SDSS J131339.98 + 515128.3, at a redshift of 1.875 with an image separation of 1 24 . The lensing galaxy is clearly detected in visible-light follow-up observations. We also identify three absorption-line doublets in the spectra of the lensed quasar images, from which we measure the lens redshift to be 0.194 .

Like several other known lenses, the lensed quasar images have different continuum slopes. This difference is probably the result of reddening and microlensing in the lensing galaxy. The lensed quasar was selected by correlating Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) spectroscopic quasars with Two Micron All Sky Survey (2MASS) sources and choosing quasars that show near-infrared (IR) excess. The near-IR excess can originate, for example, from the contribution of the lensing galaxy at near-IR wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper detects lensed radio-quiet quasar SDSS J1313+5151 with LoTSS, linking its radio and far-infrared luminosities to star formation in the host.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1313+5151 (SDSS J131339.98+515128.4) was discovered in the SQLS by . The background quasar is at z_s=1.88 and is gravitationally lensed into two images with a separation of 1.24 by a foreground galaxy at z_l=0.19 . Heated dust emission that is assumed to be from the quasar host galaxy was detected only in the 250 m band, and is equivalent to a lensing-corrected SFR of 160^ +170 _ 80 M_ \ yr^ 1 , based on the median and distribution of dust temperatures of the sample, and assuming a typical magnification of 10.

Here, we present the detection of the gravitationally-lensed radio-quiet quasars SDSS J1055+4628, SDSS J1313+5151 and SBS 1520+530 at 144 MHz that fall in the HETDEX Spring Field targeted in the LOFAR Two-metre Sky Survey (LoTSS) first full data release. We compare their radio and far-infrared luminosities relative to the radio--infrared correlation and find that their radio luminosities can be explained by star formation. The implied star formation rates derived from their radio and infrared luminosities are between 20 and 300 M _ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00009963.name: ShCG 016.01
gmw_00009963.overview: ShCG 016.01 is a bright spiral galaxy located within a compact group [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001]. It is a radio galaxy featuring two radio-emitting lobes positioned about 10 kiloparsecs from a weaker central radio component, and it may also be a far-infrared emitter [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001].

sources.summary: The paper describes ShCG 016.01 as a bright spiral radio galaxy with two radio lobes, a weaker central component, possible FIR emission, and group redshift information.

quotes.quote: ShCG 016.01 . The brightest galaxy, spiral. As a typical radio galaxy it has two radio emitting lobes on two sides of the galaxy, and a weaker component coinciding with the galaxy itself (Fig. 1a). Radio lobes are at a projected distance of about 10 kpc from the galaxy. The redshift of the group, known also as I Zw 167 and Arp 330, is 0.02913 (Amirkhanian ).

The consideration of Fig. 2 in TMTST shows that both galaxies, No. 3 and also No. 1 with radio emission, may be FIR emitters. FIR observations with better angular resolution may clarify the situation.

gmw_00009964.name: ShCG 062.01
gmw_00009964.overview: ShCG 062.01 is a galaxy located at the periphery of its group [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001]. It is the brightest galaxy in the group and is situated very close to a stronger radio source, which may be an ejection or a projected source [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ShCG 062.01 as the brightest peripheral galaxy and discusses a nearby stronger FIRST radio source as either an ejection or projected source.

quotes.quote: ShCG 062.01 . The brightest galaxy, located at the periphery of the group. More stronger radio source FIRST J112552.1+382153 is located very close to the first one. It may, probably be ejection from the galaxy ShCG 062.01, or a projected source.

gmw_00009965.name: ShCG 120.Anon
gmw_00009965.overview: ShCG 120.Anon is a weak galaxy located at the periphery of the ShCG 120 group [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001]. It coincides with a central radio source that appears to have ejected two weaker radio lobes, and it is also a possible far-infrared emitter [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001].

sources.summary: The paper describes ShCG 120.Anon as a weak peripheral object coincident with a central radio source, with possible radio lobes, uncertain membership, and possible FIR emission.

quotes.quote: ShCG 120.Anon . The central brightest radio source FIRST J110431.2+355157 coincides with a weak object between galaxies No. 4 and 6, located at the periphery of the group. Two weaker radio sources FIRST J110432.6+355212 and FIRST J110433.1+355222 seem to be radio lobes ejected from the central object (Fig. 1c). The group is known also as a cluster A1151. The belonging of the object to the group should be checked by spectral observations. The FIR emitter, located just here, was considered as an uncertain identification in MTST. Apparently the same object may really be a FIR emitter.

gmw_00009966.name: ShCG 219.01
gmw_00009966.overview: ShCG 219.01 is the brightest galaxy in its group and is identified with the radio source FIRST J145233.5+275751 [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001]. The galaxy features a halo or spiral arms and appears to be interacting with a neighboring galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ShCG 219.01 with a FIRST radio source, describes interaction and halo-or-spiral-arm features, and suggests two other FIRST sources may be ejected.

quotes.quote: ShCG 219.01 . Radio source FIRST J145233.5+275751 is identified with the brightest galaxy. It seem to be in interaction with galaxy No. 2. Galaxy No. 1 has halo or spiral arms. The group is known also as a cluster A1984. Two other radio sources, FIRST J145231.6+275807 and FIRST J145234.2+275751, seem to be ejected from galaxy No. 1 (Fig. 1e). This galaxy and the galaxy No. 1 are in interaction.

gmw_00009967.name: ShCG 273.06
gmw_00009967.overview: ShCG 273.06 is a relatively bright galaxy that is likely interacting with a neighboring galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001]. Detected radio emission suggests that ShCG 273.06, rather than its neighbor, is the source of the system's far-infrared emission [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001].

sources.summary: The paper describes ShCG 273.06 as a relatively bright interacting galaxy whose radio emission supports identifying it, rather than galaxy No. 3, as the FIR source.

quotes.quote: ShCG 273.06 . Relatively bright galaxy, what, probably, is in interaction with galaxy No. 3. The FIR source was identified with galaxy No. 3 (TMTST). The finding of the radio emission from this galaxy suggests that just it may be the FIR source. The errors of the IRAS positional measurements in this case are 18 6 .

gmw_00009968.name: ShCG 317.01
gmw_00009968.overview: ShCG 317.01 is the brightest galaxy in its compact group [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001]. It is associated with a complex radio source that spans an overall size of approximately 100 kpc and features multiple components connected by bridges [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001].

sources.summary: The paper says ShCG 317.01 is the brightest group galaxy associated with a complex FIRST radio structure, connected components, and an overall radio size near 100 kpc.

quotes.quote: ShCG 317.01 . The FIRST Survey shows very complex structure (Fig. 1f) of the radio source identified with the brightest galaxy of the group ( 15 72 ). It has several components connected with bridges. The overall size of the radio complex is about 100 kpc. The redshift of the group is 0.0434 (Tiersch et al., in preparation).

gmw_00009969.name: ShCG 344.07
gmw_00009969.overview: ShCG 344.07 is a spiral galaxy of the Sab type [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001]. It appears to be interacting with a relatively weak neighboring galaxy and is a possible source of far-infrared emission [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001].

sources.summary: The paper describes ShCG 344.07 as an Sab galaxy interacting with a weaker neighbor and possibly contributing to FIR emission after reconsidering IRAS isophotes.

quotes.quote: ShCG 344.07 . Galaxy of Sab type. Seems to be in interaction with another, relatively weak galaxy. The FIR source was identified in TMTST with the brightest galaxy No. 1 of S0 type. The reconsideration of the FIR isophotes here with taking into account the errors of the IRAS positional measurements in this case ( 40 23 ) allows to suggest that galaxy No. 7 may also be a FIR emitter.

gmw_00009970.name: ShCG 351.06
gmw_00009970.overview: ShCG 351.06, also known as UGC 06212, is a dominant spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001]. It is located at the periphery of its group and is identified as a far-infrared source [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ShCG 351.06, also called UGC 06212, as a dominant peripheral spiral galaxy and matches the FIR source to the same galaxy.

quotes.quote: ShCG 351.06 . The dominant spiral galaxy (UGC 06212). It is located at the periphery of the group. In TMTST the FIR source was identified with the same galaxy.

gmw_00009971.name: ShCG 376.04
gmw_00009971.overview: ShCG 376.04 is the dominant galaxy within its group [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001]. It is a radio and far-infrared source that exhibits emission lines in its spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/9905001].

sources.summary: The paper describes ShCG 376.04 as the dominant radio/FIR galaxy of its group, with spectral observations showing emission lines in this galaxy and others.

quotes.quote: ShCG 376.04 . The dominant galaxy. The FIR source was identified with the same galaxy (TMTST). Spectral observations of this group (the results of which will be published elsewhere) show that this galaxy, and also some others in the group, have emission lines.

gmw_00009972.name: GMRT J173412.8 150328
gmw_00009972.overview: GMRT J173412.8 150328 is a triple radio source with components aligned in a northeast-southwest direction [arXiv:0803.0721]. Its core-lobe morphology resembles a Fanaroff-Riley type II radio galaxy, featuring a core and two lobe components where one lobe contains a bright hot spot [arXiv:0803.0721].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GMRT J173412.8 150328 as a triple radio source whose aligned core-lobe morphology resembles a Fanaroff-Riley type II radio galaxy.

quotes.quote: Four of them have been resolved by the GMRT interferometer presenting either a double structure (GMRT J173345.7 151643, GMRT J173421.2 152222, and GMRT J173704.3 153301) or even a triple structure (GMRT J173412.8 150328). We show in Fig. figtriple the contour map of this last interesting triple object, obtained after combining the February and April runs. The position of the components and their peak and integrated flux densities are given in Table triple .

The morphology of this source thus appears clearly reminiscent of a Fanaroff-Riley type II radio galaxy, with a core and two lobe components. One of the lobes has a clear and bright hot spot. The three components are well-aligned in the northeast-southwest direction, with the outer components separated from the core by an angular distance of about 30^ .

gmw_00009973.name: NE companion
gmw_00009973.overview: The NE companion is an edge-on disc galaxy that exhibits a distorted morphology [arXiv:1409.1585]. It is characterized by a high stellar mass, ongoing star formation, and optical/UV extinction that places its inferred infrared luminosity in the luminous infrared galaxy regime [arXiv:1409.1585].

sources.summary: The NE companion is described as an edge-on distorted disc galaxy near the QSO, with high stellar mass, ongoing star formation, optical/UV extinction, and LIRG-level inferred infrared luminosity.

quotes.quote: From the analysis of the HST images, the NE companion appears as an edge--on disc galaxy, aligned with and elongated towards the QSO along the NE--SW direction. Its morphology appears distorted in the HST images (see Fig. fig_hst ).

The NE companion has a stellar mass M_*=(7.1_ 1.5 ^ +2.2 ) 10^ 10 and a star formation rate SFR= 30 20 yr ^ 1 . The rest-frame optical/UV emission of this source show significant extinction. Assuming energy balance between UV absorption and re-emission at IR wavelengths, we derive an extrapolated IR luminosity of 10^ 11 , i.e., in the LIRG regime (and yet consistent with a non-detection in our continuum map). Within the uncertainties in the conversion factors, the M_ H2 /M_* 0.07 ratio is in agreement with what typically observed in `main sequence' galaxies at this redshift see, e.g., Fig. 9 in Carilli13 .

gmw_00009974.name: SE companion galaxy
gmw_00009974.overview: The SE companion galaxy is a lower-mass star-forming galaxy with an estimated stellar mass of 7 billion solar masses [arXiv:1409.1585]. It has a star formation rate of 8 solar masses per year and a tentative CO(2-1) line detection [arXiv:1409.1585].

sources.summary: The SE companion has a tentative CO(2--1) line detection at the optical counterpart, a redshift consistent with the QSO host, and photometry indicating a lower-mass star-forming galaxy.

quotes.quote: We also report a tentative (3- ) CO(2--1) line detection in the SE companion galaxy (see Fig. fig_chnmap - fig_mom ). The line is detected over 4 independent channels (at 25 wide binning). The CO line is centered at the position of the optical counterpart (see Fig. fig_mom ). It peaks at 135.84 0.01 GHz ( z=0.6973 0.0001 ). Its flux is 0.20 0.6 Jy , implying a CO(2--1) luminosity of L_ =(4.8 1.4) 10^5 or L'=(1.2 0.4) 10^9 K pc ^2 .
For the SE companion, our MAGPHYS fit of the available photometry suggests that the galaxy has a stellar mass M_*=(7_ 3 ^ +4 ) 10^9 and a SFR= 8_ 2 ^ +8 yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00009975.name: SW companion
gmw_00009975.overview: The SW companion is a massive galaxy located southwest of a QSO [arXiv:1409.1585]. It features a relatively red stellar population with an estimated stellar mass of 1.4 x 10^11 solar masses and a star formation rate of 15 solar masses per year [arXiv:1409.1585].

sources.summary: The SW companion is treated as a massive galaxy southwest of the QSO, with photometric and spectroscopic redshifts near 0.704 and an estimated stellar mass and SFR.

quotes.quote: Finally, we estimate a stellar mass M_*=(1.4 0.4) 10^ 11 and a star formation rate SFR= 15_ 8 ^ +15 yr ^ 1 for the SW companion.

Finally, the SW companion is a massive galaxy found 21.4'' 153 kpc South-West of the QSO. Our analysis of the BUSCA images suggests the galaxy has a photometric redshift z_ phot =0.76_ 0.06 ^ +0.05 . The optical spectrum of this galaxy is shown in Fig. fig_SWspec . The shape of the continuum emission suggests that the galaxy has a relatively red stellar population. The Calcium break feature pins down the continuum redshift at 0.7043 0.0009 .

gmw_00009976.name: SDSS J152130.72+074916.5
gmw_00009976.overview: SDSS J152130.72+074916.5 is a non-active galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.17901 [arXiv:1509.00840]. Spectroscopic analysis indicates that the galaxy hosts an old, passive stellar population with an estimated age of 10 billion years [arXiv:1509.00840].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J152130.72+074916.5 as the likely host of J1521+0749, finding a tiny chance alignment probability, non-active spectrum, redshift 0.17901, and old passive stellar population.

quotes.quote: This new position is 0 33 away from the center of SDSS J152130.72+074916.5, consistent within the uncertainty (Figure fig:cfhtimg ). The number density of the optical sources that are as bright as or brighter than SDSS J152130.72+074916.5 in the band within 10 is 0.00038 per square arcsec. Thus the chance probability for our X-ray source to be within 0 33 from the center of SDSS J152130.72+074916.5 is only 0.0001.

The SDSS took a spectrum of the galaxy on 2011 May 28, which is shown in Figure fig:s13s2740gsp (from the SDSS archive). Although the spectrum is a little noisy, the typical absorption features indicating a non-active galaxy can be seen. The redshift of the galaxy is z=0.17901 0.00007 ( D_L=866 Mpc).

Using Penalized Pixel Fitting ( PPXF ) software caem2004 and synthetic spectra spanning a grid of 48 ages up to 14 Gyr, with [ M/H ] = +0.22 , 0.00 , 0.40 , 0.71 , 1.31 , 1.71 , we found that J1521+0749 is consistent with an old ( 10 Gyr), passive stellar population. Full-spectrum kinematics implied a stellar dispersion _ 66 km s ^ 1 , which is comparable to the limit from SDSS instrumental dispersion _ 69 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00009977.name: PKS 2247-131
gmw_00009977.overview: PKS 2247-131 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2204.13051] and blazar [arXiv:2104.04124] located at a redshift of z=0.22 [arXiv:2204.13051]. It exhibits a quasi-periodic oscillation of approximately 34 days [arXiv:2204.13051, arXiv:2104.04124], which has been interpreted as a helical structure within its jet [arXiv:2104.04124].

sources.summary: The paper gives special attention to PKS 2247-131, finding two BL Lac QPO candidates, including a high-significance 34-day signal consistent with a previous month-scale claim.

quotes.quote: PKS 2247-131 The wavelet analysis of PKS 2247-131, a BL Lac object at z=0.22, shows two QPO candidates (see Fig. fig:CWT2247 (A) and (C)). The first of the QPO candidates is found to be at around 217 38 d ( 214 43 d), at above 5 confidence level in the CWT of monthly (weekly) binned light curves. This QPO candidate seems to span at least from MJD 57600 to MJD 58500 approximately (July 2016 to January 2019) in the CWT map. Just by looking at the light curve one can notice the tentative periodic oscillations of this source.

More interestingly, we identify a QPO candidate in the time interval around MJD 57600 to MJD 57900 approximately (July 2016 to May 2017) with much shorter period. We can appreciate it in Fig. fig:CWT2247 (C) which shows the QPO centered around 34 14 d, with a significance larger than 5 , and only noticeable in the full-range weekly binned light curve. Tentative oscillations can also be seen, with at least 7 full cycles, shown in Fig. fig:CWT2247 (D) with the fitted periodic function.

sources.summary: The paper uses PKS 2247 131 as a blazar comparison: its 34.5-day gamma-ray QPO was interpreted as helical jet structure and motivates J0849+5108's jet scenario.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, in a blazar-type AGN PKS 2247 131, a 34.5-day QPO after an initial flux peak of an outburst was reported by Zhou2018 , and it has been the only one having relatively short, monthly period in rays thus far.
Because of the middle position in the periodicity range of QPOs, the month-long one in PKS 2247 131 was interpreted to be due to the helical structure in the jet Zhou2018 .
Recently a helical structure of jets has been invoked to explain the 34.5 day QPO observed in PKS 2247 131 Zhou2018 .

gmw_00009978.name: host galaxy of GRB 200826A
aliases_external: GRB 200826A host galaxy / ZTF20abwysqy underlying galaxy counterpart
gmw_00009978.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 200826A is a low-mass, star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.75 [arXiv:2105.03829, arXiv:2105.05021]. It is dominated by a young stellar population [arXiv:2105.03829] and exhibits a high star formation rate and specific star formation rate [arXiv:2105.03829, arXiv:2105.05021].

sources.summary: The paper finds the host galaxy of GRB 200826A is a low-mass, young star-forming galaxy at z=0.748577 with typical metallicity but unusually high SFR and sSFR for an LGRB host.

quotes.quote: As first reported in , the continuum of the host galaxy was clearly detected between 0.38 m 0.98 m. A redshift of z=0.748577 0.000065 was determined from the simultaneous detection of the following emission lines: [ O 2 ] , [ O 3 ] and [ Ne 3 ] doublets, He 1 3889, and Balmer H 4101, H 4340, and H 4861 (Fig. fig:spectrum ).

Using the derived redshift as a fixed input, the optical/NIR SED of the host galaxy was modeled (Table tab:photall ) with the code Le PHARE Arnouts1999a,Ilbert2006a . The best fit ( ^2/d.o.f.=2.2/9 ) points to a galaxy dominated by a young population ( 0.04_ 0.02 ^ +0.08 Gyr) with global dust reddening E(B-V) 0.2 mag using the Small Magellanic Cloud (SMC) curves from and low stellar mass M_ /M_ = 8.6 0.2 (Fig. fig:host ).

Taken together, these results indicate that the host galaxy of GRB 200826A is characterized by a typical metallicity but larger than usual SFR and sSFR for a LGRB host. For the adopted cosmological parameters and using the last deep H band observation, the half-light radius of the host galaxy is R_ h =0 16 or 1.2 kpc at the redshift of the GRB. This size is consistent with LGRB hosts of similar masses Kelly2014a .

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB 200826A host at z=0.7481, measures its half-light radius and afterglow offset, and finds light-fraction and star-formation properties typical of long GRB hosts.

quotes.quote: We performed a follow-up observation of GRB 200826A using Gran Telescopio Canarias (GTC) and identified the host galaxy (Figure 3a) which was found to lie at redshift 0.7481 0.0003 (ref. ). We measured its half-light radius as 0.77", corresponding to 5.7 kpc, in our GTC band image. The optical afterglow of GRB 200826A has a 0.35" offset from the center of the host galaxy, which corresponds to 2.56 kpc.

The normalized offset is =R_ off /R_ 50 =0.45 . We estimate the cumulative light fraction F_ light =0.79 . We also calculated the star formation rate (SFR; see Methods) to be >1.44 \ M_ yr^ 1 and specific star formation rate (sSFR) to be >0.35 \ Gyr^ 1 .

gmw_00009979.overview: The radio galaxy 1203+043 exhibits an X-shaped radio structure characterized by a pair of secondary lobes oriented perpendicular to the main source axis [arXiv:astro-ph/0003076]. This source lacks a bright compact component, which suggests that its reported low-frequency variability may be spurious [arXiv:astro-ph/0003076].

sources.summary: The paper finds 1203+043 lacks a bright compact component, is probably spuriously variable at low frequency, and has an unusual X-shaped radio morphology with secondary lobes.

quotes.quote: The radio source 1203 + 043 lacks any bright compact component thereby ruling out a refractive scintillation mechanism for its variability. Consequently, it is possible that claims of variability in this source are spurious. However, the 320 MHz VLA observations show that 1203 + 043 has an `X'-shaped radio structure. This is a rare morphology for the brightness distribution of a radio galaxy; the implications of this are examined.

The lack of polarized emission at 320 MHz for 1203 + 043 and a radio structure which lacks a bright compact component rules out both of the mechanisms for low frequency variability in this source. Consequently, we conclude that this source might be a spurious case of variability. However, 1203 + 043 has an interesting structure at 320 MHz. It shows a pair of secondary lobes in a direction perpendicular to the main source axis, making the object one of a few known `X'-shaped sources.

gmw_00009980.name: HS 2134+0400
gmw_00009980.overview: HS 2134+0400 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy with an extremely low oxygen and heavy-element abundance [arXiv:astro-ph/0508255]. It is located in an atypical global environment within the Pegasus void, a region characterized by a very low density of normal or high-luminosity galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0508255].

sources.summary: The paper presents spectroscopy of HS 2134+0400, identifying it as an extremely metal-poor blue compact dwarf in the Pegasus void with low oxygen abundance and typical heavy-element ratios.

quotes.quote: We present the SAO 6 m telescope spectroscopy of a blue compact galaxy (BCG) HS 2134+0400 discovered in frame of the dedicated Hamburg/SAO survey for Low Metallicity BCGs (HSS-LM). Its very low abundance of oxygen (12+ (O/H) = 7.44), as well as other heavy elements (S, N, Ne, Ar), assigns this dwarf galaxy to the group of BCGs with the lowest metal content. The abundance ratios for the heavy elements (S/O, Ne/O, N/O, and Ar/O) are well consistent with the typical values of other very metal-poor BCGs. The global environment of HS 2134+0400 is atypical of the majority of BCGs. The object falls within the Pegasus void, the large volume with the very low density of galaxies with the normal (M _ B ^ * =--19.6) or high luminosity.
Here we report on a new galaxy HS 2134+0400 (J213658.95+041404.1), found in the HSS-LM (part II), which belongs to the group of eight the most metal-poor BCGs.

gmw_00009981.name: Maffei 2
gmw_00009981.overview: Maffei 2 is a galaxy that exhibits five or more detected molecular spectral features [arXiv:1101.0132]. Its chemistry may be strongly influenced by shocks, and it is expected to have a low average gas density of 10^4 cm^-3 [arXiv:1101.0132].

sources.summary: The paper detects multiple molecular features in Maffei 2, finds the highest listed HCN ratio endpoint, and argues its chemistry may be shock influenced with low average gas density.

quotes.quote: In only six galaxies, Arp 220, NGC 1068, NGC 253, M82, IC 342, and Maffei 2, five or more spectral features are detected.

The spread in this ratio was relatively small, ranging from 3.3 in NGC 3079 to 7.8 in Maffei 2. We note that HNCO and CH _3 OH are even stronger relative to HCN in IC 342 and Maffei 2, both whose chemistry may also be dominated by shocks ala10 . The largest CH _3 OH/HCN ratio was measured in NGC 3079, and thus the chemistry in NGC 253, IC 342, Maffei 2 and NGC 3079 may all be strongly influenced by shocks.
Based on model comparisons shown in Figures 2 and 4, IC 342, Maffei 2 and NGC 3079 would be expected to have the lowest average gas density of 10 ^4 cm ^ 3 .

gmw_00009982.name: NGC 245
gmw_00009982.overview: NGC 245 is a late-type Sb Markarian galaxy characterized by asymmetric spiral arms and numerous star-forming complexes [arXiv:1509.02356]. The galaxy features a blue nucleus, and its stellar population shows evidence of past secondary star formation bursts in both the disk and the bulge [arXiv:1509.02356].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 245 as a late-type Markarian Sb galaxy with asymmetric spiral arms, a blue nucleus, matched disk and nuclear colors, and evidence for past secondary star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 245. This galaxy has a blue nucleus with a nearly constant star-formation rate over the past several billion years (the characteristic decay time for the SFR is _1 10 Gyr). NGC 245 is a Makarian galaxy with asymmetric spiral arms and numerous star-forming complexes. The color indices of the disk and nucleus coincide within the uncertainties. According to its photometric characteristics, this is a late-type Sb galaxy.

The components of NGC 245 lie to the left of the NCS on the (U-B) (B-V) diagram and to the right of the NCS on the (B-V) (V-I) diagram. This can be explained in models in which a secondary burst of star formation has ended ( _2 < T_2 ). The disk is described well by a model with a moderately powerful ( M_2/M_1 = 0.1 ) burst of star formation occurring about 300 Myr ago; the bulge of NGC 245 is described well by a model with a powerful secondary burst of star formation long in the past ( T_2 3-10 Gyr).

gmw_00009983.name: IC 1525
gmw_00009983.overview: IC 1525 is a symmetric, intermediate-Sb barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622, arXiv:1509.02356] that features a bar aligned at a position angle of 48 degrees and an encircling ring of HII regions [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622, arXiv:1509.02356]. Numerous star-forming complexes are located in its spiral arms, and its nucleus and bar contain a mix of young and old stellar populations [arXiv:1509.02356].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 1525 as a barred spiral whose H and [NII] kinematics show central measurement complications plus several off-arm anomalous-velocity regions with enhanced line ratios.

quotes.quote: The continuum image (Fig. ic1525 a) reveals the presence of a prominent bar aligned at PA=48 . The bar ends are encircled by the ring of HII regions which has a radius of 15 - 17 (Fig. ic1525 b). In the central part of the bar, R< 5 7 , due to the superposition of strong absorption lines of bulge stars, the H emission line sinks in absorption, so the velocity field (Fig. ic1525 ) has a hole in the center.

The residual velocity distribution, obtained by the subtraction of the mean rotation field from the observed velocity field, demonstrates several regions where a projection of non-circular velocities onto the line of sight exceeds 50 . The sizes of these regions corrected for the beam smearing are 1 5 (up to 1.6 kpc for the adopted distance of 67 Mpc).

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 1525 as a symmetric intermediate-Sb barred ring galaxy with numerous star-forming complexes and two-color evidence for young plus old stellar populations.

quotes.quote: IC 1525. This symmetric galaxy with an intermediate Sb morphological type has a bar and ring. Numerous star-forming complexes are observed in the spiral arms and at the ends of the bar bru11 . The nucleus and bar of IC 1525 are shifted to the right of the NCS on the (U-B) (B-V) diagram. This provides evidence for the presence of an appreciable fraction of relatively young stars, together with the old stellar population.

gmw_00009984.name: IGR J18249-3243
gmw_00009984.overview: IGR J18249-3243 is a radio-bright active galactic nucleus optically classified as a Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:2009.03232]. It exhibits GeV emission, partial covering absorption, and a potentially extended structure that may include a jet [arXiv:2009.03232].

sources.summary: The review treats IGR J18249-3243 as an unclear GeV INTEGRAL AGN, optically Seyfert 1 and radio-bright, with possible jet structure, no required low-energy cutoff, and partial covering absorption.

quotes.quote: Among INTEGRAL AGN with GeV emission, there are a couple of sources of unclear class: IGR J18249-3243 and IGR J13109-5552. Optically they are both classified as Seyfert 1. They are both bright in the radio waveband and, although repeatedly observed, are poorly studied. Their structure seems to be extended but a dedicated observational campaign is necessary to confirm the presence of jet and related features. The broad band ( XMM plus IBIS/BAT data) properties of both objects have been explored by 2014ApJ...782L..25M .

Unlike other Seyfert galaxies and similarly to what we observe in blazars neither source shows a low energy cut-off ( 357 keV in IGR J13109-552 and not required in IGR J18249-3243). Furthermore, in IGR J18249-3243 partial covering absorption is present, like in an ordinary radio quiet Seyfert, making the picture even more confused.

gmw_00009985.name: brightest protocluster member
gmw_00009985.overview: The brightest protocluster member is a high-redshift galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2404.15910]. This active galactic nucleus is identified by its intense, broad Lyman-alpha emission and the presence of a carbon IV emission line [arXiv:2404.15910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the brightest protocluster member as an AGN, supported by intense broad Ly emission and an additional emission line interpreted as C iv.

quotes.quote: Moreover, we note that in this case the brightest protocluster member deviates from the general relation between M_ UV and EW_0 , and its Ly emission is found not only to be intense but also broad as shown in the left panel of Figure fig:sp1dAGN . The full width at half maximum of its Ly emission is FWHM =1.7 in the rest frame or FWHM =420 km s^ 1 . The brightest protocluster member has another emission line at 7300 , corresponding to 1560 in the rest frame. Its integrated S/N over _ rest =1550 1575 is S/N 7 . Although this wavelength is slightly different from that of C iv emission ( =1549.05 ), the observed wavelength of Ly or C iv emissions could be shifted from the galaxy's systemic redshift due to galactic kinematics see, e.g., rankine20 .

Thus, the emission line at 7300 can be regarded as C iv emission. Both intense/broad Ly emission and the higher order ionisation emission of C iv can be attributed to the presence of an active galactic nucleus (AGN).

gmw_00009986.name: RXJ 1347-1216
gmw_00009986.overview: RXJ 1347-1216 is a gravitationally lensed, high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 6.76 to 6.77 [arXiv:1511.04205, arXiv:2206.03503]. It is classified as a Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:2206.03503] that exhibits line emission [arXiv:1511.04205] but lies below the local carbon-two star formation rate relation [arXiv:2206.03503].

sources.summary: The paper treats RXJ1347 01037 as a confirmed z=6.76 line-emitting galaxy, using GLASS and Keck spectra to favor the high-redshift line identification and constrain UV line ratios.

quotes.quote: In the following we describe RXJ1347 01037 which has been independently confirmed to be a galaxy at z=6.76 with Keck DEIMOS spectroscopy, MACS2129 00899, a very promising z=8.1 candidate, and the two potential z 10 objects from table tab:dropouts_EL MACS1423 01018 and RXJ2248 00404 which are possible low-redshift contaminants. RXJ1347 01037 RXJ1347 01037 has been independently confirmed to be a line emitter from Keck-DEIMOS observations as presented by . They detect emission at 9440 . RXJ1347 01037 is in our photometric Gold sample.

Based on this probability, the non-detection of [ OIII ] and the [ OIII ] / [ OII ] flux ratio limit we conclude that the line detected in the GLASS and Keck-DEIMOS spectra is \ at z=6.76 , in agreement with the conclusion based on the line profile by , and strongly favored by the photometry. Given the \ emission and the redshift, the GLASS wavelength coverage allows us to search for CIV 1549 and CIII ] 1909 emission at 12020 \ and 14815 \ (marked by the left- and right-most white circles in the G141 spectra in Figure fig:z7obj ). We do not detect any significant CIV or CIII ] emission from RXJ1347 01037 in the GLASS spectra.

sources.summary: The paper treats RXJ 1347-1216 as a lensed Ly-emitting galaxy below the local [C II]-SFR relation, with tentative Ly emission and a C III] upper limit.

quotes.quote: Our third target, RXJ 1347-1216 is a Ly emitting (LAE) galaxy at z 6.77 lensed by the RXJ1347.1-1145 cluster ( 2016ApJ...817...11H ). detected the [ C II ] line emission with ALMA and measured a luminosity of L_ [ C II ] = 1.5_ 0.4 ^ +0.2 10^7 L_ , and estimated a SFR =3.2 0.4 \ M_ \ yr^ 1 . These authors found that the galaxy lies below the local [ C II ] SFR relation ( 2014A&A...568A..62D ), in agreement with the expectations from the model for a low-metallicity galaxy at z 7 .
We also identified a hint of the Ly emission in RXJ 1347-1216, previously published in with DEIMOS.

For the other two targets, HZ1 and RXJ 1347-1216, we did not detect C III ] emission at the expected redshifted wavelength and we place a 3 upper limit on the line flux. For HZ1 and RXJ 1347-1216, we estimate the integrated flux upper limit assuming a full-width at half maximum (FWHM) of the C III ] line to be equal to the FWHM of the [ C II ] line, where FWHM_ [ C II ] = 72 11 \ km \ s^ 1 ( 2015Natur.522..455C ) and FWHM_ [ C II ] = 75 25 \ km \ s^ 1 ( 2017ApJ...836L...2B ), respectively.

gmw_00009987.name: ID1
gmw_00009987.overview: ID1 is a spiral galaxy that has been classified as a regular rotating disk [arXiv:2303.16227]. Observations of carbon monoxide emission reveal typical rotating-disk features with a rotation velocity of approximately 200 kilometers per second [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID1 is described as a spiral, regular rotating disk in the IMAGES survey; the ALMA CO data show rotating-disk PVDs and a rotation velocity consistent with previous work.

quotes.quote: ID1 ID1 is part of the IMAGES survey that investigated the [ OII ] 3726, 3729 kinematics using the FLAMES-GIRAFFE multi-object IFU Yang_2008 . Based on their kinematic analysis, Yang_2008 classified ID1 as a regular disk. The HST data for this galaxy clearly show that ID1 is a spiral galaxy (Fig. fig:galfit1 ).

The channel maps and PVDs from the CO(2-1) ALMA data have the typical features of a rotating disk (e.g., see the S-shape profile in Fig. fig:pv1 ). With our analysis, we find a rotation velocity for ID1 of 200 km s ^ 1 , consistent within the 1 uncertainties with the value of 230 33 km s ^ 1 found by the IMAGES collaboration Puech_2008 .

gmw_00009988.name: ID2
gmw_00009988.overview: ID2 is a disk galaxy that exhibits asymmetric features, including a smooth disk and a southeastern clump [arXiv:2303.16227]. This structure has been interpreted as two interacting systems undergoing a late-stage minor merger [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID2 is a PHIBBS2 galaxy modeled as a disturbed disk with asymmetric HST structure, compact CO emission, and velocity-field/PVD distortions interpreted using one-sided fitting.

quotes.quote: ID2 This galaxy is part of the PHIBBS2 survey with ID: XV53. Based on the morphology derived by visually inspecting the HST I band (HST/ACS F814W) images, ID2 is classified as a disk with asymmetric features Freundlich_2019 . The HST image shows, in fact, a smooth disk and a clump in the south east direction (Fig. fig:galfit1 ). Lackner_2014 interpreted the presence of these two clumps as due to two interacting systems and classify this galaxy as a late-stage minor merger.

Here, we model the HST image using two off-center Sérsic components. The CO(3-2) emission is compact and does not extend to the south-east peak. Overall, the channel maps and the PVD are well reproduced by a rotating disk model. However, in the velocity field, the iso-velocity contours on the receding side of the velocity fields are more distorted than the approaching side.

gmw_00009989.name: ID4
gmw_00009989.overview: ID4 is a galaxy located in the XMMXCS J2215.9-1738 cluster [arXiv:2303.16227]. Its velocity gradient suggests rotation, but distorted contours indicate a possible bar structure or radial motions, and it features a north-west ultraviolet clump [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID4 is an XMMXCS J2215.9-1738 cluster galaxy classified as uncertain; its velocity gradient suggests rotation, but distorted contours, possible bar motion, and a UV clump complicate interpretation.

quotes.quote: ID4 - 9 These galaxies are part of the XMMXCS J2215.9-1738 cluster detected in the XMM Cluster Survey Romer_2001 at z = 1.46 . Global and spatially resolved properties are studied using multi-wavelength observations Hayashi_2017, Hayashi_2018, Ikeda_2022 . Based on our kinematic analysis, we classify ID4 and 5 as uncertain, and ID6 - 9 as disks. The velocity gradient in the velocity field of ID4 is likely due to the presence of rotation, but the distorted iso-velocity contours may be ascribed to the presence of a bar e.g., Hogarth_2021 . However, due to the angular resolution of these data discriminating between radial motions driven by a bar or other mechanisms is not feasible.

The best-fit Sérsic index obtained by modeling the HST data is consistent with a disk ( n 1 ) rather than a bar structure ( n 0.5 ). In addition, the HST data of ID4 show a clump in the north-west direction that is not detected in CO and it is an [ OII ] emitter at z 1.46 , detected using a narrow-band filter Hayashi_2014, Ikeda_2022 .

gmw_00009990.name: ID5
gmw_00009990.overview: ID5 is a galaxy located in the XMMXCS J2215.9-1738 cluster at a redshift of z = 1.46 [arXiv:2303.16227]. Its kinematic classification remains uncertain due to strongly asymmetric position-velocity diagrams, which may indicate either a lopsided disk or unresolved interacting nuclei [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID5 is classified as uncertain because its PVDs are strongly asymmetric, possibly from unresolved interacting nuclei or a lopsided disk, and higher-resolution data are required.

quotes.quote: ID4 - 9 These galaxies are part of the XMMXCS J2215.9-1738 cluster detected in the XMM Cluster Survey Romer_2001 at z = 1.46 . Global and spatially resolved properties are studied using multi-wavelength observations Hayashi_2017, Hayashi_2018, Ikeda_2022 . Based on our kinematic analysis, we classify ID4 and 5 as uncertain, and ID6 - 9 as disks.

ID5 has strong asymmetric features in the PVDs. The brightest regions of the major axis PVD are located at 0 and 100 km s ^ 1 , respectively; they may be the nuclei of two unresolved interacting galaxies (see black arrows in Fig. fig:pv2 ). However, no clumpy structures are identified in the HST data. The minor axis PVD of ID5 is asymmetric and not reproduced by the rotating disk model. Another potential explanation of the irregularity of the ID5 PVDs is that this galaxy is a lopsided disk Noordermeer_2001 .

gmw_00009991.name: ID6
gmw_00009991.overview: ID6 is a disk galaxy located within the XMMXCS J2215.9-1738 cluster [arXiv:2303.16227]. Its total-flux maps appear smooth and align with the stellar distribution, though minor-axis asymmetries and distorted velocity contours suggest possible gravitational interactions, gas accretion, or environmental effects [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID6 is grouped with XMMXCS J2215.9-1738 cluster galaxies and classified as a disk; its maps are smooth and aligned but show minor-axis asymmetries possibly driven by environment or interactions.

quotes.quote: ID4 - 9 These galaxies are part of the XMMXCS J2215.9-1738 cluster detected in the XMM Cluster Survey Romer_2001 at z = 1.46 . Global and spatially resolved properties are studied using multi-wavelength observations Hayashi_2017, Hayashi_2018, Ikeda_2022 . Based on our kinematic analysis, we classify ID4 and 5 as uncertain, and ID6 - 9 as disks.

Galaxies 6 - 9 have, instead, the typical features of rotating disks. However, they show some asymmetries, especially along the minor-axis PVD (see arrows in Fig. fig:pv2 ), and distorted iso-velocity contours in the velocity fields that may be driven by environmental effects e.g., ram pressure stripping Lee_2017, Zabel_2019, Cramer_2020, Bacchini_2023 , gravitational interactions Boselli_2022 or gas accretion deBlok_2014 .
For ID6 - 9, the total-flux maps appear smooth in all cases, likely because of the resolution of the observations, and aligned with the stellar distribution (Fig. fig:hstalma1 ).

gmw_00009992.name: ID7
gmw_00009992.overview: ID7 is classified as a rotating disk galaxy that exhibits structural and kinematic asymmetries [arXiv:2303.16227]. These asymmetric features align with a nearby source detected in HST imaging, suggesting that the perturbations are driven by gravitational interactions [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID7 is classified as a disk with asymmetries; a nearby HST source aligns with its velocity-field and minor-axis PVD perturbations, suggesting gravitational interaction effects.

quotes.quote: Galaxies 6 - 9 have, instead, the typical features of rotating disks. However, they show some asymmetries, especially along the minor-axis PVD (see arrows in Fig. fig:pv2 ), and distorted iso-velocity contours in the velocity fields that may be driven by environmental effects e.g., ram pressure stripping Lee_2017, Zabel_2019, Cramer_2020, Bacchini_2023 , gravitational interactions Boselli_2022 or gas accretion deBlok_2014 .

However, the HST data of ID7 show a close-by source (Fig. fig:hstalma1 ) that is not visible in the ALMA data. For ID7, the asymmetric features visible in the velocity field and in the minor-axis PVD (see black arrow in Fig. fig:pv2 ) are in the same direction of the nearby source, indicating that the asymmetries of the ID7 disk could be due to gravitational interactions. \ tries to reproduce these kinematic perturbations by inflating the velocity dispersion (see the comparison between the models and the data in the minor-axis PVDs). For this reason, in Sect. sec:discussion , we consider the values derived by \ for ID7 as upper limits.

gmw_00009993.name: ID8
gmw_00009993.overview: The galaxy ID8, also known as SSTU J221559.68-173758.9, is a rotating disk galaxy [arXiv:2303.16227] that is likely associated with a cluster [arXiv:1005.4692]. It has a close companion source located approximately 8 kiloparsecs away [arXiv:2303.16227] and is a bright 24-micron infrared source [arXiv:1005.4692].

sources.summary: The paper treats SSTU J221559.68-173758.9 as a bright 24 micron source near the cluster center with ambiguous counterparts, likely cluster association, late-type counterpart candidates, and possible red-sequence high star formation.

quotes.quote: In addition to these objects, we found that there is a very prominent, bright (239 9 Jy) 24 source located 17 away ( 0.15 Mpc at z=1.46 ) from the cluster center that is not included by the above sample selection criteria. It has an insecure ( Q=1 ) spectroscopic redshift that hints it could be a cluster member, and in addition, is not unambiguously identified with a single optical/near-infrared object: an object with a photometric redshift consistent with cluster membership from the Hilton_2009 catalog is also located within the matching radius. Furthermore, the local environment of this object is somewhat crowded, as Figure f_confused shows; there are two other galaxies within 2.5 that were selected as photometric members of the cluster in the Hilton_2009 catalog. This suggests that the source is likely to be physically associated with the cluster. We will refer to this object (SSTU J221559.68-173758.9) as the `unconfirmed source' in the following discussion of the nature of the sources.

The two possible optical counterparts to the unconfirmed 24 source (SSTU J221559.68-173758.9, Figure f_confused ) are highlighted in Figure f_mipsCMD . These objects are both slightly redder than the cluster red-sequence, but lie within <1 of it, and were morphologically classified as late type galaxies by Hilton_2009 . If this source is truly within the cluster, it is therefore likely to be an example of a red-sequence galaxy with high SFR.

sources.summary: ID8 is classified with the cluster disks but has a nearby low-significance HST and CO source about 8 kpc away whose signal is insufficient for kinematic analysis.

quotes.quote: Galaxies 6 - 9 have, instead, the typical features of rotating disks. However, they show some asymmetries, especially along the minor-axis PVD (see arrows in Fig. fig:pv2 ), and distorted iso-velocity contours in the velocity fields that may be driven by environmental effects e.g., ram pressure stripping Lee_2017, Zabel_2019, Cramer_2020, Bacchini_2023 , gravitational interactions Boselli_2022 or gas accretion deBlok_2014 .

ID8 has, instead, a close-by source that is visible both in HST and in CO(2-1) at low significance yellow controur in Fig. fig:hstalma1 ; see also Ikeda_2022 . To recover this source using the 3D source finder described in Sect. sec:barolo , we assumed SNRCUT of 2 and GROWTHCUT of 1.8. This object is located at an angular distance of about 1.0 (8 kpc) in projection from the main galaxy while its spectral distance from the systemic redshift of ID8 is of 800 km s ^ 1 . We note that the SNR of the ID8's companion is not sufficient to study its kinematics.

gmw_00009994.name: ID9
gmw_00009994.overview: ID9 is a disk galaxy located within the XMMXCS J2215.9-1738 cluster [arXiv:2303.16227]. While its total-flux maps appear smooth and align with its stellar distribution, the galaxy exhibits asymmetries and distorted velocity contours that may be driven by environmental effects or gravitational interactions [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID9 is grouped among XMMXCS J2215.9-1738 cluster disks; its smooth flux maps align with the stellar distribution while asymmetries may reflect environmental effects or interactions.

quotes.quote: ID4 - 9 These galaxies are part of the XMMXCS J2215.9-1738 cluster detected in the XMM Cluster Survey Romer_2001 at z = 1.46 . Global and spatially resolved properties are studied using multi-wavelength observations Hayashi_2017, Hayashi_2018, Ikeda_2022 . Based on our kinematic analysis, we classify ID4 and 5 as uncertain, and ID6 - 9 as disks.

Galaxies 6 - 9 have, instead, the typical features of rotating disks. However, they show some asymmetries, especially along the minor-axis PVD (see arrows in Fig. fig:pv2 ), and distorted iso-velocity contours in the velocity fields that may be driven by environmental effects e.g., ram pressure stripping Lee_2017, Zabel_2019, Cramer_2020, Bacchini_2023 , gravitational interactions Boselli_2022 or gas accretion deBlok_2014 .
For ID6 - 9, the total-flux maps appear smooth in all cases, likely because of the resolution of the observations, and aligned with the stellar distribution (Fig. fig:hstalma1 ).

gmw_00009995.name: ID11
gmw_00009995.overview: ID11 is a cluster member galaxy that exhibits the typical features of a rotating disk [arXiv:2303.16227]. However, it also displays minor-axis asymmetries and channel map residuals, which are likely caused by tidal disturbances or ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID11 is a cluster member lacking public photometry; it shows rotating-disk features but also minor-axis asymmetries and residuals attributed to tidal disturbances or ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: The only exceptions are ID11 and ID12, for which photometric data are not publicly available. Throughout the rest of the paper, for these two galaxies, we will refer to the stellar masses and SFR reported in Noble_2017 and obtained after making assumptions consistent with those used for the rest of our sample see for a detailed discussion on the M_ and SFR obtained with different tools Kokorev_2021 .

ID11 - 12 ID11 and 12 are members of two galaxy clusters that are recently studied in Cramer_2022 (see also Noble_2019 for ID11). Both galaxies have the typical features of a rotating disk (e.g., S-shape major-axis PVD) but they show some asymmetries in the minor-axis PVDs -- despite at low significance, see arrows in Fig. fig:pv3 - and residuals in the channel maps, likely due to tidal disturbances or ram-pressure stripping. htbp ID13 This galaxy is a protocluster member.

gmw_00009996.name: ID12
gmw_00009996.overview: ID12 is a cluster member galaxy that exhibits the typical features of a rotating disk [arXiv:2303.16227]. However, it also displays minor-axis asymmetries and channel map residuals, which are likely caused by ram-pressure stripping or tidal disturbances [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID12 is a cluster member lacking public photometry; it shows rotating-disk features but also minor-axis asymmetries and residuals attributed to tidal disturbances or ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: The only exceptions are ID11 and ID12, for which photometric data are not publicly available. Throughout the rest of the paper, for these two galaxies, we will refer to the stellar masses and SFR reported in Noble_2017 and obtained after making assumptions consistent with those used for the rest of our sample see for a detailed discussion on the M_ and SFR obtained with different tools Kokorev_2021 .

ID11 - 12 ID11 and 12 are members of two galaxy clusters that are recently studied in Cramer_2022 (see also Noble_2019 for ID11). Both galaxies have the typical features of a rotating disk (e.g., S-shape major-axis PVD) but they show some asymmetries in the minor-axis PVDs -- despite at low significance, see arrows in Fig. fig:pv3 - and residuals in the channel maps, likely due to tidal disturbances or ram-pressure stripping. htbp ID13 This galaxy is a protocluster member.

gmw_00009997.name: ID13
gmw_00009997.overview: ID13 is a protocluster member galaxy with well-studied stellar mass, dust content, and star formation rate [arXiv:2303.16227]. Position-velocity diagrams indicate that the galaxy is a rotating disk, which may be oriented almost face-on [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID13 is a protocluster member with studied global properties, neighboring HST sources lacking ALMA emission, rotating-disk PVDs, and a large PA mismatch likely affected by geometry or contamination.

quotes.quote: Its global properties (stellar masses, SFR, dust content) are studied in numerous papers e.g., Hung_2016, Zavala_2019 . In the HST cutout, two additional sources are visible, one located in the north east and the other in the south east directions. However, the redshifts of these sources are unknown and no dust or CO emission are detected with the ALMA data employed in this paper. The PVDs of ID13 show the typical features of a rotating disk. Despite this, ID13 has a relative difference between PA_ kin and PA_ HST of 53 .

Two reasons may be responsible for such a large difference: 1. being almost face-on, a robust estimation of the morphological position angle is not straightforward; 2. the light contamination from the two sources in the HST cutout may bias the estimation of the \ parameters.

gmw_00009998.name: ID16
gmw_00009998.overview: ID16 is a galaxy that, along with its neighbor ID17, was originally identified as a single bright submillimeter galaxy known as HXMM01 [arXiv:2303.16227]. The two galaxies are separated by 3 arcseconds and connected by a bridge of gas and dust, though ID16's classification remains uncertain due to strong kinematic disturbances [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID16 is part of a close gas-and-dust connected system first identified within HXMM01; despite earlier disk interpretation, its asymmetric PVDs lead the paper to classify it as uncertain.

quotes.quote: However, higher angular resolution data are needed to robustly analyze them and constrain the driving mechanisms. htbp ID16 - 17 ID16 and 17 are two galaxies, separated by 3 and connected by a bridge of gas and dust Fu_2013 . These sources are mildly magnified by two foreground galaxies, with magnification factors of 1.5. Due to their close proximity, ID16 and 17 were first identified as a unique bright SMG, HXMM01, in the Hershel Multi-tiered Extragalctic survey Oliver_2012 . Subsequently, higher-angular resolution observations resolve HXMM01 into three sources, ID16, 17 and its companion Fu_2013 , also visible in the total-flux maps in Fig. fig:hstalma1 .

A kinematic analysis of this complex system was already presented in Xue_2018 who interpreted ID16 and 17 as two rotating disks. Despite ID16 showing some symmetric features along the minor-axis PVD (Fig. fig:pv4 ), the major-axis PVD is strongly asymmetric, being bright only at negative velocities.
Due to the presence of strong disturbances, we classify ID16 as uncertain and 17 as an interacting system.

gmw_00009999.name: ID17
gmw_00009999.overview: ID17 is an interacting galaxy system that is part of the HXMM01 complex [arXiv:2303.16227]. The system is strongly asymmetric and is interacting with a companion located to its north [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID17 is part of the HXMM01 complex and is classified as an interacting system because it is strongly asymmetric and has a companion visible in total-flux maps and the major-axis PVD.

quotes.quote: However, higher angular resolution data are needed to robustly analyze them and constrain the driving mechanisms. htbp ID16 - 17 ID16 and 17 are two galaxies, separated by 3 and connected by a bridge of gas and dust Fu_2013 . These sources are mildly magnified by two foreground galaxies, with magnification factors of 1.5. Due to their close proximity, ID16 and 17 were first identified as a unique bright SMG, HXMM01, in the Hershel Multi-tiered Extragalctic survey Oliver_2012 . Subsequently, higher-angular resolution observations resolve HXMM01 into three sources, ID16, 17 and its companion Fu_2013 , also visible in the total-flux maps in Fig. fig:hstalma1 .

A kinematic analysis of this complex system was already presented in Xue_2018 who interpreted ID16 and 17 as two rotating disks. Despite ID16 showing some symmetric features along the minor-axis PVD (Fig. fig:pv4 ), the major-axis PVD is strongly asymmetric, being bright only at negative velocities. Similarly, ID17 is strongly asymmetric as it is interacting with a companion, clearly visible in the total-flux map (Fig. fig:hstalma1 ) and major-axis PVD (Fig. fig:pv5 ) located at 1 in the north direction with respect to the ID17 center. Due to the presence of strong disturbances, we classify ID16 as uncertain and 17 as an interacting system.

gmw_00010000.name: ID19
gmw_00010000.overview: ID19 is a high-redshift galaxy located in the forming cluster CLJ1001 [arXiv:2303.16227]. The bulk of its motions are reproduced by a rotating disk [arXiv:2303.16227], and its CO(3-2) emission appears slightly resolved and compact [arXiv:2104.02098].

sources.summary: ID19 is a CLJ1001 forming-cluster member whose CO(3-2) motions are reproduced by a rotating disk; its velocity dispersion agrees with Xiao et al., though rotation velocity differs.

quotes.quote: ID19 - 21 These galaxies are members of the forming cluster CLJ1001 at z = 2.51 Wang_2016, Gomez_2019, Champagne_2021 . The CO(3-2) kinematics of ID19 - 21, along with another galaxy member of the same cluster (130901), are recently studied by Xiao_2022 using the same data employed here. We exclude 130901 from our analysis due to the low SNR. For the kinematic fitting, Xiao_2022 used GALPAK ^ 3D Bouche_2015 and they fit simultaneously the kinematic and geometric parameters, finding values of the inclinations of 24, 45 and 19 and position angles of 12, 148 and 10 . For fitting the kinematics with , we used, instead, inclination angles from HST data modeling of 56, 45 and 26 .

The kinematic position angles (32 15, 133 8, 13 5 ) are consistent at 1.5- level with the values reported by Xiao_2022 . htbp The comparison between the \ model and the data indicates that the bulk of the motions in ID19 and 20 are reproduced by a rotating disk. The value of the velocity dispersion for ID19 is consistent within 1.5- with the one found by Xiao_2022 , while we find a value of rotation velocity which is a factor of 1.5 smaller than the value found by Xiao_2022 .

sources.summary: The paper reports ID1 as the only CO(3-2) detection among covered galaxies, with a thermalized line ratio and slightly resolved, compact CO(3-2) emission relative to CO(1-0).

quotes.quote: Of these, only ID1 is detected in CO(3-2), with SNR of 9.

We find that the ratio of (3-2) to (1-0) line luminosities, r_ 31 , = 1.37 0.20 for ID1 and an upper limit of r_ 31 < 0.72 for ID2, suggesting a thermalized state of the ISM in line with r_ 31 = 0.90 0.40 found for combined populations of submillimeter star-forming and AGN host galaxies at 2<z<3 Sharon2016a, Kirkpatrick2019a .

The CO(3-2) emission in ID1 appears slightly resolved by ALMA and more compact than the CO(1-0) emission (see Figure fig:co32 ), although since the CO(1-0) sources are unresolved, higher resolution observations are needed to verify this.

gmw_00010001.name: ID20
gmw_00010001.overview: ID20 is a high-redshift galaxy located within the forming cluster CLJ1001 [arXiv:2303.16227]. Kinematic modeling indicates that the bulk of its internal motions are reproduced by a rotating disk [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID20 is a CLJ1001 forming-cluster galaxy whose CO kinematics are reproduced by a rotating disk, with rotation velocities matching Xiao et al. but poorly constrained velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: ID19 - 21 These galaxies are members of the forming cluster CLJ1001 at z = 2.51 Wang_2016, Gomez_2019, Champagne_2021 . The CO(3-2) kinematics of ID19 - 21, along with another galaxy member of the same cluster (130901), are recently studied by Xiao_2022 using the same data employed here. We exclude 130901 from our analysis due to the low SNR. For the kinematic fitting, Xiao_2022 used GALPAK ^ 3D Bouche_2015 and they fit simultaneously the kinematic and geometric parameters, finding values of the inclinations of 24, 45 and 19 and position angles of 12, 148 and 10 . For fitting the kinematics with , we used, instead, inclination angles from HST data modeling of 56, 45 and 26 .

The kinematic position angles (32 15, 133 8, 13 5 ) are consistent at 1.5- level with the values reported by Xiao_2022 . htbp The comparison between the \ model and the data indicates that the bulk of the motions in ID19 and 20 are reproduced by a rotating disk. The value of the velocity dispersion for ID19 is consistent within 1.5- with the one found by Xiao_2022 , while we find a value of rotation velocity which is a factor of 1.5 smaller than the value found by Xiao_2022 . This discrepancy is due to the different inclination angles used to recover the intrinsic rotation velocity. On the contrary, for ID20 we find rotation velocities consistent with those found in Xiao_2022 , while the velocity dispersion values are not well constrained, having relative uncertainties 60 , due to the low angular resolution and SNR of the data.

gmw_00010002.name: ID21
gmw_00010002.overview: ID21 is a high-redshift galaxy that belongs to the forming cluster CLJ1001 [arXiv:2303.16227]. It exhibits minor-axis asymmetries and lacks typical rotating disk features along its major-axis position-velocity diagram, leading to its classification as an uncertain galaxy [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID21 is a CLJ1001 member with minor-axis asymmetries and a major-axis PVD lacking typical disk features; the paper conservatively classifies it as uncertain despite a velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: ID19 - 21 These galaxies are members of the forming cluster CLJ1001 at z = 2.51 Wang_2016, Gomez_2019, Champagne_2021 . The CO(3-2) kinematics of ID19 - 21, along with another galaxy member of the same cluster (130901), are recently studied by Xiao_2022 using the same data employed here. We exclude 130901 from our analysis due to the low SNR.

ID21 has, instead, asymmetries in the minor-axis PVD, while its major-axis PVD lacks the bright features typical of rotating disks. One potential explanation for this latter feature is that ID21 has a rotation curve that is slowly rising in the inner regions. However, since the angular resolution of the data employed in this paper does not allow us to robustly test this scenario, we classify this target as an uncertain galaxy. Xiao_2022 interpreted, instead, the gradient in the velocity map of ID21, also visible in Fig. fig:pv6 , as due to the presence of a rotating disk.

gmw_00010003.name: ID22
gmw_00010003.overview: ID22 is a high-redshift galaxy that is well described as a rotating disk [arXiv:2303.16227]. However, its morphological parameters derived from Hubble Space Telescope data may be biased by dust attenuation or light from young stars [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID22 is analyzed with high-resolution CO(5-4) observations; a rotating disk describes its data, although HST-derived morphology may be biased by young stars or dust.

quotes.quote: ID22, 26 - 27 These galaxies are recently studied by Cassata_2020 using ALMA observations with angular resolutions of 0.6 . The 0.17 observations of the CO(5-4) used in our work allow us to resolve their kinematic structures. A rotating disk is a good description of the data only for ID22, despite the strong misalignment between the morphological position angle derived from HST data and PA_ kin . However, we note that, similarly to ID3, the HST data for ID22 probe the rest-frame UV and, therefore, the morphological parameters may be biased due to the contamination from the light of young stars and/or by dust attenuation.

gmw_00010004.name: ID24
gmw_00010004.overview: ID24 is a high-redshift galaxy located within the SSA 22 protocluster [arXiv:2303.16227]. High-resolution observations of the galaxy reveal the characteristic features of an edge-on rotating disk, along with minor-axis gas emission that is not reproduced by modeling [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID24 is an SSA 22 protocluster galaxy whose high-resolution data show edge-on rotating-disk features, but it also has minor-axis gas emission not reproduced by the model.

quotes.quote: ID23 - 25 These galaxies are members of the SSA 22 protocluster Steidel_1998 that was extensively studied with ALMA Umehata_2015, Umehata_2017, Umehata_2018 .

Extended Lyman- emission from multiple filaments between galaxies within SSA 22 are identified using MUSE observations Umehata_2019 and are thought to be responsible for the accretion of gas within the protocluster and the growth of galaxies and their supermassive black holes. htbp The high-angular resolution observations employed here allow us to clearly see the typical features of edge-on rotating disks in ID 23, 24, and 25. The three galaxies have very extended S-shape major-axis PVDs (Fig. fig:pv4 ). ID24 has gas emission at 400 km s ^ 1 along the minor-axis PVD that is not reproduced by the model (see arrow in Fig. fig:pv4 ). However, due to the high inclination of the galaxy, these asymmetries are difficult to interpret.

gmw_00010005.name: ID25
gmw_00010005.overview: ID25 is a high-redshift galaxy located within the SSA 22 protocluster [arXiv:2303.16227]. High-resolution observations reveal that it is an edge-on rotating-disk galaxy hosting an X-ray luminous active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID25 is an SSA 22 protocluster member with an X-ray luminous AGN; high-resolution observations reveal edge-on rotating-disk features and extended S-shaped major-axis PVDs.

quotes.quote: ID23 - 25 These galaxies are members of the SSA 22 protocluster Steidel_1998 that was extensively studied with ALMA Umehata_2015, Umehata_2017, Umehata_2018 . Lehmer_2009 identified X-ray luminous AGNs in ID 23 and 25 using observations from the Chandra Space telescope. Extended Lyman- emission from multiple filaments between galaxies within SSA 22 are identified using MUSE observations Umehata_2019 and are thought to be responsible for the accretion of gas within the protocluster and the growth of galaxies and their supermassive black holes. htbp The high-angular resolution observations employed here allow us to clearly see the typical features of edge-on rotating disks in ID 23, 24, and 25. The three galaxies have very extended S-shape major-axis PVDs (Fig. fig:pv4 ).

gmw_00010006.name: ID26
gmw_00010006.overview: ID26 is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:1509.01101, arXiv:2303.16227] that has been identified as an ambiguous Lyman continuum candidate [arXiv:1509.01101]. High-resolution CO(5-4) observations reveal minor-axis irregularities and distorted iso-velocity contours, which may indicate the presence of a warp [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: The paper presents ID=72687 as an ambiguous z=3.3 LyC candidate with unphysical escape fraction, possible HST-resolved blending, uncertain redshift features, and colors favoring lower-redshift contamination.

quotes.quote: The situation is less clear for source ID=72687 in Table table:gal . This galaxy has an escape fraction of 292 , clearly not physical under reasonable assumptions for the stellar populations involved and for the IGM absorption. From an analysis of the HST image (Fig. obj72687ima ) we cannot confirm with high confidence the presence of two different objects as in the previous cases, although two distinct blobs are seen in the HST thumbnail. From a detailed analysis of the LBC images, we find a small shift of 0.5 pixel, corresponding to 0.1 arcsec, from the center of the source in the R band and in the U band. The separation between the two blobs in the HST image is slightly larger, of 0.3 arcsec.

This could be an other case of blending of a z 3.3 galaxy with a foreground source, but based on the present data we cannot exclude that the two blobs detected by HST are all at z 3 , and this could be an example of a stellar-powered LyC emission. A detail analysis of the VUDS spectrum indicates that the redshift of this source is based only on a noisy Lyman- and strong CIV and SiIV lines, all in absorption. There are also other absorption lines not associated with the z=3.32 solution, but it was not possible to derive a secure redshift from these lines.

sources.summary: ID26 is studied with high-resolution CO(5-4) data and classified as uncertain because minor-axis irregularities, residuals, and distorted iso-velocity contours may indicate a warp.

quotes.quote: ID22, 26 - 27 These galaxies are recently studied by Cassata_2020 using ALMA observations with angular resolutions of 0.6 . The 0.17 observations of the CO(5-4) used in our work allow us to resolve their kinematic structures.

ID26 has irregularities in the minor-axis PVD (see arrow in Fig. fig:pv7 ), systematic residuals in the channel maps and distorted iso-velocity contours in the approaching side of the galaxy. These features may be due to the presence of a warp in a disk. However, the angular resolution of the observations do not allow us to verify this scenario. Therefore, to be conservative, we classify ID26 as uncertain.

gmw_00010007.name: ID27
gmw_00010007.overview: ID27 is a high-redshift galaxy classified as a merger [arXiv:2303.16227]. It is likely an unresolved interacting system, exhibiting a highly irregular velocity field and position angle misalignments [arXiv:2303.16227].

sources.summary: ID27 is classified as a merger because its PVDs and velocity field are very irregular and its ALMA and kinematic position angles are strongly misaligned.

quotes.quote: ID22, 26 - 27 These galaxies are recently studied by Cassata_2020 using ALMA observations with angular resolutions of 0.6 . The 0.17 observations of the CO(5-4) used in our work allow us to resolve their kinematic structures.

On the contrary, we classify ID27 as a merger. This target has a very irregular PVDs and velocity field and a difference between PA_ ALMA and PA_ kin of 45 , another indication that it is, likely, a non-resolved interacting system.

gmw_00010008.name: WISEA J032210.70-425303.9
gmw_00010008.overview: WISEA J032210.70-425303.9 is an elliptical, early-type galaxy [arXiv:2308.06019] located at a redshift of approximately z=0.0619 [arXiv:2308.06019]. It is the host galaxy of the supernova SN 2022ywc, which exploded in its remote outskirts [arXiv:2308.06019].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISEA J032210.70-425303.9 as the elliptical host of SN 2022ywc, giving its redshift, distance, supernova offset, and low expected host extinction.

quotes.quote: Host galaxy, redshift and distance \ was discovered in the outskirts of the elliptical galaxy WISEA J032210.70-425303.9. The redshift of the host galaxy, obtained from the NASA Extragalactic Database (NED), is z=0.061913 0.000103 2009MNRAS.399..683J . Assuming H_0 = 70 and a flat Universe with _ M =0.3 , the derived luminosity distance is 278 Mpc, or a distance modulus of 37.22 mag. The SN is offset by 15 from the galaxy nucleus, corresponding to a projected radial separation of 21 kpc at this distance.
The host extinction is expected to be minimal, given the remote location of the SN within its early-type host.

gmw_00010009.name: source 369
gmw_00010009.overview: Source 369 is an optically faint active galactic nucleus located at a spectroscopic redshift of 2.914 [arXiv:1006.0746]. Its spectrum displays a characteristic C IV emission line, and its position is well-matched across radio, infrared, and X-ray observations [arXiv:1006.0746].

sources.summary: The paper identifies source 369 as an optically faint AGN at spectroscopic redshift 2.914, matching radio, IRAC, and X-ray positions and a consistent photometric redshift.

quotes.quote: Note that source 369, despite being very faint in the optical, has a reported spectroscopic redshift of z=2.914 in Chapman2005 . The position of the spectroscopic source comes from the radio (VLA - 1.4 GHz) catalogue of Richards2000 and agrees within 0.15 with the IRAC position. The spectrum is typical of an AGN (C IV line) and the X-ray position is 1.1 away, so we are confident that this is the correct counterpart. The photometric redshift derived by EAZY (2.80) is close to the spectroscopic value.

gmw_00010010.name: source 246
gmw_00010010.overview: Source 246 is a V606 dropout galaxy with red optical and near-infrared colors [arXiv:1006.0746]. Although its spectroscopic redshift is undetermined, its mid-infrared colors and photometric data suggest it is a normal galaxy at a redshift between 2 and 3 [arXiv:1006.0746].

sources.summary: The paper describes source 246 as a V606 dropout with red colors, uncertain spectroscopy, no MIPS detection, and mid-infrared colors favoring normal galaxy templates at z between 2 and 3.

quotes.quote: The other V_ 606 dropout (source 246) has a red i_ 775 z_ 850 colour (1.1), like most of the sources in the HST detected sample, and its V_ 606 i_ 775 colour is a lower limit ( V_ 606 i_ 775 >1.275 if we take the V_ 606 2 limit), being compatible both with early-type templates at z 1.5 and with very high redshift late-type templates, depending on the true V_ 606 i_ 775 colour. This source is also observed spectroscopically, but neither a redshift nor a spectral type could be derived Barger2003,Trouille2008 . It has no MIPS 24 m detection, but its mid-infrared colours ( [ 5.8 ] [ 8.0 ] =0.265 ) argue against a high redshift, they are explained by normal galaxy templates with 2<z<3 agreeing with the photometric redshift (2.80) derived using 7 optical and infrared bands.
