gmw_00010011.overview: The object 005055.7 + 293328 is a red quasar hosted by a spiral galaxy [arXiv:1210.1894]. This host galaxy features a dust lane in its central region, and it has been modeled with a disk, a bulge, and spiral arms [arXiv:1210.1894].

sources.summary: The paper describes 005055.7 + 293328 as a red QSO hosted by a spiral galaxy with a central dust lane, modeled with spiral arms, disk, bulge, and an added Sersic component.

quotes.quote: The object 005055.7 + 293328 is hosted by a spiral galaxy and has a prominent dust lane in the central region. In order to obtain the best estimate for the nuclear flux we fit the host galaxy with multiple components so as to minimize the residuals after subtraction. The best fit included the spiral arms, disk, bulge, and an additional narrow S e rsic profile (not included in Table table:galfit ) to account for the asymmetric light distribution due to the dust lane. While fitting all these components allows us to obtain a good fit for the PSF, it is likely that we are underestimating the flux and the effective radius of the bulge, since we are splitting it into two components.

gmw_00010012.overview: The object 015721.0+171248 is a red quasar host galaxy [arXiv:1210.1894]. It is unique among its sample in possessing a stellar disk that is more luminous than its central bulge [arXiv:1210.1894].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 015721.0+171248 as the red QSO host whose disk is more luminous than its bulge, with disk rotation and QSO contamination complicating velocity-dispersion fitting.

quotes.quote: The other object, 015721.0+171248, is the only one in the sample that has a disk that is more luminous than the bulge. The rotating component from a dynamically cold disk can bias \ toward higher values (when the disk is edge-on) or lower values when the disk is face-on; see e.g., bennert2011a . Although the disk of 015721.0+171248 has a lower inclination than that of 005055.7+293328, its velocity dispersion may still be somewhat increased by the dynamically cold component. More importantly, 015721.0+171248 is the only object for which there is significant QSO contamination in the stellar absorption line spectrum, so that we were not able to obtain a reliable fit of \ for the red region.

gmw_00010013.name: PKS 1717+177
gmw_00010013.overview: PKS 1717+177 is an active galaxy [arXiv:2311.14231] located at a distance of approximately 600 Mpc [arXiv:2311.14231]. It is classified as a flaring source [arXiv:2311.14231].

sources.summary: The paper considers PKS 1717+177 as a possible active-galaxy source direction for the ultrahigh-energy cosmic ray, but rejects it because its 600 Mpc distance is too large for propagation.

quotes.quote: We find the active galaxy PKS 1717+177 is located within 2.5 ^ of the calculated direction for a proton primary. PKS 1717+177 is a flaring source , which has been proposed as potential cosmic ray sources . However, its distance of 600 Mpc (corresponding to a redshift of 0.137) is expected to be too large for UHECR propagation to Earth, because the average propagation distance at an energy of 244 EeV is calculated to be 30 Mpc for both proton and iron primaries . We therefore disfavor PKS 17171+177 as the source of this event.

gmw_00010014.name: HDFS 85
gmw_00010014.overview: HDFS 85 is a Lyman-break galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z=3.16 [arXiv:astro-ph/0501382]. It has an estimated diameter of 0.72 arcseconds and was not detected in OII+44 imaging, which provides an upper limit on its relative escape fraction [arXiv:astro-ph/0501382].

sources.summary: The paper treats HDFS 85 as an L* Lyman-break galaxy at z=3.170, undetected in OII+44 imaging, yielding lower UV-to-Lyman-continuum flux limits and a relative escape-fraction upper limit.

quotes.quote: Diameters of 0.''72 (HDFS 85) and 0.''41 (HDFS 1825) are estimated from the half-light radii of the galaxies through the B_ 450 filter reported in the HDFS WFPC2 catalog version 2 cas00 . We adopt 2.5 2.5 pix as the smoothing scale. The FWHM of the PSF in the smoothed image is 8.33 pixel ( =1.''7 ). None of the sample galaxies was detected at a 3- level in the smoothed OII+44 image. The upper limit on their flux density has been based on the smoothed PSF size because they are smaller than the PSF. The measured rms fluctuations within a square box of 1.''7 1.''7 in the smoothed image around the sample galaxies translate into 3- limiting magnitudes of 27.37 mag(AB) and 27.40 mag(AB) for HDFS 85 and 1825, respectively.

Therefore, we obtain the 3- lower limits of the corrected flux density ratio F_ 1400 /F_ 900 of 15.57 and 10.17 for HDFS 85 ( z=3.16 ) and HDFS 1825 ( z=3.27 ), respectively. Assuming (L_ 1400 /L_ 900 )_ int =3.0 as found from Table 3 and mean IGM opacities integrated over the filter transmission, we obtain 3- upper limits on the relative escape fraction as 72 and 216 for HDFS 85 and 1825, respectively.

gmw_00010015.name: HDFS 1825
gmw_00010015.overview: HDFS 1825 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 3.275 [arXiv:astro-ph/0501382]. It has an estimated diameter of 0.41 arcseconds and was undetected in OII+44 imaging [arXiv:astro-ph/0501382].

sources.summary: The paper treats HDFS 1825 as an L* Lyman-break galaxy at z=3.275, undetected in OII+44 imaging, with reported UV-to-Lyman-continuum flux and relative escape-fraction limits.

quotes.quote: Among the new redshifts, we got only one galaxy with a high enough redshift, z_ sp =3.275 (HDFS 1825), in contrast to the expectation from photometric redshifts. Indeed, for the 13 galaxies with z_ sp , we always find z_ sp < z_ ph , again. There is another galaxy with z_ sp =3.170 (HDFS 85) which was confirmed by our spectroscopy. This redshift is slightly smaller than the limiting redshift, however, the transmission efficiency of the OII+44 filter at the Lyman limit of the galaxy is as small as a few percent of the peak efficiency (see Fig. 1). The contamination of nonionizing photons is small enough, especially in the context of upper limit measurements. Therefore, our final sample consists of the two galaxies.

Diameters of 0.''72 (HDFS 85) and 0.''41 (HDFS 1825) are estimated from the half-light radii of the galaxies through the B_ 450 filter reported in the HDFS WFPC2 catalog version 2 cas00 . We adopt 2.5 2.5 pix as the smoothing scale. The FWHM of the PSF in the smoothed image is 8.33 pixel ( =1.''7 ). None of the sample galaxies was detected at a 3- level in the smoothed OII+44 image. The upper limit on their flux density has been based on the smoothed PSF size because they are smaller than the PSF. The measured rms fluctuations within a square box of 1.''7 1.''7 in the smoothed image around the sample galaxies translate into 3- limiting magnitudes of 27.37 mag(AB) and 27.40 mag(AB) for HDFS 85 and 1825, respectively.

gmw_00010016.name: HFF4C-YJ3
gmw_00010016.overview: HFF4C-YJ3 is a faint galaxy candidate located at a high redshift of approximately z~9 [arXiv:1510.06400]. It lies close to the galaxy candidate HFF4C-YJ1, and the two objects may potentially be multiple images of the same lensed galaxy [arXiv:1510.06400].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HFF4C-YJ3 as a faint z~9 candidate close to HFF4C-YJ1 whose colors and critical-curve position are consistent with, but do not prove, multiple imaging.

quotes.quote: HFF4C-YJ1 and HFF4C-YJ3 -- HFF4C-YJ1 is a bright z 9 galaxy candidate in \ discovered by zheng12 . We find a faint z 9 galaxy candidate, HFF4C-YJ3, close to this galaxy (see Figures fig:colorimage and fig:multiple_candidates ). Our best-fitting mass model has a critical curve that is placed near these galaxies.

Therefore, it is possible that these two galaxies are in fact multiple images of a z 9 galaxy. The reliability of this multiple image system is not very high, because there are not many known multiple images around this system, and therefore our mass model in this region includes relatively large uncertainties. The \ colors of YJ1 and YJ3 are 0.24 0.04 and 0.16 0.23 , respectively, and are consistent with being multiple images.

gmw_00010017.name: HFF4C-YJ4
gmw_00010017.overview: HFF4C-YJ4 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate located at a redshift of approximately 9 [arXiv:1510.06400]. It is positioned near a gravitational lensing critical curve and is paired with a nearby faint red galaxy, with both objects identified as multiple images of a single lensed source [arXiv:1510.06400].

sources.summary: The paper describes HFF4C-YJ4 as a z~9 candidate near a critical curve, paired with a faint red object as reliable multiple images used as system 38 constraints.

quotes.quote: HFF4C-YJ4 -- This is a z 9 galaxy in \ near the critical curve. We find another faint red galaxy nearby this galaxy (see Figure fig:multiple_candidates ). The color of this faint red galaxy is consistent with being at z 9 , but it is below the detection limit used for the dropout selection. The relative positions of these two galaxies are fully consistent with being multiple images of a single z 9 galaxy. Given its high reliability, we include the positions of these galaxies as constraints in our mass modeling as system 38.
The \ colors of YJ4 and the faint red galaxy are 0.15 0.24 and 0.19 0.24 , respectively. This is consistent with being multiple images.

gmw_00010018.name: IRAS 02044 + 7048
gmw_00010018.overview: IRAS 02044 + 7048 is a nearby barred spiral galaxy classified as SBa [arXiv:2301.01000]. Located at a distance of approximately 50 Mpc, the galaxy hosts the fast radio burst FRB 20220319D [arXiv:2301.01000].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 02044 + 7048 as the secure nearby barred-spiral host of FRB 20220319D, deriving its redshift, size, FRB offset, inclination, and spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: The galaxy coincident with the FRB location is cataloged in the NASA Extragalactic Database as IRAS 02044 + 7048, with a spectroscopic redshift of 0.011 indicating a distance of approximately 50 Mpc. Using observations described below, we derive a spectroscopic redshift of 0.0111 0.0004 , indicating a luminosity distance of 49.6 Mpc. An isophotal fit to the PS1 i band image indicates an effective radius of 2.7 0.2 kpc, and the FRB is offset by 2.3 0.3 kpc. The isophotal analysis also indicates an inclination of 23 3 deg. A barred spiral morphology, with a classification of SBa, is evident from the image.

Under the assumption that FRB 20220319D is extragalactic, the spatial association between FRB 20220319D and IRAS 02044 + 7048 is secure. It is unlikely that FRB 20220319D lies significantly beyond IRAS 02044 + 7048; the morphology of the galaxy indicates the presence of a warm-ISM phase, and scattering in this ISM would likely cause temporal broadening of several hundred milliseconds at our observing frequencies coc22,occ+22 .
We obtained long-slit optical spectroscopy of IRAS 02044 + 7048 using the Double Spectrograph DBSP; og82 mounted on the Palomar 200-inch Hale telescope on 2022 June 01 (UT).

gmw_00010019.name: KK96
gmw_00010019.overview: KK96 is a low-surface-brightness dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1406.6982]. It displays clear tidal tails toward the east and west, which indicates a recent interaction [arXiv:1406.6982].

sources.summary: The paper identifies KK96 as a low-surface-brightness dwarf spheroidal with tidal tails, uncertain M96 Group membership, and optical properties consistent with a dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: In addition to the linear streams, we also detect diffuse starlight around the galaxy KK96 (located southwest of NGC 3384). This galaxy, classified as a low surface brightness dwarf spheroidal (dSph) by karachentseva98 and karachentsev13 , displays clear tidal tails toward the east and west in our images (clearly visible even in the unbinned images: see Figure fig:overlay ), indicating a recent interaction. Due to a lack of distance constraints for this galaxy, its relation to the M96 Group is unclear. However, aside from the M96 Group, it appears to have no obvious nearby neighbors that it may have interacted with. We can attempt to constrain KK96's location to some degree through its optical properties.

If placed at the distance of the M96 Group, the galaxy would have an absolute magnitude of M_ V = -13.3 , similar to the Fornax dwarf vandenbergh08 , and an effective radius of 960 pc, consistent with the empirical M_ V - _ e,V and M_ V - r_ e relations for Sph type galaxies from kormendy12 .

gmw_00010020.name: J1000+0234
gmw_00010020.overview: J1000+0234 is a submillimeter galaxy system at a redshift of 4.55 that is dominated by a young starburst [arXiv:1607.07534]. The system contains multiple components observed in Lyman-alpha emission and likely represents a galaxy merger [arXiv:1607.07534].

sources.summary: The paper studies J1000+0234 as a z=4.55 SMG merger/starburst, using SINFONI to set [O II] and unobscured SFR limits that imply strong extinction.

quotes.quote: We present deep SINFONI K band integral field spectra of two submillimeter (SMG) galaxy systems: BR 1202-0725 and J1000+0234, at z=4.69 and 4.55 respectively. Spectra extracted for each object in the two systems do not show any signature of the [ O ii ] 3726,29 emission-lines, placing upper flux limits of 3.9 and 2.5 10^ 18 for BR 1202-0725 and J1000+0234, respectively. Using the relation between the star formation rate (SFR) and the luminosity of the [ O ii ] doublet from kennicutt98 , we estimate unobscured SFR upper limits of 10-15 and 30-40 for the objects of the two systems, respectively. For the SMGs, these values are at least two orders of magnitude lower than those derived from SED and IR luminosities. The differences on the SFR values would correspond to internal extinction of, at least, 3.4 - 4.9 and 2.1 - 3.6 mag in the visual for BR 1202-0725 and J1000+0234 SMGs, respectively.

COSMOS J100054+023436 (hereafter J1000+0234, z = 4.55 ) is a SMG dominated by a starburst forming stars at a rate of > 1000 with a young age of 2-8 Myr, estimated using infrared and radio measurements capak08 . This SMG presents multiple components observed in the Ly emission-line, distributed along a region of 3'' in size, and residing at the same redshift. This galaxy presents different morphology at different wavelengths, from the UV (Ly ) to the near-infrared (rest-frame optical with [ O ii ] doublet), likely indicating the presence of spatially resolved stellar populations on arcsec scales capak08 .

gmw_00010021.name: RA = 12h 25m 24.10s, dec = +08 16 54 0
aliases_external: AGES Virgo optically dark cloud at RA 12h25m24.10s Dec +08d16m54.0s
gmw_00010021.overview: RA = 12h 25m 24.10s, dec = +08 16 54 0 is an optically dark cloud located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2009.11300]. It has an estimated neutral hydrogen mass of 10^7.64 solar masses and a V-band luminosity below 1.1 million solar luminosities [arXiv:2009.11300].

sources.summary: The paper deeply images a Virgo optically dark cloud identified by coordinates, finds no optical counterpart, constrains radius, H I mass, and optical luminosity, and weighs possible origins.

quotes.quote: Eight optically dark clouds were discovered in Virgo by AGES. We selected as our target (coordinates RA = 12h 25m 24.10s, dec = +08 16 54 0) because it has one of the highest signal-to-noise ratios (11.4) and velocity widths (W20 = 164 ), though these features are similar to those of the other clouds. The width of the Arecibo beam puts an upper limit on the radius of of 105 .
The mass of was estimated as 10^ 7.64 M_ ( t12 ).

Using Fig. fig:artificial , we estimated that a reasonable upper limit on the apparent V band magnitude of the dark cloud is 19.0 mag. We neglected the small uncertainty of the photometric calibration, as well as the foreground extinction of 0.06 mag given by the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ . At the adopted distance, the V band luminosity of the dark cloud is estimated to be below 1.1 10^ 7 L_ .

gmw_00010022.name: IC 4051
gmw_00010022.overview: IC 4051 is a giant elliptical galaxy located on the outskirts of the Coma Cluster [arXiv:1811.11794]. It hosts a large population of globular clusters that form a symmetrical, elongated elliptical shape and represent the second-highest cluster surface density in the region [arXiv:1811.11794].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4051 as a giant elliptical on Coma’s outskirts with a large globular-cluster population, a third symmetric elongated overdensity, and the second-highest measured cluster density.

quotes.quote: These new pointings are crucial, as they include NGC 4889 and IC 4051. IC 4051 is another giant elliptical on the outskirts of Coma that is known to have a large population of globular clusters woodworth2000 .

A third, less populous, agglomeration of globular clusters can be associated with IC \ 4051, which defines the third peak of the surface density of globular clusters. The globular clusters associated with the IC \ 4051 overdensity form a rather symmetrical and elongated elliptical shape. Interestingly, the second highest surface density corresponds to IC \ 4051 instead of the other cD, NGC \ 4874.

gmw_00010023.name: COS-DR3-202019
gmw_00010023.overview: COS-DR3-202019 is a massive, dusty star-forming galaxy with a star formation rate of 82 solar masses per year [arXiv:2208.04329]. It is characterized by ongoing star formation, emission lines, and a radius that is three times larger than any other galaxy in its sample [arXiv:2208.04329].

sources.summary: The paper treats COS-DR3-202019 as the sample’s reddest, most massive object, with SFR 82 solar masses per year, an unusually large radius, ongoing star formation, and significant emission lines.

quotes.quote: The two notable exceptions to this are COS-DR3-202019 and SXDS-27434 Tanaka2019 . The former has SFR=82 M_ /yr and is the reddest and most massive of the sample, consistent with a dusty star-forming galaxy Forrest2020b , while the latter has SFR=24 M_ /yr and has the bluest (U-V)_ REST color of the sample Valentino2020 .

The most obvious exception to this in the MAGAZ3NE sample is COS-DR3-202019 (the most massive galaxy in the sample), which has a radius 3 larger than any other galaxy in the sample, and is also the only one that shows evidence of ongoing star formation (see Figure fig:colors ), but is still consistent with a 1-to-1 ratio between stellar and dynamical mass within 1 . However, Forrest2020b model stellar masses for the MAGAZ3NE galaxies in this sample after correcting broadband photometry for any strong emission lines seen in the spectra ( , , ), though of the sample here only COS-DR3-202019 shows significant emission.

gmw_00010024.name: XMM-VID1-2075
gmw_00010024.overview: XMM-VID1-2075 is a high-redshift, massive galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z = 3.45 [arXiv:2208.04329]. Spectroscopic analysis indicates that the galaxy has a stellar age of 300 to 500 million years [arXiv:2208.04329].

sources.summary: The paper gives XMM-VID1-2075 independent NIRES spectroscopy, compares it with MOSFIRE data, finds similar redshifts and spectra, and adopts the full NIRES fit for velocity-dispersion work.

quotes.quote: In the case of XMM-VID1-2075, the only MAGAZ3NE UMG in this work without H band MOSFIRE spectroscopy, J , H , and K band NIRES spectroscopy was independently obtained (PI: Gomez; Gomez et al., in prep). A comparison of the two (very similar) K band spectra is presented in Appendix App:K2075 .

One of the UMGs in this work, XMM-VID1-2075, has both a MOSFIRE K band spectrum and a NIRES spectrum, which also includes both K band and H band data with some signal-to-noise. The spectra appear quite similar (Figure fig:App_2075 ). We fit the K band spectrum from each instrument with the galaxy photometry using FAST++ and compare the results. The redshifts from the two fits are very similar, with z_ MOSFIRE =3.4523 and z_ NIRES =3.4482 , a difference of 0.1 . In both cases, the best fit indicates a galaxy with 11.5 , A_V 0.3 , and age 300-500 Myr.

gmw_00010025.name: UGC 12549
gmw_00010025.overview: UGC 12549 is a gas-rich Im-type dwarf galaxy located near the galaxy Arp 212 [arXiv:0808.1696]. It has been identified as a possible donor for the polar ring of Arp 212 through a past interaction [arXiv:0808.1696].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 12549 as a gas-rich Im dwarf near Arp 212 with similar velocity, high HI content, and a possible role as donor for Arp 212’s polar ring.

quotes.quote: The NED database contains only one galaxy---UGC 12549, inside the region of 1 radius (414 kpc) centered on Arp 212 within 1000 interval of the line-of-sight velocity of Arp 212. It is an Im-type galaxy, which has almost exactly the same systemic velocity as Arp 212 --- 1634 , but is 3^m fainter in terms of integrated magnitude. Its distance from Arp 212 (projected onto the sky-plane) is 23 6 (163 kpc), or 15D_ 25 . This galaxy is very rich in gas: the total 21-cm flux of UGC 12549 is only twice lower than that of Arp 212 (Springob et al., ). The total HI mass in UGC 12549 then must be 10^9 M_ , and the HI-to-the-total-mass ratio in this galaxy must be several times higher than in Arp 212, where it is about 10 .

The polar ring may have formed as a result of the interaction with the gas-rich dwarf galaxy UGC 12549, however, new observations of the distribution and kinematics of neutral hydrogen are required to either confirm or disprove this scenario.

gmw_00010026.name: MCG+07-47-10
gmw_00010026.overview: MCG+07-47-10 is a face-on, late-type Sbc spiral galaxy characterized by distinctive spiral arms and a bulge [arXiv:1609.04820]. The galaxy exhibits modest star formation, and its radio luminosity is dominated by this star-forming activity [arXiv:1609.04820].

sources.summary: The paper reports MCG+07-47-10 as a face-on Sbc spiral DRAGN host with visible spiral arms, disk radio emission, modest star formation, and unusually low radio luminosity.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy, MCG+07-47-10, is a face-on late-type Sbc galaxy with distinctive spiral arms and prominent bulge suggesting a high black hole mass. Using WISE infra-red and GALEX UV data we show that this galaxy has a star formation rate of 0.16-0.75 M _ yr ^ 1 , and that the radio luminosity is dominated by star-formation. We demonstrate that this spiral DRAGN has similar environmental properties to others of this class, but has a comparatively low radio luminosity of L_ 1.4 GHz = 1.12 10 ^ 22 W Hz ^ 1 , two orders of magnitude smaller than other known spiral DRAGNs.

On inspection of the Digitized Sky Survey (DSS), the host galaxy (denoted ``C'' in Fig. fig:radioimage ), MCG+07-47-10, is a late type galaxy with a classification of Sbc Voro1968 , a galaxy with spiral arms and a bulge. The limited resolution of the DSS means that the presence of a bar cannot be ruled out. The optical DSS image of this galaxy can be seen in Fig. fig:opticalimage with the 5 radio contour overlaid. The spiral arms of this face-on galaxy are clearly visible and extended radio emission is seen coincident with the optical disk.

gmw_00010027.name: J221024.49+114247.0
gmw_00010027.overview: J221024.49+114247.0 is a pre-coalescence merging system at z=0.09 that exhibits a disrupted merger morphology [arXiv:2106.07060]. The system contains two counter-rotating stellar and gas disks aligned along the line of sight, with a dust lane associated with the smaller, foreground galaxy [arXiv:2106.07060].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J221024.49+114247.0 as a pre-coalescence merger with two counter-rotating disks along the line of sight, disrupted morphology, and a dust lane associated with the smaller galaxy.

quotes.quote: Pre-coalescence merger We identify J221024.49+114247.0 as a merging system caught in action. We observe two distinct counter-rotating discs, aligned along the line of sight, with a line-of-sight velocity difference of V = 450 km s^ 1 . We find one main galaxy with an inclination of i_1 = 49 ^ and a second galaxy of i_2 = 46 ^ as a smaller counterpart. Using high-resolution imaging from the Megacam-CFHT footprint, we identified a dust lane that is aligned with the stellar component of the smaller galaxy and is associated with measured extinction in the main galaxy, suggesting a scenario where the small galaxy is positioned in front of the main galaxy along the line of sight, as shown in Fig. fig:schematic_view .

We have presented a multi-wavelength analysis of a pre-coalescence merger, J221024.49+114247.0, at z=0.09 . Originally identified with a double-peaked feature in the SDSS fibre and a disrupted merger morphology, this galaxy has been observed with MaNGA. We developed a fitting procedure based on a double-Gaussian function and applied it throughout the MaNGA field of view. We have been able to highlight the presence of two counter-rotating discs along the line of sight in gas as well as in stars.

gmw_00010028.name: SDSS J125809.31+351943.0
gmw_00010028.overview: SDSS J125809.31+351943.0 is a changing-state quasar that brightened by four magnitudes in the optical band between 1983 and 2015 [arXiv:2011.01127]. This extremely variable active nucleus hosts a supermassive black hole and features a broad-line region composed of two distinct components [arXiv:2306.13930].

sources.summary: The paper studies SDSS J125809.31+351943.0 as an extremely variable changing-state quasar, deriving its black-hole mass and two BLR components tied to accretion-state changes.

quotes.quote: We targeted SDSS J125809.31+351943.0, an extremely variable CSQ, to study its central core structure and kinematics. We conducted reverberation mapping with optical spectroscopy to explore the structure of the BLR and estimate the black hole mass. The results from H reverberation mapping indicated a black hole mass of 10^ 9.64^ +0.11 _ 0.20 M_ .

These variations in _ ox and the Eddington ratio (from 0.4 to 2.4 ) exhibitied behavior similar to state transitions observed in X-ray binary systems. Spectral analysis of H revealed a predominantly double-peaked profile during dim periods, transitioning to include a single-peaked component as the quasar brightened, suggesting that H contains a mixture of two components. Each of these components has its distinct characteristics: the first is a double-peaked profile that remains stable despite changes in the accretion rate, while the second is a variable single-peaked profile. Using time lags from reverberation mapping, we estimated the spatial relationships between these BLR components, the accretion disk, and the dust torus. Our results suggest that the BLR consists of two distinct components, each differing in location and origin.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J125809.31+351943.0 as a changing-state quasar that brightened by four magnitudes, with coordinated optical, mid-infrared, and broad-emission-line changes.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a quasar, SDSS J125809.31+351943.0 (J1258), which brightened in optical for 4 mag from 1983 to 2015, which is one of the largest quasar brightening events so far. The history of optical photometry data of this quasar from the Catalina Real-time Transient Survey and All Sky Automated Survey for Super Novae (ASAS-SN), mid-infrared photometry data from the WISE satellite, and the broad emission line (BEL) flux obtained by spectroscopy of Sloan Digital Sky Survey shows their significant increases between 2003 and 2015. Investigating its CFHT photometric observations in 1983 and the USNO-B catalog, which contains data in 1975 and 1969, we found that the source was 4 mag fainter before than the peak of the recent ASAS-SN photometry. From the history of these data, we identified J1258 as a new Changing-State Quasar (CSQ).

Figure fig:curve_HB shows the time-series of the total H and continuum fluxes of this source. The ratio of H / [ OIII ] was found to have changed by 100 during the observation period. Consequently, we identify J1258 as a new CSQ.

gmw_00010029.name: G09.83808
gmw_00010029.overview: G09.83808 is a strongly lensed submillimeter starburst galaxy located at a redshift of z=6.0 [arXiv:2202.09945]. Its dust continuum emission is characterized by a compact disk with a high infrared surface brightness and substantial gas-phase metallicity [arXiv:2202.09945].

sources.summary: The paper studies G09.83808 as a strongly lensed z=6 submillimeter starburst galaxy with compact dust emission, intense infrared surface brightness, and already substantial gas-phase metallicity.

quotes.quote: We present observations of [ N  ii ] 205 m, [ O  iii ] 88 m and dust emission in a strongly-lensed, submillimeter galaxy (SMG) at z=6.0 , G09.83808, with the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA). Both [ N  ii ] and [ O  iii ] line emissions are detected at >12 in the 0.8 '' resolution maps. Lens modeling indicates that the spatial distribution of the dust continuum emission is well characterized by a compact disk with an effective radius of 0.64 0.02 kpc and a high infrared surface brightness of _ IR =(1.8 0.3) 10^ 12  L_ kpc ^ 2 .

This result supports that G09.83808 is the progenitors of compact quiescent galaxies at z 4 , where the majority of its stars are expected to be formed through a strong and short burst of star formation. G09.83808 and other lensed SMGs show a decreasing trend of the [ N  ii ] line to infrared luminosity ratio with increasing continuum flux density ratio between 63 m and 158 m, as seen in local luminous infrared galaxies (LIRGs). The decreasing trend can be reproduced by photoionization models with increasing ionization parameters. Furthermore, by combining the [ N  ii ] / [ O  iii ] luminosity ratio with far-infrared continuum flux density ratio in G09.83808, we infer that the gas phase metallicity is already Z 0.5-0.7 Z_ .

gmw_00010030.name: NGC 5990
gmw_00010030.overview: NGC 5990 is characterized by spatially extended CO (4-3) emission [arXiv:2107.12529]. It represents an exception where gas density does not correlate with star formation, which may be due to dust obscuration or a prior outflow phase from an active galactic nucleus or starburst [arXiv:1806.02839].

sources.summary: The paper marks NGC 5990 as an exception where gas density does not correlate with star formation, possibly due to obscuration or a prior AGN or starburst outflow phase.

quotes.quote: This correlation between the star formation and high density is also seen in the maps of the majority of the targets shown in the right panels of Figs. fig:density_maps_correlation and fig:density_maps_2 except NGC 5990 and NGC 4303.

As mentioned earlier, exceptions to this spatial correlation does exist in targets like NGC 4303 and NGC 5990 as shown in the bottom two panels of Fig. fig:density_maps_2 which will be discussed in Sect. sect5 .

In targets such as NGC 5990 and NGC 4303, the correlation between gas density and SFR is absent which might be due to obscuration from the dust or the host galaxy itself or if the target went through an outflowing phase from the AGN or starburst previously e.g. riffel16 .

sources.summary: The survey flags NGC 5990’s [C I] observation as calibration-compromised and uses a larger aperture because Gaussian fitting underestimates its spatially extended CO emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 5990 The [ C 1 ] (1--0) observation has issues in calibration, and the data are declared as ``QA2 semipass". Because CO (4--3) emission is spatially extended, single 2D Gaussian fitting by the `` imfit " underestimates the flux. Therefore, we used the 20 aperture to measure the CO (4--3) continuum.

gmw_00010031.name: ESO 069-IG006
gmw_00010031.overview: ESO 069-IG006 is a system consisting of two colliding galaxies [arXiv:2107.12529]. Carbon monoxide, neutral carbon, and dust continuum emissions are detected in the main galaxy, but they are undetected in the smaller companion [arXiv:2107.12529].

sources.summary: The survey describes ESO 069-IG006 as two colliding galaxies, with CO, [C I], and dust continuum detected in the main galaxy but not in the smaller companion.

quotes.quote: ESO 069-IG006 ESO 069-IG00 consists of two colliding galaxies. In the main galaxy, CO (4--3), [ C 1 ] (1--0), and dust continuum were detected. In the smaller pair galaxy (ESO 069-IG00-2), emission were not detected. [ C 1 ] (1--0) emission was observed at the edge of the spectral window.

gmw_00010032.name: ESO 467-G027
gmw_00010032.overview: ESO 467-G027 is classified as a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2107.12529]. Observations of the galaxy have robustly detected carbon monoxide CO (4-3) emission, while neutral carbon [C I] (1-0) emission remains undetected in its nuclear region [arXiv:2107.12529].

sources.summary: The survey finds robust CO in ESO 467-G027 but only marginal nuclear [C I], treating [C I] as undetected and flagging the galaxy as a low-quality C-poor/CO-rich candidate.

quotes.quote: ESO 467-G027 CO (4--3) was robustly detected but the red-shifted components may have been outside of the spectral window. The [ C 1 ] (1--0) was marginally detected in the nuclear region. However, the measured flux is less than the upper limits, and we assume [ C 1 ] (1--0) to be non-detection. This galaxy is a candidate of C ^0 poor/CO-rich galaxy but the data quality is not very good; the upper limit of L'_ [ CI ] (1-0) / L'_ CO(4--3) is > 0.1 .

gmw_00010033.name: GRB990123
gmw_00010033.overview: The host galaxy of GRB990123 is a high-redshift system located at a redshift of z=1.6004 [arXiv:astro-ph/0203154]. Observations of its absorbing gas reveal a warm-halo depletion pattern with a dust extinction of approximately 1.1 magnitudes [arXiv:astro-ph/0203154].

sources.summary: The paper treats the GRB990123 sightline as host-galaxy absorbing gas, deriving Fe and Zn column densities and a warm-halo depletion pattern with about 1.1 mag of dust extinction.

quotes.quote: GRB990123 The optical transient of GRB990123 at z=1.6004 was observed with the Keck/LRIS (Kulkarni et al. 1999) for a final resolution and signal--to--noise ratio of FWHM =11.6 ( 400-700 ) and S/N 30 , respectively. The very weak Fe 2 2260 EW gives inconsistent results with other Fe 2 lines, and thus this detection has not been considered in our analysis. For the four remaining Fe 2 lines, we get a best--fit of N( Fe 2 )=14.78^ +0.17 _ 0.10 and b=50 (Figure f1a ). From the EW of Zn 2 2026 and considering b=50 , we derive a Zn 2 column density of N( Zn 2 ) 14 , and calculate a Zn 2 2062 contribution to the Zn 2 2062 + Cr 2 2062 blend of W_r = 0.58 , to be compared with the detected W_r = 0.48 0.04 .
Best--fits for these two GRB--DLAs give a warm halo cloud depletion pattern (Figure f5 ), and dust extinctions of A_V 1.1 and 0.7 mags for GRB990123 and GRB010222, respectively.

gmw_00010034.name: GRB000926
gmw_00010034.overview: The host galaxy of GRB000926 is a high-redshift system located at a redshift of z=2.0379 [arXiv:astro-ph/0203154]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy reveal at least two absorbing clouds, a high metallicity, and approximately 0.9 magnitudes of extinction [arXiv:astro-ph/0203154].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes GRB000926 as host-galaxy or circumburst absorbing gas, finding multiple absorbing clouds, high metal columns, high metallicity, and about 0.9 mag of extinction.

quotes.quote: GRB000926 GRB000926 at z=2.0379 was observed with the Keck/ESI echelle spectrograph (Castro et al. 2001), with a good signal--to--noise ratio (S/N =10-20 ) and relatively high resolution (FWHM 80 ). Castro et al. (2001) also show a low resolution spectrum, with EW measurements that are systematically larger than those obtained from the high resolution spectrum, but the authors claim that the high resolution measurements are more accurate, so we only use these. The spectrum shows at least two absorbing clouds separated by 168 . In our analysis, we consider the total equivalent widths of the two components together, since the resolution does not allow them to be clearly separated.
Fynbo et al. (2001) report a tentative H 1 column density measurement of N( H 1 ) 2 10^ 21 . This leads to a relatively high metallicity with [ Zn/H ] 0.13 . For GRB000926, we find A_V 0.9 mag.

gmw_00010035.name: UCD5
gmw_00010035.overview: UCD5 is an ultracompact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:0808.0320]. Its stellar population parameters remain highly uncertain due to a cosmic ray hit that contaminated its spectrum [arXiv:0808.0320].

sources.summary: The paper reports that UCD5 has uncertain stellar-population parameters because its LR04 spectrum is contaminated, with possible young and older metal-poor fitting solutions.

quotes.quote: The UCD5 spectrum does not contain strong absorption lines in the FLAMES/Giraffe LR04 spectral range, the Mg b triplet is strongly contaminated by a cosmic ray hit. This causes very vague stellar population parameter determination: 3 confidence contour remains open in the age direction, i.e. age is undetermined. The global ^2 minimum corresponds to a young population ( t=1.2 Gyr, [ Fe/H ] =- 0.49 dex). There is a 1.7 confidence secondary minimum ( t=3.9 Gyr, [ Fe/H ] = - 0.95 dex). In order to chose between the two possible solutions for UCD5 we have reduced and fit its FLAMES/Giraffe spectra obtained in the blue LR02 setup with the wavelength coverage between 3970 \ and 4545 .

gmw_00010036.name: BCG
gmw_00010036.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) of Abell S1063 has an effective radius of 17.0 kpc [arXiv:2003.08475]. Its total stellar mass is estimated to be 1.2 x 10^12 solar masses based on spectral energy distribution and spectrum fitting [arXiv:2003.08475].

sources.summary: The paper measures the Abell S1063 BCG kinematics, light profile, effective radius, and stellar mass, using them with member-galaxy dynamics to reconstruct the cluster mass profile.

quotes.quote: The MUSE data on the BCG are divided into two halves in the two pointings, approximately along the major axis of the BCG (see the inset in Figure fig:sigmaprof ). While this poses some challenges which require masking high-variance pixel regions near the edge of the MUSE fields, it does offer an independent measurement of the profile in elliptical regions on each side of the BCG, where relatively faint cluster galaxies projected in the inner core are masked out. In Figure fig:sigmaprof , we show the measurements in five radial elliptical bins ( b/a=0.75 , PA =319^ ) in the NE portion and 4 bins in the SW portion of the BCG, as a function of the circularized projected radius. We note that the BCG light profile peaks in the NE side, whereas the central bin in the SW side is affected by the pointing edge.

The scale radius r_j is determined by fitting a de Vaucouleurs model to the surface-brightness profile of the BCG, knowing that it is related to the effective radius by = R_e / 0.76 jaffe83 . The effective radius R_e is defined as the radius that contains half the total BCG luminosity (computed as the Kron magnitude in the R band, in our case). The BCG total stellar mass in Equation eq:mbcg , M_* , is obtained by fitting the spectral energy distribution (SED) and the MUSE spectrum assuming a Salpeter stellar initial mass function (IMF; see M20 for details). We find the effective radius R_e= 17.0 1.6 kpc see also table A1 in tor18 , that corresponds to r_j = 22.7 2.1 kpc, and the total stellar mass M_* = 1.2 ^ +0.2 _ 0.6 10^ 12 M_ .

gmw_00010037.name: ESO 217-G29
gmw_00010037.overview: ESO 217-G29 is a bright barred spiral galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 0.032 [arXiv:0904.3825]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus classified as a Seyfert galaxy, which exhibits a complex X-ray spectrum with intrinsic absorption [arXiv:0904.3825].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 217-G29 as a barred spiral Seyfert-2 galaxy and refines its X-ray spectral modeling, absorption, Fe line fit, redshift, and nuclear luminosity.

quotes.quote: Source 239 (XMMU J121029.0 522148) was originally identified as a new Seyfert galaxy in Paper I (source 127), due to its X--ray spectrum and to its positional coincidence with the galaxy ESO 217-G29, a bright ( R =14.93) barred spiral with a spectroscopic redshift of 0.032 Visvanathan1992 also detected in the Digitised Sky Survey images. From the merged image (see SourceDet ) we have now obtained a total of 821 counts in the energy range 0.3--8 keV for source 239, which is 38 higher with respect to that of the data set used in Paper I. For this reason, we repeated the source spectral analysis in order to achieve a more accurate characterisation of the X--ray spectrum. The spectrum of the source between 1 and 12 keV is complex and cannot be fitted by a single-component model.

The fit with the free z better accounts for the Fe line whose fitted profile is now significant at the 90 confidence level, with a fitted centroid energy of 6.0 keV. The intrinsic absorption associated to the dust torus ( A_ T ) is 72.16 10 ^ 22 cm ^ 2 , very similar to the previous case. All best-fit parameters for the two cases are summarised in the Table fit . The 2--10 keV unabsorbed flux (calculated with XSPEC ) of the primary nuclear component is 6.59 ^ +2.13 _ 1.23 10 ^ 13 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and the X--ray luminosity, computed for a redshift of 0.032, is 2.75 ^ +0.89 _ 0.51 10 ^ 42 erg s ^ 1 .

gmw_00010038.name: WISEA J213541.80-534413.2
gmw_00010038.overview: WISEA J213541.80-534413.2 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.06254 [arXiv:2304.14692]. It is a member of the Abell 3785 cluster and features a radio jet that exhibits a steepening spectral index as it propagates outward from the core [arXiv:2304.14692].

sources.summary: The paper considers WISEA J213541.80-534413.2 as a possible, but unlikely, source of nearby diffuse emission, noting its AGN jet and steepening spectral index.

quotes.quote: Just 15 arcmin to the south-east, there is another Abell 3785 member WISEA J213541.80-534413.2 at z=0.06254 ( D_c 264 Mpc) that shows an AGN jet pointing towards this extended emission and directly via other Abell 3785 member J213522.85--534200.3 at the redshift z=0.08114 ( D_c 341 Mpc) (see Fig. figure:large_field top-left inset). The prominent jet of WISEA J213541.80-534413.2 shows a flat spectral index at its core ( = 0.15 ) and steepens with distance as the jet propagates to =-2 . While possible, it is unlikely that the diffuse emission originates from WISEA J213541.80-534413.2.

gmw_00010039.name: E0454
gmw_00010039.overview: E0454 is a giant brightest elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.26 within the J0454 galaxy association [arXiv:1002.1080]. It acts as a strong gravitational lens and features an extended optical halo with a northern tidal tail, indicating the ongoing accretion of mass [arXiv:1002.1080].

sources.summary: The paper identifies E0454 as the giant brightest galaxy of J0454, confirms its membership spectroscopically, models it as a strong lens, and interprets its tidal feature as accretion evidence.

quotes.quote: J0454 consists of at least 60 galaxies and was identified by us in Subaru/Suprime-Cam images. It is dominated by a giant elliptical galaxy (hereafter: E0454), which strongly lenses a distant background source. We use Subaru/Suprime-Cam and CFHT/MegaPrime for photometry, XMM-Newton to study the intra-cluster gas and HST/ACS for the weak and strong lensing analysis. The imaging data (see Fig. fields for an overview) are complemented by VLT and Keck spectroscopy. Terminology and assumptions In this work we present evidence that J0454 is composed of a poor cluster and an infalling fossil group. We refer to the global system as J0454, but also to the cluster without the fossil group. The latter distinction is only made in Sect. 8 when we discuss the results. E0454 is the brightest galaxy of the system.

In deep ground-based Subaru/Suprime-Cam data we discovered a galaxy strongly lensed by a very bright elliptical galaxy (E0454). Using VLT/FORS2 spectroscopy we confirmed that E0454 is a member of a larger association of galaxies (J0454) at z=0.26 .

E0454 is embedded in an extended optical halo (see Fig. j0454_centreofmass ), forming a tidal tail in the North at a distance of about 90 kpc from the core. This feature is too small and too localised to be attributed to a current major merger event. It could be a residual of a recently disrupted small companion galaxy, or caused by tidal disturbances of galaxies orbiting very close to the halo (objects 2, 3 and 4 from Table galsample ). A similar feature is observed in the BCG of the fossil cluster ESO 3060170 sfv04 . E0454 is currently accreting mass, but the rate is too small to have destroyed a previously existing cool core.

gmw_00010040.overview: The galaxy 9403800123 is classified as a passive early-type galaxy [arXiv:2203.03648]. Structurally, it is identified as a non-edge-on oblate system [arXiv:2203.03648].

sources.summary: The paper uses 9403800123 as a representative SAMI passive galaxy, classifying it as non edge-on oblate and presenting best-fit kinematic maps plus internal mass and orbital diagnostics.

quotes.quote: The kinematic maps for the best-fit models of example galaxies 9403800123, 9011900793, 220465 and 9008500323 are presented in Fig. fig:123_bestfit , Fig. fig:793_bestfit , Fig. fig:465_bestfit and Fig. fig:323_bestfit . We selected these four galaxies as representative of the sample, with 9403800123 being a non edge-on oblate galaxy (with 255 Voronoi bins within 1 R_ e ), 9011900793 an edge-on oblate galaxy (with 87 Voronoi bins within 1 R_ e ), 220465 a triaxial galaxy (142 Voronoi bins within 1 R_ e ) and 9008500323 a prolate galaxy (with 104 Voronoi bins within 1 R_ e ).

We also show the explored parameter grids and the obtained internal mass distribution, orbit circularity, triaxiality and tangential anisotropy for the best fits of these four galaxies in Appendix sec:example_gals .

gmw_00010041.overview: 9011900793 is a passive galaxy observed as part of the SAMI survey [arXiv:2203.03648]. Structurally, it is classified as an edge-on oblate galaxy [arXiv:2203.03648].

sources.summary: The paper uses 9011900793 as a representative SAMI passive galaxy, classifying it as edge-on oblate and presenting best-fit kinematic maps plus internal mass and orbital diagnostics.

quotes.quote: The kinematic maps for the best-fit models of example galaxies 9403800123, 9011900793, 220465 and 9008500323 are presented in Fig. fig:123_bestfit , Fig. fig:793_bestfit , Fig. fig:465_bestfit and Fig. fig:323_bestfit . We selected these four galaxies as representative of the sample, with 9403800123 being a non edge-on oblate galaxy (with 255 Voronoi bins within 1 R_ e ), 9011900793 an edge-on oblate galaxy (with 87 Voronoi bins within 1 R_ e ), 220465 a triaxial galaxy (142 Voronoi bins within 1 R_ e ) and 9008500323 a prolate galaxy (with 104 Voronoi bins within 1 R_ e ).

We also show the explored parameter grids and the obtained internal mass distribution, orbit circularity, triaxiality and tangential anisotropy for the best fits of these four galaxies in Appendix sec:example_gals .

gmw_00010042.overview: 220465 is a passive early-type galaxy [arXiv:2203.03648]. Kinematic modeling of its internal structure reveals that the galaxy has a triaxial shape [arXiv:2203.03648].

sources.summary: The paper uses 220465 as a representative SAMI passive galaxy, classifying it as triaxial and presenting best-fit kinematic maps plus internal mass and orbital diagnostics.

quotes.quote: The kinematic maps for the best-fit models of example galaxies 9403800123, 9011900793, 220465 and 9008500323 are presented in Fig. fig:123_bestfit , Fig. fig:793_bestfit , Fig. fig:465_bestfit and Fig. fig:323_bestfit . We selected these four galaxies as representative of the sample, with 9403800123 being a non edge-on oblate galaxy (with 255 Voronoi bins within 1 R_ e ), 9011900793 an edge-on oblate galaxy (with 87 Voronoi bins within 1 R_ e ), 220465 a triaxial galaxy (142 Voronoi bins within 1 R_ e ) and 9008500323 a prolate galaxy (with 104 Voronoi bins within 1 R_ e ).

We also show the explored parameter grids and the obtained internal mass distribution, orbit circularity, triaxiality and tangential anisotropy for the best fits of these four galaxies in Appendix sec:example_gals .

gmw_00010043.overview: 9008500323 is a passive early-type galaxy observed as part of the SAMI survey [arXiv:2203.03648]. Structurally, it is classified as a prolate galaxy with a distinct internal mass distribution and orbit circularity [arXiv:2203.03648].

sources.summary: The paper uses 9008500323 as a representative SAMI passive galaxy, classifying it as prolate and presenting best-fit kinematic maps plus internal mass and orbital diagnostics.

quotes.quote: The kinematic maps for the best-fit models of example galaxies 9403800123, 9011900793, 220465 and 9008500323 are presented in Fig. fig:123_bestfit , Fig. fig:793_bestfit , Fig. fig:465_bestfit and Fig. fig:323_bestfit . We selected these four galaxies as representative of the sample, with 9403800123 being a non edge-on oblate galaxy (with 255 Voronoi bins within 1 R_ e ), 9011900793 an edge-on oblate galaxy (with 87 Voronoi bins within 1 R_ e ), 220465 a triaxial galaxy (142 Voronoi bins within 1 R_ e ) and 9008500323 a prolate galaxy (with 104 Voronoi bins within 1 R_ e ).

We also show the explored parameter grids and the obtained internal mass distribution, orbit circularity, triaxiality and tangential anisotropy for the best fits of these four galaxies in Appendix sec:example_gals .

gmw_00010044.name: SDSS J0938
gmw_00010044.overview: SDSS J0938 is classified as a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus that contains star-forming regions [arXiv:2307.03182]. Its coronal emission lines and overall spectral shape have remained unchanged over a timescale of at least two decades [arXiv:2307.03182].

sources.summary: The paper confirms SDSS J0938 as a Seyfert 2 AGN with star-forming regions, showing unchanged coronal lines and spectral shape over roughly two decades.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0938 SDSS J0938 was reclassified by as a Seyfert 2 AGN with star-forming regions rather than being related to a transient TDE. This reclassification was based on the lack of variability in the coronal emission lines between the original 2006 SDSS spectrum and their 2011 MMT follow-up spectrum. Our 2021 NTT and 2022 DESI spectra show no detectable changes in any of the coronal lines (beyond the expected width changes as a result of instrumental resolution) or in the overall spectral shape. Based on these findings, we concur with this AGN classification. However, the processes involved in generating such strong coronal lines relative to the rest of the AGN population over timescales of at least two decades are still unclear.

gmw_00010045.name: SDSS J1055
gmw_00010045.overview: SDSS J1055 is classified as a Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2307.03182]. This classification is supported by its spectral invariance over multiple decades and long-term photometric undulations [arXiv:2307.03182].

sources.summary: The paper supports the Seyfert 1 AGN classification of SDSS J1055, citing spectral invariance and photometric fading/undulations consistent with AGN behavior.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1055 In a similar manner to SDSS J0938, SDSS J1055 was reclassified by as a Seyfert 1 AGN based on its spectral invariance between 2002 and 2011. Our 2021 Kast spectrum confirms this lack of evolution, supporting the AGN reclassification.

SDSS J1055 In the study conducted by , SDSS J1055 was observed to have faded between the SDSS photometric and spectroscopic observations. This behaviour is consistent with both its AGN classification and its more recent photometric evolution, which, like SDSS J0938, has consisted of long-term undulations.

gmw_00010046.name: SDSS J1241
gmw_00010046.overview: SDSS J1241 is an emission line galaxy [arXiv:2307.03182]. Although initially classified as non-variable, follow-up observations revealed spectral variability characterized by faded coronal lines, alongside a late-time optical behavior that is largely constant [arXiv:2307.03182].

sources.summary: The paper revises SDSS J1241 from non-variable to variable, finding faded coronal lines and mostly constant late-time optical behavior after accounting for imaging artifacts.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1241 This object was originally identified by as non-variable. In their analysis they lacked the red component of the spectrum owing to observational issues, with the blue component showing that the 3759 \ and 3896 \ emission lines remained prominent and that there were no significant changes to the continuum or overall spectral shape within the blue region of the spectrum. Our follow-up spectrum of SDSS J1241 covers the full range of the original SDSS observation and reveals that the object has in fact displayed spectral variability consistent with the other variable candidates. Specifically, the coronal lines can now be seen to have faded, with none detected in our 2021 Kast spectrum.

In contrast, ZTF gri observations of this object, whilst not covering the time period of the decline observed by ATLAS, are stable across all three bands for the full duration of available observations. We note again here that the ATLAS photometry used in this work is based on observed images rather than difference imaging for direct comparison with other surveys. When difference imaging is used, this decline is not observed in the ATLAS data. As such, we do not view this decline as physical and treat the late-time optical behaviour of SDSS J1241 as largely constant.

gmw_00010047.name: SDSS J092713.82+294335.5
gmw_00010047.overview: SDSS J092713.82+294335.5 is a quasar companion to the object 0927 [arXiv:1001.1086]. Located at a redshift of z=0.705, it exhibits blue optical colors, bright ultraviolet emission, and hosts a supermassive black hole with an estimated mass of 1.4 billion solar masses [arXiv:1001.1086].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J092713.82+294335.5 as a newly discovered quasar companion to 0927, with quasar-like colors, UV emission, z=0.705, and a black-hole mass estimate.

quotes.quote: The blue object selected as a quasar candidate 17.5'' far from 0927 ( SDSS J092713.82+294335.5 ; RA(J2000): 09^ h 27^ m 13^ s .8 , Dec(J2000): +29^ d 43^ m 35^ s ; u = 19.38 , g = 19.18 , = 19.14 , i = 19.01 , z = 19.13 ) appears in the USNO-A2 catalogue monet98 . The GALEX All-Sky Survey provides estimates of its UV magnitudes ( FUV = 19.94 0.19 , NUV = 19.22 0.11 ). The blue optical colours and the bright UV emission are consistent with the typical Spectral Energy Distribution (SED) of a quasar see Figure fig_sed and drd09 . The spectrum shows a broad emission line at 4772 and another possible emission line at 7395 (see Figure fig_spectra and table tab_lines ). We identify the former with the and the latter with , yielding z=0.705 .
Referring to standard techniques for single-epoch spectra of quasar e.g., paperI , we infer the BH mass of the newly discovered quasar from the continuum luminosity and the line width: 1.4 10^ 8 .

gmw_00010048.name: LAE B1
gmw_00010048.overview: LAE B1 is an intrinsically faint Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2.7483 [arXiv:1903.09645]. It is associated with a multi-component absorption system known as Complex B, which is probed by a background quasar sightline at a projected distance of 187 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1903.09645].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LAE B1 as a faint z=2.7483 Ly-alpha emitter 187 kpc from the QSO sightline, associated in redshift with multi-component Complex B absorption.

quotes.quote: There are three foreground LAEs at d<300 kpc from the QSO sightline. Two of these objects, LAE A1 at peak redshift z_ peak = 2.7659 and LAE A2 at z_ peak = 2.7713 , are closely situated in redshift space with their Ly emission peaks separated by merely v 400 . In addition, both LAEs are situated on opposite sides of the QSO at a projected separation of 248 kpc, with the QSO sightline probing the LAE A1 at d=87 kpc and LAE A2 at d=161 kpc (top-left panel of Figure 3). A third emitter, LAE B1, occurs at z_ peak =2.7483 , and is probed by the background QSO at d=187 kpc (top-left panel of Figure 4).

The observed Ly line fluxes of the three LAEs are F_ Ly = (2.7 0.4) 10^ 18 , (3.3 0.6) 10^ 18 , and (3.5 0.5) 10^ 18 erg s^ 1 cm^ 2 for LAEs A1, A2, and B1 (R08). These observed line fluxes correspond to Ly luminosities of L_ Ly =(1.7-2.2) 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 . The inferred Ly luminosities of the three LAEs are a factor of 20-30 lower than the characteristic luminosity of the LAE luminosity function at z 3 (e.g., Cassata \ 2011; Konno \ 2016; Drake \ 2017), which indicate that these LAEs are intrinsically faint compared to the general LAE population at the same epoch. This conclusion would stand even if the observations were subject to significant slit losses of 50 (e.g., R08; Cassata \ 2011).

Complex B is found at the redshift of LAE B1. In contrast to the more kinematically compact absorption profile of Complex A, the Ly profile of Complex B consists of multiple components spread over nearly 900 \ in line-of-sight velocity (see the bottom panels of Figure 4). The integrated rest-frame equivalent width of the Ly absorption is W_r(1215)=1.99 0.03 .

gmw_00010049.name: SDSS J1319 + 5202
gmw_00010049.overview: SDSS J1319 + 5202 is a highly luminous, relatively young quasar [arXiv:1810.03391]. Observations of its sightline reveal robust, low effective optical depths that indicate high levels of ionization, as well as an off-centered flux spike at z ~ 3.45 [arXiv:1405.7405].

sources.summary: The paper reports robust low effective optical depths toward SDSS J1319 + 5202 and uses decontaminated orbital-night data to identify an off-centered z=3.45 flux spike.

quotes.quote: The majority (60 ) of the _ eff,HeII measurements at z>3.34 are from the two highest-redshift sightlines in our sample (SDSS J1711 + 6052 and SDSS J1319 + 5202), which also contribute the four lowest _ eff,HeII 3 values (Table tab:he2tau ). These low effective optical depths indicate surprisingly high levels of ionization, in contrast to current models. Improved background subtraction and accounting for possible remaining systematics indicates that these low _ eff,HeII values are robust.

For SDSS J1319 + 5202 we include data at 3.44<z<3.51 that has been decontaminated from geocoronal O 1 ] emission using orbital night time data, revealing a flux spike at z 3.45 (Zone C). During orbital night of the target visits, the stronger geocoronal O 1 1302 \ emission almost vanished (just a few counts remaining), so that O 1 ] 1356 \ is safely excluded. Additional support for the presence of this spike comes from its off-centered position with respect to the geocoronal line. The other zones are the same as for SDSS J1711 + 6052 to facilitate the comparison ( 3.110<z<3.246 and 3.340<z<3.440 ).

sources.summary: The paper infers that SDSS J1319 + 5202 is relatively young, with a tightly constrained quasar lifetime enabled by high luminosity and a small proximity-zone redshift uncertainty.

quotes.quote: The red histogram in Fig. fig:posterior_tQ illustrates the marginalized lifetime posterior probability distribution for the quasar SDSS J1319 + 5202. We infer the lifetime of log _ 10 t_ Q / Myr = -0.20^ +0.36 _ 0.45 , indicating that this quasar is relatively young. It is also clear from the shape of the posterior that for this case of highly constraining data we are totally insensitive to our choice of priors. The reason we are able to constrain t_ Q so tightly for SDSS J1319 + 5202 is that it is the second most luminous object in our sample, which also has the smallest redshift uncertainty (R_ pz ) = 0.98 Mpc as inferred from its H emission line redshift (see Table tab:table1 ). Proximity zone sizes R_ pz increase with luminosity Khrykin2016 , implying that the relative error on R_ pz should be smallest for the brightest sources. This appears to be reflected in SDSS J1319 + 5202 which has the third largest proximity zone size of R_ pz = 3.62 0.98 Mpc.

gmw_00010050.name: SDSS J1137 + 6237
gmw_00010050.overview: SDSS J1137 + 6237 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:1810.03391]. It is characterized by a relatively low luminosity and possesses the second-largest proximity zone in its studied sample [arXiv:1810.03391].

sources.summary: The paper finds SDSS J1137 + 6237 has the second-largest proximity zone, but saturation effects prevent a clear lifetime measurement, leaving only a lower limit.

quotes.quote: The black histogram in Fig. fig:posterior_tQ shows the marginalized posterior probability distribution for the lifetime of quasar SDSS J1137 + 6237. This quasar has the second biggest proximity zone ( R_ pz = 4.92 1.68 Mpc) in our sample. Taking into account its relatively low luminosity, it is possible that it has a long lifetime. However, while this quasar has only 34 relative error on the proximity zone size (comparable to 27 error for SDSS J1319 + 5202), which, in principle, should provide good constraint on quasar lifetime, the resulting posterior distribution looks different than in case of SDSS J1319 + 5202 and HE2QS J1630 + 0435.

Given the shape of its posterior, we can only quote a 95 lower limit on its lifetime. To this end we calculate the 5th percentile of the posterior distribution for quasar SDSS J1137 + 6237, which yields log _ 10 t_ Q / Myr > -0.90 .

gmw_00010051.name: z 6.2 galaxy
gmw_00010051.overview: The z 6.2 galaxy is a high-redshift dwarf galaxy that is gravitationally lensed into four images by the foreground cluster MACS J0329.6-0211 [arXiv:1111.5006]. It exhibits a low dust content, subsolar metallicity, and a demagnified star formation rate of 3.2 solar masses per year [arXiv:1111.5006].

sources.summary: The paper presents the z 6.2 galaxy as a quadruply lensed, high-redshift dwarf with strong magnification, a compact source-plane size, modest stellar mass, low dust, and vigorous star formation.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a z_ phot =6.18^ +0.05 _ 0.07 ( 95 confidence level) dwarf galaxy, lensed into four images by the galaxy cluster MACS J0329.6-0211 ( z_ l =0.45 ). The galaxy is observed as a high-redshift dropout in HST /ACS/WFC3 CLASH and Spitzer /IRAC imaging. Its redshift is securely determined due to a clear detection of the Lyman-break in the 18-band photometry, making this galaxy one of the highest-redshift multiply-lensed objects known to date with an observed magnitude of F125W =24.00 0.04 AB mag for its most magnified image.

The mass model independently supports the high photometric redshift and reveals magnifications of 11.6^ +8.9 _ 4.1 , 17.6^ +6.2 _ 3.9 , 3.9^ +3.0 _ 1.7 , and 3.7^ +1.3 _ 0.2 , respectively, for the four images of the high-redshift galaxy. By delensing the most magnified image we construct an image of the source with a physical resolution of 200 pc when the universe was 0.9 Gyr old, where the z 6.2 galaxy occupies a source-plane area of approximately 2.2 kpc ^ 2 . Modeling the observed spectral energy distribution (SED) using population synthesis models, we find a demagnified stellar mass of 10^ 9 M _ , subsolar metallicity ( Z/Z_ 0.5 ), low dust content ( A_ V 0.1 mag), a demagnified SFR of 3.2 M _ yr ^ 1 , and a specific SFR of 3.4 Gyr ^ 1 , all consistent with the properties of local dwarf galaxies.

gmw_00010052.name: CIG 692
gmw_00010052.overview: CIG 692 is a Seyfert galaxy and active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:0803.0335]. It exhibits warm infrared colors [arXiv:0803.0335], lies above the far-infrared-radio correlation [arXiv:0803.0335], and hosts a symmetric triple radio source spanning approximately 3 kiloparsecs [arXiv:0803.0335].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CIG 692 as a Seyfert galaxy above the FIR-radio correlation, notes its compact triple radio source, and classifies it as an AGN candidate with warm IRAS colors.

quotes.quote: We find 3 Seyfert galaxies located by more than a factor of 3 above the FIR-radio correlation: CIG 72 , 692 and 877.
CIG 692 has been observed with the VLA in its A configuration at 8.3 GHz by Schmitt2001b finding a symmetric triple source with a total extent of 3 kpc.

Four of the radio-excess candidate galaxies can be also classified with the IRAS colour method and all of them (CIG 41, CIG 248, CIG 692 and CIG 877) are found to be AGN candidates. These galaxies have very warm IRAS colours with S_ 25 m /S_ 60 m being higher than 0.45 in all cases.

gmw_00010053.name: CIG 877
gmw_00010053.overview: CIG 877 is an elliptical galaxy classified as an active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:0803.0335]. It is a Seyfert galaxy that exhibits a radio excess, positioning it significantly above the far-infrared-radio correlation [arXiv:0803.0335].

sources.summary: The paper treats CIG 877 as a Seyfert/AGN-candidate elliptical with radio excess; possible stellar contamination of its IRAS emission would make the radio excess stronger.

quotes.quote: We find 3 Seyfert galaxies located by more than a factor of 3 above the FIR-radio correlation: CIG 72 , 692 and 877.

CIG 877 is an elliptical galaxy studied by Marcum2004 . They suggest that the IRAS emission assigned to this galaxy might be produced by two nearby stars. If this is true the radio excess would be even higher.

Four of the radio-excess candidate galaxies can be also classified with the IRAS colour method and all of them (CIG 41, CIG 248, CIG 692 and CIG 877) are found to be AGN candidates. These galaxies have very warm IRAS colours with S_ 25 m /S_ 60 m being higher than 0.45 in all cases.

gmw_00010054.name: UDG-C02
gmw_00010054.overview: UDG-C02 is an ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the massive galaxy cluster Abell 370 [arXiv:2004.01340]. It is characterized by a blue color that indicates a stellar population younger than other cluster ultra-diffuse galaxies, suggesting it is an extended dwarf galaxy containing recently formed stars [arXiv:2004.01340].

sources.summary: The paper highlights UDG-C02 as the bluest Abell 370 UDG, with color evidence for young stars and an interpretation as an extended dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: First, UDG-C02 (cyan star) is the bluest UDG of our sample ( F814W-F105W=-0.07 0.01 ). The blue color indicates that this UDG is mainly composed of young stars. This is in strong contrast to the fact that most of cluster UDGs are located in the red sequence. Thus, this provides a rare sample of a blue UDG in a massive galaxy cluster. It is noted that a few blue and irregular UDGs were discovered in various environments Lee17, rom17b, tru17 , but they are much fainter than UDG-C02 with M_ ' =-16.8 .
UDG-C02 seems to have an age younger than the other UDGs by a few Gyr. This implies that UDG-C02 includes recently formed stars.
Interestingly, UDG-C02 in Abell 370 can be an extended dwarf galaxy due to its remarkably blue color.

gmw_00010055.name: UDG-C22
gmw_00010055.overview: UDG-C22 is an ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Abell 370 cluster [arXiv:2004.01340]. It is a massive, quenched system composed of old stars that lies on the cluster's red sequence [arXiv:2004.01340].

sources.summary: The paper describes UDG-C22 as Abell 370's largest UDG, red and old, unusually massive, and a possible primordial failed L* galaxy.

quotes.quote: Second, UDG-C22 (yellow star) is the largest UDG of our UDG sample with =6.16 0.36 and =25.18 0.08 . This UDG is significantly larger than the other UDGs in Abell 370 which have =1.5-3.0 kpc ( R_ eff, =2.0 kpc). This UDG is located right at the red sequence. UDG-C22 is one of the largest UDGs among the known UDGs including Coma DF17 ( R_ eff =4.4 kpc), Coma DF44 ( R_ eff =4.6 kpc), and a few Virgo UDGs ( R_ eff =2.9-9.7 kpc) mih15 .
In contrast, UDG-C22 shows red colors close to the mean values of the UDGs. This means that UDG-C22 is made of old stars, which is similar to most UDGs in Abell 370.
However, UDG-C22 in Abell 370 is so large ( R_ eff, =6.16 kpc ) and massive ( M_ 200 >10^ 11 M_ ) that it can be considered as an example of a primodal failed L^ * galaxy.

gmw_00010056.overview: The radio source 0547+234 is dominated by a compact eastern radio core [arXiv:1012.2804]. This core is accompanied by a fainter western emission component and exhibits detectable flux-density variations over time [arXiv:1012.2804].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0547+234 as a compact-core ICRF2 radio source, modeling its eastern radio core, fainter western emission, flux-density changes, and apparent southward core motion.

quotes.quote: The Structure and Evolution of 0547+234 The source 0547+234 is dominated by a compact component toward the east. At 43 GHz, the source structure was fit with an elliptical Gaussian component for component 0 (clearly the radio core), and with a point component for component 1 (it is too weak for an angular size to be determined). At 23 GHz the bright component radio core and the fainter component 1 were each fit with point source.

Observations of the source at 23 GHz in Aug 2005 show a similar structure with a dominant core and a faint component about 1 mas to the east kq-survey2 . The parameters for the radio core for the five VLBA sessions at 43 GHz and 23 GHz are shown in Table tab:0547 , with the same format as that for Table tab:0556 . The component 0 flux density reaches a minimum for the 08-Jun-2009 session at both 43 and 23 GHz, when components 1 and 2 are near their maximum, about 15 of the flux density of the radio core, and the component spectral index is about -0.6.

gmw_00010057.overview: The radio source 0554+242 is resolved into two major components [arXiv:1012.2804]. Component 0 is identified as the likely compact, flatter-spectrum radio core, showing little variability over a one-year period [arXiv:1012.2804].

sources.summary: The paper resolves 0554+242 into two radio components, identifying component 0 as the likely compact, flatter-spectrum core and finding little variability but only marginal component separation.

quotes.quote: The Structure and Evolution of 0554+242 The contour plots of 0554+242 in Fig. fig:f0554_images show two major components. The emission was fit with a two-component elliptical Gaussian model for all sessions and at 43 and 23 GHz, and the parameters are shown in Table tab:0554 . Component 0 is only slightly resolved since the total to peak flux density is about 1.1, corresponding to an angular size <0.2 mas. There is no significant variability over the year experiment.

The spectral index of the two components are significantly different. For component 0 , =-0.7 ; for component 1 , =-1.2 . It is, thus, extremely likely that the radio core of 0554+242 is component 0 because it is more compact and has a flatter spectrum than component 1 .

gmw_00010058.overview: The radio source 0556+238 is a compact, nearly point-like radio core [arXiv:1012.2804]. It exhibits a faint emission extension to the southwest and has shown an increase in flux density over a one-year period [arXiv:1012.2804].

sources.summary: The paper treats 0556+238 as the compact ICRF2 defining source used for phase referencing, finding a nearly point-like core, slight southwest extension, and increased flux over the year.

quotes.quote: The Structure and Evolution of 0556+238 The source 0556+238 is nearly point-like at all frequencies and sessions, which is the main reason that it was used as the phase reference calibrator. The last session at 43 GHz shows a faint extension of the emission to the south-west. The VLBA images were fit to a single elliptical Gaussian component, labeled 0 in Fig. fig:f0556_images , and its parameters are listed in Table tab:0556 .

Over the experimental period of about one year, the flux density of the radio core increased 46 at 43 GHz and 18 at 23 GHz, with the spectral index, (where flux density ^ ), changing from 1.2 to 0.8 .

gmw_00010059.overview: The radio source 0601+245 is composed of three major components, including two close components in the east and an extended component to the west [arXiv:1012.2804]. The easternmost compact component has the flattest spectral index and is identified as the likely radio core [arXiv:1012.2804].

sources.summary: The paper models 0601+245 as a complex three-component radio source, identifying the eastern compact, flatter-spectrum component as the likely radio core and measuring westward motion of component 2.

quotes.quote: The Structure and Evolution of 0601+245 The 43 GHz images of 0601+245 suggest that the source is composed of three major components. There are two close components in the eastern part of the source and an extended component to the west (see Fig. fig:f0601_images ). The location of a radio core, perhaps corresponding to one of these components, cannot be easily deduced from this display without deeper analysis.

Component 0 has the flattest spectral index with with =-0.7 , component 1 has a steep index of =-1.7 , and the western, extended component 2 has =-0.8 . Component 0 shows a similar proper motion as the radio cores in 0547+234 and 0554+242 with _x=-0.029 0.015 mas/yr, _y=-0.064 0.025 mas/yr. Again, this anomalous proper motion is discussed in the next sections. This component is likely the radio core since it also has the flattest spectral index, is compact, and is at one end of the radio emission.
Component 2 is moving with _x=-0.183 0.025 mas/yr, _y=-0.064 0.033 mas/yr, or =0.193 0.029 mas/yr in position angle 109^ .

gmw_00010060.name: ID 310
aliases_external: Rafelski UVUDF object 10185 / AMUSED DR2 source 399
gmw_00010060.overview: ID 310 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy located at a redshift of 5.13 [arXiv:2002.00214]. Although it is not visible in MUSE white light images, it is clearly detected in deep Hubble Space Telescope F775W imaging and exhibits a broad, asymmetric emission line [arXiv:2002.00214].

sources.summary: The paper uses ID 310 as a blind-detection example: a z=5.13 emitter visible in HST but not MUSE white light, with a broad asymmetric emission line.

quotes.quote: The first case ( \ ID 310, first row of Fig. fig:udf2 ) shows the detection of a \ emitter. The source cannot be seen in the MUSE white light image but is clearly visible in the HST deep F775W image. The corresponding \ detection peaked at 7459 .

However, the smoothed narrow band image displays a clear signal, in line with \ detection. The corresponding bright emission line seen in the source spectrum is broad and asymmetric with a tail on the red side of the peak, a characteristic of \ emission line. The redshift of the source is 5.13.

gmw_00010061.name: ID 253
gmw_00010061.overview: ID 253 is a high-redshift emission-line galaxy located at a redshift of z=4.7 [arXiv:2002.00214]. It is positioned in close proximity to the foreground spiral galaxy ID 23794, which lies at a redshift of z=0.62 [arXiv:2002.00214].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID 253 as a z=4.7 emission-line source near foreground spiral ID 23794, showing its line is not an H II region in the foreground galaxy.

quotes.quote: The third source ( \ ID 253) is a good example of the power of \ blind detection in the vicinity of a bright continuum object. \ has detected an emission line in the vicinity of the nearby (z=0.62) bright (AB 22) spiral galaxy ID 23794 Rafelski2016 . The detection and narrow-band images display a coherent structure that spatially matches a small source in the F775W image.

Without the \ detection, one would have assumed that this small source in the HST image is one of the H _2 region, which can be seen along the spiral arm of the galaxy. The fact that the multi-band HST segmentation map did not identify the source as a different object from the main galaxy would have strengthened this (false) assumption. However, the spectrum confirmed that the \ line does not belong to the main galaxy, but is a \ emitter at z=4.7 with a typical asymmetric line profile.

gmw_00010062.name: J1225+4011
gmw_00010062.overview: J1225+4011 is a giant radio galaxy spanning a projected size of 1.35 Mpc [arXiv:2307.08553]. It is a brightest cluster galaxy that exhibits a tidal tail and displays three distinct episodes of radio jet activity [arXiv:2307.08553].

sources.summary: The paper discovers J1225+4011 as a 1.35 Mpc giant triple-double radio galaxy with three jet episodes, a BCG host, tidal tail, and measured redshift.

quotes.quote: The excellent sensitivity and optimum resolution of LoTSS DR2 at 144 MHz has enabled us to discover a giant radio galaxy (J1225+4011) with three distinct episodes of jet activity, making it a member of a class of objects called triple-double radio galaxies (TDRGs). The source extends overall up to 1.35 Mpc in projected size, with the second episode extending to 572 kpc, and the inner episode to 118 kpc. J1225+4011 is only the fourth radio source showing a triple-double radio structure.

While inspecting the giant radio galaxy (GRG) data from LoTSS DR2 Shimwell2022,Oei2022 , we found that the GRG associated with the host galaxy SDSS J122537.87+401122 appears to show three episodes of activity in terms of three distinct pairs of radio lobes (see Fig. \ fig:image1 ). Previously, this galaxy has been identified as a GRG by Oei2022 as part of a large sample ( 2000) of GRSs identified from LoTSS DR2. Using SDSS DR6, Szabo2011 suggest that the galaxy SDSS J122537.87+401122 ( r_ band (mag) = 18.95) is the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG). Several galaxies are seen in its vicinity, with SDSS J122537.87+401122 also exhibiting a tidal tail which is clearly seen in the optical I band image from PanSTARRS (Fig. \ fig:image1 ).

gmw_00010063.name: TDRG J0929+4146
gmw_00010063.overview: TDRG J0929+4146 is a triple-double radio galaxy [arXiv:2307.08553]. It has an updated largest angular size of 8.1 arcminutes, which corresponds to a projected linear size of 2.5 Mpc [arXiv:2307.08553].

sources.summary: The paper updates TDRG J0929+4146 using LoTSS DR2, measuring an 8.1 arcmin angular size and 2.5 Mpc projected size, the largest reported for a TDRG.

quotes.quote: We also present the LoTSS 144 MHz map of the TDRG J0929+4146 and report its updated size.

TDRG J0929+4146 TDRG J0929+4146 was first reported by Brocksopp2007 and here we present its updated largest angular size to be 8.1 measured using the higher quality low-frequency map from LoTSS DR2 (see Fig. \ fig:J0929 ), which was able to detect the more extended diffuse emission from the outermost lobes. This results in its projected linear size of 2.5 Mpc, which is the largest size for any TDRG reported so far. Other properties for this source are listed in Table tab:compare .

The middle lobes appear edge brightened in J0929+4146 Brocksopp2007 and J1409-0302 Hota2011 , but not in the case of J1216+0709 Singh2016 with one component being marginally resolved and the other extended along the direction of flow of relativistic plasma. Similarly, the inner doubles of J0929+4146 Brocksopp2007 and J1216+0709 Singh2016 do not appear edge-brightened, while the ones in J1409-0302 Hota2011 appear as weak compact components.

gmw_00010064.name: J0948+0958
gmw_00010064.overview: J0948+0958 is a nearby dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1408.4451]. It is identified as an active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:1408.4451].

sources.summary: The paper flags J0948+0958 as a nearby dwarf-galaxy AGN candidate, discussing foreground-star contamination, custom aperture photometry, and adopted magnitudes for its host-galaxy calculations.

quotes.quote: J0948+0958 : This galaxy has two superposed foreground stars and it lies 1' from a very bright star.
