quotes.quote: Fits to the radio galaxies 3C324 and 3C356 The first thing that is striking about the two radio galaxies we studied here (3C324 and 3C356) is that their SEDs are distinctly different from those of the quasars. They show a much weaker near- to mid IR continuum relative to the far IR, a completely different slope in the 1 - 10 m range and evidence of shallow absorption features at 10 m. All of these differences are understood in terms of the optically thick torus model and are strongly supportive of the idea of unification. For the radio galaxies we add to the pure AGN torus or the AGN+SB models contributions from black bodies at temperature of 4000 - 5000 K, which model the emission from old stars in the host galaxies in these objects.

The SED of both galaxies are fit by pure AGN models with parameters as given in Table para.tab . In the combined models the starburst component is 8 for 3C324 and 19 for 3C356 of the intrinsic AGN luminosity (Table paragnsb.tab ).

sources.summary: The paper discusses two possible AGN locations in 3C 356 and uses IRAC, IRS, MIPS, and polarization evidence to favor object a as the radio-galaxy host.

quotes.quote: 3C 356 ( z=1.079 ): There are two potential identifications for the location of the radio-loud AGN. Based on the detection of scattered broad line regions with a polarization angle perpendicular to the ``dumbell'' structure of the galaxy, sim02 argued that the northwestern object a is the most likely location, but object b , offset by 4 5 (36 kpc), may also contain an AGN. Both targets were spectroscopically confirmed to be at z=1.079 . cim97 showed that object a has a high restframe UV polarization, clearly indicating the presence of a hidden AGN. We detected both objects in all four IRAC bands, though only object a has strong mid-IR dust emission seen with IRS and MIPS.

sources.summary: 3C 356 is associated with two bright elliptical galaxies and smaller intervening sources; optical emission and connecting sources indicate a merging system.

quotes.quote: 3C 356 (z=1.079) The radio source 3C 356 appears in our IR image associated with two main bright elliptical galaxies lying on a southeast-northwest line, separated by 5 . The northern source appears brighter and more compact than the southern source. There are also 5-6 smaller distinct sources between the two main sources. In contrast, our optical image shows emission in the form of a bi-conical structure from the northern of the two main sources. There is also faint signal in the optical image associated with two of the more extended surrounding objects, as well as the compact object located between and equidistant from the two main sources.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 356 as a z=1.08 radio galaxy in a poor cluster where two disturbed AGN-hosting components and merging galaxy groups likely triggered the radio jets and shock-heated X-ray gas.

quotes.quote: We present deep near-infrared images, taken with Subaru Telescope, of the region around the z=1.08 radio source 3C 356 which show it to be associated with a poor cluster of galaxies. We discuss evidence that this cluster comprises two subclusters traced by the two galaxies previously proposed as identifications for 3C 356, which both seem to harbour AGN, and which have the disturbed morphologies expected if they underwent an interpenetrating collision at the time the radio jets were triggered. We explain the high luminosity and temperature of the diffuse X-ray emission from this system as the result of shock-heating of intracluster gas by the merger of two galaxy groups.

We have demonstrated with new deep near-infrared imaging the absence of a rich cluster around the z=1.08 radio galaxy 3C 356, apparently at odds with the observed X-ray emission. We have proposed that the X-rays arise from shocked gas at the interface of two merging galaxy groups and that the two components of 3C 356 suffered a strong interaction during this merger, stripping material from one or both galaxies and funnelling it into the nucleus of `a', which we believe is the source of the radio jets (although we believe that `b' is also an AGN).

gmw_00010189.name: G23.6644 0.0372
gmw_00010189.overview: G23.6644 0.0372 is a compact extragalactic radio source with a peaked radio spectrum [arXiv:2112.12526]. It is classified as a high-frequency peaked-spectrum source and is likely an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2112.12526].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G23.6644 0.0372 as the only VLBI-detected source, interpreting it as a compact extragalactic peaked-spectrum radio source with likely AGN-like spectrum and decadal variability.

quotes.quote: The object is intrinsically compact and relatively bright. Its radio spectrum is optically thick at 5 GHz and flat between 8.4 and 11.0 GHz. Based on the spectrum of the source and its relatively high flux densities observed at 5 GHz for more than three decades, we cannot identify as a radio pulsar e.g. Zhao2020 , a magnetar e.g. Kaspi2017 , or a stellar-mass black hole in an X-ray binary system e.g. Pietka2015 . Moreover, there are no X-ray, optical and infrared counterparts found by Becker2010 and by us in the latest catalogues of VizieR http://vizier.u-strasbg.fr/index.gml Ochsenbein2000 and the online multi-band images provided by the CORNISH survey Hoare2012 .

is most likely an extragalactic source with a peaked radio spectrum. The rising radio spectrum of in Fig fig:g23_alpha is frequently seen in AGN .f. a review by ODea2021 . As a non-thermal radio source, is expected to have an optically thin radio spectrum at frequencies > 11 GHz, and thus can be classified as a high-frequency peaked-spectrum source e.g. Orienti2006, ODea2021 . According to the correlation, would have a linear size of 10 pc.

gmw_00010190.name: G22.9116 0.2878
gmw_00010190.overview: G22.9116 0.2878 is a radio source classified as a likely active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2112.12526]. It exhibits a steep radio spectrum and a 4.5-arcsecond southern extension interpreted as collimated jet activity [arXiv:2112.12526].

sources.summary: The paper classifies G22.9116 0.2878 among likely AGN jets, noting its steep spectrum, southern arcsecond extension, and interpretation as collimated jet activity.

quotes.quote: Together with the simultaneous spectral indices between 1 and 2 GHz provided by Bihr2016 , we notice that two sources (G22.9116 0.2878/No. 2 in Fig. fig:g22 and G37.7596 0.1101/No. 16 in Fig. fig:g37 ) have steep spectra with < -0.5 and one source (G26.2818 + 0.2312/No. 14) has a flat spectrum with | | < 0.5 . According to the observed structures and radio spectra, three sources are very likely AGN jets. In the images provided by the CORNISH at 5 GHz Hoare2012, Purcell2013 and the VLASS at 3 GHz Lacy2020 , the other steep-spectrum source G22.9116 0.2878 shows a 4.5-arcsec extension toward south.

This extension is also displayed in Fig. fig:g22 . The faint extensions are not clearly seen in the 5-GHz CORNISH images probably because of their steep spectra. Together with their linear structure, we explains these faint arcsec-scale extensions as the collimated jet activity.

gmw_00010191.name: G37.7596 0.1001
gmw_00010191.overview: G37.7596 0.1001 is a radio source interpreted as a likely active galactic nucleus jet [arXiv:2112.12526]. It exhibits a steep radio spectrum and an elongated morphology at 1.4 GHz [arXiv:2112.12526].

sources.summary: The paper interprets G37.7596 0.1001 as a likely AGN jet source, citing its steep spectrum, elongated 1.4 GHz morphology, and persistent detection despite crowded-field imaging issues.

quotes.quote: According to the observed structures and radio spectra, three sources are very likely AGN jets. The source has been discussed in detail in Section sec:discussion_g23 . Furthermore, the steep-spectrum source G37.7596 0.1001 shows an elongated morphology at 1.4 GHz with a de-convolved length of 12.5 arcsec in the survey MAGPIS Helfand2006 . The structure is also shown in Fig. fig:g37 .

The two sources G37.7347 0.1126 and G37.7596 0.1001 are located in a very crowded field (cf. the 1.4-GHz VLA image, Fig. fig:g37 ). The online 5-GHz VLA image of the two sources in the first epoch 1990 + has a poor quality and shows some strong image artefacts most likely resulting from a poor de-convolution of the complex field. Furthermore, G37.7596 0.1001 was detected with a peak brightness of 11.8 1.8 mJy beam ^ 1 at 1.4 GHz in 1983 Becker1990 .

gmw_00010192.name: JADES-NS-20086025
gmw_00010192.overview: JADES-NS-20086025 is a high-redshift galaxy characterized by a highly complex morphology [arXiv:2308.09742]. The system exhibits apparent extended diffuse line emission, which complicates morphological modeling and results in larger uncertainties for its inferred dynamical parameters [arXiv:2308.09742].

sources.summary: The paper describes JADES-NS-20086025 as a high-redshift object with complex diffuse line emission, difficult morphology fitting, weakened dynamical priors, and convergent but uncertain dynamical parameters.

quotes.quote: The sixth object (JADES-NS-20086025) has a highly complex morphology with apparent extended diffuse (line) emission. Due to the relatively low SNR of this emission and the crowded region around the target, the morphological fit to the F410M image with lenstronomy for this object does not converge. To model the dynamics we therefore significantly weaken the morphological priors for this object: we set a uniform prior on the S ersic index, and set only weak constraints on the effective radius (Gaussian prior of r_ e =0.3 0.3 ), axis ratio (Gaussian prior of q=0.3 0.2 ) and position angle ( PA=90 15 ). We do find convergence for the dynamical model, although several of the inferred parameters have larger uncertainties than the other objects in our sample.

gmw_00010193.name: SDSSJ1433+6007
gmw_00010193.overview: SDSSJ1433+6007 is a quadruply lensed quasar system located at a source redshift of approximately 2.74 [arXiv:1702.03942, arXiv:2312.09311]. The system is gravitationally lensed by a foreground elliptical galaxy at a redshift of 0.407, accompanied by a less luminous satellite perturber [arXiv:2312.09311].

sources.summary: The paper reports J1433+6007 as a quadruply lensed quasar, gives source and lens redshifts, identifies the deflector through spectra, and models the lens with a 1.80 arcsec Einstein radius.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of the quadruply lensed quasar J1433+6007, mined in the SDSS DR12 photometric catalogues using a novel outlier-selection technique, without prior spectroscopic or UV excess information. Discovery data obtained at the Nordic Optical telescope (NOT, La Palma) show nearly identical quasar spectra at z_s=2.74 and four quasar images in a fold configuration, one of which sits on a blue arc. The deflector redshift is z_ l =0.407, from Keck-ESI spectra. We describe the selection procedure, discovery and follow-up, image positions and BVRi magnitudes, and first results and forecasts from simple lens models.

From the relative displacements in Table 1, we fit a simple lens model to obtain the enclosed (2D) mass and predicted magnifications and time-delays. We do a conjugate-point analysis using Glee suy10,suy12 , adopting 25 mas uncertainties on the positions of A,B,C and G, and 50 mas uncertainties on the position of image D. The surface density of the lens is described by (x,y)= (x,y) _ cr = b 2 X^ 2 /(1+e)^ 2 +Y^ 2 /(1-e)^ 2 kas93 , along the principal axes (X,Y) of G, where _ cr = ^ 2 D_ s /(4 G D_ l D_ ls ) factors the dependence on angular-diameter distances. The Einstein radius is defined as the geometric mean of the major and minor axes of the critical curve, _ E =2b q /(1+q).

sources.summary: The paper models SDSSJ1433+6007 as a quadruply lensed QSO system with an elliptical foreground lens, companion perturber, four lensed images, and infrared host arcs.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we study the quadruply-lensed QSO SDSSJ1433+6007 (Figure fig:J1433_color ), discovered by agnello_SDSSJ1433 , henceforth A18. Previous analyses of this system were carried out in A18, in shajib_SDSSJ1433 and in schmidt22_STRIDES , which differed in data quality (A18), modelling procedure and scope. The system is composed of a large elliptical foreground galaxy ( G ) at redshift z_ lens =0.407 (A18), which acts as the main gravitational lens (henceforth ``main lens'') and a less luminous companion, which we assume to be located at the same redshift and will refer to as satellite galaxy which we treat as a ``perturber'' ( Pert. ) of the lens model.

The lensed object is a bright QSO located at redshift z_ source =2.737 (A18) and it appears quadruply lensed (images A, B, C and D). All components are shown in the colour Figure fig:J1433_color . The host galaxy of the QSO, due to cosmological redshift, is only visible in the infrared, forming partial arcs or arclets at and around the positions of the images (visible in the individual infrared filters, Figure fig:all_HST_fig ).

gmw_00010194.name: IRAS 06269 0543
gmw_00010194.overview: IRAS 06269 0543 is an infrared-bright quasar host galaxy [arXiv:1910.10360]. Its molecular gas exhibits disk-like rotation [arXiv:1910.10360], but the galaxy displays a spatial offset between its CO emission peak and its 3-mm continuum peak, which coincides with a northwestern velocity-dispersion enhancement [arXiv:1910.10360].

sources.summary: The paper finds IRAS 06269 0543 has offset CO and 3-mm continuum peaks, a northwest velocity-dispersion enhancement, and disk-like molecular rotation.

quotes.quote: The molecular gas in the IR QSO hosts mapped in CO emission are found to distribute in the nuclear regions and the peaks of CO emission are coincident with the 3-mm continuum emission peaks (the exception is IRAS 06269 0543, where the peak of continuum emission is shifted by 0. 19, equivalent to 0.4 kpc, with respect to the CO emission The 1 positional uncertainties of CO and continuum peaks are 0. 014 and 0. 020, respectively.

The velocity dispersion peaks in these galaxies roughly correspond to the peaks of CO emission, except for IRAS 06269 0543 where there is a shift of the peak between the CO and 3-mm continuum emission. The velocity dispersion map of IRAS 06269 0543 shows an increase in the northwestern direction with respect to the CO peak, with a peak dispersion of 100 \ which is spatially coincident with the location of 3-mm continuum peak. Given the limited spatial resolution of current data, we are not able to infer whether the 3-mm continuum emission is related to AGN activity, although the velocity dispersion is usually found to increase at the AGN location e.g., trakhtenbrot17 .

Combining the analysis of CO morphology and gas kinematics, we find that the molecular gas in four (I ZW 1, IRAS 06269 0543, IRAS F11119+3257, and IRAS F15462 0450) out of the eight IR QSO hosts show signatures of rotating gas disks with ordered velocity field, one (IRAS Z11598 0112) is compact CO source with disturbed velocity, and the rest three (IRAS F15069+1808, IRAS F22454 1744, and IRAS F23411+0228) resolved into distinct objects showing evidence for strong interactions and merging.

gmw_00010195.name: IRAS F15069+1808
gmw_00010195.overview: IRAS F15069+1808 is an infrared-bright quasar system currently in the middle stage of a galaxy merger [arXiv:1910.10360]. The system is resolved into northwest and southeast components with a projected physical separation of 7.2 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1910.10360].

sources.summary: ALMA resolves IRAS F15069+1808 into NW, SE, and overlap CO components, interpreting the system as a mid-stage merger with 7.2 kpc projected separation.

quotes.quote: For IRAS F15069+1808, one of the IR QSO systems resolved into multiple distinct CO sources, we identify two bright sources in the CO map which we denote as NW and SE (northwest and southeast) and refer the overlap region as Overlap (see Figure fig:moment ). Both bright sources show an elongated morphology along the north-east-south-west axis. IRAS F15069+1808 is likely in the mid-stage of a merger when the galaxies have collided and begun to merge. The projected separation between the NW and SE sources is 2. 48, equivalent to a projected physical distance of 7.2 kpc. In the continuum image, we do not detect any signal above 3 , placing a 3 upper limit on 3-mm continuum emission S_ <0.15 mJy.

gmw_00010196.name: IRAS F15462 0450
gmw_00010196.overview: IRAS F15462 0450 is a quasar host galaxy that exhibits a clear CO velocity gradient from the northwest to the southeast in its main body [arXiv:1910.10360]. The galaxy features clumpy CO structures located symmetrically on its northeast and southwest sides, which may indicate a massive molecular outflow [arXiv:1910.10360].

sources.summary: The paper describes clumpy CO structures around IRAS F15462 0450, suggests a possible molecular outflow, and finds a clear velocity gradient in the main galaxy.

quotes.quote: The CO map of IRAS F15462 0450 exhibits clumpy structures outside the central bright compact source (F15462 0450C). The integrated CO line flux suggests that the detection is significant at the 5 and 7 level for the clumps to the northeast (NE) and southwest (SW), respectively. It is worth noting that these two clumps are almost symmetrically located in a straight line across the CO peak of the central source with a separation of 1. 6 ( 2.9 kpc) between the central peak and the clump in each side. One possibility for the symmetric structure may be related to a massive molecular outflow in the QSO host.

The CO velocity map of IRAS F15462 0450 shows a clear gradient from the northwest to the southeast in the main body of galaxy, while the CO line of the NE clump is too narrow to show a gradient at our spectral resolution, and the SW clump shows a small velocity gradient along the same direction as that of main body.

gmw_00010197.name: IRAS F22454 1744
gmw_00010197.overview: IRAS F22454 1744 is an interacting system undergoing a merger between two bright nuclear sources [arXiv:1910.10360]. The southeastern nucleus likely hosts a quasar [arXiv:1910.10360], while a southwest molecular tail traces a tidal arm visible in optical emission [arXiv:1910.10360].

sources.summary: The paper resolves IRAS F22454 1744 into merging nuclear sources, detects 3-mm continuum in the SE nucleus, and traces a southwest molecular tail along optical tidal emission.

quotes.quote: Similar to the interacting system IRAS F15069+1808 described above, the CO gas in IRAS F22454 1744 is also resolved into multiple sources with two arm-like structures (north and southwest arms, denoted as N and SW) originating from the two bright nuclear sources (denoted as NW and SE, Figure fig:moment ), which are undergoing merging with a projected separation of 0. 78 ( 1.7 kpc) between the two central CO peaks. The 3-mm continuum emission is detected in the nuclear region of F22454 1744SE at the 4-5 level with a flux density of 0.253 0.078 mJy, whereas non-detected in the source NW. The SW arm is resolved into two clumps (SW1 and SW2), which form a filamentary structure.

Combining with the fact that the UV emission peak coincides with the nucleus of the SE source, we speculate that F22454 1744SE is probably the QSO merging with a companion galaxy to the NW which is about to coalesce. The SW molecular tail of IRAS F22454 1744 clearly follows the tidal arm seen in the optical emission of PS1 image, whereas the NW arm has no counterpart in optical wavelength.

gmw_00010198.name: IRAS F23411+0228
gmw_00010198.overview: IRAS F23411+0228 is a galaxy with molecular gas strongly concentrated in its central region [arXiv:1910.10360]. This central body is accompanied by a northeastern arm-like feature and an eastern clumpy structure that may indicate an ongoing merger or interaction [arXiv:1910.10360].

sources.summary: The paper finds IRAS F23411+0228 has a central CO concentration, NE arm, eastern clump, and evidence for interaction or a minor merger.

quotes.quote: The CO distribution of IRAS F23411+0228 shows that the molecular gas is strongly concentrated in the central region (F23411+0228C) accompanied by an arm-like feature to the NE and a clumpy structure outside the main body of the galaxy to the east (F23411+0228E). We detect 3-mm continuum emission in F23411+0228C at 8 level with flux of 0.433 0.055 mJy. Most importantly, we also tentatively detect 3-mm continuum emission with flux of 86 30 Jy from F23411+0228E, supporting the reality of such a feature. The angular separation between C and E components in IRAS F23411+0228 system is 1. 77 ( 3.0 kpc).

The spectrum extracted for F23411+0228E demonstrates that the line emission (see Figure fig:spec ), if associated with a redshifted CO(1 0) transition, comes from the same redshift as the main galaxy, suggesting that the source E is likely physically related to the IRAS F23411+0228 system. However, it is not clear whether this is due to an ongoing merger or interaction, or whether it is a clumpy star-forming region at large radii.

gmw_00010199.name: DW1
gmw_00010199.overview: DW1 is a faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy located approximately 25 kiloparsecs southeast of NGC 4631 [arXiv:1410.6368]. It is a companion satellite to NGC 4631 and has a measured g-r color of 0.59 [arXiv:1410.6368].

sources.summary: The paper identifies DW1 as a faint dwarf spheroidal companion candidate near NGC 4631, gives its projected separation, and measures its g-r color.

quotes.quote: Our deep image also detects the presence of two overdensities, marked as DW1 and DW2 in Fig. 3. DW1, DW2, and the most luminous, inner portion of stream _ NW were reported as dwarf companions of NGC 4631 by Karatchensev et al. (2014). The first smaller object, DW1, is well separated from NGC 4631 ( 10 South-East; 25 kpc) and it is clearly a faint dwarf spheroidal satellite of NGC 4631.
For DW1, we measure g- = 0.59 0.08 .

gmw_00010200.name: DW2
gmw_00010200.overview: DW2 is a faint, extended dwarf galaxy satellite located approximately 12 kiloparsecs north of the core of NGC 4631 [arXiv:1410.6368]. Situated near the inner stellar halo of its larger companion, DW2 has a measured g-r color of 0.79 [arXiv:1410.6368].

sources.summary: The paper identifies DW2 as a faint extended dwarf-galaxy satellite candidate near NGC 4631 and measures its color within a limiting isophote.

quotes.quote: Our deep image also detects the presence of two overdensities, marked as DW1 and DW2 in Fig. 3. DW1, DW2, and the most luminous, inner portion of stream _ NW were reported as dwarf companions of NGC 4631 by Karatchensev et al. (2014). The first smaller object, DW1, is well separated from NGC 4631 ( 10 South-East; 25 kpc) and it is clearly a faint dwarf spheroidal satellite of NGC 4631. The second DW2 feature is located 5 ( 12 kpc) North of the NGC 4631 core and its morphology is consistent with that of a faint, more extended dwarf galaxy satellite.

Since DW2 is located spatially near the inner stellar halo of NGC 4631, we measure the g- color inside of the faintest isophote that closes without including emission from the larger galaxy. This isophote corresponds to _ = 26.5 mag arcsec ^ 2 , the g- color inside of which is 0.79 0.07 .

gmw_00010201.name: NGC4378
gmw_00010201.overview: NGC 4378 is an early-type anemic Sa spiral galaxy belonging to the Virgo S cloud [arXiv:0908.3564]. It features a large nuclear bulge, a low-surface-brightness disk with visible ultraviolet star formation, and a single tightly-wound spiral arm likely created by a companion galaxy's recent passage [arXiv:0908.3564].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4378 as a Virgo Sa/anemic spiral with a low-surface-brightness disk, visible UV star formation, a one-armed spiral from a companion passage, and inefficient star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC4378 is a Sa galaxy belonging to the Virgo S cloud. It has a prominent nuclear bulge surrounded by a low surface brightness disk which contains a single, tightly-wound, spiral arm. Given the low surface brightness of the disk component, classified this object as an early-type anemic spiral, although we now know that its atomic hydrogen content is not significantly different from the one in high surface brightness disks.

UV star formation is clearly visible across all the disk. Numerical simulations byrd94 suggest that the unusual one-armed spiral structure in NGC4378 is in reality an impulsive trailing arm created by the recent passage ( 200 Myr) of a small companion ( 1/30 the galaxy's mass). Since the satellite is supposed to survive the interaction, it seems unlikely that a significant fraction of the present in NGC4378 has been recently accreted. The low SSFR of NGC4378 may therefore have an internal origin.

gmw_00010202.name: G1
aliases_external: G1, likely host galaxy of GRB 161104A
gmw_00010202.overview: G1 is an early-type, quiescent galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.793 [arXiv:2007.10372]. It hosts an older stellar population with low dust extinction and a low star formation rate of 0.099 solar masses per year [arXiv:2007.10372].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G1 as the likely GRB 161104A host, classifies it as an early-type quiescent galaxy at z=0.793, and derives an older, massive, low-star-formation stellar population.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: We calculate G1 to be at a position of R.A. = 5^ h 11^ m 34.47^ s , decl. = 51 27' 36.29" (J2000).

quotes.quote: The spectrum of G1 exhibits a red continuum, lacks emission lines, has a discernible 4000 \ break at 7172 , and has distinguishable Ca II H and K absorption lines. These features classify G1 as an early-type, quiescent galaxy at z 0.79 . To determine the precise redshift and uncertainty, we cross-correlated with a galaxy template at age 2.5 Gyr using the model described in and find that z = 0.793 0.003 .

Our best-fit solution shows that G1 has an older stellar population, with a median age of 2.12^ +0.23 _ 0.21 Gyr and a stellar mass of 1.62 10^ 10 M_ , typical for short GRB hosts Berger_2014 . The metallicity of log( Z/Z_ ) 0.08 is consistent with the M-Z relation at z 0.7 given the stellar mass and redshift of G1 MZRelation, MetModel . The low dust extinction of A_V 0.08 mag is expected, as quiescent galaxies do not contain much dust. We also find ( ) 0.54 . Using the SFH, we determine an SFR at z=0.793 of 0.099 M_ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00010203.name: NGC 4660
gmw_00010203.overview: NGC 4660 is classified as a power-law galaxy that exhibits central saturation in Hubble Space Telescope Advanced Camera for Surveys imaging [arXiv:astro-ph/0609762]. It forms a tight pair with the galaxy M59, situated in the foreground of M60 and NGC 4638 [arXiv:0803.3181].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4660 as a power-law galaxy, notes central saturation in the ACS image, and says the VCS pure model falls below central data.

quotes.quote: VCS Models of Power-law Galaxies NGC 4434, 4464, 4621, and 4660 are galaxies that we have classified as power-laws that lf fitted with pure laws. The VCS models for all four galaxies fall below the data as 0 (Figure fig:acscomp2 ). Again, this behavior is echoed in VCS Figure 103, which shows that the fits fall below the observed domain ACS VCS data for <0 3 in NGC 4464, 4621, and 4660, and <0 15 in NGC 4434. lf note that the ACS F475W images of both NGC 4621 and 4660 are actually centrally saturated.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4660 lies with M59 in the foreground of M60 and NGC 4638, making the compact group a chance alignment.

quotes.quote: Taken at face value, the SBF distances suggest that M59 and NGC 4660 lie roughly 2 Mpc to the foreground of M60, NGC 4638, and the bulk of the Virgo cluster. The statistical analysis confirms that the CG is, indeed, the result of a chance alignment of its galaxies, with NGC 4638 lying at least 800 kpc further away (with 99 confidence) than either M59 or NGC 4660. The first two galaxy distances are consistent with M59 and NGC 4660 constituting a tight pair. The dominant galaxy, M60, is at least 440 kpc more distant (95 confidence) than the M59+NGC 4660 pair, and over 1 Mpc (99 confidence) more distant if one uses the broken linear calibration of the SBF distances.

gmw_00010204.name: SdI-1
gmw_00010204.overview: SdI-1 is a distant dwarf irregular galaxy with an irregular morphology [arXiv:1701.07632]. Deep imaging reveals that the galaxy has an elongated blue appearance and may be interacting with smaller companions located to its west [arXiv:1701.07632].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SdI-1 as a distant dwarf irregular with conflicting prior redshifts, irregular morphology, and possible smaller companions.

quotes.quote: In particular, following pap2 we call them SECCO-dI-1 and SECCO-dI-2 (where dI = dwarf Irregular), abbreviated as SdI-1 and SdI-2.

SdI-1 has a determination of redshift z=0.025988 from H I by rob04 that places it far beyond the realm of local mini-haloes D 3 Mpc; adams . However a negative redshift ( z=-0.05545 ) was also reported from optical spectroscopy liske03 In the Millennium Galaxy Catalog http://www.hs.uni-hamburg.de/jliske/mgc/ . While extremely implausible, this may suggest an improper association between the H I detection by rob04 and the stellar counterpart. Given the identification of an ICV along the same line of sight, a new attempt to get a reliable velocity from an optical spectrum was worth doing.

Our deep images (see Fig. imaC1C2 ) reveal two remarkably elongated irregular blue galaxies, with some compact knots superimposed. SdI-1 may be interacting with some smaller companions, located 15 westward of its center. The main body of SdI-2 is surrounded by a very low SB asymmetric halo, more extended in the North-East direction. A fluffy nucleus is visible at the very center of SdI-2, which can be perceived also in the light profile (as a slight change of slope in the innermost 2 , see below). While SdI-2 displays and an overall elliptical shape, the apparent morphology of SdI-1 is remarkably irregular independently of the adopted image cuts.

gmw_00010205.name: Object 1 in the 0153+0009 field
gmw_00010205.overview: Object 1 in the 0153+0009 field is a galaxy with a photometric redshift of 0.745 [arXiv:1103.4047]. This redshift is consistent with the galaxy being the sub-damped Lyman-alpha absorber along the quasar sightline [arXiv:1103.4047].

sources.summary: Object 1 is selected as the 0153+0009 subDLA absorbing galaxy because its photometric redshift matches the absorber and it is closest to the quasar sightline.

quotes.quote: Object 1 is at = 4.9 , which is equivalent to 36.6 kpc at the absorber redshift. SDSS photometry for Object 1 was obtained from S. Zibetti (private communication) since it is not in the `` photoObj '' catalogue made available in the SDSS database. S. Zibetti ran his PSF subtraction software on the SDSS image (Zibetti et al. 2007), and provided us with the photometry of Object 1 in all five SDSS bands (Table 0153zib ). A photometric redshift of z_ phot = 0.745 0.040 is derived for Object 1 by supplementing these magnitudes with our infrared data (see Table 0153photozdata ). The stellar population template fit is shown in Figure 0153photoz . This is consistent with the absorption redshift to within the errors, and therefore, Object 1 is considered to be the absorbing galaxy.

gmw_00010206.name: Object 4 in the 0735+178 field
gmw_00010206.overview: Object 4 in the 0735+178 field is an extended galaxy that was previously classified as a star in the SDSS database [arXiv:1103.4047]. It is identified as the candidate absorbing galaxy in this field because its photometric redshift matches the absorption redshift [arXiv:1103.4047].

sources.summary: Object 4 is selected as the 0735+178 LLS absorbing galaxy after nearer objects are ruled out and its photometric redshift is consistent with the absorber.

quotes.quote: Object 4 is classified as a star in the SDSS database, however, it is extended on our images. The photometric redshift that we derive for Object 4 is consistent with the absorption redshift, and it is identified as the candidate absorber in this field.

In summary, Object 4 is selected as the absorbing galaxy in this field since its photometric redshift, 0.423 0.179 , matches the absorption redshift, and the galaxies with smaller impact parameters are ruled out as candidate absorbers.

gmw_00010207.name: Object 1 in the 1109+0051 field
gmw_00010207.overview: Object 1 in the 1109+0051 field is a galaxy detected in the g', J, and H bands [arXiv:1103.4047]. Due to its close proximity to the sightline, it is considered a candidate absorber at a redshift of 0.4181 [arXiv:1103.4047].

sources.summary: Object 1 is chosen as the lower-redshift 1109+0051 absorber candidate by proximity, despite uncertain photometry caused by overlap with the quasar PSF.

quotes.quote: Object 1 has an impact parameter = 1.3 , which at the two absorber redshifts corresponds to 7 kpc and 8 kpc, respectively. It is detected in the g', J , and H bands. Since it overlaps with the quasar PSF, its photometry is uncertain. We consider it to qualify as a candidate absorber due to its proximity to the sightline.

Given this ambiguity, we use the proximity criterion as the deciding factor, and select Object 1 as the z_ abs = 0.4181 candidate and Object 3 as the z_ abs = 0.5520 candidate, both with confidence level CL = 2.

gmw_00010208.name: Object 3 in the 1109+0051 field
gmw_00010208.overview: Object 3 in the 1109+0051 field is a galaxy with a photometric redshift of z = 0.645 [arXiv:1103.4047]. This measurement makes the galaxy a potential candidate for the absorption system observed at z_abs = 0.5520 [arXiv:1103.4047].

sources.summary: Object 3 is selected as the higher-redshift 1109+0051 absorber candidate because its photometric redshift is consistent with the z_abs = 0.5520 system.

quotes.quote: The photometry of Object 3 results in a photometric redshift of z = 0.645 0.157 , which is consistent with the absorption system at z_ abs = 0.5520.

Therefore, Objects 1, 3, 4, and 5 are all potential absorber candidates for the absorption systems in this field, while Object 2 is marginally consistent at the lower redshift. Given this ambiguity, we use the proximity criterion as the deciding factor, and select Object 1 as the z_ abs = 0.4181 candidate and Object 3 as the z_ abs = 0.5520 candidate, both with confidence level CL = 2.

gmw_00010209.name: TXS 0604+728
gmw_00010209.overview: TXS 0604+728 is a high-redshift radio source at z=3.52965 that exhibits year-scale variability in its ultraviolet luminosity [arXiv:2010.10565]. The galaxy features a core-jet radio structure with two connected radio lobes spanning a projected size of 35 kpc [arXiv:2010.10565].

sources.summary: The paper reports high-redshift associated absorption in 8C 0604+728, arguing that neutral hydrogen survives despite high AGN UV luminosity and describing its radio-lobe and core-jet structure.

quotes.quote: We report a uGMRT detection of absorption associated with the radio source 8C 0604+728, at z=3.52965 . The source is at the highest redshift at which associated absorption has been discovered to date, surpassing earlier known absorber at z 3.39 . We estimate ultraviolet luminosities of (3.2 0.1) 10^ 23 W Hz^ 1 and (6.2 0.2) 10^ 23 W Hz^ 1 , and ionising photon rates of (1.8 0.1) 10^ 56 s^ 1 and (5.0 0.1) 10^ 56 s^ 1 , using data at different epochs; the source shows year-scale variability in both its luminosity and photon rate.

The 1.4 GHz VLA continuum image reported by taylor1996 , with a spatial resolution of 10 kpc, shows strong emission from two connected radio lobes (with peak flux densities of 831 mJy and 79 mJy), where the weaker lobe possibly represents a jet component extending towards North-West from the core see also for description aditya2016 . The projected size between the radio lobes is 35 kpc taylor1996 . The milliarcsec-scale Very Large Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) image at 5 GHz shows the resolved structure of the stronger lobe that consists of multiple compact and diffuse emission components, with flux densities in the range of few 10 mJy to 121 mJy britzen2007 . The separation between the outermost components in the VLBI image is 157 pc. Overall, the radio structure resembles a core-jet, where the core possibly lies at the eastern end of the VLBI emission structure.

sources.summary: The paper reports a tentative z=3.530 absorption detection toward TXS 0604+728, notes that RFI prevented confirmation, and describes the feature as wide and symmetric.

quotes.quote: A single tentative absorption detection was obtained at z 3.530 towards TXS 0604+728. If confirmed, this would be the highest redshift at which H i 21 cm absorption has ever been detected.

In the case of TXS 0604+728, at z=3.530 , the data from multiple GMRT observing runs following the original tentative detection were affected by strong RFI, and it was hence not possible to confirm or rule out the reality of the absorption feature. We note that the absorption feature towards TXS 0604+728 was detected via independent analysis procedures carried out by two of the authors, with independent data editing, and was also seen in the two independent polarizations, with consistent strengths. However, we can still not formally rule out the possibility that it arises from low-level RFI and hence will refer to it in the later discussion as a tentative detection.

The putative \ absorption is extremely wide, with a full width between 20 points of 850 . For comparison, the associated \ absorbers in the samples of gereb15 or vermeulen03 all have velocity widths (between 20 points) 825 . The absorption appears symmetric about the source redshift, but the signal-to-noise ratio of the present spectrum is not sufficient to derive detailed kinematic information.

gmw_00010210.name: TXS 2356+390
gmw_00010210.overview: TXS 2356+390 is a radio-loud active galactic nucleus classified as a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:1511.04767]. Initial radio observations suggested a weak absorption feature at a redshift of 1.198, but follow-up data ruled out this detection as likely radio frequency interference [arXiv:1511.04767].

sources.summary: The paper reports an initial weak z=1.198 absorption feature toward TXS 2356+390, then says follow-up GMRT observations ruled it out as likely low-level RFI.

quotes.quote: The initial GMRT searches yielded two absorption features, at z = 1.198 towards TXS 2356+390, and z=3.530 towards TXS 0604+728. The first of these was a weak feature (with 5 significance, after integrating over the profile), while the second had high ( 20 ) significance. In addition, data on two targets (TXS 0707+476 and TXS 0014+813) were affected by RFI. We hence used the GMRT with its new software backend and 512 channels to re-observe all four systems in 2013 and 2014, using bandwidths of 4.17 MHz for TXS 0604+728 and 16.7 MHz for TXS 0014+813 (both observed with the 327 MHz band) and of 33.33 MHz for TXS 2356+390 and TXS 0707+476 (both observed with the 610 MHz band).

For two sources, TXS 2356+390 at z = 1.198 and TXS 0604+728 at z=3.530 , weak absorption features (with 5 significance) were seen in the initial GMRT spectra. In the case of TXS 2356+390, the follow-up GMRT observations (with somewhat worse sensitivity) ruled out the presence of \ absorption at the level detected in the first observing run, indicating that the original feature is likely to have arisen due to low-level RFI.

gmw_00010211.name: F2M0830
gmw_00010211.overview: F2M0830 is a red quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.4 [arXiv:2307.06059]. The system shows evidence of an ongoing merger and features an extended ionized gas nebula with a pair of superbubbles [arXiv:2307.06059].

sources.summary: The paper includes F2M0830 among three z~0.4 red quasars with IFU-mapped superbubble pairs, extended ionized nebulae, organized kinematics, and evidence of an ongoing merger.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present 3 red quasars at z 0.4 with unambiguous signatures of superbubble pairs. We conduct IFU mapping of these targets utilizing the Gemini-North Multi-Object Spectrographs (GMOS-N) equipped on the Gemini 8m Telescope.

All three red quasars show extended ionized gas nebulae, with each of them having a pair of red-shifted and blue-shifted bubbles. In Fig. 1, Fig. 2 and Fig. 3, we map the distribution of the above-mentioned parameters across the entire quasars and their individual blue- and red-shifted bubbles. We find clear signatures of superbubbles in their well-resolved dual-bubble morphology, which is further aided by the information rendered by \ kinematics: the narrowness of the \ line in the outskirts of the bubbles is in line with a geometrically thin shell, and the well-defined double-peak velocity profiles near the galactic centers indicate where the pair of bubbles overlap in projection .
One of our red quasars, F2M0830, shows evidence of an ongoing merger .

gmw_00010212.name: F2M1106
gmw_00010212.overview: F2M1106 is a radio-quiet red quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.4 [arXiv:2307.06059]. It features an extended ionized gas nebula and host galaxy kinematics that reveal a pair of expanding superbubbles [arXiv:2307.06059].

sources.summary: The paper includes F2M1106 among three z~0.4 red quasars with IFU-mapped superbubble pairs, extended ionized nebulae, organized kinematics, and radio-quiet classification.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present 3 red quasars at z 0.4 with unambiguous signatures of superbubble pairs. We conduct IFU mapping of these targets utilizing the Gemini-North Multi-Object Spectrographs (GMOS-N) equipped on the Gemini 8m Telescope.

All three red quasars show extended ionized gas nebulae, with each of them having a pair of red-shifted and blue-shifted bubbles. In Fig. 1, Fig. 2 and Fig. 3, we map the distribution of the above-mentioned parameters across the entire quasars and their individual blue- and red-shifted bubbles. We find clear signatures of superbubbles in their well-resolved dual-bubble morphology, which is further aided by the information rendered by \ kinematics: the narrowness of the \ line in the outskirts of the bubbles is in line with a geometrically thin shell, and the well-defined double-peak velocity profiles near the galactic centers indicate where the pair of bubbles overlap in projection .
Under this definition, F2M0830 and F2M1106 are radio-quiet, while F2M1618 is radio-intermediate, regardless of radio fluxes from FIRST or from ref. in an NVSS-like beam size.

gmw_00010213.name: F2M1618
gmw_00010213.overview: F2M1618 is a red quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.4 [arXiv:2307.06059]. The host galaxy features an extended ionized gas nebula with a pair of superbubbles and exhibits elevated, extended radio emission [arXiv:2307.06059].

sources.summary: The paper includes F2M1618 among three z~0.4 red quasars with IFU-mapped superbubble pairs, extended ionized nebulae, organized kinematics, and elevated radio power.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present 3 red quasars at z 0.4 with unambiguous signatures of superbubble pairs. We conduct IFU mapping of these targets utilizing the Gemini-North Multi-Object Spectrographs (GMOS-N) equipped on the Gemini 8m Telescope.

All three red quasars show extended ionized gas nebulae, with each of them having a pair of red-shifted and blue-shifted bubbles. In Fig. 1, Fig. 2 and Fig. 3, we map the distribution of the above-mentioned parameters across the entire quasars and their individual blue- and red-shifted bubbles. We find clear signatures of superbubbles in their well-resolved dual-bubble morphology, which is further aided by the information rendered by \ kinematics: the narrowness of the \ line in the outskirts of the bubbles is in line with a geometrically thin shell, and the well-defined double-peak velocity profiles near the galactic centers indicate where the pair of bubbles overlap in projection .
F2M1618 shows a higher radio power ( L_ 1.4GHz 10^ 25.4 W Hz ^ 1 according to the flux density measured in a larger beam size, which indicates extended radio emission.

gmw_00010214.name: 2MASX J03341605-0111297
gmw_00010214.overview: 2MASX J03341605-0111297 is an elliptical cD galaxy located near the geometric center of its host galaxy cluster [arXiv:1602.01094]. It is the most near-infrared luminous galaxy in the cluster and is coincident with an X-ray emission peak [arXiv:1602.01094].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MASX J03341605-0111297 as the cluster's most NIR-luminous galaxy, closer to the geometric center, coincident with an X-ray peak, and the cD galaxy of the main cluster.

quotes.quote: Compared to 3C89 (BCG 2), 2MASX J03341605-0111297 (BCG 1) is closer to the geometric center of the cluster and the most luminous in the NIR. Although 3C89 is not the most NIR luminous galaxy in the cluster, it is likely the dominant galaxy of the infalling subcluster. The velocity difference between two galaxies is only 300 km sec ^ 1 from SDSS . estimated the velocity of 3C89 to be 41551 km sec ^ 1 , which reduces the velocity difference between two galaxies to 70 km sec ^ 1 . This implies that the merger is taking place near the plane of sky.

The second peak in the X-ray contours is consistent with the brighter elliptical galaxy 2MASX J03341605-0111297 (marked by BCG 1) which is the cD galaxy of the main cluster. The velocity difference of these two galaxies is only 300 km sec ^ 1 , which suggests that their relative motion is near the plane of sky.

The location of two galaxies (BCG 1 2 in Fig. 1) are consistent with a merger scenario between two subclusters, where BCG 2 is the BCG of the infalling subcluster and BCG 1 is considered the cD galaxy of the main cluster.

gmw_00010215.name: 2MASX J033434406-0109527
gmw_00010215.overview: 2MASX J033434406-0109527 is a massive galaxy located approximately 700 kpc east of 3C89 [arXiv:1602.01094]. It has a luminosity comparable to a typical brightest cluster galaxy and is likely the dominant galaxy of a third infalling subcluster [arXiv:1602.01094].

sources.summary: The paper places 2MASX J033434406-0109527 about 700 kpc east of 3C89 and associates it with the overpressurized eastern extension and a possible third infalling subcluster.

quotes.quote: The 2MASX J033434406-0109527 (BCG 3) is located 700 kpc east from 3C89. With luminosity comparable to that of a typical brightest cluster galaxy (BCG), BCG 3 is likely to be the BCG of the third subcluster.
The EE hosts BCG 3 (Fig. 1). This suggests that the EE is associated with another infalling subcluster. This third merging subcluster could explain the overpressurized gas in the region.
A massive galaxy (BCG 3 in Fig. 1) is likely the dominant galaxy of a third infall subcluster that is responsible for the EE.

gmw_00010216.overview: The galaxy 0413 is a post-starburst system that has been detected in continuum emission [arXiv:2210.06522]. Observations of its carbon monoxide distribution indicate that the galaxy is best fitted by a single exponential disc model [arXiv:2210.06522].

sources.summary: The paper reports that 0413 was newly analyzed with 7 m ALMA data, is detected in continuum emission, and is best fitted by a single exponential CO disc model.

quotes.quote: Two sources (0413 and 2276) observed with these ALMA programmes were not presented in smercina22 . 0413 was only observed with the 7 m array with a 1.3 hr on-source integration; 2276 was observed with the 12 m array for 14 min and 7 m array for 28 min on-source.
0413 and 2276 were not included in smercina22 , but both are detectable in continuum emission.

0379 is best fitted with a single uniform disc model, and 0413 is best fitted with a single exponential disc model. We note, however, that 0413 has not been observed with the 12 m array, and therefore the potential existence of a compact CO component cannot be ruled out.

gmw_00010217.overview: 0480 is a post-starburst galaxy that exhibits secure and strong continuum emission [arXiv:2210.06522]. Its carbon monoxide profile consists of two components, including an extended component that traces gas in the galaxy disk [arXiv:2210.06522].

sources.summary: The paper finds secure and strong continuum emission in 0480, a lower 1.3 mm flux without SED excess, and a two-component CO profile with extended disc gas.

quotes.quote: Among them, we identified secure continuum emission associated with five sources (0413, 0480, 0637, 2276 and 2777) through a comparison between the signal-to-noise (S/N) ratios of positive and negative peaks in the maps.

Among them, 0480 and 2777 exhibit strong continuum emission ( S_ >4 mJy ) as reported in smercina22 , although our measured flux densities are significantly different ( 4.11 0.05 and 4.72 0.24 mJy in this work versus 7.54 0.22 and 1.00 0.35 mJy in smercina22 , respectively) . Compared with smercina22 , the better recovery of extended emission with uv tapering resulted in the increase of measured S_ of 2777. For 0480, the decreased S_ suggests no excess of 1.3 mm emission to the best-fit spectral energy distribution model, in contrast to the findings in smercina22 .

Among the eight PSBs in our sample, six sources (0480, 0570, 0637, 2276, 2360, and 2777) exhibit a smaller reduced ^2 when their visibility profiles are fitted with models consisting of two components of different scale lengths. The extended CO components of these sources typically have effective radii of R_ e = 1.6_ 0.7 ^ +3.5 kpc, tracing the gas component in the galaxy disc. They are 1.4_ 0.3 ^ +0.6 times brighter (in terms of integrated luminosity) than the inner compact CO components with R_ e = 0.4_ 0.2 ^ +0.4 kpc, which trace the circumnuclear gas.

gmw_00010218.overview: The post-starburst galaxy 0637 features secure continuum emission that indicates an extended structure [arXiv:2210.06522]. Its carbon monoxide visibility profile is composed of an extended disk component and a brighter, compact circumnuclear gas component [arXiv:2210.06522].

sources.summary: The paper reports secure continuum emission in 0637 despite an earlier non-detection and resolves its CO visibility profile with extended and compact gas components.

quotes.quote: Among them, we identified secure continuum emission associated with five sources (0413, 0480, 0637, 2276 and 2777) through a comparison between the signal-to-noise (S/N) ratios of positive and negative peaks in the maps.
The continuum of 0637 was reported as a non-detection in smercina22 ( < 0.45 mJy), but is detectable in the uv tapered map ( S/N =4.7 , 0.86 0.25 mJy), indicating its extended nature.

Among the eight PSBs in our sample, six sources (0480, 0570, 0637, 2276, 2360, and 2777) exhibit a smaller reduced ^2 when their visibility profiles are fitted with models consisting of two components of different scale lengths. The extended CO components of these sources typically have effective radii of R_ e = 1.6_ 0.7 ^ +3.5 kpc, tracing the gas component in the galaxy disc. They are 1.4_ 0.3 ^ +0.6 times brighter (in terms of integrated luminosity) than the inner compact CO components with R_ e = 0.4_ 0.2 ^ +0.4 kpc, which trace the circumnuclear gas.

gmw_00010219.overview: The post-starburst galaxy 2276 is detectable in continuum emission [arXiv:2210.06522]. Its carbon monoxide visibility profile is best modeled with two components of different scale lengths, which trace an extended gas component in the galaxy disk [arXiv:2210.06522].

sources.summary: The paper reports that 2276 was newly analyzed with ALMA 12 m and 7 m data, is detected in continuum emission, and has a two-component CO visibility profile.

quotes.quote: Two sources (0413 and 2276) observed with these ALMA programmes were not presented in smercina22 . 0413 was only observed with the 7 m array with a 1.3 hr on-source integration; 2276 was observed with the 12 m array for 14 min and 7 m array for 28 min on-source.
0413 and 2276 were not included in smercina22 , but both are detectable in continuum emission.

Among the eight PSBs in our sample, six sources (0480, 0570, 0637, 2276, 2360, and 2777) exhibit a smaller reduced ^2 when their visibility profiles are fitted with models consisting of two components of different scale lengths. The extended CO components of these sources typically have effective radii of R_ e = 1.6_ 0.7 ^ +3.5 kpc, tracing the gas component in the galaxy disc.

gmw_00010220.overview: The post-starburst galaxy 2777 exhibits secure, strong continuum emission [arXiv:2210.06522] and has a low dense-gas fraction [arXiv:2204.07465]. Its carbon monoxide visibility profile is modeled with an extended disk component and a brighter, compact circumnuclear component [arXiv:2210.06522].

sources.summary: The paper finds secure, strong continuum emission in 2777, revises its flux upward with uv tapering, and models its CO visibility profile with extended and compact components.

quotes.quote: Among them, we identified secure continuum emission associated with five sources (0413, 0480, 0637, 2276 and 2777) through a comparison between the signal-to-noise (S/N) ratios of positive and negative peaks in the maps.

Among them, 0480 and 2777 exhibit strong continuum emission ( S_ >4 mJy ) as reported in smercina22 , although our measured flux densities are significantly different ( 4.11 0.05 and 4.72 0.24 mJy in this work versus 7.54 0.22 and 1.00 0.35 mJy in smercina22 , respectively) . Compared with smercina22 , the better recovery of extended emission with uv tapering resulted in the increase of measured S_ of 2777.

Among the eight PSBs in our sample, six sources (0480, 0570, 0637, 2276, 2360, and 2777) exhibit a smaller reduced ^2 when their visibility profiles are fitted with models consisting of two components of different scale lengths. The extended CO components of these sources typically have effective radii of R_ e = 1.6_ 0.7 ^ +3.5 kpc, tracing the gas component in the galaxy disc. They are 1.4_ 0.3 ^ +0.6 times brighter (in terms of integrated luminosity) than the inner compact CO components with R_ e = 0.4_ 0.2 ^ +0.4 kpc, which trace the circumnuclear gas.

sources.summary: The paper finds H03 has elevated HCN/CO and very high HCN/HCO+ ratios, likely from mechanical or cosmic-ray heating, while its low HCO+ luminosity implies a low dense-gas fraction.

quotes.quote: The post-starburst galaxy with high HCN/CO (H03) may have HCN luminosity increased via mechanical heating or cosmic ray heating, as it has a very high HCN/HCO + ratio (see discussion in sec:densegasstate ). Given the low HCO + luminosity of this galaxy, it is likely that the dense gas fraction is low.
One of the PSBs (H03) has a very high HCN/HCO + ratio (low HCO ^+ /HCN), even compared to AGN and ULIRGs.
Mechanical heating or cosmic ray heating from past AGN activity are the most likely causes for the high HCN/HCO + ratio in H03.

gmw_00010221.name: GALEX1421+5239
gmw_00010221.overview: GALEX1421+5239 is classified as a star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1106.0496]. Its spectrum lacks high-ionization lines, which contradicts an earlier classification of the source as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1106.0496].

sources.summary: The paper classifies GALEX1421+5239 as a star-forming galaxy, noting absent high-ionization lines and disagreement with an earlier AGN classification.

quotes.quote: Finally, we find that the third source, GALEX1421+5239 (EGS14), lies on the star-forming galaxy track in the BPT diagram. We do not detect high-ionization lines. This is in contrast to Finkelstein et al. \ (2009b), who classified the source as an AGN on the basis of both its position on the BPT diagram and the presence of high-ionization lines in its spectrum.

gmw_00010222.name: GALEX1420+5313
gmw_00010222.overview: GALEX1420+5313 is a post-starburst galaxy identified in the DEEP2 survey [arXiv:1106.0496]. The galaxy exhibits weak optical emission lines and may host Lyman-alpha emission dominated by an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1106.0496].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GALEX1420+5313 as a DEEP2 post-starburst galaxy with weak optical emission lines and possible AGN-dominated Ly emission, excluding it from the LAE sample.

quotes.quote: The remaining source (GALEX1420+5313; see Figure  optical_spectra (e)) has a post-starburst spectrum. (This galaxy is also identified in the DR3 release of the DEEP2 survey; Davis et al. \ 2007.) We label it as ``A'' in column 8 of Table  tab2 for its weak optical emission lines. It is possible that the Ly emission is AGN-dominated in this galaxy. We therefore exclude this source from the z=0.65-1.25 LAE sample.

gmw_00010223.name: GALEX0959+0151
gmw_00010223.overview: GALEX0959+0151 is an emission-line galaxy characterized by a very high H-alpha luminosity [arXiv:1106.0496]. It is radio-faint for its luminosity, which may be due to a young spectral energy distribution age of 9 Myr where radio-generating supernovae have not yet occurred [arXiv:1106.0496].

sources.summary: GALEX0959+0151 is singled out as radio-faint for its high H luminosity, with very large EW(H) and a young SED age suggesting delayed supernova radio emission.

quotes.quote: There is one significantly deviant point, GALEX0959+0151 in COSMOS 00, where the H luminosity is very high compared to the radio power. This source has the highest EW(H ) in the figure ( 811   ) and an age of 9 Myr from SED fitting (Figure  ewha_time (b)). This suggests that the radio power is low because the supernovae that generate the radio emission have yet to occur.

gmw_00010224.name: GRB 050819
gmw_00010224.overview: GRB 050819 is a high-redshift emission-line galaxy [arXiv:1205.4036]. Its spectrum displays a bright emission line interpreted as [O III] at a redshift of z=2.504, which is supported by marginal flux at the expected positions of other lines [arXiv:1205.4036].

sources.summary: The paper interprets the GRB 050819 host spectrum as [O III] at z=2.504, rejecting alternative line identifications and noting marginal supporting flux.

quotes.quote: GRB 050819 A bright emission line is detected in the X-shooter spectrum of GRB 050819 at a wavelength of 17545 , which could be interpreted as   at z=3.71 , ( 5007 ) at z=2.504 or  at z=1.673 . In the case of , we would expect to resolve the doublet of ( 3726, 3729) with two components of comparable intensity separated by 14 . If the line was , then ( 5007 ) and/or  should be detected for typical line ratios. Hence, we interpret the line as ( 5007) at z=2.504 . The marginal flux seen at the expected positions of  and  at z=2.504 with S/N 2 (Figure  lines ) further supports our association.

gmw_00010225.name: GRB 060719
gmw_00010225.overview: GRB 060719 is a dark gamma-ray burst host galaxy [arXiv:1205.4036]. It is classified as an emission-line galaxy with a robust redshift of z=1.532 [arXiv:1205.4036].

sources.summary: The paper reports a single bright X-shooter emission line for the dark GRB 060719 host and argues for a robust z=1.532 redshift.

quotes.quote: GRB 060719 We detect a single, bright emission line at 16616 in the X-shooter spectrum of the dark GRB 060719 ( _ OX <-0.1 ).  is readily excluded as the line is not resolved into two components. Using the redshift constraint ( z < 2.0 ) from the host continuum emission 2012ApJ...752...62J , we interpret this line as  at z=1.532 .

The flux increase seen at the corresponding wavelength of  ( _ obs =9440  ) strongly supports this interpretation. ( 5007) at z=1.532 is located at _ obs =12677  , a region dominated by bright skylines. We hence consider the redshift robust.

gmw_00010226.name: GRB 071021
gmw_00010226.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 071021 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.452 [arXiv:1205.4036]. Its interstellar medium contains dust that causes extinction [arXiv:1205.4036], and modeling favors a non-AGN star-forming state [arXiv:2007.11874].

sources.summary: The paper favors a non-AGN Chabrier IMF model for GRB071021, finds stellar mass consistent with prior work, and attributes its lower SFR to FIR photometry.

quotes.quote: Based on the reduced ^2 , if we add the AGN component to this modelling, the resulting fit is also an over-fit. The reduced ^2 with AGN is 0.68 and without AGN is 0.99. The results of two conditions with and without AGN component are summarised in Table tab11 . The IMF configuration of Chabrier2003 performed better in the two conditions. Here we compare our results involving stellar mass, SFR, and dust luminosity to those in Hunt2014 and Perley2013a in Table tab13 . The stellar mass is consistent within the uncertainty between Perley2013a and ours.

The SFR values in the three previous works differ from each other, and comparing them with our result shows that our value SFR_ SED =90 5(M_ yr^ 1 ) is the lowest. The SFR transformation of IR luminosity by Kennicutt1998b indicates SFR _ Kennicutt = 129 28(M_ yr^ 1 ) which is very close to our results compared with previous studies. Moreover, there was no FIR photometry included in previous works, which is probably the cause of over-estimation of SFR values.

sources.summary: The paper associates GRB 071021 with a galaxy at z=2.452, ruling out a higher-redshift event and attributing the red afterglow to dust in the host ISM.

quotes.quote: GRB 071021 The afterglow of the dark ( _ OX 0.4 ) GRB 071021 was detected as a fading source in the NIR only 2007GCN..6968....1C . The galaxy at a position consistent with the afterglow (Figure  fc ) has a redshift of z=2.452 based on the detection of the emission lines of , , and  (Figure  lines ). Our spectroscopic redshift rules out a high-redshift nature for this event, and the red color of the afterglow is attributed to extinction by dust in the ISM of the host.

gmw_00010227.name: IC 4889
gmw_00010227.overview: IC 4889 is an isolated elliptical or lenticular galaxy that exhibits isophotal twisting and a dusty disk [arXiv:1502.06148]. It hosts a poor globular cluster system with a shallow profile and a blue cluster population [arXiv:1502.06148].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4889 as an isolated E/S0 with isophotal twisting, a dusty disk, a young/intermediate age estimate, a blue globular-cluster population, and a shallow cluster profile.

quotes.quote: IC 4889 is an E/S0 laurikainen11 present in the smith04 catalogue, showing isophotal twisting tal09 as well as a dusty disk smith04 . serra10 estimate an age of 3.6 Gyr. Its GCS has been studied by gebhardt99 using HST data, without finding evidence for bimodality.

IC 4889 stands out by the presence of a significant blue population throughout the entire magnitude range ( 21 i 24 ), which seems absent in the rest of the galaxies (compare, for example, with NGC 3962).

NGC 4240 and IC 4889 present very shallow GC profiles, probably an effect of the low number of GCs present in these systems. Particularly problematic is the case of IC 4889 where an abrupt jump in the cluster distribution is visible at 40 . IC 4889 not only possesses a poor cluster system, but also is the galaxy with the shallowest observations of the set.

gmw_00010228.name: the BCG
gmw_00010228.overview: The BCG is a multi-nucleus cD galaxy located at the center of its cluster [arXiv:2106.15583]. It features six distinct merging nuclei within a common symmetric envelope and is surrounded by multiple gravitationally lensed arc systems [arXiv:2106.15583].

sources.summary: The BCG in MS 0440.5 + 0204 is described as a multi-nucleus cD galaxy with six merging nuclei, a common envelope, and surrounding lensed arcs.

quotes.quote: In its center, the cluster is dominated by a multi nucleus cD galaxy hereafter the BCG, luppino1993 , with six distinct nuclei merging within a common symmetric envelope of 48 ( 157  kpc) in diameter. The BCG in MS 0440.5 + 0204 is quite similar to the central galaxy in Abell 3827, a super-giant elliptical that appears to be in the process of cannibalizing at least four other galaxies and references therein carrasco2010 . Surrounding the BCG are at least five gravitational lensed arc systems and two radial arcs gioia1998 luppino1993 , corresponding to lensed sources located at different redshifts.

gmw_00010229.name: IRAS 20210 + 1121
gmw_00010229.overview: IRAS 20210 + 1121 is an infrared luminous merging galaxy system that hosts a dual active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1312.2031]. Infrared observations of the system reveal double nuclear emission, though only one of these nuclei exhibits a red K-L' color [arXiv:1312.2031].

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS 20210 + 1121 as a known X-ray dual-AGN merger, detects double nuclear emission, but finds only one red K-L' nucleus.

quotes.quote: Infrared properties of X-ray dual AGNs Among the 29 observed infrared luminous merging systems, signatures of dual AGNs with separations of > 0 5 were previously reported from X-ray observations for the following five sources: Mrk 463 bia08 , Mrk 739 kos11 , NGC 3393 fab11 , NGC 6240 kom03 , and IRAS 20210 + 1121 pic10 . Using our methods, we detect double nuclear emission in Mrk 463, Mrk 739, NGC 6240, and IRAS 20210 + 1121, but not in NGC 3393. Mrk 463, Mrk 739, and IRAS 20210 + 1121 show only one red ( K-L' 1.0 mag) nucleus, with a blue K-L' color ( < 1.0 mag) at the other nucleus.

If the detected X-ray emission from both nuclei originates in luminous AGNs, one nucleus in Mrk 463, Mrk 739, NGC 3393, and IRAS 20210 + 1121 could be a hot-dust-deficient AGN, in which case the contribution from AGN-heated hot dust emission to the observed L' band flux would be small, and the observed infrared K and L' band fluxes would be dominated by nuclear stellar-origin emission.

gmw_00010230.name: dw1335-29
gmw_00010230.overview: The dwarf galaxy dw1335-29 is a satellite of the M 83 subgroup located at a distance of approximately 5.03 Mpc [arXiv:1803.02406]. It is classified as either a dwarf irregular or a transition-type dwarf galaxy with an estimated metallicity of [Fe/H] = -1.79 dex [arXiv:1803.02406].

sources.summary: The paper resolves dw1335-29 into stars, measures its TRGB distance and metallicity, confirms it as an M 83 satellite, and notes possible young stars but no tidal features.

quotes.quote: We confirm the two candidates, dw1335-29 and dw1340-30, to be dwarf satellites of the M 83 subgroup, with estimated distances of 5.03 0.24 Mpc and 5.06 0.24 Mpc, respectively. Similarly, but given the much smaller spatial extent and thus significantly lower amount of foreground+background contamination, we blindly detect the dwarf galaxy candidates dw1335-29 and dw1340-30 in our CCD images as pronounced stellar overdensities. The centers are at ( _ 2000 =13:35:46, _ 2000 = 29:42:28) for dw1335-29 and ( _ 2000 =13:40:19, _ 2000 = 30:21:31) for dw1340-30, respectively, which agree with the centers estimated in 2015A&A...583A..79M based on galaxy surface photometry.

For dw1335-29 a metallicity of [ Fe/H ] = -1.79 dex is estimated, corresponding to a calibrated absolute TRGB magnitude of M_ I =-4.08 0.02 mag, which gives a final distance of 5.03 0.24 Mpc. Hence dw1335-29 is either a dwarf irregular or transition type dwarf.

gmw_00010231.name: dw1340-30
gmw_00010231.overview: The dwarf galaxy dw1340-30 is a satellite companion of the M 83 subgroup [arXiv:1803.02406]. Located at a distance of approximately 5.06 Mpc, the galaxy exhibits a low metallicity and a lopsided distribution of its red giant branch stars [arXiv:1803.02406].

sources.summary: The paper resolves dw1340-30 into stars, derives a 5.06 Mpc TRGB distance and low metallicity, confirms it as an M 83 satellite, and notes lopsided RGB stars.

quotes.quote: We confirm the two candidates, dw1335-29 and dw1340-30, to be dwarf satellites of the M 83 subgroup, with estimated distances of 5.03 0.24 Mpc and 5.06 0.24 Mpc, respectively. Similarly, but given the much smaller spatial extent and thus significantly lower amount of foreground+background contamination, we blindly detect the dwarf galaxy candidates dw1335-29 and dw1340-30 in our CCD images as pronounced stellar overdensities. The centers are at ( _ 2000 =13:35:46, _ 2000 = 29:42:28) for dw1335-29 and ( _ 2000 =13:40:19, _ 2000 = 30:21:31) for dw1340-30, respectively, which agree with the centers estimated in 2015A&A...583A..79M based on galaxy surface photometry.

For dw1340-30 the metallicity is [ Fe/H ] = -2.27 dex, the calibrated absolute TRGB magnitude of M_ I =-4.09 0.02 mag and a final distance estimate of 5.06 0.24 Mpc. However, within the galaxy dw1340-30 there is a small lopsidedness of RGB stars in the direction South-East.

gmw_00010232.name: dw1325-33
gmw_00010232.overview: The object dw1325-33 is a dwarf galaxy candidate [arXiv:1803.02406]. Because observations have failed to resolve the candidate into individual stars, it has a conservative lower distance limit of 5.3 Mpc, leaving its membership in the Centaurus group unconfirmed [arXiv:1803.02406].

sources.summary: The paper observes dw1325-33 but cannot resolve it into individual stars, setting a conservative lower distance limit and leaving Centaurus-group membership unconfirmed.

quotes.quote: The third candidate, dw1325-33, could not be resolved into stars due to insufficiently deep images, implying its distance must be larger than 5.3 Mpc. The third dwarf candidate dw1325-33 could not be resolved into individual stars. The case of dw1325-33 The third dwarf galaxy candidate, dw1325-33, could not be resolved into individual stars with our data.

With our current observations we cannot rule out nor confirm the possibility that this candidate is a member of the Centaurus group. Considering the steeply dropping completeness below the 50 limit of I=24.4 mag, we conservatively set a lower limit for the dw1325-33 distance to 5.3 Mpc.

gmw_00010233.name: KK208
gmw_00010233.overview: KK208 is a nearby peculiar dwarf galaxy that lacks a well-defined center [arXiv:1803.02406]. Stretched over 10 arcminutes, it is located near M83 and dw1335-29 and is believed to be a tidally disrupted stellar stream [arXiv:1803.02406].

sources.summary: The paper describes KK208 as a nearby peculiar object close to dw1335-29 and M 83, stretched over 10 arcmin and possibly tidally disrupted or stream-like.

quotes.quote: Noteworthy is the physical separation between dw1335-29 and KK208 of only 28 kpc. KK208 is a peculiar object, as it lacks a well defined center and is stretched over 10 arc minutes (corresponding to 15 kpc at a distance of 5 Mpc). It was resolved into individual stars with HST 2002A&A...385...21K .

The authors suggest that this object is tidally disrupted by M 83 similar as the Sagittarius galaxy by the Milky Way 1994Natur.370..194I . In Fig. decam we show a DECam image that includes dw1335-29, KK208, and M 83. We note that 2017MNRAS.465.5026C refer to KK208 as a tidal stream.

gmw_00010234.name: J0028 + 0035
gmw_00010234.overview: J0028 + 0035 is a double-double radio source candidate that features co-linear relic lobes and putative inner lobes [arXiv:2011.09293]. This system exhibits renewed active galactic nucleus activity after a period of quiescence [arXiv:2011.09293].

sources.summary: The paper reports J0028 + 0035 as a double-double radio source, with relic lobes, putative inner lobes, matching jet powers, and renewed activity after quiescence.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a double--double radio source (DDRS) J0028 + 0035. We observed it with LOFAR, GMRT, and the VLA. By combining our observational data with those from the literature, we gathered an appreciable set of radio flux density measurements covering the range from 74 MHz to 14 GHz.

It follows that since the relic lobes and the inner two components at RA = 00^ h 28^ m 38 76 , Dec. = +00 35 38 1 and RA = 00^ h 28^ m 39 16 , Dec. = +00 35 51 6 are co-linear, J0028 + 0035 could be a DDRS. To support the classification of J0028 + 0035 as a DDRS, its optical identification and higher resolution observations of its putative inner lobes are needed. Based on that material and the literature, we have collected radio flux densities in the range from 74 MHz to 14 GHz for all components of J0028 + 0035.

gmw_00010235.name: host galaxy of DES13Sm
gmw_00010235.overview: The host galaxy of DES13Sm is a faint emission-line galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.663 [arXiv:1501.07232]. It is characterized by a low stellar mass and a distinctly sub-solar metallicity [arXiv:1501.07232].

sources.summary: The paper measures the DES13Sm host as a faint emission-line galaxy at z=0.663 with low stellar mass and distinctly sub-solar metallicity.

quotes.quote: We highlight the obvious narrow nebular emission lines in the host galaxy of DES13Sm, specifically [ O ii ] 3727 , H 4861 , and [ O iii ] 4959,5007 . We assume these lines originate from the underlying host galaxy as their spatial extent along the vertical axis is greater than the observed trace of the main supernova spectrum. Using these nebular emission lines, we determine a redshift of z=0.663 0.001 for the host galaxy of DES13Sm, where the uncertainty is derived from the wavelength dispersion between individual emission lines.

Using the DES host-galaxy photometry and the spectroscopic redshift from VLT, we estimate that the stellar mass of the host galaxy is (M/M_ )=9.3 0.3 using stellar population models from PEGASE.2 1997A&A...326..950F , and (M/M_ )=9.0 0.3 using the stellar population templates from 2005MNRAS.362..799M (both corrected for Milky Way extinction and fixing the redshift of the templates to z=0.663 ). Such a low stellar-mass host galaxy is consistent with the findings for other SLSNe Neill11,Chen13,lunnan14 .

Assuming the lower (upper) branch, we find a metallicity ( 12+ [ O/H ] ) of 8.30 (8.38) from M91 and 8.30 (8.42) from KK04 , which is consistent with the median metallicity of 8.35 found by lunnan14 for a sample of 31 SLSNe host galaxies. We thus find a host-galaxy abundance ratio that is distinctly sub-solar 8.69; Asplund09 , though not as extremely low as has been seen for other SLSNe, such as SN2010gx ( 12+ [ O/H ] =7.5; Chen13 ).

gmw_00010236.name: 53W002
gmw_00010236.overview: 53W002 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.39 [arXiv:2109.02019]. It lies on the outskirts of a large-scale, high-density protocluster structure of Lyman-alpha emitting galaxies [arXiv:2109.02019].

sources.summary: The paper studies massive galaxy populations around the z=2.39 radio galaxy 53W002, finding LAE structure nearby and that the radio galaxy lies on the outskirts.

quotes.quote: In this study, we use optical and NIR imaging data taken with Suprime-Cam and MOIRCS on the Subaru telescope to search for massive galaxies in a large-scale high-density structure of LAEs at z=2.39 discovered by Mawatari_2012 near the radio galaxy 53W002. The 53W002 protocluster was initially discovered as an overdensity of 18 LAEs selected by the intermediate-band excess of HST/WFPC2 data around the radio galaxy Pascarelle_1996a,Pascarelle_1996b . Mawatari_2012 observed 31^ 24^ field with Suprime-Cam and a custom-made narrow-band filter for the Ly emission at z 2.39 . They discovered the large-scale high-density structure of LAEs north or northeast of 53W002, and found that the radio galaxy is located at outskirt of the structure.

The FOV1 is the most southern one and contains the radio galaxy 53W002. The radio galaxy 53W002 is located at outskirt of the density peak of LAEs, and there are few bright and red galaxies in vicinity of 53W002, which is consistent with the previous study of Yamada_2001 .

gmw_00010237.name: No.18
gmw_00010237.overview: No.18 is a massive, high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.393 [arXiv:2109.02019]. It is a spectroscopically confirmed member of a protocluster and exhibits active galactic nucleus activity alongside very extended Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:2109.02019].

sources.summary: The paper identifies No.18 as a spectroscopically confirmed z=2.393 protocluster member near 53W002, with AGN-related emission, extended Ly emission, high mass, and blue color.

quotes.quote: One is the object No.18 in Pascarelle_1996b , which is located about 40^ northwest of 53W002 and one of the spectroscopically confirmed protocluster members at z=2.393 . Its rest-UV and optical emission lines indicate the AGN contribution Pascarelle_1996a,Motohara_2001 , and it has very extended Ly emission Mawatari_2012 . The estimated stellar mass is large ( 9.4 10^ 10 \ M_ ), and its relatively blue color of J-K_s 1.1 may be affected by the AGN emission.

gmw_00010238.name: No.19
gmw_00010238.overview: No.19 is a quasar at a redshift of z=2.39 [arXiv:2109.02019]. It features a bright K-band counterpart and extended Lyman-alpha emission that may cover a nearby faint companion galaxy [arXiv:2109.02019].

sources.summary: The paper describes No.19 as a z=2.39 QSO near No.18 with a bright K-band counterpart and extended Ly emission possibly covering a faint companion galaxy.

quotes.quote: We note that another faint JHK_s selected galaxy with K_s=22.9 lies at 1 5 from LAE No.19 in Pascarelle_1996b that is located about 10^ northwest of the No.18. The No.19 is QSO at z=2.39 Pascarelle_1996a , and has a bright corresponding source in the K_s band ( K_s =20.3), which does not satisfy the JHK_s color criteria probably due to the strong AGN emission. The extended Ly emission of the No.19 Mawatari_2012 seems to cover this object, and this may be a companion of the No.19.

gmw_00010239.name: SBS1415+437
gmw_00010239.overview: SBS1415+437 is a low-mass galaxy with a stellar mass of log(M*/Msun) = 7.3 [arXiv:1412.2139]. It exhibits a mass outflow rate that is 53 times larger than its star formation rate, which will rapidly exhaust its fuel without an external gas supply [arXiv:1412.2139].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SBS1415+437 as a low-mass outlier with an exceptionally high mass-loading factor, so its outflow rate greatly exceeds its star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: SBS1415+437 is a remarkable case-study: with of 1.2 , this galaxy has = 52.6 . The mass outflow rate is 53 times larger than the SFR. Without an external gas supply, this low mass galaxy (log( ) = 7.3) will rapidly exhaust the available fuel for star-formation.

gmw_00010240.name: COOL J1241+2219
gmw_00010240.overview: COOL J1241+2219 is a strongly lensed galaxy at a redshift of z = 5.043 [arXiv:2011.06601]. It is lensed by a moderate-mass galaxy cluster and has an estimated star formation rate of 27 solar masses per year [arXiv:2011.06601].

sources.summary: The paper reports COOL J1241+2219 as a z=5.043 strongly lensed, exceptionally bright high-redshift galaxy, using imaging, spectroscopy, lens modeling, and SED fitting to infer magnification, mass, and star formation.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of COOL J1241+2219, a strongly-lensed galaxy at redshift z =5.043 0.002 with observed magnitude z_ AB =20.47 , lensed by a moderate-mass galaxy cluster at z =1.001 0.001. is the brightest lensed galaxy currently known at optical and near-infrared wavelengths at z 5; it is 5 times brighter than the prior record-holder lensed galaxy, and several magnitudes brighter than the brightest unlensed galaxies known at these redshifts. It was discovered as part of COOL-LAMPS, a collaboration initiated to find strongly lensed systems in recent public optical imaging data. We characterise the lensed galaxy, as well as the central galaxy of the lensing cluster using ground-based griz JH imaging and optical spectroscopy.

We report model-based magnitudes, and derive stellar masses, dust content, metallicity and star-formation rates via stellar-population synthesis modeling.
