Our lens mass modeling, based on ground-based imaging, implies a median source magnification of 30, which puts the stellar mass and star formation rate (in the youngest age bin, closest to the epoch of observation) at logM _ * = 10.11^ +0.21 _ 0.26 and SFR = 27^ +13 _ 9 M _ /yr, respectively. We constrain a star formation history for consistent with constant star formation across 1 Gyr of cosmic time, and that places this galaxy on the high-mass end of the star-forming main sequence.

gmw_00010241.name: MS1358-arc
gmw_00010241.overview: MS1358-arc is a star-forming galaxy at a redshift of z=4.9296 [arXiv:1210.0899]. It is gravitationally lensed by the foreground cluster MS 1358+62, revealing a rotating system with star formation occurring in five bright clumps [arXiv:1210.0899].

sources.summary: The paper describes MS1358-arc as a lensed z=4.9296 star-forming galaxy with clumpy rotation, a tentative CO(5--4) detection, a narrow molecular gas velocity range, and high gas fraction.

quotes.quote: 1997ApJ...486L..75F reported the detection of a multiply-imaged z=4.9 galaxy which is gravitationally lensed by the massive galaxy cluster MS 1358+62. Correcting for lensing, this galaxy (hereafter MS1358-arc) appears to be representative of the star-forming population at this epoch (its lensing-corrected apparent magnitude is I _ AB =24.9, marginally brighter than the characteristic luminosity of I _ AB =25.3 at z 5 ; 2004ApJ...611..660O ). hereafter S09 2009MNRAS.400.1121S carried out a detailed study of one image of the MS1358-arc system, using optical and infrared imaging combined with integral field spectroscopy, revealing a rotating system across 2 kpc in projection, with star formation occuring in five bright clumps.

We have used the IRAM Plateau de Bure Interferometer to search for the CO(5--4) emission in a z=4.9296 galaxy lensed by the foreground cluster MS1358+62. We were able to observe two images of the galaxy simultaneously, with a total magnification factor of 22 5 . We measure a line flux of 0.104 0.024 Jy km s ^ 1 at the position of the galaxy, yielding a detection at 3.5-4.3 . The molecular gas shows a relatively narrow velocity range around the systemic redshift, unlike the [ O ii ] emission, which mainly arises from two clumps in the galaxy at 150 km s ^ 1 . This may suggest that the gas is more centrally concentrated than the star formation.

gmw_00010242.name: host of Swift J1112-8238
gmw_00010242.overview: The host of Swift J1112-8238 is a galaxy at a redshift of 0.8900 [arXiv:1708.09668]. It exhibits a complex and distorted morphology with at least two spatially distinct components, suggesting that the host is undergoing an interaction with another galaxy [arXiv:1708.09668].

sources.summary: The host of Swift J1112.2-8238 is described as a redshift 0.8900, distorted two-component galaxy, likely interacting with another galaxy, with the transient near a bulge-like component.

quotes.quote: We confirm the previously determined redshift of z=0.8900 0.0005 based on multiple emission line detections. HST imaging of the host galaxy indicates a complex and distorted morphology with at least two spatially distinct components. These are offset in velocity space by less than 350 km s ^ 1 in VLT/X-Shooter observations, suggesting that the host is undergoing interaction with another galaxy. The transient position is consistent to 2.2 with the centre of a bulge-like component at a distance of 1.1 0.5 kpc from its centre.

gmw_00010243.name: 3C 040
gmw_00010243.overview: 3C 040 is a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy and a low-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:1903.10768]. It features diffuse radio plumes extending over 20 arcseconds and a compact, resolved emission-line region with rotating gas [arXiv:1903.10768].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 040 as an FR I/LEG source with diffuse radio plumes, compact resolved emission lines, and gas rotation around PA 75 degrees.

quotes.quote: 3C 040: FR I/LEG, 1 = 0.37 kpc. This source shows a FR I morphology, with diffuse radio plumes (not fully shown in Fig. 6) extending by more than 20 . Emission lines are detected in a compact but resolved region extending by 3 ( 1 kpc). Gas rotation is detected, around PA 75 ^ , with an amplitude of 300 .

gmw_00010244.name: 3C 386
gmw_00010244.overview: 3C 386 is a low-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:1604.05946] with an elliptical host [arXiv:1604.05946] and relaxed radio lobes [arXiv:1604.05946]. It features a cool X-ray-emitting gas belt [arXiv:1604.05946] and linear emission-line structures that lie perpendicular to its lobes [arXiv:1903.10768].

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 386 as a low-excitation radio galaxy with a cool X-ray gas belt, relaxed radio lobes, and likely buoyancy-driven inflow from group gas.

quotes.quote: We report measurements from an XMM-Newton observation of the low-excitation radio galaxy 3C 386. The study focusses on an X-ray-emitting gas belt, which lies between and orthogonal to the radio lobes of 3C 386 and has a mean temperature of 0.94 0.05 keV, cooler than the extended group atmosphere.

In this paper, we add a fourth source, 3C 386, to the detailed study of belted sources, using new data from XMM-Newton . We present evidence that 3C 386 does indeed exhibit behaviour that is opposite to that seen in the previously studied cases. 3C 386 3C 386 is a low-excitation radio galaxy with an elliptical host which lies around 75 Mpc ( z =0.0177) away.
When the AGN of 3C 386 was at the height of its activity, it would have been feeding the radio lobes with jets, causing the lobes to expand into the intragroup gas.

sources.summary: MUSE detects 3C 386 line emission to the field edge, forming east-west structures perpendicular to the lobes and showing central gas rotation with rising western velocities.

quotes.quote: 3C 386: FR II, 1 = 0.35 kpc. Line emission is detected out to the edges of the MUSE field of view, covering a distance of at least 16 kpc. Beside the central source, it forms linear diffuse structures in the E-W direction, perpendicular to the radio lobes. Gas rotation around PA 30 ^ is seen in the central regions. On the west side the velocity amplitude increases steadily reaching 300 .

gmw_00010245.name: And X
gmw_00010245.overview: And X is a dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy of Andromeda [arXiv:1912.02186]. Spectroscopic observations of its red giant stars have been used to constrain its velocity dispersion, dynamical mass, and bulk rotation [arXiv:1912.02186].

sources.summary: The paper includes And X among five M31 dwarf spheroidals observed with Keck/DEIMOS, measuring member-star kinematics, dynamical masses, rotation limits, metallicities, and abundance trends.

quotes.quote: We obtained spectroscopy with Keck/DEIMOS fab03 of individual red giants in five dSphs: Andromeda VII, I, III, V, and X . They are numbered in the order in which they were discovered: And I and III van72 , And V arm98 , And VII kar99 , and And X zuc07 . However, in this paper, we list them in decreasing order of stellar mass whenever they appear in a list (e.g., in tables and in figures).

We present deep spectroscopy from Keck/DEIMOS of Andromeda I, III, V, VII, and X, all of which are dwarf spheroidal satellites of M31. The sample includes \ spectroscopic members across all five dSphs. We confirm previous measurements of the velocity dispersions and dynamical masses, and we provide upper limits on bulk rotation. Our measurements confirm that M31 satellites obey the same relation between stellar mass and stellar metallicity as Milky Way (MW) satellites and other dwarf galaxies in the Local Group. The metallicity distributions show similar trends with stellar mass as MW satellites, including evidence in massive satellites for external influence, like pre-enrichment or gas accretion.

gmw_00010246.name: SDSS J112611.63+425246.4
gmw_00010246.overview: SDSS J112611.63+425246.4 is an active galactic nucleus that exhibits blueshifted Balmer absorption lines [arXiv:1410.6889]. Its narrow emission-line region displays stratified kinematics, suggesting a de-accelerated outflow in the inner region and an accelerated inflow in the outer region [arXiv:1410.6889].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J112611.63+425246.4 as a new Balmer-absorption AGN and uses host starlight to infer blueshifted Balmer absorption plus stratified NLR kinematics.

quotes.quote: We here report the identification of SDSS J112611.63+425246 as a new Balmer-absorption AGN through our spectral analysis, and study the kinematics of its narrow emission-line region (NLR). We model the continuum by a linear combination of a starlight component, a powerlaw from the central AGN and the emission from the FeII complex. After the subtraction of the modeled continuum, each emission/absorption line is profiled by a sum of multi Gaussian functions. All the line shifts are determined with respect to the modeled starlight component.

By using the host starlight as a reference for the local system, both H and H show AGN's absorptions with a blueshift of 300 km \ s^ 1 . We identify a strong anti-correlation between the inferred velocity shifts and ionization potential for various narrow emission lines, which suggests a stratified NLR kinematics. A de-accelerated outflow is implied for the inner NLR gas, while an accelerated inflow for the outer NLR gas. This complicated NLR kinematics additionally implies that AGN's narrow emission lines, even for the low-ionized lines, might not be a reliable surrogate for the velocity of the local system.

gmw_00010247.name: SDSS J105352.17+123328.5
gmw_00010247.overview: SDSS J105352.17+123328.5 is a galaxy located at coordinates RA 163.4678 and Dec +12.5581 [arXiv:2212.07628]. It is the likely host galaxy of the superluminous supernova SN 2021bnw and exhibits a negligible intrinsic degree of polarization [arXiv:2212.07628].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J105352.17+123328.5 as the likely SN 2021bnw host, gives its coordinates and magnitudes, and finds negligible intrinsic host-galaxy polarization.

quotes.quote: SN 2021bnw is very likely associated with the galaxy observed in the Dark Energy Camera Legacy Survey (DECaLS) Legacy Survey, https://www.legacysurvey.org/ , Data Release 9 (DR9) at position ( RA, Dec ) = (163.4678^ , +12.5581^ , J2000), of apparent magnitudes g=19.79, =19.31, z=19.01 mag, i.e. of absolute magnitudes -18.45, -18.93 and -19.23 mag, respectively, assuming it is at the same redshift as the SLSN at z=0.098. This galaxy has also been observed in the Sloan Digitized Sky Survey (SDSS) imaging Data Release 9 (DR9) as is identified as SDSS J105352.17+123328.5 but the photometry obtained on that source may be unreliable http://skyserver.sdss.org/dr17/VisualTools/explore/summary .

Since a contribution to the degree of polarization foreground to SN 2021bnw could also come from its host galaxy, we observed SN 2021bnw and its host a second time about one year after maximum light (+347 days), when the SN was more than 4 magnitudes fainter than its host (see Figure fig:LCandPOL , top). After bias corection, the IP and ZPA corrected measured degree of polarization, given in column 8 in Table tab:pol_results , is p=0.09 0.21 . This measurement is consistent with the first epoch measurement, and at the same level than the Galactic interstellar polarization estimated from the heiles2000 catalog in the vicinity of the system. Since the flux from the SN was negligible at this epoch, this means that the intrinsic degree of polarization of SN 2021bnw host galaxy is negligible and can be considered consistent with 0 at both epochs. This result suggests that an intrinsic null-polarization degree was measured on SN 2021bnw during the first epoch measurement (+89 days).

gmw_00010248.name: 3C 49
gmw_00010248.overview: 3C 49 is a compact steep spectrum radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.6207 [arXiv:astro-ph/0510563]. Its radio structure extends mostly east-west over 6.8 kiloparsecs, and it exhibits blueshifted H I absorption centered on its western component [arXiv:astro-ph/0510563].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C 49 as a compact steep spectrum galaxy with east-west radio structure and blueshifted H I absorption centered on its western component.

quotes.quote: 3C 49 is a CSS galaxy Spinrad85 at a redshift z=0.6207 with radio components extended mostly east-west over a total angular size of 1 arcsec or 6.8 kpc Ho = 71 km s Mpc , _M=0.27 and _ =0.73 . We detect the three components (labeled East, Center-West (CW) and West) which have been seen previously i.e. Nan91, Breugel92, Neff95, Sanghera95, Ludke98 . Multifrequency observations have shown that CW contains the flat spectrum core Sanghera95,Ludke98 .

3C 49 shows H i absorption in the center of the Western component with a peak depth of 4 of the continuum level. The reduced ^2 indicated that two Gaussian lines were needed to fit the absorption with peak depths of 1.5 and 2.1 . The column densities are 1.5 and 0.8 10^ 20 ( T_ S /100K) cm ^ 2 . The absorption seems to be blueshifted in the host galaxy rest frame.

gmw_00010249.name: MM J154506.4 344318
gmw_00010249.overview: MM J154506.4 344318 is a candidate distant submillimeter galaxy [arXiv:1506.06768]. Photometric redshift estimates suggest the galaxy is located at a redshift of approximately 4 to 5 [arXiv:1506.06768].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MM J154506.4 344318 as an extremely bright candidate distant SMG, likely at z~4-5, with SMA confirmation and a well-constrained far-infrared to millimeter SED.

quotes.quote: We report detections of two candidate distant submillimeter galaxies (SMGs), MM J154506.4 344318 and MM J154132.7 350320, which are discovered in the AzTEC/ASTE 1.1 mm survey toward the Lupus-I star-forming region. The two objects have 1.1 mm flux densities of 43.9 and 27.1 mJy, and have Herschel /SPIRE counterparts as well. The Submillimeter Array counterpart to the former SMG is identified at 890 and 1.3 mm. Photometric redshift estimates using all available data from the mid-infrared to the radio suggest that the redshifts of the two SMGs are z_ photo 4 -5 and 3, respectively.

MM-J1545 is the brightest ( S_ = 43.9 5.6 mJy) of 1400 SMGs identified through the whole AzTEC 1.1-mm galaxy surveys. SMA (890 and 1.3 mm) interferometry confirms the exact position, and photometry from VLA, ATCA, NMA, and Herschel , in addition to AzTEC/ASTE and SMA, constrains the SED well, which is in good agreement with a single gray-body with T_ dust / (1+z) = 7.1 0.3 K and = 1.4 0.1 ( sect:mmj1545 ).

gmw_00010250.name: MM J154132.7 350320
gmw_00010250.overview: MM J154132.7 350320 is a bright candidate distant submillimeter galaxy discovered in the AzTEC/ASTE 1.1 mm survey [arXiv:1506.06768]. Photometric redshift estimates suggest the galaxy is located at a redshift of approximately z ~ 3, and its spectral energy distribution is well modeled by the starburst galaxy Arp 220 [arXiv:1506.06768].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MM J154132.7 350320 as a bright candidate distant SMG, likely around z~3, with Herschel/SPIRE emission and photometric-redshift fits favoring a luminous star-forming galaxy.

quotes.quote: We report detections of two candidate distant submillimeter galaxies (SMGs), MM J154506.4 344318 and MM J154132.7 350320, which are discovered in the AzTEC/ASTE 1.1 mm survey toward the Lupus-I star-forming region. The two objects have 1.1 mm flux densities of 43.9 and 27.1 mJy, and have Herschel /SPIRE counterparts as well. The Submillimeter Array counterpart to the former SMG is identified at 890 and 1.3 mm. Photometric redshift estimates using all available data from the mid-infrared to the radio suggest that the redshifts of the two SMGs are z_ photo 4 -5 and 3, respectively.

The redshift of MM-J1541 is estimated to be z 3 , although the available photometric data are very limited. Among the templates, Arp 220 better reproduces the actual SED than the others, and the 24 detection is well accounted for by the 7.7- feature of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs). The photometric redshifts obtained the templates are z = 2.96^ +0.33 _ 0.40 (Arp 220), 3.58^ +0.54 _ 0.34 (M 82), 2.65^ +0.40 _ 0.27 (average-SMG) and 2.53^ +0.49 _ 0.42 (SMM J2135).

gmw_00010251.name: HE 0441-2826
gmw_00010251.overview: HE 0441-2826 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy with a stellar content typical of a disk galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0605288]. Although a previous near-infrared study suggested an elliptical morphology, the galaxy exhibits a young stellar population and low gas metallicity [arXiv:astro-ph/0605288].

sources.summary: The paper describes the host as a disc-like stellar system with uncertain morphology, no usable extended emission-line kinematics, low gas enrichment, and a mismatch between young stellar content and elliptical-profile claims.

quotes.quote: HE 0441-2826: Stellar content typical of disc. No information from acquisition image (saturated quasar), but radial profile pointing to elliptical morphology from a previous NIR study kuhl02 . No extended emission line suitable for radial velocity curve extraction. It is the only object with a clear mismatch between stellar population (young) and morphology (elliptical). The gas metallicity also points to a low enrichement by stars.

gmw_00010252.name: Galaxy A
gmw_00010252.overview: Galaxy A is an inclined disk galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.4721 [arXiv:1205.4037]. It exhibits strong iron and magnesium absorption, with cool gas that is entirely blueshifted relative to the star-forming disk [arXiv:1205.4037].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Galaxy A as a z=0.4721 inclined disk near 3C 336, with strong Fe II/Mg II absorption, eight modeled components, and entirely blueshifted cool gas relative to the disk.

quotes.quote: Galaxy A in the field aroud 3C 336 ( z_ QSO =0.927 ) was spectroscopically identifed at z_ gal =0.4721 0.0002 by steidel1997a . The galaxy is at projected distance =33.6 kpc from the QSO line of sight. Chen \ (1998, 2001b) analyzed available HST WFPC2 images of the field (top-right panel of Figure 2) and measured =96.8 degrees and i_0=74 degrees for the disk (Table 1).

We detect strong absorption complex in Fe II and Mg II, but not in Mn II or Mg I transitions. A Voigt profile analysis that simultaneously takes into account the observed Fe II and Mg II absorption profiles yields a minimum of eight individual absorption components and ^2_r=1.1 . The total rest-frame Mg II absorption equivalent width over all observed components is =0.8 0.1 . The absorbing clumps display relative line-of-sight motions ranging from v_ los =-54.8 \ to v_ los =-143.7 \ with respect to the systemic redshift of the galaxy. Figure 2 shows that the cool gas probed by the Mg II absorption transitions is entirely blueshifted with respect to the star-forming disk with a total line-of-sight velocity spread of 90 .

gmw_00010253.name: Galaxy B
gmw_00010253.overview: Galaxy B is an inclined disk galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.8909 [arXiv:1205.4037]. Its circumgalactic medium exhibits strong Fe II and Mg II absorption, alongside weak Mn II and Mg I absorption features distributed across at least 14 individual velocity components [arXiv:1205.4037].

sources.summary: The paper treats Galaxy B as a z=0.8909 inclined disk near 3C 336, detecting strong Fe II/Mg II absorption, weak Mn II/Mg I absorption, and 14 velocity components around the galaxy.

quotes.quote: Galaxy B in the field aroud 3C 336 ( z_ QSO =0.927 ) was spectroscopically identifed at z_ gal =0.8909 0.0002 by steidel1997a . The galaxy is at projected distance =23.3 kpc from the QSO line of sight. Chen \ (1998, 2001b) analyzed available HST WFPC2 images of the field (top-right panel of Figure 4) and measured =124.1 degrees and i_0=81 degrees for the disk (Table 1).

We detect strong absorption complex in Fe II and Mg II transitions. Mn II and Mg I absorption features are also detected, but are weak. A Voigt profile analysis that takes into account all the observed absorption transitions yields a minimum of 14 individual absorption components and ^2_r=1.1 . The total rest-frame Mg II absorption equivalent width over all observed components is =1.53 0.05 . The absorbing clumps display relative line-of-sight motions ranging from v_ los =+42.2 \ to v_ los =-147.2 \ with respect to the systemic redshift of the galaxy.

gmw_00010254.name: Galaxy D
gmw_00010254.overview: Galaxy D is a nearly edge-on disk galaxy located at a redshift of 0.199 [arXiv:1205.4037]. High-resolution imaging reveals a clear dust lane along its disk, yet the galaxy shows no evidence of large-scale outflows reaching a projected distance of 48 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1205.4037].

sources.summary: The paper describes Galaxy D as a nearly edge-on z=0.199 disk near Q 0827+243 with no detected absorption, implying absent, narrow, or short-reaching large-scale outflows despite its dust lane.

quotes.quote: Galaxy D in the field aroud Q 0827+243 ( z_ QSO =0.939 ) was spectroscopically identifed at z_ gal =0.199 by Steidel \ (2002). The nearly edge-on galaxy is at projected distance =48 kpc from the QSO line of sight. We analyzed available HST images of the field and estimated i_0=85 degrees and =86.3 degrees.

However, we detect no trace of absorption features at the redshift of the galaxy in the QSO spectrum (Figure 8). We determine a 3- upper limit to the Mg II absorption strength of W_r(2796)=0.01 . The lack of absorption features associated with Galaxy D at z_ gal =0.199 implies that large-scale galactic outflows may not exist in this galaxy, despite a clear dust lane feature along the disk in the high-resolution HST image (upper right panel of Figure 8). Alternatively, the opening angle of the outflows may be small, _0 4 degrees, or outflows from the star-forming disk do not reach out to 50 kpc.

gmw_00010255.overview: The galaxy 450.25 is undergoing an early-stage merger or interaction, as evidenced by tidal tails and disordered kinematics [arXiv:2003.00016]. Despite this merging activity, the galaxy exhibits an unexpectedly low star formation rate that lies below the main sequence [arXiv:2003.00016].

sources.summary: The paper finds 450.25 is an early-stage merger or interaction with tidal tails, poor Sersic fitting, disturbed kinematics, disk-region Gini-M20 values, and unexpectedly low main-sequence SFR.

quotes.quote: Rest-frame UV and optical imaging of galaxy 450.25 shows tidal tails, which are clear evidence it is undergoing a merger or interaction. The rest-frame optical emission is not well fit by a S ersic profile because the best-fit parameter values are unphysical. This is due to warps caused by the interaction/merger. Position-velocity and position-dispersion diagrams reveal disorder characteristic of a merger. The Gini coefficient and M _ 20 values, G=0.534 0.006 and M _ 20 = -1.725 , place 450.25 in the disk region of the Gini-M _ 20 diagram, possibly because this is an early stage merger or interaction.

Despite merging activity, 450.27 lies within the scatter of the MS, and 450.25 lies 4 below the MS. It is unexpected that a merging galaxy has a lower star formation rate than typical galaxies on the main sequence.

gmw_00010256.overview: The galaxy 450.27 is identified as a likely merger near coalescence [arXiv:2003.00016]. Rest-frame ultraviolet imaging reveals two distinct star-forming knots [arXiv:2003.00016], and its Gini and M20 values place the system within the merger region of the Gini-M20 diagram [arXiv:2003.00016].

sources.summary: The paper interprets 450.27 as a likely merger near coalescence, with two UV star-forming knots, poor Sersic fitting, asymmetric dispersion, and merger-region Gini-M20 values.

quotes.quote: Rest-frame UV imaging of galaxy 450.27 shows two distinct star forming knots which we conclude are likely two galaxy cores close to coalescence. Rest-frame optical imaging shows one distinct emission region rather than two knots, although it is observed through seeing with a FWHM comparable to the separation between the two UV knots. The rest-frame optical emission is not well fit by a S ersic profile because the best-fit parameter values are unphysical. The position-velocity diagram looks Keplerian while the position-dispersion diagram looks asymmetric in the magnitude of velocity dispersion on each side of the galaxy. The Gini coefficient and M _ 20 values, G=0.633^ +0.007 _ 0.005 and M _ 20 = -1.808 , place 450.27 in the merger region of the Gini-M _ 20 diagram.

Galaxy 450.27 lies within the merger region, while galaxies 450.25 and 850.95 lie within the disk region. Our kinematic and morphological analyses conclude 450.25 is a merger, 850.95 is a disk, and 450.27 is possibly a merger, although it is a bit ambiguous. Considering this together with the location of 450.27 in the Gini-M _ 20 diagram, we conclude that it is indeed a merger.

gmw_00010257.name: JADES-NS-GS-10013609
gmw_00010257.overview: JADES-NS-GS-10013609 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy [arXiv:2311.18731]. It exhibits a secured high-ionization [Ne V] 3427 emission line detection, with line-ratio lower limits that lie on the border of active galactic nuclei photoionization models [arXiv:2311.18731].

sources.summary: The paper reports a secured high-ionization 3427 detection for JADES-NS-GS-10013609, no solid 1640 counterpart, and line-ratio lower limits at the edge of AGN photoionization models.

quotes.quote: We detect 3427 and NV 1240 in the objects JADES-NS-GS-10013609 and JADES-NS-GS-00021842, respectively. In both cases, the detections are secured at SNR 4.5. However, the objects do not have solid detections of either 1640 or . The non-detections of put a lower limit on the log( 3420/ ) or log( 1240/ ) of > 0. According to Feltre16 , this lower limit is on the border of what can be reasonably predicted by AGN photo-ionisation models.

gmw_00010258.name: JADES-NS-GS-00021842
gmw_00010258.overview: JADES-NS-GS-00021842 is a high-redshift galaxy with a systemic redshift of 7.980 [arXiv:2306.00487]. It is classified as an active galactic nucleus based on the detection of nitrogen emission lines [arXiv:2311.18731], and it exhibits blueshifted Lyman-alpha emission indicative of a large surrounding ionized bubble [arXiv:2306.00487].

sources.summary: The paper identifies JADES-NS-GS-00021842 as an AGN after detecting NV 1240, finding no solid 1640 counterpart, and rejecting a star-formation explanation as inconsistent with its ionization parameter.

quotes.quote: We detect 3427 and NV 1240 in the objects JADES-NS-GS-10013609 and JADES-NS-GS-00021842, respectively. In both cases, the detections are secured at SNR 4.5. However, the objects do not have solid detections of either 1640 or . The non-detections of put a lower limit on the log( 3420/ ) or log( 1240/ ) of > 0. According to Feltre16 , this lower limit is on the border of what can be reasonably predicted by AGN photo-ionisation models.

The 1240 detection can be potentially explained by star formation, assuming a very high logU=-0.5, which is inconsistent with that measured from and \ (logU=-2). As such, we identify JADES-NS-GS-00021842 as an AGN.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts 00021842 with CEERS2 588, finding blueshifted flux beyond Ly and modeling its sharp break with Ly emission, low IGM absorption, and a large ionized bubble.

quotes.quote: The 2D and 1D spectra of CEERS2 588 (00021842) with systemic redshift at 11.04 (7.980) is shown in the left (right) panel. The red dotted lines represent the systemic redshift determined by emission line. The spectra of CEERS2 588 shows redshifted, softened Ly break relative to systemic redshift, while we see blueshifted flux with 4.2 significance beyond the rest-frame 1216 for 00021842.

In contrast, the sharp and blue-shifted break feature at the rest-frame 1216 for 00021842 can be explained with Ly emission together with high Ly transmission environment realized with small IGM absorption (i.e., x_ 0 ) and a large ionized bubble (i.e., R_b/ cMpc >1 ).

gmw_00010259.name: GP15
gmw_00010259.overview: GP15 is a Green Pea galaxy identified as a Lyman continuum leaker with high Lyman-alpha and Lyman continuum escape fractions [arXiv:2309.09585]. Driven by an intense starburst, it exhibits extreme emission-line ratios and nebular helium emission without Wolf-Rayet stellar features [arXiv:2309.09585].

sources.summary: The paper describes GP15 as a Green Pea LyC leaker with high Ly and LyC escape fractions, extreme ionization-line ratios, and detected nebular HeII 4686 without WR features.

quotes.quote: The GP15 galaxy is identified as an LyC leaker exhibiting an escape fraction for LyC, f_ esc (LyC) = 0.08 izotov2016detection and for Ly , f_ esc (Ly ) = 0.51 jaskot2017kinematics . This aligns with the understanding that the most intense starburst events typically generate a higher quantity of ionizing photons. Furthermore, this galaxy present among the most extremes line ratios favoring escape of ionizing photons (high [ O iii ] / [ O ii ] and low [ S ii ] /H and [ N ii ] /H ) see Table. table:gas_properties .

We detected the nebular HeII 4686 line in the galaxies GP06, GP15 (this particular galaxy being a confirmed LyC leaker izotov2016detection ), and GP20, indicating the presence of very hard ionizing photons (E > 4Ry). We checked that none of these GPs show WR features in their spectra, which suggests that WR stars are not the main HeII excitation contributors e.g., senchyna2017ultraviolet,kehrig2015extended,kehrig2018extended . We also note that two of these GPs present among the highest sSFR (> 8 10^8 yr^ 1 ), suggesting that, besides a low metallicity, high sSFR can be a dominant factor to determine the HeII emitting nature of a galaxy kehrig2020mapping,perez2020photon .

gmw_00010260.name: BCG of Abell 3526
gmw_00010260.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy of Abell 3526 is a double-nucleus galaxy containing an unresolved central peak [arXiv:astro-ph/0211074]. This point-source nucleus is associated with active galactic nucleus emission, and the galaxy features a dust lane wrapping around its center that suggests a recent gas-rich galaxy infall [arXiv:astro-ph/0211074].

sources.summary: The paper describes the BCG of Abell 3526 as a double-nucleus brightest cluster galaxy with an unresolved central peak and a dust lane attributed to recent gas-rich infall.

quotes.quote: By contrast, in the BCG of Abell 3526 (the Centaurus Cluster) one of the peaks is unresolved. Such point source nuclei are generally due to optical emission from an AGN component, as discussed in Section  ss:pointnuclei below. Abell 3526 also has a spectacular dust lane that wraps around the center (see Figure  f:dust ). This has been interpreted as evidence for a recent infall of a gas-rich galaxy into the BCG spa89 . Such an event may also explain the double nucleus.

gmw_00010261.name: NGC 4452
gmw_00010261.overview: NGC 4452 is an edge-on lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1811.00018, arXiv:2006.14896] that features a tiny pseudo-bulge and five distinct stellar components [arXiv:1811.00018]. The galaxy exhibits a warped, tilted outer disk [arXiv:1811.00018, arXiv:2006.14896] that likely originated from a past fast tidal encounter with IC 3381 [arXiv:2006.14896].

sources.summary: The paper describes VCC 1125 as an edge-on lenticular with a tiny pseudo-bulge, warped outer disk from encounters, five stellar components, and 5 Gyr mean age.

quotes.quote: VCC 1125 (NGC 4452). This is an edge-on lenticular galaxy. 2012ApJS..198....2K showed that the galaxy has an extremely low pseudo-bulge-to-total luminosity ratio ( =0.017 0.004 ) and a warped outer disk that seems to stem from gravitational encounters. They also showed that the galaxy consists of five stellar components like VCC 2095. The luminosity-weighted mean age using higher-order Balmer absorption lines is 5 Gyr 2003AJ....125.2891C .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4452 as an SB0 edge-on galaxy with a discy tilted outer structure, likely caused by a past fast tidal encounter with IC 3381.

quotes.quote: We consider two SB0 edge-on galaxies, NGC 4469 and NGC 4452, which exhibit apparent tilted outer discs with respect to the inner structure. In NGC 4469, this structure has a boxy shape, inclined by PA 3 with respect to the inner disc, whereas NGC 4452 harbours a discy outer structure with PA 6 . In spite of the different shapes, both structures have surface brightness profiles close to exponential and make a large contribution ( 30 ) to the total galaxy luminosity.
In the case of NGC 4452, we propose that its tilted disc likely originates from a former fast tidal encounter (probably with IC 3381).

gmw_00010262.name: J1420 0545
gmw_00010262.overview: J1420 0545 is a giant radio galaxy with a size exceeding 1 Mpc [arXiv:1711.05200]. It features two edge-brightened, narrow, highly collinear, and symmetric lobes that propagate into a particularly low-density external medium [arXiv:1711.05200].

sources.summary: The paper tests whether J1420 0545, the largest radio galaxy, formed through restarted jet activity, using its narrow symmetric lobes, low-frequency excess emission, and low-density lobe environment.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we test the possibility that the structure of the largest radio galaxy may have been formed by restarted rather than primary jet activity. This hypothesis was motivated by the unusual morphological properties of the source consisting of two edge-brightened, narrow, highly collinear, and symmetric lobes, thus suggesting an almost ballistic propagation of powerful jets into a particularly low-density external medium. New observations made with the together with the currently available and data releases allow the detection of an excess emission at low frequencies. An extracted part (88 MHz -- 200 MHz) of the spectrum of the emission is fitted with the model, giving a forecast for the environmental conditions and the energetic requirements for the presumed old cocoon related to a preceding epoch of jet activity. galaxies: active --- galaxies: jets --- galaxies: individual (J1420 0545) The largest size ( 1 Mpc) radio galaxies are believed to have evolved from smaller sources; however, it is almost certain that most of the observed small sources will never evolve into a giant sizes.
On the other hand, the models presented here and in MJSK provide similar very low densities of the gas surrounding the hypothetical primary lobes of J1420 0545 ( _ out 10^ 29 g cm ^ 3 ).

In the case of J1420 0545, proper X-ray observations and eventual detection of radial surface brightness and gas density profiles in the vicinity of its host galaxy would be crucial for discriminating between the two possible origins of the observed radio structure: (i) repeated periods of jet activity, or (ii) a unique (primary) episode of the jet activity appearing in a deep void of the IGM at the outskirts of a filamentary Warm-Hot IGM (WHIM).

gmw_00010263.name: NVSS J080518 + 614423
gmw_00010263.overview: NVSS J080518 + 614423 is a high-redshift flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar located at a redshift of z = 3.033 [arXiv:1710.02904, arXiv:2402.09908]. It features a compact core-jet structure with superluminal, bending jet components [arXiv:2402.09908] and exhibits intraday gamma-ray variability [arXiv:1710.02904].

sources.summary: The paper finds NVSS J080518+614423 is a z=3.033 radio blazar detected by WISE and Swift BAT, with two gamma-ray flares and evidence for intraday variability.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: the optimized ray position at this time is R.A. 121.25 ^ and DEC. 61.73 ^ with 95 C.L. error radius of 0.12 ^

quotes.quote: NVSS J080518+614423 ( z =3.033, 2005ApJ...626...95S ) was also firstly known as radio emitters 1991ApJS...75....1B , then its optical counterpart was identified 2002MNRAS.329..700S . Similar with NVSS J053954 283956, it has been detected by WISE and Swift BAT 2012ApJ...748...68D,2013ApJS..207...19B . In its monthly ray light curve, after two flares (i.e. flare-A and flare-B) in the first and second year of Fermi observation, it maintains at a low flux state for several years.

More importantly, an evidence of intraday ray variability is found from the 3-day time bin ray light curve of flare-A, of which the variability index is given as _ var = 10.8 . But no similar behavior can be found in flare-B due to large uncertainties, also see Figure fig:lca-15-3 . In consideration of the ascent time of 6 days, together with the variability amplitude of 3.5, the intrinsic doubling timescale in flare-A can be estimated as _ doub,source 19.6-hr. Note that the 3-day peaking flux ( (3.9 0.7) 10^ 7 ph cm^ 2 s^ 1 ) is roughly 15 times of the 105 months averaged flux.

sources.summary: The paper targets NVSS J080518 + 614423 as a bright z = 3.033 FSRQ, gives its ICRF3 coordinates, and measures superluminal, bending VLBI jet components.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: right ascension _ VLBI =08^ h 05^ m 18 179562 and declination _ VLBI =61 44 23 70056

quotes.quote: To expand this limited sample in order to work towards improving the statistical constraints, we specifically targeted two FSRQs, NVSS J080518 + 614423 (J0805 + 6144 in short, z = 3.033 , 2005ApJ...626...95S ) and NVSS J165844 073918 (J1658 0739, z = 3.742 , 2008ApJS..175...97H ) because they are among the brightest high-redshift blazars accessible to VLBI imaging. Their flat radio spectra and compact mas-scale core--jet structures made them suitable for multi-epoch model-fitting analysis to measure jet kinematics. Both can be found among the targets of geodetic and astrometric VLBI observations 2017JGeod..91..711N , but they have not been the subjects of dedicated VLBI monitoring. J0805 + 6144, with coordinates in the 3rd realization of the International Celestial Reference Frame (ICRF3, 2020A&A...644A.159C ) right ascension _ VLBI =08^ h 05^ m 18 179562 and declination _ VLBI =61 44 23 70056 , is also known as a ray source in the Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT) catalogues 2010ApJS..188..405A,2015ApJS..218...23A .

For J0805 + 6144, the apparent speeds of the innermost two jet components, J1 ( _ J1 ^ J0805 = 4.5 0.9 ) and J2 ( _ J2 ^ J0805 = 6.3 1.0 ), are superluminal and consistent with each other within the uncertainties. The trajectory of J1 seems to follow that of J2, its position angle changes from 190 to 160 while travelling up to 2 mas projected distance from the core (Fig. fig:J0805+6144-pm ). From that point on, the bending jet becomes remarkably straight up to 8 mas separation.

gmw_00010264.name: COSMOS 0018+3845
gmw_00010264.overview: COSMOS 0018+3845 is an early-type lens galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 0.9755 [arXiv:1009.1545]. The galaxy acts as a gravitational lens for a background source estimated to be at a redshift between 3.9 and 4.1 [arXiv:1009.1545].

sources.summary: The paper reports a DEIMOS spectrum for COSMOS 0018+3845, identifies its lens redshift, constrains the source redshift, and flags biased photometric redshift from lensed-image contamination.

quotes.quote: Using the Deep Extragalactic Imaging Multi-Object Spectrograph (DEIMOS) a spectrum of COSMOS 0018+3845 has been obtained on February 11, 2010 (Fig.  deimos_spec ). The data were collected in 7 1800s exposures under photometric conditions with 0.5-0.7 seeing. We used a 1 slit together with the 830 line/mm grating tilted to 7860 and the OG550 blocking filter. The resulting spectral resolution is <3.3 , depending on seeing and object morphology.

The redshift of the lens has been identified using the CaII H K absorption lines and G-band. The source redshift is estimated based on the Lyman break which is somewhere in the Subaru IA624-band ( _ effective =6226  , FWHM=299 ), putting constraints on the source redshift: 3.9 < z _s< 4.1.

We notice that three systems show a strongly deviant photometric redshift (COSMOS 0254+1430: z _ phot =0.46 0.01 and z _ spec =0.417 0.001; J095930.93+023427.7: z _ phot =1.10 ^ +0.02 _ 0.00 and z _ spec =0.892 0.001; COSMOS 0018+3845: z _ phot =0.44 0.01 and z _ spec =0.9755 0.0003). In those three cases, the photometry of the lens galaxy obtained from ground-based data is contaminated by the close and bright images of the source, and the photometric redshift determination is consequently biased.

gmw_00010265.name: ESO 467-G051
gmw_00010265.overview: ESO 467-G051 is an edge-on disk galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.006 [arXiv:1811.11771]. It has an estimated stellar mass of approximately 10^9 solar masses and a star formation rate of 0.07 solar masses per year [arXiv:1811.11771].

sources.summary: The paper studies ESO 467-G051 as ASASSN-14jb's edge-on Scd host, measuring its stellar mass, age, star-formation rate, oxygen abundance, H I content, and interaction evidence.

quotes.quote: This supernova, in the nearby galaxy ESO 467-G051 ( z=0.006 ), was discovered and followed-up by the All Sky Automated Survey for SuperNovae (ASAS-SN). We obtained well-sampled LCOGTN BVgri and Swift w2m1w1ubv optical and near-UV/optical light curves and several optical spectra in the early photospheric phases. ASASSN-14jb exploded 2  kpc above the star-forming disk of ESO 467-G051, an edge-on disk galaxy.

The stellar mass, age and SFR of ESO 467-G051 can be estimated from these data. We fitted stellar population synthesis (SPS) models to the FUV , NUV , BVgri , K_s , 3.6  m, and 4.5  m magnitudes using FAST kriek09 . We assumed a Galactic extinction law, a Salpeter IMF, an exponentially declining star formation law ( SFR e^ t/ , with = 1  Gyr), and the SPS models. The best-fit model provides the following parameters: M_ 1 10^ 9  M _ , age 3.2  Gyr, and SFR 0.07  M _ /yr, where the uncertainties are 1 . The SFR can be also estimated from the H extinction-corrected fluxes see below, kennicutt98 , which provides SFR(H_ ) 0.07  M _ /yr.

gmw_00010266.name: NGC 2964
gmw_00010266.overview: NGC 2964 is a galaxy featuring a well-developed two-arm spiral structure and a pseudo-ring of concentrated HII regions [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622]. It possesses a possible small bar and a nearby companion, NGC 2968, though it shows no clear signs of interaction [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2964 has a two-arm spiral structure, a pseudo-ring, a possible small bar, and a south-side anomalous region with strong non-circular H velocities.

quotes.quote: The galaxy possesses a well-developed two-arm spiral structure; HII regions at =30 35 are concentrated to a pseudo-ring (Figs. ngc2964 a and ngc2964 b). It has a close companion, NGC 2968, at the distance of 5 8 , but there are no evident signatures of interaction of galaxies. At =1 5 the kinematic major axis (Fig. ngc2964 d) turns abruptly by more than 15 , which can be assumed to be the effect of a small bar (note that there is a remark about the ``very faint bar'' in RC1).

There is a region between the nucleus and a spiral arm, at =5 12 to the south of the nucleus, where H emission-line profiles are clearly two-component ( Fig. fig_prof i). The main line component traces the mean rotation; the secondary component is redshifted by 150 , sometimes being as strong as the main one. At the outskirts of this region the H profiles often possess a red wing, so at the residual velocity map the whole area located between bright HII regions is distinguished by strong non-circular motion reaching 50 - 70 .

gmw_00010267.name: NGC 4814
gmw_00010267.overview: NGC 4814 is a galaxy featuring a gaseous disk where the main fraction of H-alpha emission is confined to the inner disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622]. Beyond a radius of 50 arcseconds, this emission is concentrated within a pair of spiral arms [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4814 has inner-disk H emission, spiral-arm emission beyond 50 arcseconds, a rotation curve matching earlier data, and residual velocities west of the nucleus.

quotes.quote: The main fraction of the H emission in this galaxy is confined to the inner disk having a radius of 50 ; beyond this radius the emission regions are concentrated in a pair of spiral arms (Fig. 13b). A mean rotation curve (Fig. ngc4814 d) agrees well with the rotation curve of the inner region, <30 , we obtained from the earlier long-slit observations at the 6m telescope (Zasov \ Sil'chenko 1987; Afanasiev et al. 1988b). A turn of the kinematic major axis at <15 may be related to a small bar, but it is not accompanied by the significant continuum isophote turn. Afanasiev et al. (1988b) reported non-circular gas motion to the west of the nucleus, at 20 . After subtracting the mean rotation field from our velocity data, we have found that the residual velocities in this region are about 40 , evidently produced by an effect of the spiral pattern on the kinematics of the gaseous disk.

gmw_00010268.name: P11695
gmw_00010268.overview: P11695 is an isolated asymmetric spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.04648 [arXiv:1802.07296]. The galaxy is characterized by a small U-shaped warp and trails of bright knots across its disk [arXiv:1802.07296].

sources.summary: The paper presents P11695 as an isolated asymmetric spiral whose lopsided disk, young western component, HII knots, metallicity gradients, and gas kinematics point to cold gas accretion.

quotes.quote: This gives a scale of 0.912 kpc/ ^ at the redshift of P11695, which is z = 0.04648 . Focus of this analysis is (RA: 10:46:14.78, DEC: +00:03:01.5), a galaxy at z= 0.04648 drawn from the Millennium Galaxy Catalog Liske2003,Driver2005 . Figure fig:rgb_image shows a color composite image obtained combining the reconstructed g , and i filters from the MUSE datacube. It appears evident that is an asymmetric spiral galaxy possessing trails of bright knots across the disk. The galaxy seems also to be characterized by a small U-shaped warp.

The environment does not belong to any recognizable cluster or group: it has been selected as isolated from both our own environmental catalog Calvi2011 and an independent catalog assembled by . Paccagnella et al. (in prep) estimate that it is embedded in a halo of mass M_ =3.9 10^ 11 M_ . Figure fig:env shows the spatial distribution of all galaxies around P11695 with measured redshift in the range 0.025<z<0.07 .

gmw_00010269.name: AGC 248945
gmw_00010269.overview: AGC 248945 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy that is only marginally resolved in neutral hydrogen observations [arXiv:2101.01753]. The galaxy exhibits a very regular velocity gradient [arXiv:2101.01753] and a very low gas velocity dispersion [arXiv:2004.14392].

sources.summary: The paper says AGC 248945 is only marginally resolved in H I, has low recovered VLA flux, a regular velocity gradient, and an uncertain H I peak position.

quotes.quote: In the \ maps, most sources are resolved with 3 or 4 independent beams across the full extent of detected \ emission. Beam sizes range from 14.5 \ to 18.1 \ in major axis, which corresponds to physical resolutions from 4.6 kpc to 9.1 kpc at the distances of the sources (details listed in Table table:observations and table:HIproperties ). Sources AGC 238764 and AGC 248945 are only marginally resolved, with measured \ semi-major axes at 1 pc ^ 2 equal to or less than the beam size, and with only 2 beams across the full detected extent of the galaxies. These two sources are also the only two sources with recovered fluxes F_ VLA /F_ ALFA well below 1 (0.55 and 0.59 respectively).

We see velocity gradients and evidence of ordered rotation in the moment 1 maps for all of the sources. While there is little evidence for irregular motions in their \ gas, some sources (e.g., AGC 248945 and AGC 334315) have very regular gradients, while others (e.g., AGC 749251 and AGC 748738) show gradients that are somewhat less regular, though the low resolution of our images does not allow for a more detailed analysis. In most cases, the orientation of the velocity gradient appears aligned along the \ major axis, though, in three cases (AGC 229110, AGC 749251, AGC 749290), there is a moderate offset in measured position angles based on the kinematics and the morphology (see Section sec:discussionclin ).

sources.summary: The paper models AGC 248945 using two H I resolution elements, finds very low gas velocity dispersion, and places it within the relation scatter in the M-j plane.

quotes.quote: AGC 248945: Beam size of 18.10" 14.11", with a position angle of 57.3 ^ . The projected beam radius along the kinematic major axis of the galaxy is 14.7". Given the extension of the H i emission we adopt RADSEP = 14.5", ending up with two resolution elements.
The mean velocity dispersion for AGC 114905, AGC 219533, AGC 248945 and AGC 749290, with a channel width of _ v 4 km s ^ 1 , is = 3 2 km s ^ 1 , which is below _ v .
Three of our galaxies (AGC 114905, AGC 248945 and AGC 749290) lie within the 1 scatter of the relation.

gmw_00010270.name: AGC 238764
gmw_00010270.overview: AGC 238764 is an ultra-diffuse galaxy containing neutral hydrogen [arXiv:2101.01753]. It is marginally resolved in radio observations, which show a low recovered flux and an uncertain peak column-density position relative to its stellar population [arXiv:2101.01753].

sources.summary: The paper treats AGC 238764 as a marginally resolved H I-bearing ultra-diffuse galaxy with low recovered VLA flux and an uncertain peak column-density position.

quotes.quote: In the \ maps, most sources are resolved with 3 or 4 independent beams across the full extent of detected \ emission. Beam sizes range from 14.5 \ to 18.1 \ in major axis, which corresponds to physical resolutions from 4.6 kpc to 9.1 kpc at the distances of the sources (details listed in Table table:observations and table:HIproperties ). Sources AGC 238764 and AGC 248945 are only marginally resolved, with measured \ semi-major axes at 1 pc ^ 2 equal to or less than the beam size, and with only 2 beams across the full detected extent of the galaxies. These two sources are also the only two sources with recovered fluxes F_ VLA /F_ ALFA well below 1 (0.55 and 0.59 respectively).

In many cases the centroid of the stellar population is aligned with the peak in the \ column density, but there are several exceptions, including AGC 114905, AGC 238764, and AGC 248945. However, we note that AGC 238764 and AGC 248945 are less well resolved and lower signal-to-noise detections than the other sources in the sample, so the position of their peak column density is somewhat less well defined. Importantly, we note that we only poorly constrain the galaxies' inclinations using the stellar populations.

gmw_00010271.name: AGC 334315
gmw_00010271.overview: AGC 334315 is an ultra-diffuse galaxy that exhibits optical arc-like features reminiscent of spiral arms [arXiv:2101.01753]. The galaxy displays a highly regular neutral hydrogen velocity gradient [arXiv:2101.01753] and possesses a specific angular momentum three to four times larger than the best-fit relation [arXiv:2004.14392].

sources.summary: The paper describes AGC 334315 as an ultra-diffuse galaxy with a very regular H I velocity gradient and optical arc-like features reminiscent of spiral arms.

quotes.quote: We see velocity gradients and evidence of ordered rotation in the moment 1 maps for all of the sources. While there is little evidence for irregular motions in their \ gas, some sources (e.g., AGC 248945 and AGC 334315) have very regular gradients, while others (e.g., AGC 749251 and AGC 748738) show gradients that are somewhat less regular, though the low resolution of our images does not allow for a more detailed analysis. In most cases, the orientation of the velocity gradient appears aligned along the \ major axis, though, in three cases (AGC 229110, AGC 749251, AGC 749290), there is a moderate offset in measured position angles based on the kinematics and the morphology (see Section sec:discussionclin ).

Two sources, AGC 334315 and AGC 749251, have some arc-like features reminiscent of spiral arms, but without distinct patterns or strong evidence of a stellar disk. Three sources, AGC 114905, AGC 748738, and AGC 749290, have observed optical components that loosely resemble stellar disks in our optical images, but still would be better classified as irregular galaxies at the detected level. Also, one source, AGC 219533, has a bright, very blue higher surface brightness clump superimposed on its low surface brightness emission; it is not fully clear if it is a bright star forming region associated with the galaxy, or if it is a foreground or background source.

sources.summary: The paper models AGC 334315 with two independent resolution elements, adopts a 7 km/s gas velocity dispersion, and finds specific angular momentum 3-4 times above the best-fit relation.

quotes.quote: AGC 334315: The galaxy has a beam size of 15.83" 13.94", oriented at 65^ . Along the major axis of the galaxy, the projected size of the beam is 14". We use two independent resolution elements with RADSEP = 16".
For AGC 334315, with the same _ v 4 km s ^ 1 , we find = 7 2 km s ^ 1 , and we adopt this value.
AGC 219533 and AGC 334315 have a j_ about 3--4 times larger than the best-fit line, although the observational scatter is relatively large at those values of M_* .

gmw_00010272.name: AGC 749251
gmw_00010272.overview: AGC 749251 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy that exhibits arc-like optical features reminiscent of spiral arms [arXiv:2101.01753]. It displays a somewhat less regular neutral hydrogen velocity gradient, along with a moderate offset between its kinematic and morphological position angles [arXiv:2101.01753].

sources.summary: The paper describes AGC 749251 as having less regular H I velocity gradients, a kinematic-morphological position-angle offset, and arc-like optical features.

quotes.quote: We see velocity gradients and evidence of ordered rotation in the moment 1 maps for all of the sources. While there is little evidence for irregular motions in their \ gas, some sources (e.g., AGC 248945 and AGC 334315) have very regular gradients, while others (e.g., AGC 749251 and AGC 748738) show gradients that are somewhat less regular, though the low resolution of our images does not allow for a more detailed analysis. In most cases, the orientation of the velocity gradient appears aligned along the \ major axis, though, in three cases (AGC 229110, AGC 749251, AGC 749290), there is a moderate offset in measured position angles based on the kinematics and the morphology (see Section sec:discussionclin ).

Two sources, AGC 334315 and AGC 749251, have some arc-like features reminiscent of spiral arms, but without distinct patterns or strong evidence of a stellar disk. Three sources, AGC 114905, AGC 748738, and AGC 749290, have observed optical components that loosely resemble stellar disks in our optical images, but still would be better classified as irregular galaxies at the detected level. Also, one source, AGC 219533, has a bright, very blue higher surface brightness clump superimposed on its low surface brightness emission; it is not fully clear if it is a bright star forming region associated with the galaxy, or if it is a foreground or background source.

gmw_00010273.name: AGC 748738
gmw_00010273.overview: AGC 748738 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy with an optical component that loosely resembles a stellar disk [arXiv:2101.01753]. Despite this disk-like appearance, it is classified as an irregular galaxy and exhibits a somewhat less regular neutral hydrogen velocity gradient [arXiv:2101.01753].

sources.summary: The paper says AGC 748738 has a somewhat less regular H I velocity gradient and an optical component resembling a disk but still best classified as irregular.

quotes.quote: We see velocity gradients and evidence of ordered rotation in the moment 1 maps for all of the sources. While there is little evidence for irregular motions in their \ gas, some sources (e.g., AGC 248945 and AGC 334315) have very regular gradients, while others (e.g., AGC 749251 and AGC 748738) show gradients that are somewhat less regular, though the low resolution of our images does not allow for a more detailed analysis. In most cases, the orientation of the velocity gradient appears aligned along the \ major axis, though, in three cases (AGC 229110, AGC 749251, AGC 749290), there is a moderate offset in measured position angles based on the kinematics and the morphology (see Section sec:discussionclin ).

Two sources, AGC 334315 and AGC 749251, have some arc-like features reminiscent of spiral arms, but without distinct patterns or strong evidence of a stellar disk. Three sources, AGC 114905, AGC 748738, and AGC 749290, have observed optical components that loosely resemble stellar disks in our optical images, but still would be better classified as irregular galaxies at the detected level. Also, one source, AGC 219533, has a bright, very blue higher surface brightness clump superimposed on its low surface brightness emission; it is not fully clear if it is a bright star forming region associated with the galaxy, or if it is a foreground or background source.

gmw_00010274.name: AGC 749290
gmw_00010274.overview: AGC 749290 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2004.14392, arXiv:2101.01753] that is classified as an irregular galaxy despite having a disk-like optical morphology [arXiv:2101.01753]. It exhibits a moderate offset between its kinematic and morphological position angles [arXiv:2101.01753], with circular speed and velocity dispersion values typical of similar dwarf systems [arXiv:2004.14392].

sources.summary: The paper oversamples AGC 749290 to model two resolution elements per side, flags its kinematic parameters as less certain, but finds velocities and dispersion similar to the sample.

quotes.quote: AGC 749290: Beam size of 21.63" 17.88", oriented at 61^ . The projected radius of the beam along the major axis of the galaxy is 21.4". Given the extension of the galaxy we oversample by a factor 1.7 to get two resolution elements per galaxy side, with RADSEP = 12", although the resulting two rings are not independent. Because of this, the kinematic parameters of this galaxy are less certain than for the rest of our sample, and we plot the galaxy as an empty symbol when using its kinematic parameters. Nevertheless, we note that the specific values of its circular speed and velocity dispersion are similar to the values of the rest of the sample.
The mean velocity dispersion for AGC 114905, AGC 219533, AGC 248945 and AGC 749290, with a channel width of _ v 4 km s ^ 1 , is = 3 2 km s ^ 1 , which is below _ v .
Three of our galaxies (AGC 114905, AGC 248945 and AGC 749290) lie within the 1 scatter of the relation.

sources.summary: The paper says AGC 749290 shows a kinematic-morphological position-angle offset and disk-like optical morphology, but remains better classified as irregular.

quotes.quote: We see velocity gradients and evidence of ordered rotation in the moment 1 maps for all of the sources. While there is little evidence for irregular motions in their \ gas, some sources (e.g., AGC 248945 and AGC 334315) have very regular gradients, while others (e.g., AGC 749251 and AGC 748738) show gradients that are somewhat less regular, though the low resolution of our images does not allow for a more detailed analysis. In most cases, the orientation of the velocity gradient appears aligned along the \ major axis, though, in three cases (AGC 229110, AGC 749251, AGC 749290), there is a moderate offset in measured position angles based on the kinematics and the morphology (see Section sec:discussionclin ).

Two sources, AGC 334315 and AGC 749251, have some arc-like features reminiscent of spiral arms, but without distinct patterns or strong evidence of a stellar disk. Three sources, AGC 114905, AGC 748738, and AGC 749290, have observed optical components that loosely resemble stellar disks in our optical images, but still would be better classified as irregular galaxies at the detected level. Also, one source, AGC 219533, has a bright, very blue higher surface brightness clump superimposed on its low surface brightness emission; it is not fully clear if it is a bright star forming region associated with the galaxy, or if it is a foreground or background source.

gmw_00010275.name: VC5110377875
gmw_00010275.overview: VC5110377875 is a massive and bright high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:2311.08474]. It is classified as an ALPINE rotator candidate, and high-resolution observations have successfully recovered its integrated flux [arXiv:2311.08474].

sources.summary: The paper presents VC5110377875 as a massive bright ALPINE rotator candidate whose high-resolution data recover the integrated flux and show no significant radial depletion-time trend.

quotes.quote: In addition, we observed a massive and bright ALPINE rotator candidate VC5110377875 (2022.1.01118.S) in C43-4 ( 0.3") configuration during 286 min. These observations were supposed to be followed by higher-resolution observations (C43-7, 0.07"), which were not completed. The data were calibrated by the standard observatory pipeline, and imaged using the common astronomy software applications for radio astronomy (CASA; CASA2022 ).

This was not necessary for VC5110377875 since the full ALPINE integrated flux is already recovered by the new high-resolution observations alone. The data reduction is described in detail in a companion paper focusing on the morpho-kinematical analysis of these objects Devereaux2023 . The source properties and the achieved ALMA performance are listed in Table tab:data .

gmw_00010276.name: ESO 154-G010
gmw_00010276.overview: ESO 154-G010 is a barred spiral galaxy of SBa type located at a luminosity distance of approximately 83.2 Mpc [arXiv:1509.05389]. It is classified as a luminous infrared galaxy that hosts a circumnuclear star-forming ring [arXiv:1509.05389].

sources.summary: ESO 154-G010 is described as a luminous infrared SBa barred spiral hosting SN 2013fc in a circumnuclear star-forming ring; the adopted distance and infrared luminosity make it a LIRG.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage 2015 We present photometric and spectroscopic observations of SN 2013fc, a bright type II supernova (SN) in a circumnuclear star-forming ring in the luminous infrared galaxy ESO 154-G010, observed as part of the Public ESO Spectroscopic Survey of Transient Objects (PESSTO).
The host galaxy ESO 154-G010, an SBa-type barred spiral galaxy, has a heliocentric redshift of 0.01863 0.00008 and a recession velocity of 5586 24 km s ^ 1 cat1 .

Thus we find the luminosity distance, corrected for Virgo infall, Great Attractor and Shapley supercluster, to be D_ L = 83.2 1.1 Mpc and the distance modulus = 34.60 0.03 mag. Calibrating with this distance, we in turn obtain L_ IR /L_ = 11.05 0.05 . Therefore the galaxy qualifies as a LIRG ( L_ IR > 10^ 11 L_ ).

gmw_00010277.name: PSO J155.6730-16.9216
gmw_00010277.overview: PSO J155.6730-16.9216 is an interacting host galaxy system composed of three distinct components [arXiv:1611.09910]. Components A and C are an interacting pair that has triggered star formation, while component B is a newborn region [arXiv:1611.09910].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PSO J155.6730-16.9216 as LSQ14mo's three-component interacting host system, with components at different redshifts, sizes, stellar populations, star-formation rates, and metallicities.

quotes.quote: We chose the best-seeing (0.8 ) i band image taken at +293.1 d to determine that there are three components in the host galaxy system PSO J155.6730-16.9216. We have named these positions A, B, and C from south to north, respectively (see Fig. fig:vlt_slit_position ) and use these names throughout this paper. The SN is located coincident with the flux at position C.

All three systems, A, B, and C, are in close proximity to each other. Their projected separations are proj,AB 10.5 kpc, proj,AC 15.2 kpc, and proj,BC 4.7 kpc, and their line-of-sight velocity ( v los cz ) differences are v los,AB = 89.94 km/s, v los,AC = -209.86 km/s, and v los,BC = -299.80 km/s, respectively. This means that all three systems are easily close enough to be considered interacting under typical selection criteria ( Kewley+06, Ellison+08, Michel-Dansac+08 ).

We propose that components C and A are an interacting galaxy system that has triggered star-formation, and that component B is a new-born region, which thus shows the strongest EW of line and the weakest stellar continuum.

gmw_00010278.overview: 0034+25 is an elliptical radio galaxy located in the Zwicky cluster 0034.4+2532 [arXiv:0908.4391]. The galaxy exhibits a wide-angle-tail radio morphology with an inner stellar disk that may indicate a past merger [arXiv:0908.4391].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0034+25 as an E-galaxy radio source in a Zwicky cluster with wide-angle-tail morphology, an inner stellar disk, and unresolved VLBA emission.

quotes.quote: 0034+25 (UGC367) -- This radio galaxy, identified with an E galaxy at z=0.032 , shows a wide angle tail morphology with an inner symmetric jet structure in P.A. =90^ . It belongs to the Zwicky cluster 0034.4+2532. The host galaxy has an inner stellar disk perpendicular to the radio jet that has been interpreted as the signature of merging in the past ( ). In our VLBA observation the source appears unresolved with total flux density 3.6 mJy. As the ratio S_ VLBA /S_ is only 36 , most of the arcsecond flux is still missing on the milliarcsecond scale.

gmw_00010279.name: 0708+32B
gmw_00010279.overview: 0708+32B is a low-power compact radio source [arXiv:0908.4391]. It exhibits symmetric kiloparsec-scale lobes in VLA images and a double structure at VLBA resolution, which may indicate a compact symmetric object with recurrent radio activity [arXiv:0908.4391].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0708+32B as a low-power compact source with symmetric VLA lobes and a VLBA double structure interpreted as a possible recurrent CSO.

quotes.quote: 0708+32B . This source is classified as a low power compact source. In VLA images at 20 cm ( ) it shows a nuclear emission with two symmetric lobes < 10'' ( 10 kpc) in size oriented in the N-S direction. Higher resolution VLA data will be presented in to address the kiloparsec-scale structure of this source. In our VLBA images (Fig. f0708.1 and Fig. f0708.2 ), we detected a double structure oriented along PA 150 ^ and extended 4 mas. Since no spectral index information is available we are not able to identify the nuclear source, but the high degree of symmetry suggests an identification as a CSO with recurrent radio activity.

gmw_00010280.overview: The radio galaxy 0915+32 features a two-sided radio jet with mirror symmetry [arXiv:0908.4391]. This radio structure may be shaped by gravitational interactions with a nearby spiral galaxy, while high-resolution imaging shows an unresolved core that retains its arcsecond flux [arXiv:0908.4391].

sources.summary: The paper reports that 0915+32 has a two-sided mirrored VLA jet, possible interaction-driven radio structure, and unresolved VLBA emission retaining the arcsecond core flux.

quotes.quote: 0915+32 -- The VLA radio map by shows a two-sided jet in P.A. \ =28^ with mirror symmetry. The radio structure could be due to gravitational interactions with a nearby spiral galaxy ( ). In our VLBA image, the source appears unresolved with a total flux density of S_5 13.6 mJy. No arcsecond core flux is lost on the mas scale ( S_ VLBA /S_ 100 ).

gmw_00010281.overview: The radio source 1116+28 is associated with a double-galaxy system located at a redshift of z = 0.0667 [arXiv:0908.4391]. At arcsecond resolution, the source exhibits a narrow-angle-tail structure with two symmetric jets [arXiv:0908.4391].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1116+28 as a double-galaxy radio source with a narrow-angle-tail structure, revising earlier two-sided VLBI claims to an unresolved compact source.

quotes.quote: 1116+28 -- The galaxy associated with this radio source is a double system at a distance of z = 0.0667 . At arcsecond resolution ( ), the radio source shows a Narrow Angle Tail (NAT) structure with two symmetric jets. showed a slightly resolved parsec-scale structure with two-sided jets oriented in the same direction as the kpc-scale jets. Because of the large fraction of missing flux in the VLBI image we reobserved this source at 5 and 1.6 GHz. We do not confirm the two-sided jets previously reported.

gmw_00010282.overview: The radio galaxy 1204+24 is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley class I source [arXiv:0908.4391]. Very Large Array observations reveal that it possesses a bright core and faint, symmetric lobes [arXiv:0908.4391].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1204+24 as an FR I source with faint symmetric lobes, a bright core, and unresolved VLBA emission recovering part of the VLA core flux.

quotes.quote: 1204+24 -- In the VLA image, this source appears as a FRI with faint symmetric lobes and a bright core ( ). In our VLBA image, it appears unresolved with a total flux density S_5 = 5.3 mJy, corresponding to a fraction of the VLA core S_ VLBA /S_ 66 .

gmw_00010283.overview: The radio galaxy 1339+26 is a member of the cluster Abell 1775 [arXiv:0908.4391]. It exhibits a head-tail morphology and is identified as the easternmost component of a dominant double system [arXiv:0908.4391].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1339+26 as a head-tail radio galaxy in Abell 1775, associated with a dominant double-system member and undetected in the VLBA image.

quotes.quote: 1339+26 (UGC 08669) -- This radio galaxy has a head-tail morphology. It is the easternmost of a double system identified with the dominant member of the cluster Abell 1775. A small dark nuclear band (P.A. 0^ ) characterizes this otherwise regular galaxy ( ). In our VLBA image, this source is not detected above 0.3 mJy beam ^ 1 .

gmw_00010284.overview: The host of the radio source 1357+28 is a round elliptical galaxy featuring a small nuclear dust lane [arXiv:0908.4391]. It hosts a slightly extended VLBA core and a two-sided inner radio jet that bends and decollimates [arXiv:0908.4391].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1357+28 as a round elliptical galaxy with a nuclear dust lane, bent two-sided radio jet, and slightly extended VLBA core.

quotes.quote: 1357+28 -- The host of this radio source is a round elliptical galaxy with a small dust lane (P.A. 90^ ) bisecting the nuclear region ( ). The radio map by shows a two-sided inner jet in P.A. 0.5 ^ that decollimates and bends at 30 arcsec from the core. Our VLBA image (Fig. f1357.1 ) shows a slightly extended core suggesting a two-sided structure in PA 20 ^ . The VLBA core flux density is 5.6 mJy and some of the arcsecond core flux is missing on the milliarcsecond scale ( S_ VLBA /S_ 60 ).

gmw_00010285.name: 1615+35B
gmw_00010285.overview: 1615+35B is an elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of 0.0296 [arXiv:0908.4391]. It is associated with a very extended head-tail radio source that hosts a nuclear radio jet aligned with its kiloparsec-scale jet [arXiv:0908.4391].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1615+35B as a very extended head-tail radio source in an elliptical galaxy, with VLBA nuclear emission and a jet aligned with the kiloparsec jet.

quotes.quote: 1615+35B (NGC6109) -- This is a very extended Head Tail (HT) radio source with a structure more than 10 ^ in size, oriented N-S. It is associated with an elliptical galaxy of m _ pg =14.9 at a redshift of 0.0296. In our VLBA image (Fig. f.1615 ), the nuclear emission is seen with a jet on the same side as the kiloparsec-scale jet. The total flux density detected in our map is 23.6 mJy, the core flux is 17.1 mJy and the ratio between the VLBA correlated flux and the arcsecond core flux density at 5 GHz is 85 . At 1.2 mas from the nucleus, the jet/counter-jet surface brightness ratio is 30.

gmw_00010286.overview: 1621+38 is a head-tail radio galaxy [arXiv:0908.4391]. Very Long Baseline Array observations at 1.6 GHz reveal a core-jet structure with a portion of the core flux density missing [arXiv:0908.4391].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1621+38 as a head-tail radio galaxy with a 1.6 GHz core-jet VLBA structure, spectral index measurement, and missing core flux density.

quotes.quote: 1621+38 -- A head-tail radio galaxy ( ). In our VLBA 1.6 GHz data it shows a core-jet structure in PA 160^ (Fig. f.1621 ) in agreement with the 5 GHz image of . The core spectral index is _ 1.6 ^5=0.25 and a significant fraction of core flux density is missing.

gmw_00010287.overview: The radio source 1736+32 resolves into two unrelated radio sources [arXiv:0908.4391]. The northern main source exhibits a Fanaroff-Riley I structure with an unresolved VLBA core, while the southern secondary source appears as a background Fanaroff-Riley II source [arXiv:0908.4391].

sources.summary: The paper reports that 1736+32 resolves into unrelated sources, with the northern main source having FR I structure and an unresolved VLBA core retaining arcsecond flux.

quotes.quote: 1736+32 -- The B2 source is resolved in two unrelated radio sources. The main source (the Northern one) has a FR I structure, the secondary (Southern) appears as a background FR II source projected onto the SE lobe of the FR I galaxy ( ). In our VLBA image of the main source, we found an unresolved structure with a total flux density 7.6 mJy. No arcsecond core flux is missing on the milliarcsecond scale.

gmw_00010288.name: 1752+32B
gmw_00010288.overview: The radio galaxy 1752+32B is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley class I source [arXiv:0908.4391]. Very Long Baseline Array imaging of the galaxy reveals nuclear emission alongside a two-sided parsec-scale emission aligned with its kiloparsec-scale jet [arXiv:0908.4391].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1752+32B as an FR I source whose VLBA image shows nuclear emission and two-sided parsec emission aligned with the kiloparsec jet.

quotes.quote: 1752+32B : In VLA images, this source appears as a FRI source ( ). Our VLBA image shows nuclear emission with a two-sided emission at the same PA as the kiloparsec-scale jet (Fig. f.1752 ). The total flux density detected in our map is 9.3 mJy, the core flux is 7.4 mJy and the ratio between the VLBA correlated flux and the arcsecond core flux density at 5 GHz is 77 .

gmw_00010289.name: J0759+5050
gmw_00010289.overview: The active galaxy J0759+5050, also known as 2MASX J075941, exhibits highly asymmetric [O III] emission structures [arXiv:1802.06184]. It hosts near-nuclear multicomponent kinematics with a measured outflow radius of 620 pc [arXiv:1802.06184], and has shown optical microvariability during a single night of observation [arXiv:1504.08254].

sources.summary: The paper observed J0759+5050 over three nights and detected one optical microvariability event, but did not confirm persistent variability across the campaign.

quotes.quote: J0759+5050 was observed during three nights. The observations from March 30, 2011, was disturbed by clouds, so it is not strictly continuous. Microvariability was only detected during the observations carried out on March 31. The event was observed during the period between 3:21 and 5:42 UT. The upper level of the amplitude is 0.04 mag, which is well above our estimated precision set by the high S/N.
The variability amplitude is 4.6 . Because the microvariations were only observed during one of the nights of the campaign, we conclude that we cannot confirm microvariability in this source.

sources.summary: 2MASX J075941 has asymmetric [O III] emission, near-nuclear multicomponent kinematics, and a measured outflow radius of 620 pc, with undisturbed rotation farther out.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J075941 The [ O III ] structure in this target is highly asymmetric, with faint, extended knots of emission to the east of the nucleus. Kinematics are obtained near the nucleus (total extent < 1''), with a two-component emission line (FWHMs of 1500 and 100 km s ^ 1 ) observed near systemic throughout, and an intermediate width component (FWHM 500 km s ^ 1 ) traveling in different directions on either side of the nucleus inside 500 pc. We measure the maximum extent of these kinematics as the observed outflow radius R _ out = 620 pc. At greater distances, we measure single component emission-lines at systemic with FWHMs < 250 km s ^ 1 , which we define as undisturbed rotation. As such, we do not measure a distinct disturbed kinematics maximum radius, R _ dist in this target.

gmw_00010290.name: IC 1029
gmw_00010290.overview: IC 1029 is an edge-on host galaxy [arXiv:1806.05470]. It hosted the early-phase Type II supernova SN 2015ba, which was discovered in November 2015 [arXiv:1806.05470].

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 1029 as the edge-on host of SN 2015ba, gives the supernova location within it, and derives host extinction and distance constraints.

quotes.quote: SN 2015ba was discovered by Koichi Itagaki (Teppo-cho, Yamagata, Japan) on 2015 November 28.8071 UT (JD = 2457355.31) in the galaxy IC 1029 at an unfiltered magnitude of 16.7 mag (CBET 4209 www.cbat.eps.harvard.edu/iau/cbet/004200/CBET004209.txt ). The precise location of the SN was given by T. Yusa and Osaki to be around 19 ^ E, 43 ^ S of the centre of IC 1029. obtained an optical spectrum on 2015 December 2.13 UT showing weak and shallow P-Cygni profiles of the Balmer lines superimposed on a blue continuum. This is consistent with a SN II spectrum at early phase. The expansion velocities reported from the measurement of the position of the minima of the H and H Balmer lines were around 8900 and 8400 km s ^ 1 , respectively. The details of SN 2015ba and its host galaxy IC 1029 are given in Table tab:sn15ba_ic1029_detail .

The estimated A_ V(host) is 1.37 0.20 and E(B V) _ host = 0.44 0.06 mag. The reddening estimate using the equivalent width and the colour method are consistent with each other within the errors, and are listed in Table ebv . We, therefore, compute the weighted mean of the host galaxy reddening as E(B V) _ host = 0.44 0.05 mag. The total E(B V) due to host and Milky Way is 0.46 0.05 mag, which is adopted throughout the paper.

gmw_00010291.name: ESO 565-G019
gmw_00010291.overview: ESO 565-G019 is an elliptical Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:2109.00572]. Its active nucleus is obscured by a Compton-thick structure, and it exhibits extended soft X-ray emission spanning approximately 3.5 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2109.00572].

sources.summary: The paper finds ESO 565-G019 is a Seyfert galaxy with Compton-thick line-of-sight and average column densities, a lower torus covering factor, and extended soft X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: ESO 565-G019 is a Sy2, E type galaxy devaucouleurs1991class . From Swift BAT and Suzaku X-ray observation of its emission results to be reflection dominated, with a column density larger than the Compton-thick threshold gandhi2013reflection .

ESO 565-G019 is classified as Compton-thick in both the l.o.s. and average components of the column density. For ESO 565-G019 the lower covering factor C _ TOR =0.47_ 0.08 ^ +0.18 , along with the N _ H , suggests a scenario in which the obscuring structure, which is Compton-thick, may be distributed in several individual clouds, responsible for the obscuration. We found significant diffuse emission in the 0.3--3.0 keV band extending for about 2 (NGC 3081) and 3.5 kpc (ESO 565-G019).

gmw_00010292.name: Tucana B
