gmw_00010292.overview: Tucana B is an isolated dwarf galaxy located in the Local Group [arXiv:2306.17454]. It is classified as an ultra-faint reionization fossil that lacks recent star formation and exhibits a notably compact structure [arXiv:2306.17454].

sources.summary: The paper uses Tucana B as an isolated ultra-faint reionization-fossil comparison object, noting its low luminosity, small half-light radius, absent recent star formation, and compact profile.

quotes.quote: Tucana B is an isolated dwarf in the Local Group with an `ultra faint' luminosity of 5 10^ 4 L_ and a half-light radius 80 40 pc , lacking recent star formation sand:2022aa_tucb . Tuc B provides a useful point of reference for an isolated, early-forming `reionization fossil' in a mass range comparable to our predictions for the most massive ghost galaxies, potentially free from effects due to interactions with a more massive galaxy. In this respect, it is notably compact, with a significantly more concentrated profile than ghosts of a similar or greater mass, regardless of our choice of f_ mb .

gmw_00010293.name: Mrk 985
gmw_00010293.overview: Mrk 985 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1203.2763]. Spectropolarimetric observations of the galaxy reveal a two-peaked intensity spectrum and a nearly symmetric polarization degree [arXiv:1203.2763].

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 985 as an AGN with a two-peaked intensity spectrum, symmetric polarization, inferred inclination, and broad-line-region magnetic fields estimated from spectropolarimetry.

quotes.quote: The intensity spectrum of Mrk 985 has a two-peak shape and the spectrum of the polarization degree p_ line ( ) is quite symmetric (the p_ line (left) 1.27 and p_ line (right) 1.16 ). In the centre of line p_ line (centre) 0.5 . The mean polarization of the continuum radiation is 1.12 .

We see that a nearly symmetric form of p_ line ( ) implies that a b_ , i.e. B_ B_ . In the atlas of Smith et al. (2002) there is no information on the inclination angle i . It is interesting to estimate this angle assuming that g_ line 1 .
Using Eq.(11) and the value 0.44 , we find the estimates: B_ 0.75 G and B_ 6.6 G.

gmw_00010294.overview: The galaxy 2114-6800 is an isolated elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9903052]. It is an X-ray over-luminous system characterized by azimuthally symmetric X-ray emission extending to a large radius, with the central galaxy dominating the optical light [arXiv:astro-ph/9903052].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2114-6800 as one of four isolated X-ray over-luminous elliptical-galaxy systems, with large-radius X-ray emission, azimuthally symmetric surface brightness, and optical light dominated by the central galaxy.

quotes.quote: These selections resulted in a sample of four objects (Table  tab:sample ). Two of them, 1159+5531 and 2114-6800 , were previously detected in the Einstein surveys (Stocke et al. \ 1991 and Griffiths et al. 1992, respectively) and identified with normal elliptical galaxies. We also rediscovered the Ponman et al. \ object, 1340+4017 . The remaining object, 2247+0337 , first appears in our sample. We have measured the redshift of 2247+0337 and adopted the redshifts of others from the literature.

In all four objects, the X-ray emission is detected with 2-3 significance out to a large radius R_ x = 0.5-1 Mpc (Table  tab:sample ). The X-ray surface brightness distribution shows no significant deviations from azimuthal symmetry.
In all objects, at least 70 of light (exluding the background) comes from the central galaxy; the contribution from other galaxies does not exceed the level of background fluctuations.

gmw_00010295.overview: The object 2247+0337 is an isolated, X-ray over-luminous elliptical galaxy system [arXiv:astro-ph/9903052]. Its X-ray emission is detected out to a large radius with an azimuthally symmetric surface brightness, and the central galaxy dominates the optical light [arXiv:astro-ph/9903052].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2247+0337 as one of four isolated X-ray over-luminous elliptical-galaxy systems, with large-radius X-ray emission, azimuthally symmetric surface brightness, and optical light dominated by the central galaxy.

quotes.quote: These selections resulted in a sample of four objects (Table  tab:sample ). Two of them, 1159+5531 and 2114-6800 , were previously detected in the Einstein surveys (Stocke et al. \ 1991 and Griffiths et al. 1992, respectively) and identified with normal elliptical galaxies. We also rediscovered the Ponman et al. \ object, 1340+4017 . The remaining object, 2247+0337 , first appears in our sample. We have measured the redshift of 2247+0337 and adopted the redshifts of others from the literature.

In all four objects, the X-ray emission is detected with 2-3 significance out to a large radius R_ x = 0.5-1 Mpc (Table  tab:sample ). The X-ray surface brightness distribution shows no significant deviations from azimuthal symmetry.
In all objects, at least 70 of light (exluding the background) comes from the central galaxy; the contribution from other galaxies does not exceed the level of background fluctuations.

gmw_00010296.overview: The galaxy 1159+5531 is an isolated giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9903052]. It exhibits a cD-like optical luminosity [arXiv:astro-ph/9903052] and possesses diffuse X-ray properties that are typical of poor galaxy clusters [arXiv:astro-ph/9903052].

sources.summary: The paper presents 1159+5531 as a representative OLEG: an isolated giant elliptical with cluster-like X-ray properties, a cooling-flow halo, cD-like optical luminosity, and no strong surrounding galaxy concentration.

quotes.quote: Although this object was discovered by Einstein , its large extent in X-rays remained unnoticed prior to the ROSAT observation. The long ROSAT exposure and the low background made it possible to trace the diffuse X-ray emission to a large radius and even perform a simple spectral analysis. The optical and X-ray properties of 1159+5531 appear to be representative of the other objects in our sample, and the main conclusions derived from the 1159+5531 data can be applied to the entire OLEG population.

The diffuse optical light can be traced to 150 kpc in R , V , and B bands. The light profile very accurately follows the de Vaucouleurs law with an effective radius r_e=16 kpc. The absolute magnitude is M_R=-23.1 + 5 h and M_V=-22.5+5 h (with K- and Galactic extinction correction applied). Such a high luminosity is rare in field ellipticals but is typical of cD galaxies in clusters (Hoessel 1980). To summarize, 1159+5531 is an object with X-ray properties typical of poor clusters and which contains an isolated galaxy whose optical properties resemble central cluster galaxies.

gmw_00010297.name: G1
gmw_00010297.overview: G1 is a red galaxy that acts as the main lens in a strong gravitational lensing system [arXiv:1702.00406, arXiv:2003.12117]. Spectral modeling of the galaxy indicates a stellar mass of approximately 3.2 billion solar masses [arXiv:1702.00406].

sources.summary: The paper measures the main lensing galaxy G1 from four spectra, describes slit contamination by quasar images, and derives its velocity dispersion with ULySS modeling.

quotes.quote: The main lensing galaxy G1 in was measured from four independently observed spectra: two from Magellan LDSS-3, one from Gemini South GMOS-S, and one from VLT MUSE. G1 was targeted on two of the four Magellan LDSS-3 masks listed in Table tab:spectro_observations , ``des0408a'' and ``des0408b''; hereafter these two masks will be denoted ``Magellan a'' and ``Magellan b''. The slit setup for G1 was the same on both masks, while the remaining targets were different on the two masks. Specifically, the slit was oriented so that it included both G1 and quasar image B the naming convention for the lensing galaxy G1 and the lensed quasar images A, B, and D are shown in Lin2017a . In addition, some contaminating flux from quasar image A was also visible in the 2D spectrum from the slit.

The remaining light from G1 was then extracted to a 1D spectrum by summing the light over a 1 1 box or a 2.2 2.2 box. The velocity dispersion of G1 was then measured from the above data using the ULySS Koleva2009 galaxy spectral modeling package.

sources.summary: G1 is the main red lens galaxy; DES modeling treats it as an extended galaxy component and finds a stellar mass of about 3.2 10^11 solar masses.

quotes.quote: As illustrated in Figures fig:puppies and fig:WFI , the system consists of a red galaxy (G1) surrounded by three blue point-like objects (A, B, D) and a redder and compact object (G2/C).
The DES cutouts are modelled as the superposition of a galaxy with a ser68 profile for G1, and four point sources for A,B,D and G2/C.
In particular, the SED of G1 suggests a stellar mass M_ 3.2 10^ 11 M_ for the lens, within 0.4 dex uncertainty.

gmw_00010298.name: 4C 65.15
gmw_00010298.overview: 4C 65.15 is a hybrid radio quasar that exhibits a Fanaroff-Riley I jet structure on one side [arXiv:0901.0925]. This structure is likely generated by a jet interacting with a dense environment, producing radio and unabsorbed X-ray luminosities typical of Fanaroff-Riley II morphologies [arXiv:0901.0925].

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C 65.15 as a hybrid radio quasar whose mildly absorbed X-ray spectrum, detected jet-bend emission, and FR II-like luminosities imply a powerful jet in a dense environment.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of 4C 65.15 can be modeled with a simple power law with perhaps mild intrinsic absorption ( N_ H 1.3 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 ). X-ray emission is detected at the bend in the FR I jet. This X-ray jet emission lies above the extrapolation from the high-frequency radio synchrotron emission and has a spectral slope flatter than _ rx , indicating that the jet spectral energy distribution is concave as with other FR II quasar jets. Both 3C 433 and 4C 65.15 have unabsorbed X-ray luminosities, radio luminosities, and optical spectra typically seen in comparable sources with FR II morphologies. Presumably the FR I structure seen on one side in these hybrid sources is generated by a powerful jet interacting with a relatively dense environment.

gmw_00010299.name: PKS B0053 016
gmw_00010299.overview: PKS B0053 016 is a head-tail radio galaxy located in the ACO 119 cluster [arXiv:1709.07421]. It features resolved twin jets that follow helical trajectories and symmetric wiggles along its tails, which exhibit spectral aging further from the core [arXiv:1709.07421].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS B0053 016 as an ACO 119 head-tail radio galaxy with symmetric radio morphology, resolved twin jets, helical wiggles, and spectral aging along the tail.

quotes.quote: The radio morphology is quite symmetric and has an overall angular extent of 6 ^ , and the core is not very prominent. The high-resolution contour map (Figure 0053-016 ) at 610 MHz resolves the jets that are ejected from the core, as two distinct radio jets. Slightly away from the core the intensity increases and the jet widens. radio galaxies. Both the jets also seem to follow a helical trajectory, and the tails seem to show symmetric wiggles.

The spectral index value steepens from 0.82 in the region near the core to 2.31 toward the farthest end of the tail. Apart from the steepening of the spectral index, also finds evidence for a spectral curvature in the tails at about a distance of 2 ^ away from the core. Both the steepening and the presence of spectral curvature are consistent with the aging of the plasma toward the ends of the tails.

gmw_00010300.name: PKS B0053 015
gmw_00010300.overview: PKS B0053 015 is a head-tail radio galaxy that exhibits wide-angle asymmetric jets [arXiv:1709.07421]. The host galaxy is displaced from the leading edge of the tails, and its spectral index map reveals a narrow filament of less-steep plasma embedded in a steeper background [arXiv:1709.07421].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS B0053 015 as a head-tail galaxy whose displaced host, wide-angle asymmetric jets, ram-pressure bending, wiggles, and spectral-index structure trace its motion through the cluster.

quotes.quote: PKS B0053 015 Figure all-ht shows that the host galaxy is not at the leading edge of the tails, unlike most head tail galaxies. The jets are clearly ejected at wide angles. The peculiar morphology might be a consequence of the fact that one of the jets is not ejected at right angles but instead at a smaller angle with the direction of motion of the galaxy. This might also result from projection effects or an interplay of both.

Although the spectral index and magnetic field steepen without any noticeable discontinuities, the spectral index map shows several interesting features. In almost all the head tail galaxies presented here, there is a steepening trend in the spectral index as one traverses away from the head along the tail. But, in PKS 0053 015, there is a region where a narrow filament of less-steep plasma seems to be embedded in a steeper background.

gmw_00010301.name: GB6 B0335 + 0955
gmw_00010301.overview: GB6 B0335 + 0955 is a head-tail radio galaxy hosted by an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1709.07421]. The galaxy features a core with unresolved jets, and its tail exhibits a deflection in direction accompanied by an increase in intensity [arXiv:1709.07421].

sources.summary: The paper describes GB6 B0335 + 0955 as a head-tail radio galaxy with a prominent core, unresolved jets, tail deflection, intensity changes, and spectral steepening from synchrotron aging.

quotes.quote: Figure all-ht shows that the elliptical host galaxy of GB6 B0335 + 0955 is located at the tip of the tail-like structure and is moving away from the cluster center. Figure 0335-0955 shows the radio morphologies of the head tail radio galaxy GB6 B0335 + 0955 at 610 and 240 MHz. The angular size of the galaxy is about 8 ^ .5. The core is prominent, and the individual jets are not resolved out. There is some evidence from the spatial profile of surface brightness near the core for a dip in surface brightness and a brightening later on.

Farther down the tail, one notices an abrupt change in the direction of motion of the galaxy along with an increase in intensity. This could be because of interactions with neighboring galaxies that came close enough to cause deflection. suggested that this was due to buoyant motions of the tail following the low-density regions of the intergalactic medium. The steepening of the spectrum as the distance increases from the host galaxy is evident and is due to synchrotron aging of the radio-emitting plasma.

gmw_00010302.name: NGC 7385
gmw_00010302.overview: NGC 7385 is a head-tail radio galaxy with a displaced optical host that is not coincident with the tip of its radio structure [arXiv:1709.07421]. The galaxy features extended low-frequency structures, a nonperpendicular jet geometry, and both optical and X-ray jets [arXiv:1709.07421].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7385 as a head-tail radio galaxy with a displaced optical host, nonperpendicular jet geometry, extended low-frequency structures, and both optical and X-ray jets.

quotes.quote: NGC 7385 The location of the host galaxy of NGC 7385 is shown in Figure all-ht . The position of the optical host galaxy is not coincident with the tip of the head tail radio galaxy. This galaxy is a possible example where the jet direction is not perpendicular to the direction of the motion, as is seen in the classic examples of head tail radio galaxies. However, the peculiar morphology may also be explained using projection effects, as was demonstrated by .

The radio maps of NGC 7385 at 610 and 240 MHz are presented in Figure n7385 . had mapped this galaxy at 5 GHz using the VLA. The lower resolution images show much more extended structures. Although the spectral index steepens along the radio tail, the 240 MHz image at lower angular resolution does not show the diffuse emission toward the ends of the tails. This is because of the poor signal-to-noise ratio in the image, due to corruption from RFI. NGC 7385 is the only sample head tail radio galaxy in which an optical jet and also an X-ray jet were detected 2015MNRAS.452.3064R .

gmw_00010303.name: VVV-J114556.04-635628.0
gmw_00010303.overview: The galaxy VVV-J114556.04-635628.0 was previously identified as the source IRAS 11435-6339 [arXiv:2302.05595]. Observations of the galaxy are contaminated by nearby sources, and its WISE counterpart lacks sufficient evidence to be classified as a blazar candidate [arXiv:2302.05595].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VVV-J114556.04-635628.0 with IRAS 11435-6339, notes nearby-source contamination, and finds its WISE counterpart satisfies one blazar criterion but lacks sufficient blazar evidence.

quotes.quote: Among our objects of study, only the galaxy VVV-J114556.04-635628.0 was previously identified as the source IRAS 11435-6339 Cutri2003 .
The VVV-J114556.04-635628.0 and VVV-J121313.50-613155.1 are also contaminated by nearby sources.

Using the WGS method, from Figure CCD1 the WISE sources coincident with VVV-J114556.04-635628.0, VVV-J121313.50-613155.1 and VVV-J131828.35-635442.3 satisfy only one of the criteria to be a blazar candidate. The sources VVV-J125631.40-611626.0 and VVV-J134416.68-632638.7 do not satisfy any of the criteria. Based on this, none of the objects present sufficient evidence to be blazar candidates.

gmw_00010304.name: VVV-J121313.50-613155.1
gmw_00010304.overview: VVV-J121313.50-613155.1 is an active galactic nucleus located within an empirically defined AGN color region [arXiv:2302.05595]. The source is contaminated by nearby objects but is associated with Gaia counterparts and a WISE counterpart that meets one blazar candidate criterion [arXiv:2302.05595].

sources.summary: The paper says VVV-J121313.50-613155.1 is contaminated but has Gaia counterparts, lies in an AGN color region, and has a WISE counterpart satisfying one blazar criterion.

quotes.quote: The VVV-J114556.04-635628.0 and VVV-J121313.50-613155.1 are also contaminated by nearby sources.

The source VVV-J121313.50-613155.1 is the only one with Gaia counterparts. Two sources were found in the field, the closest one, 6057779246181521792, with an angular separation of 0.37 arcsec, has parallax of (0.87 1.41) mas, which is not statistically significant as a positive parallax.

In addition, the objects VVV-J121313.50-613155.1 and VVV-J134416.68-632638.7 are located in the AGN region defined empirically by . The object VVV-J125631.40-611626.0 is in the YSO region of the mid-IR colour-colour diagram of and also it is not located in the region populated by galaxies in . Using the WGS method, from Figure CCD1 the WISE sources coincident with VVV-J114556.04-635628.0, VVV-J121313.50-613155.1 and VVV-J131828.35-635442.3 satisfy only one of the criteria to be a blazar candidate.

gmw_00010305.name: VVV-J131828.35-635442.3
gmw_00010305.overview: VVV-J131828.35-635442.3 is a galaxy located in the galaxy-type quasar region [arXiv:2302.05595]. Its Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer counterpart satisfies only one criterion of the WGS method, which is insufficient to classify the object as a blazar candidate [arXiv:2302.05595].

sources.summary: The paper places VVV-J131828.35-635442.3 in the galaxy-type QSO region and says its WISE counterpart satisfies one WGS criterion, but not enough to classify it as a blazar.

quotes.quote: Following , the galaxy VVV-J131828.35-635442.3 is located in the galaxy-type QSO region and VVV-J134416.68-632638.7 in the star-like QSO region.
The two galaxies with Y photometric data (VVV-J131828.35-635442.3 and VVV-J134416.68-632638.7) are both located in the QSO region following .

Using the WGS method, from Figure CCD1 the WISE sources coincident with VVV-J114556.04-635628.0, VVV-J121313.50-613155.1 and VVV-J131828.35-635442.3 satisfy only one of the criteria to be a blazar candidate. The sources VVV-J125631.40-611626.0 and VVV-J134416.68-632638.7 do not satisfy any of the criteria. Based on this, none of the objects present sufficient evidence to be blazar candidates.

gmw_00010306.name: VVV-J134416.68-632638.7
gmw_00010306.overview: VVV-J134416.68-632638.7 is an active galactic nucleus candidate located within an empirically defined AGN color region [arXiv:2302.05595]. The object is classified as a star-like quasar candidate, though it does not meet the criteria to be a blazar [arXiv:2302.05595].

sources.summary: The paper places VVV-J134416.68-632638.7 in star-like QSO and empirical AGN color regions, while noting it lacks enough WGS evidence to be considered a blazar.

quotes.quote: Following , the galaxy VVV-J131828.35-635442.3 is located in the galaxy-type QSO region and VVV-J134416.68-632638.7 in the star-like QSO region.

According to our results, the five sources satisfy both the and also, within the uncertainties, the criteria to be QSO candidates. On the other hand, galaxies VVV-J121313.50-613155.1 and VVV-J134416.68-632638.7 are located inside the box defined by .

In addition, the objects VVV-J121313.50-613155.1 and VVV-J134416.68-632638.7 are located in the AGN region defined empirically by . The object VVV-J125631.40-611626.0 is in the YSO region of the mid-IR colour-colour diagram of and also it is not located in the region populated by galaxies in . Using the WGS method, from Figure CCD1 the WISE sources coincident with VVV-J114556.04-635628.0, VVV-J121313.50-613155.1 and VVV-J131828.35-635442.3 satisfy only one of the criteria to be a blazar candidate. The sources VVV-J125631.40-611626.0 and VVV-J134416.68-632638.7 do not satisfy any of the criteria.

gmw_00010307.name: SN 2011ke
gmw_00010307.overview: SN 2011ke is located in a compact dwarf galaxy that exhibits a blue-green color due to strong oxygen emission [arXiv:1311.0026]. This host galaxy has a redder, more extended companion galaxy situated at a similar redshift [arXiv:1311.0026].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the SN 2011ke host as a compact dwarf with a redder companion at similar redshift, strong oxygen emission, and host-only photometry and spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: SN 2011ke Inspection of archival CFHT images shows that SN 2011ke exploded in a compact dwarf galaxy, with a redder and more extended companion. We obtained a spectrum with the slit going through both the SN site and the companion galaxy; while the spectrum was not taken at parallactic angle IMACS has an atmospheric dispersion corrector, so relative line fluxes should not be affected. We find that the two galaxies are at a similar redshift, though with a velocity offset of 100  km s ^ 1 . A color image combining g and band from CFHT with our own z band images from IMACS is shown in Figure  fig:babypea . The blue-green color of the dwarf galaxy is due to strong [ O 3 ] emission in band, similar to the ``Green Pea'' galaxies found in SDSS css+09 .

gmw_00010308.name: PTF09cnd
gmw_00010308.overview: PTF09cnd is associated with a host galaxy identified through deep imaging of its surrounding field [arXiv:1311.0026]. This host galaxy exhibits several emission lines in its late-time spectrum, which have been utilized to model its spectral energy distribution and determine its metallicity [arXiv:1311.0026].

sources.summary: The paper treats the nearest source to PTF09cnd as the likely host, derives an SED from deep imaging, and uses late-time galaxy emission lines for host properties.

quotes.quote: PTF09cnd We obtained deep imaging of the field of PTF09cnd with MMTCam. As can be seen in Figure  fig:galpix there are several sources near the reported location of the transient (marked by the green circle). We assume the closest source is the correct host, and use this photometry to construct a model SED.

A spectrum confirming the redshift would be necessary, however, to make a definitive association. To determine a metallicity for PTF09cnd, we download the archival spectra of the transient from qkk+11 from the WISEREP database yg12 . The late-time spectrum exhibits a number of galaxy emission lines, which we use to determine the host properties.

gmw_00010309.name: Red Virgo 4
gmw_00010309.overview: Red Virgo 4 is a lensed, hyperluminous starburst galaxy located at a redshift of 4.724 [arXiv:2001.03641]. It is gravitationally lensed into three multiple images by High-z Lens 1, a pair of likely merging galaxies at a redshift of 1.48 [arXiv:2001.03641].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Red Virgo 4 as a z=4.724 lensed hyperluminous starburst SMG with multiple radio/CO images, low gas-depletion times, high star-formation efficiency, and relatively low gas density.

quotes.quote: We serendipitously discovered in the Herschel Reference Survey an extremely bright infrared source with S_ 500 120 mJy in the line of sight of the Virgo cluster which we name Red Virgo 4 (RV4). Based on IRAM/EMIR and IRAM/NOEMA detections of the CO(5-4), CO(4-3), and [ CI ] lines, RV4 is located at a redshift of 4.724, yielding a total observed infrared luminosity of 1.1 0.6 10 ^ 14 L _ . At the position of the Herschel emission, three blobs are detected with the VLA at 10 cm. The CO(5-4) line detection of each blob confirms that they are at the same redshift with the same line width, indicating that they are multiple images of the same source.

Different estimates of the gas depletion time yield low values suggesting that RV4 is a starburst galaxy. Among all high-z submillimeter galaxies (SMGs), this source exhibits one of the lowest L _ [ CI ] to L _ IR ratios, 3.2 0.9 10 ^ 6 , suggesting an extremely short gas depletion time of only 14 5 Myr. It also shows a relatively high L _ [ CI ] to L _ CO(4-3) ratio (0.7 0.2) and low L _ CO(5-4) to L _ IR ratio (only 50 of the value expected for normal galaxies) hinting a low density of gas.

sources.summary: The paper describes High-z Lens 1 as two z=1.48 galaxies, likely merging, that lens RV4 with an unusually large Einstein radius for such a high-redshift strong-lensing system.

quotes.quote: In Spitzer and deep optical observations, two sources, High-z Lens 1 (HL1) West and HL1 East, are detected at the center of the three VLA/NOEMA blobs. These two sources are placed at z=1.48 with XSHOOTER spectra, suggesting that they could be merging and gravitationally lensing the emission of RV4. HL1 is the second most distant lens known to date in strong lensing systems. Constrained by the position of the three VLA/NOEMA blobs, the Einstein radius of the lensing system is 2.2 0.2 (20 kpc). The high redshift of HL1 and the large Einstein radius are highly unusual for a strong lensing system.

gmw_00010310.name: SDSS J232444.80-094600.3
gmw_00010310.overview: SDSS J232444.80-094600.3 is a quasar characterized by strong intermediate-width emission lines [arXiv:1605.07265]. These emission lines are produced by nearly dust-free, intermediate-density gas located at the skin of the dusty torus [arXiv:1605.07265].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes SDSS J232444.80-094600.3 as a quasar with strong intermediate-width emission lines produced by nearly dust-free, intermediate-density gas at the dusty torus skin.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed analysis of a quasar SDSS J232444.80-094600.3 (SDSS J2324 0946), which is remarkable for its strong intermediate-width emission lines (IELs) with FWHM 1800 . The IEL component is presented in different emission lines, including the permitted lines \ 1216, \ 1549, semiforbidden line \ 1909, and forbidden lines \ 4959, 5007. With the aid of photo-ionization models, we found that the IELs are produced by gas with a hydrogen density of n_ H 10^ 6.2 10^ 6.3   cm^ 3 , a distance to the central ionizing source of R 35-50 pc, a covering factor of CF 6 , and a dust-to-gas ratio of 4 times of SMC. We suggest that the strong IELs of this quasar are produced by nearly dust-free and intermediate-density gas located at the skin of the dusty torus.

gmw_00010311.name: UGC 06903
gmw_00010311.overview: UGC 06903 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2007.04430]. Its ratio map is complicated by the presence of spiral arms, and its azimuthal profile displays four maximum peaks that correspond to the maximum force ratio in each quadrant [arXiv:2007.04430].

sources.summary: UGC 06903 is used as a worked example whose ratio map is complicated by spiral arms, while its azimuthal profile shows four maximum peaks corresponding to quadrant force-ratio maxima.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig3.3.1a , we display the calculated results for NGC 5750 and UGC 06903. Figure fig3.3.1a (a) shows the deprojected image of the galaxy in the i- band and Figure fig3.3.1a (b) the ratio map Q_ T (i,j) .
However, if a galaxy has spiral arms, the patterns on the ratio map become more complex, like UGC 06903 (bottom row in Figure fig3.3.1a (b)).

We plot the azimuthal profile of UGC 06903 in Figure fig3.3.1b and find four maximum peaks, each corresponding to the maximum force ratio at each quadrant. We again overlay these four peaks on the deprojected image with red crosses in Figure fig3.3.1a (a).

gmw_00010312.name: 4XMM J151509.5+561347
gmw_00010312.overview: 4XMM J151509.5+561347 is a galaxy counterpart that experienced a soft X-ray outburst in August 2021 [arXiv:2405.13630]. This event exhibited a variability factor greater than 13, with a peak luminosity consistent with either a flaring active galactic nucleus or a tidal disruption event [arXiv:2405.13630].

sources.summary: The paper reports 4XMM J151509.5+561347 as a soft X-ray outburst associated with a galaxy counterpart, with luminosity and spectrum consistent with either a flaring AGN or TDE.

quotes.quote: 4XMM J151509.5+561347 showed a soft outburst in August 2021 (ObsID 0891801501), with a variability of a factor >13 compared to previous upper limits (see the alert fig:Tde_Alert ). Its optical counterpart (SDSS J151509.61+561347.3) is classified as a galaxy ahumada_16th_2020 , with a photometric redshift of 0.33 0.09. The nearby galaxy, SDSS J151510.27+561344.7, is brighter and has a spectroscopic redshift of 0.16. Using the photometric redshift of 0.33 0.09, the peak flux value of (7 1) 10^ 13 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 translates into a luminosity of 2.5^ +2.5 _ 1.5 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 . This type of luminosity can be reached by both high accretion episodes in AGN or bright TDEs at their peak.

gmw_00010313.name: 4XMM J000532.8+200717
gmw_00010313.overview: 4XMM J000532.8+200717 is a quasar located at a redshift of z=0.119 [arXiv:2405.13630]. Over a 20-year period, the active nucleus exhibited long-term X-ray spectral variability, including a high-emission episode in the late 2000s [arXiv:2405.13630].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 4XMM J000532.8+200717 as a quasar with long-term X-ray spectral variability, high late-2000s emission, and a harder absorbed peak spectrum.

quotes.quote: 4XMM J000532.8+200717 is a quasar at z=0.119 caccianiga_xmm-newton_2008 that showed a significant long-term spectral variability over the 20 years of available X-ray data (see alert Fig. fig:QuasarSpecVar_alert ). It underwent an episode of high emission in the late 2000s, with noticeable Swift variability of about an order of magnitude (between luminosities of 10^ 43 to 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 ). It is noticeably harder at the peak than in quiescence (see Fig. fig:Quasar_SpecVar_Spectra ). The peak spectrum is consistent with an intrinsically absorbed power law ( N_ H ^ Peak =(1.0 0.5) 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 and ^ Peak =3.2 0.1 ), with a much softer photon index in the low state and consistent intrinsic absorption ( N_ H ^ Low =(5 3) 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 , and ^ Low =5.2 0.6 ). This change is further confirmed by the fact that freezing the photon index at the peak value and fitting only the normalization and absorption on the low state significantly worsens the fit statistics, from ^2/ DoF=30/17 to 52/18.

gmw_00010314.name: 4XMM J023228.8+202349
gmw_00010314.overview: 4XMM J023228.8+202349 is a broad-line active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.029 [arXiv:2405.13630]. It is a hard X-ray source that exhibits short-term variability, ionized absorption, and a slight soft-band emission lag [arXiv:2405.13630].

sources.summary: The paper presents 4XMM J023228.8+202349 as a short-term variable absorbed AGN whose X-ray spectrum includes ionized absorption, reflection, and a soft lag.

quotes.quote: 4XMM J023228.8+202349 is a hard source showing variability by a factor of 10 over timescales of a few days (see alert in Fig. fig:AbsorbedAGN ). It is part of the NuSTAR serendipitous catalog as well zappacosta_nustar_2018 , that identified its optical counterpart as a broad-line galaxy at z=0.029 . The source, in the three available observations, is well fitted with a power law and ionized absorber and a reflection feature ( TBabs*zxipcf*(zgauss+relxilllp)) .

The brightest observation, which triggered the alert, is short-term variable as well. The EPIC MOS2 lightcurves can be seen in Fig. fig:AbsorbedAGN_lc , in several energy bands. There is no difference in the evolution of the soft (<2 keV) and hard (>2 keV) bands, meaning that the change is not in absorption but in the normalization of the power law. The cross-correlation between the soft and hard bands reveals that the soft emission lags slightly ( 0.8 0.3 ks) behind the hard emission (see Fig. fig:AGN_lag ).

gmw_00010315.name: J0122+1032
gmw_00010315.overview: J0122+1032 is an active galactic nucleus likely classified as a BL Lac type blazar [arXiv:2206.02129]. Archival optical data reveals a 2169.7-day periodicity, and the source may be associated with a nearby Fermi gamma-ray detection [arXiv:2206.02129].

sources.summary: J0122+1032 is presented as a likely BL Lac blazar with a 2169.7-day optical periodicity, ZTF/CRTS/WISE light curves, and a possible Fermi gamma-ray association.

quotes.quote: Analyzing the archival optical data obtained from large transient surveys, namely the Catalina Real-Transient Survey (CRTS) and the Zwicky Transient Facility (ZTF), we find periodicities of 2169.7, 2103.1, and 1462.6 day in sources J0122+1032, J1007+1248 (or PG 1004+1248), and J2131 1127, respectively. The optical spectra of the first two indicate that the first is likely a blazar and the second a type 1 Seyfert galaxy, and while no spectroscopic information is available for the third one, its overall properties suggest that it is likely an AGN.

J0122+1032 Little information is available for J0122+1032 (hereafter J0122), but there is an optical spectrum, taken on 2018 Nov. 29 (MJD 58451), in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) database (Figure fig:01spec ). The spectrum shows no obvious features, which indicates the source is likely a BL Lac type blazar. The source's coordinates in ZTF, as well as the object ID (oid), are provided in Table tab:data .

However, we note a ray source 4FGL J0122.4+1034, detected with the Large Area Telescope (LAT) onboard the Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope (Fermi) , has a position 2.2 away from that of J0122, while its 95 confidence error circle is 2.5 . Thus this ray source could be associated with J0122.

gmw_00010316.name: J2131 1127
gmw_00010316.overview: J2131 1127 is a candidate active galactic nucleus that lacks spectroscopic identification [arXiv:2206.02129]. However, its infrared colors place the source in the quasar category, and it exhibits a 1462.6-day optical periodicity [arXiv:2206.02129].

sources.summary: J2131 1127 is treated as a candidate AGN with a 1462.6-day optical periodicity; its infrared colors place it in the quasar category despite lacking spectroscopic identification.

quotes.quote: Analyzing the archival optical data obtained from large transient surveys, namely the Catalina Real-Transient Survey (CRTS) and the Zwicky Transient Facility (ZTF), we find periodicities of 2169.7, 2103.1, and 1462.6 day in sources J0122+1032, J1007+1248 (or PG 1004+1248), and J2131 1127, respectively. The optical spectra of the first two indicate that the first is likely a blazar and the second a type 1 Seyfert galaxy, and while no spectroscopic information is available for the third one, its overall properties suggest that it is likely an AGN.

J2131 1127 This source was detected by CRTS, ZTF, and WISE. The CRTS light curve is between 16--17 mag, and the ZTF zr is within the same range but the zg is notably 1-mag larger. The WISE W1 and W2 magnitudes are around 13 and 12 respectively. The information for the data is given in Table tab:data . There is no identification information for this source, but it was detected by the Two Micron All Sky Survey in the infrared JHK_s (2MASS; skr+06 ) and by WISE in the W1--W4 bands. Both its MIR colors (W1 W2 1.1, W2 W3 2.9; see, e.g., wri+10 ) and infrared colors (such as J-K_s 0.77 , K_s- W3 5.5 ; see, e.g., tw13 ) put the source in the quasar category.

gmw_00010317.name: MaNGA 1-113520
gmw_00010317.overview: MaNGA 1-113520 is a dwarf lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1609.01299] that features a kinematically distinct core likely composed of two counter-rotating stellar discs [arXiv:1609.01299]. The galaxy also hosts weakly-ionised gas that is kinematically misaligned from its stellar rotation [arXiv:1609.01299].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MaNGA 1-113520 as a dwarf/S0-like galaxy with a kinematically distinct core, counter-rotating discs, misaligned ionised gas, and group-like local environment.

quotes.quote: MaNGA 1-113520 At M_ = -18.29 , with R_ e = 0.94 kpc and _ e = 67 3 km s ^ 1 , MaNGA 1-113520 falls on the regions of the size-magnitude and luminosity relation typically occupied by dE galaxies. The galaxy exhibits a clear 180 ^ twist in its stellar kinematics at R = 2'' , within 1 R_ e . We therefore assume that the KDC is due to the presence of two counter-rotating discs. The galaxy has a disc-like morphology, and is listed as an S0 galaxy in the NASA Extragalactic Database.

It is located at a projected distance of 431 kpc and v= 14 km s ^ 1 from the nearest bright galaxy. All galaxies listed in the NASA Extragalactic Database within a projected radius of 1 Mpc from MaNGA 1-113520 are within v =400 km s ^ 1 , with a velocity dispersion of _ group = 160 km s ^ 1 . The galaxy exhibits weakly-ionised gas emission, and this gas is kinematically misaligned from the stellar rotation. This misaligned ionised gas may be the result of the accretion event responsible for the formation of the kinematically-distinct stellar components.

gmw_00010318.name: MaNGA 1-255220
gmw_00010318.overview: MaNGA 1-255220 is an isolated, quenched low-mass galaxy with an inclined edge-on disc morphology [arXiv:1609.01299]. Its central regions contain ionized gas that rotates with a 180-degree offset relative to the outer stellar component, indicating gas accretion that formed counter-rotating stellar discs [arXiv:1609.01299].

sources.summary: The paper describes MaNGA 1-255220 as an isolated quenched low-mass edge-on disc with ionised gas and kinematic evidence for gas accretion forming counter-rotating stellar discs.

quotes.quote: MaNGA 1-255220 MaNGA 1-255220 has M_ = -18.65 , R_ e = 0.96 kpc and _ e = 83 3 km s ^ 1 , and has a inclined edge-on disc morphology. It is remarkably isolated for a quenched low mass galaxy, and its nearest bright neighbour galaxy (UGC 05657, M_ K = -24 ) is located at a projected separation of 1.53 Mpc.

In its central regions, MaNGA 1-255220 exhibits weak H , indicating the presence of ionised gas. This ionised gas is rotating with an offset of 180 ^ to the outer stellar component of the galaxy. This suggests the two- structure seen in the stellar velocity dispersion map of MaNGA 1-255220 is the result of gas accretion, which resulted in the formation of two counter-rotating stellar discs.

gmw_00010319.name: NGC 4298
gmw_00010319.overview: NGC 4298 is an interacting spiral galaxy [arXiv:2012.00757, arXiv:2307.09154] located in the Virgo Cluster at a distance of approximately 14.92 Mpc [arXiv:2012.00757]. It forms a pair with NGC 4302 [arXiv:1304.1279, arXiv:2012.00757], displaying asymmetric optical and radio disks [arXiv:1304.1279] and hosting a newly discovered nuclear radio and X-ray source [arXiv:2202.13225].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4298 as paired with NGC 4302, with asymmetric optical, H I, and radio disks and 6 cm polarized emission only northeast of the center.

quotes.quote: NGC 4298 This galaxy forms a pair with NGC 4302. Its optical and H i disks are moderately asymmetric along the major axis with a more extended disk to the southeast. In addition, the H i is more extended and diffuse to the northwest while the other side of the disk shows a sharp cutoff (Chung et al. 2009). The radio continuum emission at 20 and 6 cm follow this asymmetry. The 6 cm emission extends beyond the optical main disk and H i disk northeast and west from the galaxy center.

sources.summary: The paper derives a new 14.92 Mpc TRGB distance for NGC 4298 and links it with NGC 4302 because their halos and H I bridge indicate common distance.

quotes.quote: NGC 4298 is a spiral galaxy that is a member of the Virgo Cluster. Previous best distance estimates relied on the TF relation, and measurements from Cosmicflows-3 found D = 13.0 3.0 Mpc. The PHANGS-HST parallel field lies in the combined halo of NGC 4298 and NGC 4302, a neighbouring edge-on spiral galaxy. The 21-cm HI maps for two galaxies show strong evidence for a bridge connecting the two galaxies 2015ApJ...799...61Z , though optical signatures of interaction are not obvious. We link the two galaxies together, and assume that they lie at the same distance. We use the stars in this parallel field to measure m_ TRGB = 26.89 0.18 mag, providing us with a new distance of D = 14.92 1.37 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper reports a newly discovered nuclear L-band, C-band, and X-ray source in the companion galaxy NGC 4298, with no previously known nuclear source.

quotes.quote: NGC 4298, which is the companion galaxy to NGC 4302 has a nuclear L-band/C-band/X-ray source right at its nucleus. This source is ID LCX =1 in Table t:cross_LCX in the NGC 4302 field. To our knowledge, there appears to have been no previously known nuclear source in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4298 as an overall radio-dim interacting galaxy, possibly reflecting a lower magnetic field and vertical cosmic-ray escape.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies are overall radio-dim: NGC 4298, NGC 4535, and NGC 4567.
On the other hand, NGC 4298 and NGC 4535 have radio luminosities about 2 or a factor of about two lower than expected based on their SFR.
It is remarkable that two out of three overall radio-dim galaxies are gravitationally interacting (NGC 4298 and NGC 4567).

gmw_00010320.name: g0927191-170053
gmw_00010320.overview: The galaxy g0927191-170053 is an old-type elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.0155 [arXiv:2205.07670]. It has been identified as the only candidate host galaxy for the gamma-ray burst GRB 130310A [arXiv:2205.07670].

sources.summary: The paper identifies g0927191-170053 as the only candidate host galaxy for GRB 130310A, gives its coordinates and redshift, and describes it as an old-type elliptical galaxy.

quotes.quote: Our search yields only one galaxy within that redshift range and error box, which is g0927191-170053 located at RA = +09^h 27^m 19.70^s and DEC = 17^ 00^ 53.0^ with a redshift of 0.0155.

Only one host galaxy, g0927191-170053 with RA = +09^h 27^m 19.07^s and DEC = 17^ 00^ 53.0^ and redshift = 0.015492, was found. The galaxy is also cross-matched in the Two Micron All Sky Survey Extended Source (2MASS), as 09271905-1700528 . The location of the galaxy is marked in Extended Data Figure fig:latloc .
We note that the merger origin is also consistent with the fact that its host, g0927191-170053, is an old-type elliptical galaxy .

gmw_00010321.name: SDSS J104257.58 + 074850.5
gmw_00010321.overview: SDSS J104257.58 + 074850.5 is a low-luminosity spiral galaxy with ongoing star formation [arXiv:0912.2575]. It contains a cold, corotating disk of neutral hydrogen gas with a surprisingly low column density [arXiv:0912.2575].

sources.summary: The paper describes a low-luminosity foreground spiral near a quasar sightline, detecting quiescent 21 cm absorption from cold disk gas with low H I column density.

quotes.quote: To search for 21 cm absorption from the ISM/halo of the foreground galaxy, SDSS J104257.58 + 074850.5 was observed with three complementary National Radio Astronomy Observatory (NRAO) telescopes: the 100 meter GBT, the VLA in the B configuration, and the VLBA.

The foreground galaxy is a low-luminosity spiral with on-going star formation (0.004 M _  yr ^ 1  kpc ^ 2 ) and a metallicity of 0.27 0.05 dex. We detect absorption from the galaxy with the Green Bank Telescope (GBT), the Very Large Array (VLA), and the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA). The absorption appears to be quiescent disk gas corotating with the galaxy and we do not find any evidence for outflowing cold neutral gas. The width of the main absorption line indicates that the gas is cold, T_ k < 283 K, and the H 1 column is surprisingly low given the impact parameter of 1.7 kpc; we find that N ( H 1 )   9.6   10^ 19  cm ^ 2 (GBT) and N ( H 1 )   1.5   10^ 20  cm ^ 2 (VLBA).

gmw_00010322.name: NGC 7733N
gmw_00010322.overview: NGC 7733N is a galaxy that overlaps with NGC 7733 and is moving at a relative mean velocity of +655 km/s [arXiv:2106.12441]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus classified as a Seyfert [arXiv:2106.12441].

sources.summary: The paper confirms NGC 7733N as a third galaxy overlapping NGC 7733, with separate redshifted emission lines, Seyfert excitation, tidal connection, and the least massive black hole in the triple AGN system.

quotes.quote: We will use `NGC 7733N' to refer to the knot in the following text. To check the nature of emission from NGC 7733N, we used the fluxes of H , [ O iii ] , H and [ N ii ] emission lines for all bins in the rectangle shown in Fig.  fig:velocity_33 . We divided the spectrum into three wavelength ranges, 4851--4883, 4996--5029, and 6532--6608 .

The resultant fits confirmed that NGC 7733N has its own set of emission lines. We calculated the mean velocity of H in NGC 7733 and NGC 7733N after fitting the emission lines. We found that NGC 7733N is moving with a mean velocity of +655 46 km s ^ 1 with respect to NGC 7733 and is at a redshift of 0.03619 0.00012.
The emission from NGC 7733N lies in the Seyfert region, indicating an AGN in this galaxy.

gmw_00010323.name: 2MASS 2342
gmw_00010323.overview: 2MASS 2342 is an extended galaxy candidate located southeast of NGC 7733 [arXiv:2106.12441]. It is connected to NGC 7733 by a bright ultraviolet bridge of hot gas, suggesting it is a tidally interacting member of the merging NGC 7733-34 group [arXiv:2106.12441].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASS 2342 as an extended fourth galaxy candidate southeast of NGC 7733, connected by a UV bridge and possibly part of the merging system.

quotes.quote: The FUV and J band images reveal an extended source (2MASS 23424898-6558257 referred to as 2MASS 2342) southeast of the galaxy NGC 7733. The FUV image reveals a bright bridge in the southeastern region connecting NGC 7733 to 2MASS 2342. The extended object in the southeastern region of NGC 7733 could be interacting with NGC 7733 and forms part of the NGC 7733--34 group. The projected spatial separation of 2MASS 2342 from NGC 7733 is 87 kpc assuming that both galaxies are at the same redshift.

The UV and NIR images show a fourth galaxy (2MASS 2342) lying southeast of the galaxy pair, which could be part of the merging system. We see a potential bridge between NGC 7733 and 2MASS 2342 in the UV map. This bridge is composed of hot gas that has been pulled out during the interaction phase.

gmw_00010324.name: RCP32
gmw_00010324.overview: RCP32 is a galaxy located at the farthest end of a trail of ultra-diffuse galaxies [arXiv:2312.11350]. It is hypothesized to be the preserved remnant of a post-collision satellite galaxy [arXiv:2312.11350].

sources.summary: The paper discusses RCP32 as a possible preserved post-collision satellite remnant at the end of the UDG trail, with globular clusters resembling those of NGC1052.

quotes.quote: vD22 postulated that NGC1052-DF7 (hereafter DF7) and RCP32, located at the farthest end of the UDG trail, represent the remnants of the progenitor galaxies. Notably, a follow-up study showed that the identified GCs near RCP32 exhibit stellar populations that are more akin to those of the host galaxy NGC1052, in contrast to the GCs associated with DF2 and DF4 Buzzo23 . This finding provides support for the notion that RCP32 may be the preserved remnant of a post-collision satellite galaxy.

In Figure fig:7 , the trajectories of the progenitors in our simulations and orbit integration are illustrated. One of the progenitors is located at the farthest from the host, roughly on the trail of DMDGs, suggesting that the progenitor might correspond to RCP32. On the other hand, the other progenitor passes through and orbits the host halo rather than remaining in the sequence of the DMDGs and the other progenitor. This suggests the possibility that one of the progenitor galaxies may have experienced strong tidal forces exerted by the host, potentially rendering it challenging to be detected in the present day.

gmw_00010325.name: NGC 1402
gmw_00010325.overview: NGC 1402 is classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1511.00431]. It is identified as an obscured, Compton-thick source with a strong iron-K emission line in its spectrum [arXiv:1511.00431].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1402 as an obscured, Compton-thick AGN candidate, with a continuum modeled by APEC plus lightly absorbed power law and a strong Fe-K line.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of NGC 1402 is not very flat, and a combination of APEC and lightly absorbed power law represents the shape of the continuum. The six objects, NGC 1402, IC 614, 2XMMi J184540.6 630522, NGC 6926, 2MASX J23404437 1151178, and NGC 7738, show an emission line at around 6.4 keV in the source rest frame with an EW in the range of 1.1--4.6 keV.
Obscured AGNs are found in NGC 1402, SDSS J085312.35+162619.4, IRAS 13443+0802NE, and IC 5264. The former object is Compton-thick and the latter three are Compton-thin.

gmw_00010326.name: IC 614
gmw_00010326.overview: IC 614 is an active galactic nucleus with a flat X-ray continuum [arXiv:1511.00431]. Its continuum is modeled by an optically thin plasma emission combined with a reflection component, and it displays a strong iron K-alpha emission line [arXiv:1511.00431].

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 614 as one of the flat-continuum objects, modeling its continuum with APEC plus reflection and reporting optically thin plasma emission and a strong Fe-K line.

quotes.quote: The six objects, NGC 1402, IC 614, 2MASX J11594382 2006579, NGC 6926, 2MASX J23404437 1151178, and NGC 7738, show excess emission around 0.6--1.0 keV suggesting the presence of emission from optically thin plasma. We used an APEC plasma model to represent this feature. An APEC + reflection model describes the continua of IC 614, 2MASX J11594382 2006579, and NGC 6926.

The six objects, NGC 1402, IC 614, 2XMMi J184540.6 630522, NGC 6926, 2MASX J23404437 1151178, and NGC 7738, show an emission line at around 6.4 keV in the source rest frame with an EW in the range of 1.1--4.6 keV.

gmw_00010327.name: 2MASX J11594382 2006579
gmw_00010327.overview: 2MASX J11594382 2006579 is an active galactic nucleus that is tentatively classified as a Compton-thick source [arXiv:1511.00431]. Its X-ray spectrum shows excess emission around 0.6 to 1.0 keV, which suggests the presence of optically thin plasma [arXiv:1511.00431].

sources.summary: The paper models 2MASX J11594382 2006579 with plasma and reflection/absorbed components, notes a possible Fe-K redshifted line, and tentatively classifies it as Compton-thick.

quotes.quote: The six objects, NGC 1402, IC 614, 2MASX J11594382 2006579, NGC 6926, 2MASX J23404437 1151178, and NGC 7738, show excess emission around 0.6--1.0 keV suggesting the presence of emission from optically thin plasma. We used an APEC plasma model to represent this feature. An APEC + reflection model describes the continua of IC 614, 2MASX J11594382 2006579, and NGC 6926. An APEC + absorbed power-law model also provided a similar quality of fit to 2MASX J11594382 2006579.

2MASX J11594382 2006579 shows a line-like emission at 5.8 keV. If this emission is an Fe-K line at 6.4 keV in the source rest frame, the redshift is estimated to be 0.112^ +0.019 _ 0.018 , though the improvement of the fit is only C = 3.0 for two additional parameters (normalization of a Gaussian and source redshift).

The Fe-K emission line is not significant in four (IRAS 00517+4556, 2MASX J05255807 6610523, 2MASX J05391963 0726190, and 2MASX J11594382 2006579) of the 10 objects, and their flat continuum could be interpreted as either a reflection-dominated spectrum or a combination of mildly absorbed ( 4 10^ 22 4 10^ 23 ) and less absorbed components. These objects are tentatively regarded as Compton-thick in the following discussion and denoted as ``Thick?" in the X-ray class column of Table tb:Xspec .

gmw_00010328.name: NGC 1297
gmw_00010328.overview: NGC 1297 is an early-type galaxy classified as a LINER in the optical bands [arXiv:1008.0009]. Its mid-infrared emission at shorter wavelengths is dominated by an old stellar population, while its polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon emission has been interpreted as evidence of a recent rejuvenation episode [arXiv:1008.0009].

sources.summary: Spitzer IRS spectra show NGC 1297 is a LINER early-type galaxy with unusual PAH emission, old stars dominating short-wavelength MIR light, and processed neutral PAHs linked to recent carbon-star rejuvenation.

quotes.quote: We present the analysis of Spitzer -IRS spectra of four early-type galaxies, NGC 1297, NGC 5044, NGC 6868, and NGC 7079, all classified as LINERs in the optical bands. Their IRS spectra present the full series of H _2 rotational emission lines in the range 5--38 m, atomic lines, and prominent PAH features. We investigate the nature and origin of the PAH emission, characterized by unusually low 6-9/11.3 m inter--band ratios.

We find the old stellar population contributes about 97.5 , 98.5 , 99.6 , and 98.5 of the observed fluxes at 5.6 m for NGC 1297, NGC 5044, NGC 6868, and NGC 7079, respectively. We then may consider that the old stellar population dominates the MIR emission at shorter wavelengths.

We advance a new scenario, where this PAH emission is naturally explained in terms of the presence of an underlying intermediate age population of carbon stars, which are continuously feeding the ISM with pristine carbonaceous material. If our interpretation is correct, the anomalous PAH spectra are strong evidence of a rejuvenation episode that affected the evolution of the ETGs in the recent past.

gmw_00010329.name: NGC 7079
gmw_00010329.overview: NGC 7079 is an early-type galaxy classified as a LINER [arXiv:1008.0009]. Its mid-infrared emission at shorter wavelengths is dominated by an old stellar population, though it also exhibits anomalous polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon emission that suggests a recent rejuvenation episode [arXiv:1008.0009].

sources.summary: Spitzer IRS spectra show NGC 7079 is a LINER early-type galaxy with unusual PAH emission, old stars dominating short-wavelength MIR light, and processed neutral PAHs linked to recent carbon-star rejuvenation.

quotes.quote: We present the analysis of Spitzer -IRS spectra of four early-type galaxies, NGC 1297, NGC 5044, NGC 6868, and NGC 7079, all classified as LINERs in the optical bands. Their IRS spectra present the full series of H _2 rotational emission lines in the range 5--38 m, atomic lines, and prominent PAH features. We investigate the nature and origin of the PAH emission, characterized by unusually low 6-9/11.3 m inter--band ratios.

We find the old stellar population contributes about 97.5 , 98.5 , 99.6 , and 98.5 of the observed fluxes at 5.6 m for NGC 1297, NGC 5044, NGC 6868, and NGC 7079, respectively. We then may consider that the old stellar population dominates the MIR emission at shorter wavelengths.

We advance a new scenario, where this PAH emission is naturally explained in terms of the presence of an underlying intermediate age population of carbon stars, which are continuously feeding the ISM with pristine carbonaceous material. If our interpretation is correct, the anomalous PAH spectra are strong evidence of a rejuvenation episode that affected the evolution of the ETGs in the recent past.

gmw_00010330.name: A370-01
gmw_00010330.overview: The galaxy A370-01 features a stellar disk that is dominated by star-forming regions [arXiv:2402.13315]. In contrast, the galaxy's tail exhibits mostly composite emission, and a shock front caused by ram pressure is present at the leading edge of the disk [arXiv:2402.13315].

sources.summary: The paper maps A370-01 metallicity, uses its disk spectrum as an example, and finds composite emission in the tail while the disk is mostly star-forming.

quotes.quote: As an example, A370-01 disk's stacked emission-only spectrum is shown in Fig. fig:Stacked_spectra . It contains the emission lines employed in the metallicity measurements.

In Fig. fig:A370_01 Met Map , we compare the metallicity map obtained by pyqz (left panel) and P04 (right panel) of the galaxy A370-01, while Fig. fig:p04_met_map and Fig. fig:pyqz_met_map cover rest of the sample. As these figures demonstrate, we can measure the gas metallicities also in regions outside of the stellar disk, in some cases only close to the disk outskirts and in other cases far from the disk, up to 50 kpc away (e.g. A370-08). h P04 Metallicity Maps h pyqz Metallicity Maps Metallicity trend with projected distance In order to investigate the behavior of oxygen abundances as we move further from the central region of galaxies to their tails, we compute the metallicity as a function of angular distance. It is important to note that while a deprojection procedure for the disk could be accomplished using the disk axial ratio, the deprojection of the tail is impossible due to the unknown orientation. Our study primarily centers on comparing metallicities in both the disk and tail regions, and as such, we exclusively employ angular and projected distances throughout the entirety of the paper. We present the metallicity gradient (projected) of all galaxies in our sample in Fig. fig:Metgrad , where we differentiate between star-forming and composite spaxels. The error bars represent the 1 percentiles, while the median values reflect the median metallicity of each radial bin, considering both star-forming and composite regions. Below, we present a list of several observed features that can be noted upon initial examination. label= . The P04 and pyqz metallicity trends qualitatively agree with one another, except in A370-03 where they differ only for the part of the tail that is closer to the disk, and in A2744-09 where the tail's trend is flat with respect to the disk using pyqz , while the metallicity increases in the tail for P04. No large systematic difference in the metallicity gradient behaviors of SF and composite spaxels is seen since they mostly follow the same path, except for the fact that in a few of the disks, the most deviant points are composite (typically towards low metallicities). A separate fit for composite and star-forming spaxels is only possible for the tails of two galaxies, A370-03 and A370-09, where the best fit of star-forming and composite spaxels is consistent within the uncertainties. The composite deviant points in the disks (Fig. fig:BPT_MAPS ) are found either in the region of the disk where the tail begins (thus they might actually be already a part of the tail seen in projection onto the disk) or at the shock front caused by ram pressure, right at the leading edge of the disk. This is evident in cases like A370-01, A370-03, A2744-06, A2744-09, and A2744-10. A370-06, the only one with an AGN in the sample, and A370-08 stand out instead for having the whole disk dominated by composite spaxels. Metallicity trends in the disks are typically negative or flat with two positive exceptions, A370-06, and A2744-10. Note that A370-06, the one with the AGN, has a large number of AGN-powered spaxels along an extended ionization cone and Fig. fig:BPT_MAPS 2022ApJ...925....4M , which are likely to affect our capability to assess the profile. In most cases, the tail metallicity declines with distance along the tail and is generally lower than in the corresponding disk, with three notable exceptions: A2744-04, A2744-09, and A2744-10. Moreover, the slope in the tail is often not the same as the slope in the disk (e.g. A370-03). This may be the result of projection effects since we are unable to establish the actual separation of tail spaxels from the galactic center. Overall, the global metallicity gradient (disk + tail) is negative or almost flat (except for A2744-10 and P04 estimate of A2744-09 which have sharp positive slopes as already discussed above). figure 9 h figure 9 h figure 9 h h In addition, ionization characteristics (composite vs. SF) of tails and disks are not necessarily coupled (see fig. fig:BPT_MAPS ). In 5 galaxies, these properties agree, being both tail and disk dominated ( >95 ) either by SF (A370-07, A2744-03, AA2744-04, A2744-10) or by composite emission (A370-06). However, in 3 other galaxies, the emission in the tail has probably a different origin from that in the disk see low-redshift studies 2019MNRAS.482.4466P,2021ApJ...922..131T . For instance, in A370-01 the tail is mostly composite while the disk is mostly SF.

gmw_00010331.name: A370-03
gmw_00010331.overview: The galaxy A370-03 features a ram pressure stripped tail with a metallicity gradient that declines with distance [arXiv:2402.13315]. The metallicity slope in this tail differs from the slope observed in the galaxy's disk [arXiv:2402.13315].

sources.summary: The paper reports that A370-03 has diagnostic-dependent tail metallicity near the disk, separable tail fits, composite deviant disk points, and slope differences between tail and disk.

quotes.quote: The P04 and pyqz metallicity trends qualitatively agree with one another, except in A370-03 where they differ only for the part of the tail that is closer to the disk, and in A2744-09 where the tail's trend is flat with respect to the disk using pyqz , while the metallicity increases in the tail for P04. The composite deviant points in the disks (Fig. fig:BPT_MAPS ) are found either in the region of the disk where the tail begins (thus they might actually be already a part of the tail seen in projection onto the disk) or at the shock front caused by ram pressure, right at the leading edge of the disk.
