Note that SINOPSIS (see Section subsec: spectral analysis ) cannot calculate the surface density of stellar mass for galaxies A2744-03 and A2744-04, since their spectra do not contain any information about the stellar continuum.

gmw_00010336.name: A2744-09
gmw_00010336.overview: A2744-09 is a ram-pressure stripped galaxy located in the Abell 2744 cluster [arXiv:2402.13315]. It features a shock front at the leading edge of its disk [arXiv:2402.13315], and its gas metallicity gradient exhibits exceptional behavior compared to other stripped galaxies [arXiv:2402.13315].

sources.summary: The paper finds diagnostic disagreement for A2744-09: pyqz gives a flat tail trend, P04 gives increasing tail metallicity, making conclusions difficult despite its exceptional gradient behavior.

quotes.quote: The P04 and pyqz metallicity trends qualitatively agree with one another, except in A370-03 where they differ only for the part of the tail that is closer to the disk, and in A2744-09 where the tail's trend is flat with respect to the disk using pyqz , while the metallicity increases in the tail for P04. No large systematic difference in the metallicity gradient behaviors of SF and composite spaxels is seen since they mostly follow the same path, except for the fact that in a few of the disks, the most deviant points are composite (typically towards low metallicities). A separate fit for composite and star-forming spaxels is only possible for the tails of two galaxies, A370-03 and A370-09, where the best fit of star-forming and composite spaxels is consistent within the uncertainties. The composite deviant points in the disks (Fig. fig:BPT_MAPS ) are found either in the region of the disk where the tail begins (thus they might actually be already a part of the tail seen in projection onto the disk) or at the shock front caused by ram pressure, right at the leading edge of the disk. This is evident in cases like A370-01, A370-03, A2744-06, A2744-09, and A2744-10. A370-06, the only one with an AGN in the sample, and A370-08 stand out instead for having the whole disk dominated by composite spaxels. Metallicity trends in the disks are typically negative or flat with two positive exceptions, A370-06, and A2744-10. Note that A370-06, the one with the AGN, has a large number of AGN-powered spaxels along an extended ionization cone and Fig. fig:BPT_MAPS 2022ApJ...925....4M , which are likely to affect our capability to assess the profile. In most cases, the tail metallicity declines with distance along the tail and is generally lower than in the corresponding disk, with three notable exceptions: A2744-04, A2744-09, and A2744-10. Moreover, the slope in the tail is often not the same as the slope in the disk (e.g. A370-03). This may be the result of projection effects since we are unable to establish the actual separation of tail spaxels from the galactic center. Overall, the global metallicity gradient (disk + tail) is negative or almost flat (except for A2744-10 and P04 estimate of A2744-09 which have sharp positive slopes as already discussed above).

As mentioned above, pyqz and P04 results do not agree for A2744-09 and it is, therefore, hard to draw any conclusion for this galaxy. For all the other galaxies (all with (M_ /M_ ) 9.7 ) the tail reaches metallicities lower than anywhere in their disk. Our findings are in line with the two existing studies to date at redshifts 0 , as reported in and . They examined the projected tail star-forming clumps of, respectively, 1 and 3 RPS galaxies with (M_ /M_ ) equal to 10.55, 10.96, 11.21, and 11.50. These studies revealed a maximum metallicity difference of 0.5 dex between the nearest and farthest parts of the tails from the disk by using the same modified version of pyqz used in this paper. It should be noted that the projected tail lengths observed in the sample of and are approximately 20-50 kpc. In contrast, our own typical projected tail lengths are equal to or less than 20 kpc (with only one exception of 50 kpc), and at considerably higher redshifts. Also, due to the differences in the approach employed, with our analysis focusing on the metallicity of individual spaxels The MUSE spatial resolution is not sufficient to identify individual star-forming clumps in z 0.3 RPS galaxies. while their work examined integrated spectra of star-forming clumps, a direct comparison with is not feasible. Mass-Metallicity Relation In this section, we first discuss the spatially-resolved mass-metallicity relation of each galaxy, distinguishing disk and tail spaxels. We then analyze the global mass-metallicity relation separately for disks and tails, to investigate whether the tail metallicity is correlated with galaxy mass as the disk metallicity is. Spatially-Resolved Mass-Metallicity Relation Fig. fig:MZR_TOT presents the resolved mass-metallicity relation of the whole sample with few exceptions. Note that SINOPSIS (see Section subsec: spectral analysis ) cannot calculate the surface density of stellar mass for galaxies A2744-03 and A2744-04, since their spectra do not contain any information about the stellar continuum. Moreover, the surface density of stellar mass for A2744-10 is not yet available. This figure illustrates that in both metallicity diagnostics, P04 and pyqz , the metallicity rises as we approach spaxels with higher stellar mass surface density, which is consistent with other findings on rMZR e.g., 2012ApJ...756L..31R,2022A&A...661A.112Y . The tail of the galaxy does not reach the highest surface mass density reached in the disk and at any given mass density its median metallicity trend follows that of the disk, with values that are similar or slightly lower. The rMZR of the disk is mostly flat or positive (only A370-01 and A370-09) and one case slightly negative (A2744-06). The stellar-mass surface densities of tail spaxels consistently populate the lower end of the density range observed in the disks, and they do not necessarily illustrate the same trend as the disks regardless of their parent galaxy stellar mass values (see Tab. tab:P04 stack+med table+z+M ). Moreover, the presence of spaxels in the tails with significantly lower metallicities than any disk spaxel of similar surface mass density is a noteworthy finding. This may indicate different enrichment processes in the tails compared to the disk, as previously mentioned. h figure 11 h Global Mass-Metallicity Relation The top panel of Fig. fig: global MZR shows the MZR (2nd order polynomial fit) that we derive for the stacked spectra of individual galaxies. For two reasons, disks and tails are investigated independently. Firstly, it is crucial to ascertain whether tails and disks exhibit a consistent mass-metallicity relation (MZR). Secondly, we can investigate the variation in global metallicity between the disk and the tail concerning parent galaxy's disk stellar mass and metallicity. Considering Fig. fig: global MZR 's top panel, for pyqz and P04, both disks and tails display a clear mass-metallicity relation. They notably follow a similar path with a positive slope (which is consistent with previous studies such as 2008A&A...488..463M, 2019MNRAS.484.3042S, 2020A&A...636A..84N ), and a flattening at high masses, in each metallicity diagnostic. Although , the sole analogous study available, utilized similar methodologies to those employed in this paper, a direct comparison of our results is unfeasible due to the use of distinct apertures. We contrast our findings with the MZR proposed by , as shown in the top right panel of Fig. fig: global MZR . employed a sample of 27,730 star-forming galaxies at z < 0.1 obtained from the SDSS DR4 catalog (thus fibre-integrated spectra) using the IMF and the P04 metallicity calibration in their analysis. Converting the IMF to the IMF, our findings are quite consistent with their relation within the reported uncertainty of 0.1 dex. h Furthermore, the global metallicity values of the disks and tails of individual galaxies exhibit a relative similarity, with the tail metallicities generally being slightly lower than those of their parent galaxy's disk, which is consistent with previous studies at low redshifts of individual galaxies by , , and the three galaxies by . Nevertheless, some exceptions exist, such as the galaxies A370-07 and A2744-03 in pyqz and a galaxy, A2744-10, in the P04 diagnostic, where the tail global metallicities are up to 0.1 dex higher than their corresponding disk values. Stacking spaxel spectra can result in biased derived properties if highly luminous spaxels with higher metallicity dominate the combined spectrum, similar to biases caused by stacking spectra with different star formation histories. By analyzing the median metallicity, we are taking into account the distribution of metallicities within the sample and are less susceptible to these biases. In the bottom panel of Fig. fig: global MZR we present the MZR using the median metallicity value for the disk and tail regions, with the inclusion of only those spaxels that meet both the BPT diagram and SNR criteria (see Sec. subsec: spectral analysis ). Also in this case, the global metallicities of the disks and tails within individual galaxies demonstrate a degree of similarity. However, it is noteworthy that the metallicities of the tails are generally slightly lower than those of the disks in their respective parent galaxies, with the exception of three cases, namely A2744-03 in the pyqz , and A2744-09 and A2744-10 in the P04 metallicity diagnostic. Therefore, the overall trends remain consistent with those observed in Figure fig: global MZR 's upper panel, where the MZR of the tail galaxies follows that of the disks, though with slightly but systematically lower metallicities at masses above 10^ 9.3 M _ . h Extraplanar tails of ionized stripped gas, extending up to several tens of kiloparsecs beyond the stellar disk, are often observed in ram-pressure stripped (RPS) galaxies in low redshift clusters. Recent studies have identified similar tails also at high redshift and we here present the first analysis of the chemical composition of such tails beyond the local universe. At first, we examine the resolved distribution of ionized gas metallicity of RPS galaxies in the Abell 2744 (z=0.308) and Abell 370 (z=0.375) clusters, the two most nearby clusters with the longest exposure time observed as part of the MUSE-GTO program. We investigate spatially-resolved and global metallicities in galactic disks and stripped tails, utilizing both a theoretical calibration through a photoionization model and an empirical calibration. The metallicity gradients and the spatially resolved mass-metallicity relations indicate that the metallicity in the tails reaches up to 0.6 dex lower values than anywhere in the parent disks, with a few exceptions. Both disks and tails follow a global mass-metallicity relation, though the tail metallicity is systematically lower than the one of the corresponding disk by up to 0.2 dex. These findings demonstrate that additional processes are at play in the tails, and are consistent with a scenario of progressive dilution of metallicity along the tails due to the mixing of intracluster medium and interstellar gas, in accord with previous low-z results. Additionally, we examined all RPS in the other 10 clusters, 0.3<z<0.55, observed via the MUSE-GTO program alongside a control sample consisting of field and cluster galaxies. Through an examination of their disk chemical compositions, we unveiled unique characteristics exhibited by RPS galaxies, providing valuable insights into the interaction between the ISM and the ICM during ram-pressure stripping and the environmental impact on galaxy evolution at intermediate redshifts. At low redshift, the gas metallicity of H bright clumps in RPS tails progressively decreases with increasing distance from the galaxy disk. This result was found for four strongly RPS galaxies at z 0.05 by and . The metallicity gradients of high-mass RPS galaxies ( (M_ /M_ )> 9.3) in clusters at z=0.3-0.4 presented in this work generally show qualitatively similar trends to those observed in low redshift RPS massive galaxies (with one notable exception, A2744-09).

gmw_00010337.name: ESO 336-G009
gmw_00010337.overview: ESO 336-G009 is a late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1510.01109, arXiv:1307.1791] located at a distance of approximately 80 megaparsecs [arXiv:1510.01109, arXiv:1307.1791]. It has a heliocentric radial velocity of 5834 km/s [arXiv:1510.01109, arXiv:1307.1791] and hosted the supernova SN 2012ca [arXiv:1510.01109, arXiv:1307.1791].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 336-G009 as SN 2012ca's late-type spiral host, gives the SN offset, host redshift/distance context, and an upper limit on local star formation.

quotes.quote: (PSN J18410706-4147374) was discovered in the late-type spiral (SABc) galaxy ESO 336-G009 by Parker on ut 2012 April 25.6 at an unfiltered magnitude of m 14.8 mag dr12 . We performed our own photometry on the discovery image and find it to be at R =14.94 0.03 mag, as reported in Tab.  table:snm (see Sect.  sec:ph for details of the photometry). The object coordinates have been measured on our astrometrically calibrated images to be = 18^ h 41^ m 07^ s .21 0^ s .05 , = -41^ o 47'03''.01 0''.05 (J2000). is located in an inner region outside the spiral arms of the host, 4.5 arcsec N/W of the centre of the host galaxy (Fig.  fig:fc ), which corresponds to a projected distance of 1.7 kpc from the nucleus for an adopted distance to ESO 336-G009 of 80 Mpc (see Section  ss:dr ).

Host galaxy properties NED NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database lists a heliocentric radial velocity of v_ hel = 5834 37 \ for ESO 336-G009. This is consistent with the redshift z=0.019 as measured from the narrow Balmer emission lines associated with SN 2012ca. Adopting a standard cosmology with H _ 0 =72 , _ M =0.27 and _ =0.73 , the distance modulus for \ is 34.52 mag (80.3 Mpc). The foreground Galactic reddening toward ESO 336-G009 is E_ g (B-V) = 0.06 mag from the dust maps.

While we were hence unable to measure the flux of the host galaxy at the position of the SN, if we take the measured flux as an upper limit we can still derive an upper limit to the star formation rate (SFR) of the host galaxy at the SN location. We measured a flux F _ Gal ( ) 7.30 10^ 15 (EW( )   45.3  ) which, given the distance of 80.3 Mpc, corresponds to L( H ) 8.65 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 and a star formation rate (SFR)   0.7 year ^ 1 , assuming solar metallicity and applying the calibration of ken94 and mad98 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 336-G009 as the late-type spiral host of SN2012ca, using its redshift and velocity for distance and noting low internal extinction at the SN location.

quotes.quote: was discovered in the late-type spiral galaxy ESO 336-G009 by dr12 , with a first detection on 2012 April 25.6 UT (m _r 14.8 mag).

From the emission component of the Balmer lines a redshift z=0.019 was measured, consistent with that of the host galaxy. Adopting a standard cosmology with H_0 = 72 , _ M =0.27 and _ =0.73 , NED NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database provides =34.54 0.15 mag from the heliocentric radial velocity of v_ host =5834 37 , which will be used throughout the letter. There is no detection of Na i interstellar medium (ISM) features from the host galaxy, nor do we have any evidence of significant extinction inside the host from the SN spectra itself. This suggests that the internal absorption is low and we assume it to be negligible. Moreover, is 3.5 kpc far from the centre of the host galaxy, this is consistent with the absence of significant extinction.

gmw_00010338.name: SDP.163
gmw_00010338.overview: SDP.163 is a highly inclined, massive disk galaxy classified as a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2111.09067]. It resides in a GAMA group and exhibits strong detections of carbon monoxide and dust continuum emission [arXiv:2111.09067].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDP.163 as the sample's most massive galaxy, a GAMA-group Sy 2, highly inclined disk with strong CO, dust continuum, and a nonuniform CO/C i ratio.

quotes.quote: SDP.163 (SK) This is the most massive galaxy with =2.3 10^ 11 , and f_g = 0.13 , residing in a GAMA group (ID104290) with estimated dynamical mass of 4.3 10^ 13 Robotham2011 . The GAMA spectrum (Fig. A163F ) has good SNR, and strong O [ ii ] and O [ iii ] . It is classed as a Sy 2 using the Lamareille2010 blue classification. The source, which is strongly detected in CO, and dust continuum, is a highly inclined massive disk galaxy with FWHM=700-800 in both CO and .

There is an extra kinematic component in the cube at the northern end of the galaxy which is not present in the CO map, but which is significant in the cube with S_ CI =1.7 0.5 Jy . The flux reported in Table CIdataT includes this component. This suggests that the CO/C i ratio is not constant across the galaxy.

gmw_00010339.name: SDP.1160
gmw_00010339.overview: SDP.1160 is a massive, gas-bearing galaxy that serves as the brightest member of a GAMA group [arXiv:2111.09067]. It features a companion galaxy at the same velocity 0.076 Mpc/h away, optical emission on a reddened continuum, and compact dust emission [arXiv:2111.09067].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDP.1160 as a massive, gas-bearing brightest GAMA-group member with a nearby same-velocity companion, optical emission, and compact dust emission.

quotes.quote: SDP.1160 (SK) This galaxy has = 1 10^ 11 and f_g = 0.07 . It is the brightest member of a GAMA group (ID103864) with a second galaxy at the same velocity a distance of 0.076 Mpc/h away. The GAMA spectrum (Fig. A163F ) shows O [ ii ] in emission on a reddened continuum. This source has bright and compact and dust emission (Fig. 1160F ).

gmw_00010340.name: SDP.2173
gmw_00010340.overview: SDP.2173 is a face-on spiral galaxy with a blue continuum and weak dust attenuation [arXiv:2111.09067]. Although it is not a member of a GAMA group, it has three dwarf companion galaxies at projected distances of 40 to 90 kpc [arXiv:2111.09067].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDP.2173 as a face-on spiral, optically bright and blue, with dwarf companions and spatially extended dust, CO, and line emission.

quotes.quote: SDP.2173 (SK) This is a face-on spiral galaxy with =7.9 10^ 10 and f_g=0.14 . The optical spectrum (Fig. A2173F ) shows broad lines of O [ iii ] lines and narrow O [ ii ] and H emission, the continuum is quite blue and there is weak dust attenuation. This is the brightest galaxy in the -band, with an absolute magnitude of R = -21.7 , and while not a member of a GAMA group, it has three dwarf companions with compatible photo-z ( z_ ph =0.37-0.38 ) at projected distances of 40--90 kpc. The dust, and CO emission are spatially extended, with a narrow profile consistent with the face-on orientation (Fig. 2173F ).

gmw_00010341.name: SDP.3132
gmw_00010341.overview: SDP.3132 is a face-on spiral galaxy with extended dust and CO emission [arXiv:2111.09067]. Its spectrum displays emission lines of O [II] and H-beta, and up to 60 percent of its continuum flux was resolved out in original 12-meter configuration observations [arXiv:2111.09067].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes SDP.3132 as a face-on spiral with extended dust, CO, and line emission, where ACA data recovered continuum flux resolved out by the 12-m configuration.

quotes.quote: SDP.3132 (SK) This is a face-on spiral galaxy with =5.4 10^ 10 and f_g= 0.21 . The GAMA spectrum (Fig. A2173F ) is noisy with emission lines of O [ ii ] and H _ . The dust, and CO emission are extended.

The inclusion of ACA data taken in Cycle 7 shows that as much as 60 percent of the continuum flux of this source was resolved out in the original 12-m configuration. The emission was not so affected, with a 10 percent increase once the ACA data were included. The CO and lines are very narrow with consistent profiles.

gmw_00010342.name: SDP.3366
gmw_00010342.overview: SDP.3366 is a massive galaxy classified in the Seyfert 2 region [arXiv:2111.09067]. It exhibits recent star formation activity and two velocity components, which suggest an ongoing interaction with a small blue satellite galaxy [arXiv:2111.09067].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDP.3366 as a massive Sy 2-region galaxy with recent star formation signatures, two velocity components, and an interaction with a blue satellite.

quotes.quote: SDP.3366 (DK) This galaxy is massive with =1.3 10^ 11 and f_g=0.04 . The GAMA spectrum (Fig. A3366F ) shows strong and broad lines of O [ iii ] and strong O [ ii ] . H _ and H _ are strong in absorption, Ca H is stronger than Ca K and there is no strong 4000 break, indicating a recent significant star formation event. The line ratios put it in the Sy 2 region of the Lamareille2010 blue diagram, however, with such strong absorption underlying the H line this will be a very uncertain classification.

The massive central galaxy has emission in two velocity components: 3366.a( v1 ) at the optical redshift and a blue-shifted component, 3366.a( v2 ), which overlaps in velocity with the broad emission from the small blue satellite galaxy (3366.b). These two components are shown in the upper right panel of Fig 3366F . This suggests an interaction between the two sources, probably responsible for the recent star formation activity in an otherwise older stellar population, as indicated by the optical spectrum and Sersic fits.

gmw_00010343.name: SDP.4104
gmw_00010343.overview: SDP.4104 is the southern member of an interacting galaxy pair [arXiv:2111.09067]. Both SDP.4104 and its northern companion galaxy exhibit dust continuum emission and detected CO line features [arXiv:2111.09067].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDP.4104 as the southern member of an interacting pair, with CO and dust emission detected in both the target and its northern companion.

quotes.quote: SDP.4104 (DK) The H-ATLAS target is the southern source (4104.a) of a galaxy pair, with =7.8 10^ 10 and f_g=0.13 . The GAMA optical spectrum (Fig. A3366F ) has a noisy continuum with detection of O [ ii ] , and very weak O [ iii ] . The neighbouring optical source to the north (4104.b) has z_ ph =0.37 from KIDs KIDSMLPz but was below the band limit for inclusion in the GAMA spectroscopic sample. There is a relatively narrow CO line feature close to the optical velocity of the target ( V_ CO =-72 ) which appears to be elongated along the major axis of the galaxy (see lower right inset panel in Fig. 4104F ).

A second, blue-shifted CO component is detected at the position of the northern companion, with a velocity offset of 360 , confirming that the two galaxies are interacting. The dust continuum image also shows emission at the location of both sources, albeit at very low SNR in the main target (which is extended).

gmw_00010344.name: SDP.5323
gmw_00010344.overview: SDP.5323 is a massive galaxy that exhibits a disturbed disk in its young stellar population and signs of a recent episode of star formation [arXiv:2111.09067]. It features a complex morphology with dust, CO, and line emission, and it has a small satellite galaxy located to its southwest [arXiv:2111.09067].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDP.5323 as a massive galaxy with recent star formation signs, a disturbed disk, and complex dust, CO, and line morphology.

quotes.quote: SDP.5323 (DK) This galaxy has =1.1 10^ 11 and f_g=0.11 . The GAMA spectrum (Fig. A5323F ) has strong O [ ii ] emission and weak but broad O [ iii ] . The Balmer series is visible in absorption with CaH being stronger than CaK and there is some residual H emission in the absorption trough, indicating a recent episode of star formation (A-star signatures). The low level optical emission is suggestive of a disturbed disk in the young stellar population and there is a small satellite of similar optical colour to the south west which has a 9-band photo-z of z_ ph =0.27 Wright2018 .

Figure 5323F ( top left ) shows a very complex morphology. Continuum dust emission is detected along the major axis of the galaxy with a peak located just south of the nucleus, while the appears in two peaks either side of the dust. The line emission is clearly resolved and so tapering was employed to improve the visibility of low level extended flux (yellow contours in Fig. 5323F ( top left ) are smoothed by 1.5 ). The top right panel shows CO contours on a tapered moment-0 map, and it can be seen that the CO appears to be more similar to the dust in its position and morphology but matched resolution observations are required to investigate further the correspondence between the CO and and dust.

gmw_00010345.name: SDP.5347
gmw_00010345.overview: SDP.5347 is a massive, mostly passive central galaxy [arXiv:2111.09067] that features two small, bluer companion galaxies to its west and north [arXiv:2111.09067]. The system contains an extended carbon monoxide structure stretching north-to-south, with dust emission detected at the northern companion's position [arXiv:2111.09067].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDP.5347 as a massive, mostly passive central galaxy with small blue companions, extended CO structure, northern dust emission, and weak dust elsewhere.

quotes.quote: SDP.5347 (DK) This galaxy has =1.3 10^ 11 and f_g=0.17 . The GAMA spectrum (Fig. A5323F ) shows a strong 4000 break and Balmer, Ca-H and K and Mg absorption features, but no emission lines are seen. H does appear right at the edge of the spectrum but there is often a problem with the throughput calibration in this region so we do not have confidence in this as the only line. The Krg image (Fig. 5347F top left ) shows the massive central galaxy `5347.a' and two small sources to the west and north (`b' and `') which have bluer optical colours.

The CO moment-0 map is integrated over the velocity range ( 200 +500 ) and shows an extended structure stretching N--S between the two small optical galaxies and the disk of the target. There is dust emission at the position of the northern optical companion (5347.), and also a background SMG (see Dunne et al. submitted ) to the North-East of the system. The dust emission elsewhere is very weak.

gmw_00010346.name: SDP.5526
gmw_00010346.overview: SDP.5526 is an isolated galaxy that exhibits rotation-like kinematics [arXiv:2111.09067]. It is well detected in carbon monoxide, neutral carbon, and dust continuum, with both its gas and dust being centrally concentrated [arXiv:2111.09067].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDP.5526 as an isolated galaxy with optical emission lines, clear CO, C i and dust detections, rotation-like kinematics, and centrally concentrated gas and dust.

quotes.quote: SDP.5526 This isolated galaxy has =6.6 10^ 10 and f_g=0.2 . The optical GAMA spectrum (Fig. A5526F ) shows strong O [ ii ] and also H in emission. At the very red end of the spectrum the N [ ii ] and H lines can be seen, though the spectrum is very noisy in this region. This source is well detected in CO, C i and dust continuum, with a narrow line-width and the signature of rotation in the velocity weighted moment map (Fig. 5526F ). The dust and gas are centrally concentrated, however there is lower level continuum emission which contributes significantly to the integrated flux in the tapered image.

gmw_00010347.name: SDP.6216
gmw_00010347.overview: SDP.6216 is an isolated, blue star-forming galaxy that is experiencing a declining recent burst of star formation [arXiv:2111.09067]. Its optical morphology is disturbed with a southern stellar caustic and northern clumpy star-forming regions, suggesting a recent interaction or disk instability [arXiv:2111.09067].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDP.6216 as an isolated blue star-forming galaxy with a fading recent burst, disturbed optical morphology, and differing dust and line emission.

quotes.quote: SDP.6216 An isolated, blue and vigorously star forming galaxy with =3.8 10^ 10 and f_g=0.33 . The optical GAMA spectrum (Fig. A5526F ) shows strong lines of O [ ii ] and H and weaker O [ iii ] . In conjunction with the strong Balmer absorption lines, this indicates a recent burst of star-formation, which is now declining. The magphys SED fit is shown in Fig. A5526F and is in agreement with the optical spectrum, favouring a high sSFR with low dust extinction.

The optical gri image (Fig. 6216F ) shows a sharp stellar caustic to the south-east and disturbed clumpy star forming regions to the North. This asymmetry suggests a possible recent interaction either with another galaxy (now subsumed), accretion from the cosmic web or simply disk instability. There are good detections of and CO, with a relatively narrow line profile (FWHM 250 ), as expected for this low inclination galaxy. The morphology of the dust and emission is different in this source.

gmw_00010348.name: SDP.6418
gmw_00010348.overview: SDP.6418 is an interacting galaxy system consisting of a main galaxy and a smaller, diffuse eastern companion [arXiv:2111.09067]. The system exhibits complex kinematics with disturbed gas and multiple offset velocity components [arXiv:2111.09067].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDP.6418 as an interacting system with a main galaxy and eastern neighbour, offset velocity components, and disturbed gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: SDP.6418 This appears to be an interacting system with the main galaxy, SDP.6418.a, having =6.2 10^ 10 and f_g=0.18 . The GAMA spectrum (Fig. A6418F ) shows a blue continuum with very strong O [ ii ] emission, and also significant O [ iii ] and H with H in absorption. There is H just visible at the red end of the spectrum. The smaller diffuse neighbour of similar colour to the east, has z_ ph =0.42 KIDSMLPz .

There are three components which are outside the systemic velocity range: v1 400 and v2 +250 are shown by the orange and red contours in Fig. 6418F (top left) and v3 +450 is shown in green, though v3 is less convincing in the spectrum. The spectral profiles are shown individually for each velocity component in Fig. 6418F with the colour of the spectrum matching the colour of the contours. The profile for the v1 component (which is present in both the main galaxy and the satellite) is shown as a solid orange line for the main galaxy and a dotted brown line for the satellite. The complex kinematics for this source, coupled with the presence of a neighbour at a similar redshift indicates that there is a disturbance of the gas but further observations would be necessary to be certain which of these features are real and related to an interaction.

gmw_00010349.name: SDP.6451
gmw_00010349.overview: SDP.6451 is a star-forming galaxy with strong dust and line emission, but relatively weak CO emission [arXiv:2111.09067]. The galaxy exhibits a wide blue line wing extended to its east, which may contain spiral arms or tidal material [arXiv:2111.09067].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDP.6451 as a star-forming galaxy with strong dust and line emission, weak CO, and an extended blue line wing with possible tidal material.

quotes.quote: SDP.6451 This galaxy has =5.6 10^ 10 and f_g=0.17 . The GAMA spectrum (Fig. A6418F ) has strong O [ ii ] and H in emission indicating ongoing star formation. There is only weak O [ iii ] indicating that this is not an AGN Lamareille2010 . Both dust and line emission are strongly detected, while CO is much weaker.

There is a wide blue wing in the line profile which is extended to the east of the galaxy. At full resolution this appears to have structure, possibly spiral arms or tidal material. The tapered map suggests that this may be another source where the dust and may be partially resolved out.

gmw_00010350.name: 4C -00.62
gmw_00010350.overview: The radio galaxy 4C -00.62 is located at a redshift of approximately z ~ 2.5 [arXiv:astro-ph/0606407]. Its surrounding field contains a sequence of very red galaxies and exhibits a high overdensity of color-selected galaxies, making it a strong candidate for a forming proto-cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0606407].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the field around 4C -00.62 as one of two strongest z~2.5 proto-cluster candidates, with red galaxy sequences and color-selected galaxy overdensities exceeding five.

quotes.quote: Some of the fields contain a bunch of very red galaxies ( J-K 2.3 ), in particular 4C  00.62 and 4C +23.56 show sequences of red galaxies which are likely to be progenitors of the colour-magnitude relation of early-type galaxies commonly seen in lower redshift clusters at z 1 . Therefore these two fields are most plausible high- z cluster candidates.

It is clear that the two fields, 4C  00.62 and 4C +23.56, contain quite high number densities of galaxies that satisfy the two-colours cut (Eq. \ 2) and they are among the most plausible proto-cluster candidates.

In particular, two radio galaxies fields, namely 4C  00.62 and 4C +23.56, show the highest overdensities of the colour-selected galaxies of more than factor of five. Therefore, we are likely to be witnessing the assembly and formation of proto-clusters with many evolved galaxies at z 2.5 associated to the radio galaxies.

gmw_00010351.name: host galaxy of GRB 050709
gmw_00010351.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 050709 is an irregular galaxy with a morphology dominated by an exponential disk profile [arXiv:0909.1804]. It is the site of the gamma-ray burst GRB 050709, which occurred at a secure sub-arcsecond offset of 3.7 kpc from the galaxy [arXiv:0909.1804].

sources.summary: The host of GRB 050709 is modeled as an exponential disk but is the sample's clearly irregular galaxy; its secure sub-arcsecond burst offset is 3.7 kpc.

quotes.quote: From the galfit analysis we find that three hosts (GRBs 050709, 051221a, and 061006) are best modeled with n 1 , corresponding to an exponential disk profile, while two hosts (GRBs 050509b and 050724) are best modeled with n 3 and 5.6 , respectively, typical of elliptical galaxies. We note that GRB 050724 possibly exhibits weak spiral structure, which may explain the resulting value of n 3 (see Figure  fig:050724 and mcd+07 ), but this putative spiral structure is clearly sub-dominant relative to the elliptical structure.

We also find significant residuals for a one-component S e rsic fit of the host galaxy of GRB 050709, which has an irregular morphology dominated by an exponential profile (Figure  fig:050709 ). This is the only clearly irregular galaxy in the sample.

The largest offsets are measured for GRBs 050509b and 051210, but these are based on Swift /XRT positions with statistical uncertainties of about 12 and 18 kpc, respectively (and possibly larger if we consider systematic uncertainties; sec:astrom ). If we consider only the bursts with sub-arcsecond afterglow positions we find that the largest offset is 3.7 kpc (GRB 050709), and that the median offset for the 6 bursts is 2.2 kpc.

gmw_00010352.name: host galaxy of GRB 051210
gmw_00010352.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 051210 is a disk galaxy that is modeled with an exponential disk structure [arXiv:0909.1804]. Radial surface brightness profiles indicate that the galaxy also exhibits a central bulge [arXiv:0909.1804].

sources.summary: The host of GRB 051210 has ambiguous GALFIT morphology but ellipse fits favor an exponential disk with a significant bulge; its XRT-position offset is large and uncertain.

quotes.quote: The final three hosts (GRBs 051210, 060121, and 060313) are equally well modeled in galfit with a wide range of n values, and we provide results for both n=1 and n=4 in Table  tab:morph . We find identical results using S e rsic model fits to the one-dimensional radial surface brightness profiles generated with ellipse (Figure  fig:sbfit_all and Table  tab:morph ). However, with this approach we find best-fit values of n 1 for the three host galaxies with ambiguous galfit results, suggesting that they are indeed better modeled as exponential disks.

Finally, we find that the hosts of GRBs 051210, 060121, and 061006 exhibit significant bulges, clearly seen in their radial surface brightness profiles (Figure  fig:sbfit_all ). For the host of GRB 061006, which was observed in two filters, the bulge component is more significant in the F814W filter than in the F555W filter, as expected for an older stellar population; the burst appears to coincide with this bulge component (Figure  fig:061006 ).

Our HST sample includes three short GRBs with strong extended emission (050709, 050724, and 061006), and one GRB (060121) with possible extended emission ( 4.5 significance; dls+06 ). The physical offsets of these bursts are about 3.7, 2.7, 1.3, and 1 kpc, respectively, leading to a mean offset of about 2.2 kpc. The physical offsets of the bursts without extended emission, but with precise afterglow positions (051221, 060313, and 061201) are 2.0, 2.3, and 14.2 or 32.5 kpc, respectively. The two events with no extended emission and with XRT positions (050509b and 051210) have offsets of about 54 12 and 28 23 kpc, respectively.

gmw_00010353.name: host galaxy of GRB 060121
gmw_00010353.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 060121 is a disk galaxy that is modeled well as an exponential disk [arXiv:0909.1804]. Radial surface brightness profiles reveal that the galaxy also exhibits a bulge component [arXiv:0909.1804].

sources.summary: The host of GRB 060121 has ambiguous GALFIT morphology but ellipse fits favor an exponential disk; it also shows a significant bulge and about 1 kpc offset.

quotes.quote: The final three hosts (GRBs 051210, 060121, and 060313) are equally well modeled in galfit with a wide range of n values, and we provide results for both n=1 and n=4 in Table  tab:morph . We find identical results using S e rsic model fits to the one-dimensional radial surface brightness profiles generated with ellipse (Figure  fig:sbfit_all and Table  tab:morph ). However, with this approach we find best-fit values of n 1 for the three host galaxies with ambiguous galfit results, suggesting that they are indeed better modeled as exponential disks.

Finally, we find that the hosts of GRBs 051210, 060121, and 061006 exhibit significant bulges, clearly seen in their radial surface brightness profiles (Figure  fig:sbfit_all ). For the host of GRB 061006, which was observed in two filters, the bulge component is more significant in the F814W filter than in the F555W filter, as expected for an older stellar population; the burst appears to coincide with this bulge component (Figure  fig:061006 ).

Our HST sample includes three short GRBs with strong extended emission (050709, 050724, and 061006), and one GRB (060121) with possible extended emission ( 4.5 significance; dls+06 ). The physical offsets of these bursts are about 3.7, 2.7, 1.3, and 1 kpc, respectively, leading to a mean offset of about 2.2 kpc. The physical offsets of the bursts without extended emission, but with precise afterglow positions (051221, 060313, and 061201) are 2.0, 2.3, and 14.2 or 32.5 kpc, respectively. The two events with no extended emission and with XRT positions (050509b and 051210) have offsets of about 54 12 and 28 23 kpc, respectively.

gmw_00010354.name: host galaxy of GRB 060313
gmw_00010354.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 060313 is a faint disk galaxy [arXiv:0909.1804] detected in Hubble Space Telescope observations [arXiv:0909.1804, arXiv:astro-ph/0611128]. It has the smallest measured effective radius among its comparison sample [arXiv:0909.1804] and is located approximately 0.32 to 0.4 arcseconds from the gamma-ray burst afterglow [arXiv:0909.1804, arXiv:astro-ph/0611128].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a faint galaxy as the likely host of GRB 060313, with a 0.32 arcsecond offset, low chance-coincidence probability, and the smallest effective radius.

quotes.quote: The smallest effective radius is measured for the host of GRB 060313, while the host of GRB 050509b has the largest effective radius.

We present the first host galaxy association for GRB 060313, using HST /ACS observations in the F475W and F775W filters. The offset between the GRB position (determined from Gemini-South observations; bfp+07 ) and the galaxy center is about 0.32'' (Table  tab:offsets ). The galaxy brightness is m( F475W )= 26.4 AB mag and m( F775W )=25.6 AB mag (Table  tab:hst ). The probability of chance coincidence at this offset and galaxy brightness is only about 3 10^ 3 bsk+06 . We thus conclude that this galaxy is the likely host of GRB 060313.

sources.summary: The paper identifies a faint HST galaxy close to the GRB 060313 afterglow, finds its chance alignment much less probable than a nearby bright galaxy, and rejects that bright galaxy association.

quotes.quote: A faint galaxy is detected about 0.4'' from the position of the afterglow in HST/ACS observations with F775W_ AB =26.2 0.2 mag and F475W_ AB =26.8 0.2 mag (Fox et al. in prep). The probability of chance coincidence is only 4 10^ 3 bsk+06 . The nearest bright galaxy has r_ AB 18.7 mag and is located about 27'' away (or about 80 kpc at z 0.2 ) from the optical afterglow position. The probability of chance coincidence for this galaxy is about 0.06, significantly larger than for the faint galaxy. Moreover, the detection of a bright optical afterglow from this burst requires a circumburst density, n 10^ 3 cm ^ 3 (e.g., sbk+06 ), which is unlikely at such a large offset from the host galaxy, where we would expect densities similar to the intergalactic medium If the burst occurred in a globular cluster associated with the nearby galaxy the density may be sufficient to produce an afterglow. .

gmw_00010355.name: BSDL 188
gmw_00010355.overview: BSDL 188 is a nearby galaxy with a radial velocity of 7100 to 8500 km/s [arXiv:astro-ph/0402623]. It is associated with a faint, slightly off-axis X-ray source and has a corresponding infrared counterpart [arXiv:astro-ph/0402623].

sources.summary: The paper treats BSDL 188 as a likely nearby galaxy associated with a faint X-ray source, noting catalog aliases, possible 2MASS counterparts, and uncertain X-ray geometry.

quotes.quote: XMMU J045427.5 662522 (source 4): One of the most interesting discoveries in this field is the X-ray emission associated with a small ellipsoidal object west of N11 L, roughly 28 24 in size. This extended object correponds to BSDL 188, also known in Simbad as MSX LMC 1241, which is classified as an emission nebula plus association in Bica et al. ( ). But this object also correlates well with an IR source (IRAS 04543 6630, or LEDA 89996, or QDOT B0454225 662949) classified as a nearby galaxy of radial velocity 7100-8500 (Lawrence et al. , LEDA database).

At that position, two extended objects are also listed in the 2MASS Extended Sources Catalog: 2MASX 04542820 6625275 (radius=29 ) and 2MASX 04542184 6625310 (radius=15 ). Since Bica et al. ( ) did only an imaging survey without any spectroscopic information, the BSDL 188 ``nebula" probably corresponds to this galaxy. Unfortunately, it lies outside the OM field-of-view and we thus cannot provide any information on the UV emission of this object. We note however that the X-ray source is not exactly centered on the extended object, but is slightly off-axis. We also remark that the X-ray source is not extended, as far as the resolution of XMM-Newton can tell.

gmw_00010356.name: GMP 3071
gmw_00010356.overview: GMP 3071 features an extraplanar ionized gas system consisting of narrow, slightly wiggled filaments that extend 25 kpc [arXiv:1202.1044]. This filament system is connected directly to the galaxy's nucleus and exhibits complex kinematics within the galaxy disk [arXiv:1202.1044].

sources.summary: GMP 3071 is described as having a narrow, slightly wiggled EIG connected to its nucleus, a 25 kpc filament system, and complex disk kinematics.

quotes.quote: The EIG of GMP 3071 consists of slightly wiggled narrow filaments (Figure 4f in YY10). The root of the EIG is connected to the nucleus of the galaxy. The angle between the galaxy major axis and the EIG is 40 degrees and the length of the EIG is 25 kpc.

The kinematics of both the GMP 2923 and GMP 3071 EIGs also reveal a monotonically-increasing pattern. Figure 13 and 14 show the velocity fields of the EIGs of the two galaxies. The acceleration rates are 10 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 and 25 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 for GMP 2923 and GMP 3071, respectively. The kinematics of the EIG of GMP 3071 is rather complex in the galaxy disk, showing a rapid drop-and-rise whose amplitude is 200 km s ^ 1 (Figure 14).

gmw_00010357.name: NGC 1149
gmw_00010357.overview: NGC 1149 is a galaxy with emission line ratios consistent with non-star-forming ionization mechanisms [arXiv:2007.08541]. Its central region exhibits extremely weak emission lines, and it cannot be confidently classified as harboring an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2007.08541].

sources.summary: The catalog note says NGC 1149 has only an X-ray upper limit, non-star-forming emission-line ratios, weak central MaNGA emission, and no confident AGN identification.

quotes.quote: Notes on Individual Objects 1-37155 - This galaxy is NGC 1149, and has an upper limit on the X-ray luminosity of L(0.3 - 10 keV ) < 2 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 , as reported in the 1SXPSC . The measured emission line ratios are consistent with non-star-forming ionization mechanisms in all three BPT diagrams, but the [ S 2 ] /H emission line ratio fall within the LINER-region section of the diagram, which are not necessarily indicative of an AGN. The central pixel from MaNGA shows extremely weak emission lines and no AGN-like continuum. We cannot confidently conclude that NGC 1149 harbors an AGN.

gmw_00010358.name: NGC 6166C
gmw_00010358.overview: NGC 6166C is the central cD galaxy of the Richness Class 2 cluster Abell 2199 [arXiv:2007.08541]. Because its observed X-rays may originate from the intracluster medium and its nuclear emission is weak, the presence of an active galactic nucleus cannot be confidently confirmed [arXiv:2007.08541].

sources.summary: NGC 6166C is identified as the central cD galaxy of Abell 2199; its X-rays may arise from intracluster gas, so the authors cannot confirm an AGN.

quotes.quote: 1-210784 - This galaxy is NGC 6166C, the central cD galaxy of the Richness Class 2 cluster Abell 2199. The observed X-rays in the 0.3--10 keV band could therefore be from to the system's intracluster medium. Additionally, the nuclear [ O 1 ] emission is very weak, with a S/N < 3 . In combination with the weak X-ray emission and the very weak [ O 1 ] emission, we cannot confidently conclude that NGC 6166C harbors an AGN.

gmw_00010359.overview: The galaxy 1-211023 has an upper limit on its X-ray luminosity of less than 4e43 erg/s [arXiv:2007.08541]. Its emission line ratios indicate non-star-forming ionization mechanisms, with oxygen and sulfur ratios that place it in the LINER region [arXiv:2007.08541].

sources.summary: Object 1-211023 has only an X-ray upper limit, non-star-forming emission-line ratios, and LINER-like oxygen and sulfur ratios, so the paper rejects an AGN identification.

quotes.quote: 1-211023 - This galaxy has an upper limit on the X-ray luminosity of L(0.3 - 10 keV ) < 4 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 , as reposrted in the 1SXPSC . The object's measured emission line ratios are consistent with non-star-forming ionization mechanisms in all three BPT diagrams, though the [ O 1 ] /H and [ S 2 ] /H ratios are in the LINER region of the corresponding diagrams. We therefore do not conclude that this object harbors an AGN.

gmw_00010360.overview: The galaxy 1-258876 exhibits a low X-ray luminosity below 9e40 erg/s [arXiv:2007.08541]. Its emission line ratios indicate non-star-forming ionization mechanisms, with sulfur emission falling within the LINER region [arXiv:2007.08541].

sources.summary: Object 1-258876 has low X-ray luminosity, non-star-forming line ratios, LINER-region sulfur emission, and incomplete oxygen measurements, preventing a robust AGN conclusion.

quotes.quote: 1-258876 - The X-ray luminosity of this galaxy is L(0.3 - 10 keV ) < 9 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 , as reported in the 1SXPSC . The measured emission line ratios are again consistent with non-star-forming ionization mechanisms, but the [ S 2 ] /H emission line ratio fall within the LINER region of the BPT diagram, which is not necessarily indicative of an AGN. In addition, the [ O I ] emission is not measured in all pixels within the central 2 92 aperture, which prevents a robust AGN identification. We therefore cannot conclude that this object harbors an AGN.

gmw_00010361.overview: The galaxy 1-281439 has an upper limit on its X-ray luminosity [arXiv:2007.08541]. It exhibits conflicting emission line ratios, with Seyfert or active galactic nucleus-like oxygen and nitrogen ratios but star-forming sulfur ratios, preventing confirmation of an active nucleus [arXiv:2007.08541].

sources.summary: Object 1-281439 has an X-ray upper limit and mixed emission-line diagnostics: AGN-like oxygen and nitrogen ratios but star-forming sulfur ratios, preventing AGN confirmation.

quotes.quote: 1-281439 - This galaxy has an upper limit on the X-ray luminosity of L(0.3 - 10 keV ) < 2 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 , as reported in the 1SXPSC . The galaxy shows Seyfert or AGN-like emission line ratios in the [ O 1 ] /H and [ N 2 ] /H diagrams, but its [ S 2 ] /H ratio is consistent with excitation by star formation. Given the conflicting evidence and non-detection in the X-ray, we cannot confirm the presence of an AGN in this galaxy.

gmw_00010362.overview: The galaxy 1-317315 displays Seyfert-like nitrogen ratios alongside LINER-like oxygen and sulfur ratios [arXiv:2007.08541]. Its central region shows very weak emission lines, and its unabsorbed X-ray flux is statistically consistent with zero [arXiv:2007.08541].

sources.summary: Object 1-317315 has poorly constrained X-rays, statistically consistent unabsorbed flux, Seyfert-like nitrogen ratios, LINER-like oxygen and sulfur ratios, and weak central MaNGA emission.

quotes.quote: 1-317315 - This galaxy has poorly-constrained X-ray emission as measured by XRT . While there is ``observed'' emission, the unabsorbed X-ray flux is statistically consistent with zero. The object also displays Seyfert or AGN-like [ N 2 ] /H ratios, but falls within the LINER-like region of the [ S 2 ] /H and [ O 1 ] /H diagrams. The central pixel of the MaNGA data shows very weak emission lines and no AGN-like continuum. We cannot confidently conclude that this object harbors an AGN.

gmw_00010363.overview: The galaxy 1-419607 shows no spectroscopic signatures of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2007.08541]. Although X-ray emission is detected from the source, it is poorly constrained and statistically consistent with zero [arXiv:2007.08541].

sources.summary: Object 1-419607 has no spectroscopic AGN signatures and poorly constrained X-rays, with unabsorbed X-ray emission consistent with zero, so it is not classified as an AGN.

quotes.quote: 1-419607 - This galaxy shows no spectroscopic signatures of an AGN, and the X-ray emission is poorly constrained. While X-rays are detected, the unabsorbed X-ray emission is still statistically consistent with zero. Therefore we do not conclude that this object harbors an AGN.

gmw_00010364.overview: The galaxy 1-569225 is classified as a star-forming galaxy by the SDSS collaboration [arXiv:2007.08541]. However, it exhibits strong X-ray emission and emission line ratios consistent with a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region, indicating that the galaxy harbors an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2007.08541].

sources.summary: Object 1-569225 is star-forming by SDSS but has strong X-rays and non-star-forming line ratios; the strong X-ray detection leads to an AGN conclusion.

quotes.quote: 1-569225 -This galaxy is identified as Star-forming by the SDSS collaboration, but exhibits strong X-ray emission. The measured emission line ratios are consistent with non-star-forming ionization mechanisms in all three BPT diagrams, but the [ O 1 ] /H and [ S 2 ] /H emission line ratios fall within the LINER-like region of the diagram, which are not necessarily indicative of an AGN. Given the strong X-ray detection, we conclude this object harbors an AGN.

gmw_00010365.name: IC 4227
gmw_00010365.overview: IC 4227 is a galaxy that harbors an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2007.08541]. Its central region exhibits strong narrow emission lines consistent with a Seyfert 2 spectrum, and its emission line ratios indicate Seyfert-like ionization mechanisms [arXiv:2007.08541].

sources.summary: IC 4227 has an X-ray upper limit compatible with an AGN, AGN/Seyfert-like line ratios in all BPT diagrams, and narrow-line Seyfert 2 central emission.

quotes.quote: 1-625513 -This galaxy is IC 4227, and the upper limit on its x-ray luminosity, L(0.3 - 10 keV ) < 2 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 as reported in the 1SXPSC does not rule out the presence of an AGN. Additionally, the measured emission line ratios are consistent with AGN or Seyfert-like ionization mechanisms in all three BPT diagrams, and the central MaNGA pixel shows strong narrow emission lines consistent with a Seyfert 2 spectrum. We conclude that IC 4227 harbors an AGN.

gmw_00010366.name: PKS 0620 52
gmw_00010366.overview: PKS 0620 52 is a Fanaroff-Riley type I radio galaxy associated with the brightest cluster galaxy of an infalling cluster [arXiv:2106.14543]. It features asymmetric wide-angle tail radio lobes [arXiv:2106.14543, arXiv:2012.08775] and exhibits weak, unresolved radio emission on milliarcsecond scales [arXiv:astro-ph/0009116].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 0620-52 as the FRI/WAT radio source associated with the Northern Clump BCG, with asymmetric bent lobes suggesting local southwest motion and gas interaction.

quotes.quote: This biggest infalling clump in the A3391/95 field is a galaxy cluster, located at redshift z=0.0511 Tritton_1972, Piffaretti_2011 . It hosts a Fanaroff-Riley type I (FRI) radio source, PKS 0620-52 Trussoni_1999, Venturi_2000 . This source is associated with the cluster's brightest cluster galaxy (BCG), 2MASX J06214330–5241333 Brueggen_2020 . This source is featured with a pair of asymmetric wide-angle tail (WAT) radio lobes Morganti_1993, Trussoni_1999, Venturi_2000 , with the northeast lobe extending up to 4' (240 kpc), while the northwestern lobe up to 2.5' (150 kpc) Brueggen_2020 .

We notice that the opening angle of the WAT radio source indicates movement towards the south. While looking at the bent direction of both radio lobes towards the northeast, the central radio source seems to move towards the southwest. We speculate that the radio lobes might have ''escaped'' from hot gas confinement and the change in direction is due to motion through a lower density medium, as indicated by the contours. The central radio source may be moving locally within the Northern Clump towards the southwest as a result of, for example, sloshing, while globally, the Northern Clump moves towards the southeast.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 0620 52 as a cluster brightest-member radio source with wide-angle tails, weak VLBI emission, unresolved 2.29 GHz structure, and likely sub-arcsecond extended flux.

quotes.quote: PKS 0620 52 This radio source is associated with the brightest member of a galaxy cluster, and its arcsecond scale morphology is typical of this large scale environment, since it exhibits a wide-angle tail morphology with two asymmetric tails (Morganti et al. 1993). Very little optical information is available for this source and its surroundings. BeppoSAX X--ray observations were not conclusive with respect to the existence of a non-thermal X--ray component of nuclear origin (Trussoni et al. 1999). PKS 0620 52 is the weakest source in our VLBI sample and it is unresolved by the present 2.29 GHZ observations. The image is shown in Figure fig:0620 .

Assuming that flux density variations in this source are negligible, comparison with the 2.3 GHz PTI flux (see Table 3), suggests that extended flux, not imaged by our observations, must be present on the sub-arcsecond scale.

sources.summary: EMU ES J0621--5241, alias PKS 0620--52, is a large wide-angle-tail radio galaxy beyond A3391's virial radius, hosted by 2MASX J06214330--5241333 and associated with RX J0621.6--5241.

quotes.quote: EMU ES J0621--5241 (PKS 0620--52) is a large wide-angle tail (WAT) radio galaxy (see Fig. fig:mainsource ) which is located far beyond the virial radius of A3391 and could be infalling into the system. Its host galaxy is 2MASX J06214330--5241333 at z = 0.0511. It is associated with RX J0621.6--5241. The two radio lobes have extents of 4 arcmin (NE; 240 kpc) and 2.5 arcmin (NW; 150 kpc).

gmw_00010367.name: PKS 1318 43
gmw_00010367.overview: PKS 1318 43 is a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy that features symmetric arcsecond-scale jets with a strong S-bend shape [arXiv:astro-ph/0009116]. The galaxy exhibits double-peaked HI absorption against its core and is believed to be interacting with the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 05091 [arXiv:astro-ph/0009116].

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 1318 43 as an FRI radio galaxy with bent symmetric arcsecond jets, possible interaction with NGC 05091, HI absorption, and resolved parsec-scale structure.

quotes.quote: PKS 1318 43 (NGC 5090) This source is a typical FRI radio galaxy (Morganti et al. 1993; Lloyd et al. 1996), with symmetric arcsecond scale jets and a total extension of 17 arcmin. The most prominent feature of the arcsecond scale jets is their strong S-bend shape, from the region closest to the core out to the end of the jets (Lloyd et al. 1996). One possible explanation for this interesting structure comes from the local environment of the optical counterpart, which is believed to be interacting with the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 05091 (Smith Bicknell 1986). HI absorption has been detected (using the VLA) against the core of this galaxy. The HI profile appears double peaked and the HI velocity derived is very close to the systemic velocity of the galaxy (v = 3421 km s ^ 1 ).

The parsec-scale morphology of PKS 1318 43 is shown in Figure fig:1318 . This image was obtained including only the baselines in the range 0 - 8.5 M , given the very noisy data in the longest baselines (due to the lower sensitivity of the array) and it was convolved with a restoring beam oriented in p.a. 0^ , so as to better show its extension. From Figure fig:1318 it is clear that the source is resolved, and we fitted it with two componens, i.e. a dominant compact one, which we assume to be the radio core, and a secondary peak at 18.8 mas, in p.a. +50^ , aligned with the northern arcsecond-scale jet.

gmw_00010368.name: IC 1291
gmw_00010368.overview: IC 1291 is a barred spiral galaxy of SBdm classification located at a distance of 31.5 Mpc [arXiv:1304.0447]. Hydrogen-alpha observations indicate that the galaxy is currently undergoing an epoch of star formation and hosts four detected X-ray sources, including two ultraluminous X-ray candidates [arXiv:1304.0447].

sources.summary: The paper reports four X-ray sources in IC 1291, with two strong ULX candidates and star-formation evidence, but notes distance, signal-to-noise, and background-contamination uncertainties.

quotes.quote: IC 1291 is an SBdm galaxy at a distance of 31.5 Mpc. We find four sources in this galaxy, two of which (X-1 and X-2) have fluxes consistent with ULXs at high significance ( >3 ), and two (X-3 and X-4) with moderate significance ( 1 ). X-1 and X-4 lie outside the D25 diameter listed in RC3, but within the D25 diameter listed in other catalogs (e.g. Tully 1988), so we include them in our table. H observations imply that this galaxy is in an epoch of star formation, and thus is expected to host a few bright X-ray sources. X-2 is perhaps the best ULX candidate, as it coincides with a bright H region and has a bright optical counterpart in HST images.

gmw_00010369.name: PGC 3853
gmw_00010369.overview: PGC 3853 is a bulgeless SABd disk galaxy located at a distance of 11.4 Mpc [arXiv:1304.0447]. It contains three detected X-ray sources, though some of these detections may be background contaminants [arXiv:1304.0447].

sources.summary: The paper describes PGC 3853 as a bulgeless SABd galaxy with three detected X-ray sources, most likely including contaminants, while one bright source may still be a ULX.

quotes.quote: PGC 3853 is an SABd galaxy at a distance of 11.4 Mpc. We find three sources in this galaxy, two that are dim ( 5 cts) and one that is bright ( 150 cts). M03 predict five CXB sources in this observation, implying that some or all PGC 3853 detections may be contaminants. Using H and HST images, we find no obvious optical counterparts to the dimmer sources, X-2 and X-3. Considering their low fluxes, we conclude they are likely distant background galaxies.

X-1 is bright enough that it is likely associated with PGC 3853, but it does not lie in an area of recent star formation, and does not have any obvious optical counterparts in HST imaging or our cross-correlation tables.

gmw_00010370.name: NGC 5964
gmw_00010370.overview: NGC 5964 is a bulgeless, gas-rich barred spiral galaxy located approximately 24.7 Mpc away [arXiv:1304.0447, arXiv:0909.1816]. It features an elongated bar surrounded by diffuse, fragmented spiral arms and lacks large-scale recent star formation [arXiv:1304.0447, arXiv:0909.1816].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5964 as a bulgeless SBd galaxy with four X-ray sources whose association is uncertain because background contamination is expected and large-scale recent star formation is weak.

quotes.quote: NGC 5964 is an SBd galaxy at a distance of 24.7 Mpc. There are four sources within the D25 ellipse of this galaxy, two (X-3 and X-4) that are below the ULX threshold ( 10 cts), and two (X-1 and X-2) that are above ( 20 cts). M03 predict 5 CXB sources in this observation, and patchy H emission implies an absence of large scale star formation in the recent past, so these sources are consistent with background contaminants. However, X-3 is relatively close to a region with a young stellar population, and might be associated with a black hole binary that has now traveled away from its birth location. The projected separation between the X-ray source and the nearby H source is 150 pc, and could be covered within a few 10s of Myrs at reasonable speeds.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5964 as a flocculent, gas-rich barred galaxy with diffuse fragmented arms, near solid-body inner rotation, and evidence for an underlying density wave.

quotes.quote: NGC 5964 hosts a very prominent and elongated bar surrounded by diffuse, extended, and fragmented spiral arms with no regular pattern ElmegreenElmegreen1987 . This flocculent galaxy is rich in neutral Hydrogen content ( M_ HI /M_ Tot = 82 ) and hosts a patchy circumnuclear structure GiovanelliHaynes1983, Bokeretal2002 . The \ rotation curve displays almost a solid body rotation in the inner 10 kpc. Our analysis shows that _p= 25_ 5 ^ +1 \ is well outlined in Fig. fig:allmaps with the location of the (CR) at 6.5 kpc.
